## lattice-system

A Lean 4 + Mathlib formalization project targeting general lattice models.
This project subsumes and generalizes the earlier
[ising-model](https://github.com/phasetr/ising-model) project, progressively
covering classical spin systems, quantum spin systems, Hubbard, BCS,
CAR algebras, and eventually lattice QCD.

## Design axis: graphs, not lattices

Despite the name, the **primary combinatorial abstraction in this
library is a graph `(Œõ, E_Œõ)`** ‚Äî finite for finite-volume work and
infinite for the thermodynamic-limit / algebraic-formulation work
that is a major long-term goal ‚Äî not "a lattice". Concrete lattices
(the 1D chain, square / cubic grids, infinite chains, ‚Ñ§^d, ‚Ä¶) appear
only as specific instances such as `SimpleGraph.pathGraph`,
`SimpleGraph.cycleGraph`, products of these, or their infinite
analogues. This convention follows the standard mathematical-physics
literature on many-body systems on graphs (Lieb's theorem on
bipartite lattices, the Marshall‚ÄìLieb‚ÄìMattis theorem, Miyao 2021
¬ß3, ‚Ä¶) and aligns the project with mathlib's `SimpleGraph`
foundations.

Finite-volume work uses `Œõ : Type*` together with `[Fintype Œõ]`
when needed (e.g. for traces, partition functions, finite sums of
local terms); infinite-volume work drops the `Fintype` assumption
and uses graphs over types like `‚Ñ§` or `‚Ñ§^d` instead.

The bridge from a `SimpleGraph` to the pairwise coupling
`J : Œõ ‚Üí Œõ ‚Üí ‚ÑÇ` consumed by `heisenbergHamiltonian` (and similar
operators) lives in `LatticeSystem.Lattice.Graph` (`couplingOf`).
Existing chain coupling families (`openChainCoupling`,
`periodicChainCoupling`) are characterised as
`couplingOf (pathGraph _) _` and `couplingOf (cycleGraph _) _`
respectively.

## Scope

| Area | Stage | Typical references |
|---|---|---|
| Classical spin systems | Inherited from ising-model | Friedli-Velenik, Glimm-Jaffe |
| Quantum spin systems | Current focus | Tasaki, Nielsen-Chuang (cross-check) |
| Hubbard / BCS | Medium term | Tasaki 1998, Bru-Pedra |
| CAR-algebraic formulation | Medium-long term | Araki-Moriya, Bru-Pedra |
| Thermodynamic limit (infinite graphs) | Long term, **major project goal** | Simon, Friedli-Velenik, Bratteli-Robinson |
| Lattice QCD | Longest term | Aarts, Davies |

## Refactoring conventions and review criteria

A **single-source-of-truth document** for refactoring conventions
applied as the review checklist on every pull request:
[Refactoring conventions and review criteria](refactoring-conventions.html).
Topics: test methods (decide / bridge / small-exhaustive / shim /
`#guard_msgs`), module-split criteria, generic / dedup conventions,
deprecation window policy, naming / docstring rules, linter
exceptions, public-doc synchronisation.

The companion page [Deprecation window](deprecations.html) tracks
currently-deprecated public declarations, the `since` date,
recommended replacement, and earliest-removal window.

| P1l (Tasaki ¬ß2.5, 2D / 3D Heisenberg) | 2D square-lattice + 2D torus + 3D cubic-lattice Heisenberg Hamiltonians via graph products `pathGraph (N+1) ‚ñ° pathGraph (N+1)` and `cycleGraph (N+2) ‚ñ° cycleGraph (N+2)`; Hermiticity + Gibbs state companion families (full 11-companion family per variant: `_isHermitian`, `_commute_hamiltonian`, `_GibbsExpectation_zero`, `_im_of_isHermitian`, `_commutator_hamiltonian`, `_mul_hamiltonian_im`, `_hamiltonian_sq_im`, `_hamiltonian_pow_im`, `_anticommutator_im`, `_commutator_re`, `_HamiltonianVariance_im`, `_partitionFn_im`, `_ofReal_re_eq`, `_pow_trace`) at parity with the 1D open / periodic chain | Done |
| P1m (Tasaki ¬ß2.5, generic-S sector form) | **Spin-S Marshall‚ÄìLieb‚ÄìMattis Theorem 2.2 on the magnetization sector**: generalisation of P1k from spin-1/2 / `H_0` to general spin `S` (`N = 2S`) and arbitrary magnetization sector `M` via the subtype `magConfigS V N M`. Sector matrices: shifted dressed (`shiftedDressedSReMatrixOnMagSector`), dressed (`dressedHeisenbergSReMatrixOnMagSector`), un-dressed real-form (`heisenbergHamiltonianSReMatrixOnMagSector`), and un-dressed complex-form (`heisenbergHamiltonianSMatrixOnMagSector`). PF application: `IsIrreducible` (#846), positive Perron eigenvector existence (#847) and uniqueness (#848) on the shifted sector matrix. Marshall sign conjugation forward (#853) + inverse (#854) gives a real-form sector eigenvector existence with Marshall sign structure. Eigenvector uniqueness (#854) at fixed `Œº` and eigenvalue uniqueness (#856, via dressed-sector symmetry + Rayleigh identity). Bundled real-form ground-state theorems: same-`Œº` form (#855) and forced-eigenvalue form (#859). Complex-form bridge: complex sector matrix Hermiticity + real-‚Üî-complex eigenvector correspondence (#857, #858, #861). Complex-form existence (#860), Marshall-positive uniqueness (#862), and **strongest bundled COMPLEX ground-state theorem** `marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergSector_complexGroundState_full` (#865) ‚Äî the COMPLEX-Hilbert-space form of Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.2 in the magnetization sector. Generic spin `S`, arbitrary bipartite-antiferromagnetic Heisenberg coupling supported on a connected bipartite graph, with the intermediate-existence hypothesis. The next step is the lift from the magnetization sector to the FULL Hilbert space ‚Äî comparing ground-state energies across magnetization sectors. | Done |
| P1n (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, final statement) | **Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3 (Lieb‚ÄìMattis, general spin-S, `\|A\| ‚âÝ \|¬¨A\|`) ‚Äî final statement.** The current `Prop` definition `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` is the structural, `h_intermediate`-free statement: it closes the conclusion from the physical hypotheses `(1 ‚â§ N, 1 ‚â§ \|A\|, 1 ‚â§ \|¬¨A\|)` rather than the older vacuous-at-`N=1` intermediate-support hypothesis. The canonical proof witnesses are `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_bipartiteToy` (PR #3891, toy coupling) and `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_of_bipartiteCompletePositive` (PR #3893, general bipartite `J` positive on the complete bipartite graph). These declarations are the structurally repaired replacements for the older `h_intermediate` capstones removed in PR #3917. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 | Truly-unconditional closure at all `N ‚â• 1` (PR #3891 toy, PR #3893 general) |
| P1o (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, Mattis‚ÄìNishimori anisotropic) | **Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4 (Mattis‚ÄìNishimori)**: ground-state uniqueness + `≈ú¬≥_tot \|Œ¶_GS‚ü© = 0` for the anisotropic XXZ + single-ion Hamiltonian (2.5.14) on a connected bipartite lattice with `\|A\| = \|B\|`, under (i) `‚àí1 < Œª ‚â§ 1, D ‚â• 0` or (ii) `Œª ‚â• 1, D ‚â§ 0`. The model is only U(1)-invariant (not SU(2)), so the proof uses even/odd-sector Perron‚ÄìFrobenius (at most double degeneracy) + a deformation argument from the SU(2) point `(Œª,D)=(1,0)` rather than MLM directly. dressed-and-bare assembly + axis-swap (#3833‚Äì#3837), giving `anisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_of_blocks_le_one` for general `N` (with `AxisSwapUnitaryS`) and bridge giving per-block submatrix-full intersection finrank equality, (h.2) #3841 strengthens involution decomposition to finrank equality, (h.3) #3842 combines (h.1)+(h.2) into `finrank ‚ÑÇ (eig M Œº) = ‚àë_p finrank ‚ÑÇ (eig M.submatrix_p Œº)` for parity-block-diagonal `M` commuting with `P`, (h.4) #3844 submatrix-based ‚â§ 2 wrappers. **(i) chain: Hermitian spectral chain** (#3846‚Äì#3855): (i.1) #3846 submatrix Hermiticity, (i.2) #3847 submatrix eigenvalue realness (`Œº.im = 0`), (i.3) #3848 submatrix minimum eigenvalue exists, (i.4) #3849 Hermitian eigenspace `= ‚ä•` below min, (i.5) #3850 block-diag eigenspace `= ‚ä•` below joint per-block min, (i.6) #3851 full eig `‚â§ 2` at min(per-block mins) given per-block `‚â§ 1` at min, (i.7) #3854 bare `ƒ§'` specialisation, (i.8) #3855 bare anisotropic `ƒ§` specialisation via axis-swap (general `N` with `AxisSwapUnitaryS` + spin-1/2 instance) ‚Äî the bridge to unconditional `‚â§ 2` modulo PF eigenvalue identification with `hermitianMinEigenvalue`. **(j) chain: PF eigenvalue identification** (#3857‚Äì#3861): (j.1) #3857 PF positive eigenvector exists for unshifted dressed submatrix, (j.2) #3858 lift to complex, (j.3) #3859 `hermitianMinEigenvalue ‚â§ Œº` from eigenvector existence, (j.4) #3860 packaged form, (j.5) #3861 consumer-friendly conditional bound at `hermitianMinEigenvalue` (needs the `ŒΩ_PF = hermitianMinEigenvalue` hypothesis explicit). **Target balanced-sector PF discharge** (Issue #3739): off-diagonal anisotropic/Heisenberg agreement, real sector matrices, the Marshall-dressed shifted anisotropic sector matrix, structural irreducibility, and Collatz‚ÄìWielandt PF/minimum identification now provide the balanced-sector `finrank ‚ÑÇ ‚â§ 1` input at the spin-1/2 target point and in the general spin-S target sector; the PF-free target wrappers no longer take an explicit `h_balanced_sector_pf` callback. **Strict-gap, SU(2)-uniqueness, and MLM endpoint wrappers** now replace the explicit balanced/full equality input by the standard strict gap over all non-balanced non-empty magnetization sectors, derive that strict gap from SU(2)-endpoint global uniqueness, and then construct the SU(2) uniqueness input from the general Theorem 2.3 MLM/Casimir/PF endpoint. **D-boundary extension**: the spin-1/2 parity-block route has a dedicated `D ‚â• 0` version because single-ion `¬±2` moves are impossible for `Fin (1+1)`. The general spin-S route now also has a bond-only parity reachability replacement for the single-ion branch, giving general `D ‚â• 0` parity-block irreducibility and the MLM/Casimir target wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_D_nonneg_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_D_nonneg_general`. **Œª=1 boundary**: at spin `1/2`, `(S^3)^2 = (1/4)I`, so `singleIonAnisotropyS D 1` is a scalar shift and `anisotropicHeisenbergS J 1 D 1` reduces to the Heisenberg Hamiltonian plus `D|Œõ|/4`; the SU(2) Theorem 2.3/MLM uniqueness transfers to target uniqueness and zero magnetization at `Œª = 1`. For general spin-S, the SU(2)-point wrappers cover `Œª = 1`, `D = 0`, and for `2 ‚â§ N` the ion-only parity reachability route gives the `Œª = 1`, `D > 0` endpoint wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_lambda_one_D_pos_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_lambda_one_D_pos_general`. **Case (ii) bridges**: the case-(ii) path-region lemmas show the deformation path stays in `Œª ‚â• 1`, `D ‚â§ 0`; the first case-(ii) target wrappers reuse the balanced-sector PF bridge while leaving balanced-sector/full-ground equality plus full `finrank <= 2` explicit; the strict-gap wrappers replace the direct balanced-sector/full-ground equality input by the strict sector gap; the no-full-finrank wrappers then use sector projection and Hermitian below-min exclusion to remove the full `finrank <= 2` input once strict gap is supplied; the crossing-callback bridge compresses the remaining strict-gap derivation to a single target crossing contradiction callback; the path-callback bridge reduces that target callback to a contradiction for crossings along the case-(ii) path; the crossing-set bridge reduces the path callback to contradiction for non-empty `perMCrossingSet M ‚à© Icc 0 1`; the first-crossing bridge reduces crossing-set non-emptiness to contradiction at `sInf (perMCrossingSet M ‚à© Icc 0 1)`; the argmin-first-crossing bridge reduces the target contradiction to the sector whose first crossing minimises those `sInf` values; the first-crossing finrank bridge proves that argmin callback from a `finrank <= 2` bound at the selected first crossing; the path-global finrank bridge moves this to every `t ‚àà Icc 0 1`; the block-path finrank bridge reduces it to full-ground-energy parity-block submatrix simplicity; and the block-PF/min bridge reduces that to pathwise bare parity-block PF simplicity plus PF/min identification; and the parity-gauge sign layer introduces the case-(ii) block gauge needed to flip exactly the ¬±2 parity-bond and single-ion moves; and the local sign layer proves strict shifted-entry positivity for transverse, parity-bond, and single-ion elementary moves; and the block reachability, block non-negativity, step-support, raw support-classification, and boundary move-set bridges reduce parity-block irreducibility to conditional irreducibility under the bipartite support-zero assumption on `J`. | The public general spin-S `2 ‚â§ N` parameter-region wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_general` package the general spin-S surface. The spin-`1/2` wrappers `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf` and `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf` now package the exact `N = 1` surface: `-1 < Œª < 1`, `D ‚â• 0`; `Œª = 1`, using the scalar-shift boundary; and strict case (ii) `1 < Œª`, `D ‚â§ 0`. |

and any ground state lies in the Hubbard hard-core subspace (`flatBand_groundState_mem_hardcoreSubspace`); **uniqueness framework subspaces (`TasakiFlatBandSubspaces.lean`)**: `flatBandAlphaFockSubmodule` (span of Œ±-Slater states, contains `|Œ¶Œ±‚ü©`) and `flatBandBKernelSubmodule = ‚®Ö_{u,œÉ} ker bÃÇ_{u,œÉ}` (contains every ground state, from 11.3.11) ‚Äî Tasaki's uniqueness is the inclusion `BKernel ‚äÜ Œ±Fock` + symmetric/maximal-spin classification; **rotated-basis Fock-spanning infrastructure toward it (`TasakiFlatBandModeCreation.lean`, `TasakiFlatBandModeMonomial.lean`, `Math/ListProdMulVec.lean`, Issue #4346)**: the single-particle-mode creation/annihilation maps `ƒà‚ÄÝ_œÉ(w)`/`ƒà_œÉ(w)` (`flatBandModeCreation`/`flatBandModeAnnihilation`, with √¢‚ÄÝ/bÃÇ‚ÄÝ/√¢/bÃÇ as values at Œ±/Œ≤), the operator-level single-particle change of basis (`flatBandModeCreation_eq_repr_sum`), the generic single-particle CAR `{ƒà_œÉ(w),ƒà‚ÄÝ_œÑ(w')}=(‚àë_x w(x)w'(x))Œ¥_œÉœÑ¬∑1` (`flatBandMode_annihilation_creation_anticomm`, giving the Œ≤-Gram and `{bÃÇ,√¢‚ÄÝ}=0`), and `flatBandModeFockSubmodule_eq_top` ‚Äî the rotated-basis Fock monomials `(‚àè ƒà‚ÄÝ_œÉ(basis i))|vac‚ü©` span the whole space (every `basisVec c` is an ordered site-creation product on the vacuum, and the span is invariant under each site creation), reindexed by occupation configs (card `2^(4K+4)=finrank`) into the **rotated occupation basis** `flatBandOccBasis`; **the hard inclusion `BKernel ‚äÜ AlphaFock` is now PROVED** (`flatBandBKernelSubmodule_le_alphaFockSubmodule`, `TasakiFlatBandUniqueness.lean`, axiom-clean): the Œ≤-Gram is invertible (PosDef, `flatBandBetaGram`), its inverse gives dual annihilators `d_{u,œÉ}=‚àë_v(G‚Åª¬π)_{uv}bÃÇ_{v,œÉ}` with `{d,bÃÇ‚ÄÝ}=Œ¥`, `{d,√¢‚ÄÝ}=0`, `d|vac‚ü©=0`; the projector `bÃÇ‚ÄÝ_{u,œÉ}d_{u,œÉ}` kills a bÃÇ-kernel vector (since `d v=0`), forcing every Œ≤-occupied occupation-basis coordinate to vanish, so the vector is a combination of Œ≤-free occ monomials (= Œ±-Slater states) ‚àà Œ±-Fock; with the easy inclusion, `BKernel = AlphaFock`; **dimension-route reduction of the uniqueness `‚â§` half (`TasakiFlatBandClassification.lean`, Issue #4346)**: `finrank(multiplet)=K+2`, `[≈ú^z_tot,ƒ§_flat]=0` (`fermionTotalSpinZ_commute_flatBandHamiltonian`, from `[≈ú^¬±,ƒ§]=0` + su(2) `≈ú‚Å∫≈ú‚Åª‚àí≈ú‚Åª≈ú‚Å∫=2≈ú^z`), `≈ú^z` preserves the ground subspace, the finite `≈ú^z`-weight decomposition `G=‚®Ü_{a:Fin(K+2)} G‚äìeigenspace(≈ú^z, a‚àí(K+1)/2)` (`flatBandHalfFilledGroundSubmodule_eq_iSup_weight`, off-weight blocks `‚ä•` since `NÃÇ=K+1` fixes the half-integer weights), and the capstone `flatBand_groundSubmodule_eq_multipletSpan_of_blocks` ‚Äî IF each `≈ú^z`-weight block of `G` is `‚â§1`-dimensional THEN `G=multiplet` (via `Math/EigenspaceWeightFinrank.finrank_le_of_weight_blocks` + `Submodule.eq_of_le_of_finrank_le`, using the proven `multiplet ‚â§ G`); the residual `finrank(block)‚â§1` (symmetric/maximal-spin core) and removal of the axiom are subsequent steps; **easy inclusion (`TasakiFlatBandAlphaFockKernel.lean`)**: `Œ±Fock ‚â§ BKernel` (`flatBandAlphaFockSubmodule_le_BKernelSubmodule`) ‚Äî every Œ±-Slater state is annihilated by all `bÃÇ` via the 11.3.7 anticommutation + move-through; the hard reverse `BKernel ‚äÜ Œ±Fock` needs the non-orthogonal-basis Fock factorisation, deferred; **Theorem 11.11 CAPSTONE (`TasakiFlatBandTheorem11_11.lean`)**: `flatBand_theorem_11_11_groundSubmodule_eq_multipletSpan` ‚Äî the half-filled (`N_e=K+1`) zero-energy ground subspace `ker ƒ§ ‚äì eigenspace(NÃÇ,K+1)` equals the ferromagnetic multiplet span (`2S_max+1`-dim, maximal spin), + maximal-spin corollary; `‚â•` (existence) fully proven, `‚â§` (uniqueness) uses a **single documented `axiom`** (Tasaki App. A classification, deferred like Theorem 11.8; existence half is axiom-free); **¬ß11.3.2 Mielke's flat-band ferromagnetism (`MielkeHamiltonian.lean`, Issue #4177)**: `mielkeHamiltonian` on a graph (uniform hopping + `2t¬∑NÃÇ` shift + `U`) with Hermiticity, `[ƒ§,NÃÇ]=0`, SU(2) invariance ‚Äî model setup; the line-graph structure + Theorems 11.12 (flat-band) / 11.13 (Mielke ferromagnetism) (`MielkeTheorems.lean`): `mielkeFlatBandDim` D(ŒõÃÉ,BÃÉ), `mielkeSingleElectronOp`, line graph via mathlib `SimpleGraph.lineGraph` + `SimpleGraph.Iso`; **`mielke_theorem_11_12`** (flat-band, connected base: single-electron kernel finrank = D) and **`mielke_theorem_11_13`** (Mielke ferromagnetism: biconnected base, N=D ‚áí ground subspace finrank = N+1 + all maximal spin) were documented **axioms**; **Theorem 11.12 is now PROVEN** (¬ß11.3.3, see below), leaving only **`mielke_theorem_11_13`** as an axiom (Tasaki states it without proof; the Hamiltonian model + symmetries are axiom-free); **¬ß11.3.3 incidence-matrix factorisation (`MielkeIncidenceMatrix.lean`, Issue #4180, DISCHARGES the 11.12 axiom)**: `mielkeSingleElectronOpOn` (single-electron operator generalised to an arbitrary finite vertex type; (11.3.36)), and **`mielkeIncidence_conjTranspose_mul_self`**: `S·¥¥S = mielkeSingleElectronOpOn (lineGraph G) t` for `t ‚â• 0` (eqs. (11.3.36)‚Äì(11.3.39)) ‚Äî the line-graph operator presented as a PSD Gram matrix with diagonal `2t` (edge degree 2) and off-diagonal `t` on shared-vertex edge pairs, kernel `= ker S`; the algebraic core for the rank‚Äìnullity + bipartite zero-mode count (11.3.41) discharging Theorem 11.12, sorry-free; **rank step (PR3)**: `mielke_lineGraph_ker_finrank_eq` ‚Äî for `t‚â•0`, `dim ker T = |B| ‚àí rank S` (via mathlib `ker_mulVecLin_conjTranspose_mul_self` giving `ker S·¥¥S = ker S` + rank‚Äìnullity), the Lemma 11.14 rank form; **zero-mode count + dim (PR4)**: `mielke_conjTranspose_ker_finrank` ‚Äî for `t>0` and a **connected** base, `dim ker(SS·¥¥) = dim ker S·¥¥ = (Colorable 2 ? 1 : 0)`, proved directly via `ker S·¥¥` (`(S·¥¥x)_b = ‚àöt(x_u+x_v)`; bipartite ‚áí 1-dim span of the alternating ¬±1 colouring vector, non-bipartite ‚áí trivial since a nonzero kernel vector would 2-colour the graph); and `mielke_lineGraph_ker_finrank_eq_dim` ‚Äî the assembled flat-band dimension `D = |B| ‚àí (|ŒõÃÉ| ‚àí bip)` (Theorem 11.12 general-base form; inner subtraction keeps it sound under ‚Ñï truncation, = Tasaki's `|B|‚àí|ŒõÃÉ|+1` once `|ŒõÃÉ|‚â§|B|`). **`mielke_theorem_11_12` (PR5, capstone)** ‚Äî **now a proved theorem** (formerly the ¬ß11.3.2 axiom): transports the flat-band dimension to the `Fin (M+1)` line-graph realisation along the `SimpleGraph.Iso` via `Matrix.rank_submatrix` (rank/kernel-dim invariance under equiv-reindex), with hypothesis `|ŒõÃÉ| ‚â§ |B|` (base has a cycle ‚Äî exactly where Tasaki's `|B|‚àí|ŒõÃÉ|+1` is the true dimension). Issue #4180 CLOSED); **¬ß11.3.4 Theorem 11.15 (general flat-band ferromagnetism, PROVED axiom-free, `GeneralFlatBandTheorem1115.lean`, Issue #4453)**: Mielke's general nec-&-suf condition ‚Äî for a Hermitian PSD hopping matrix `T`, flat band `h‚ÇÄ=ker T`, `D‚ÇÄ=dim h‚ÇÄ>0`, `U>0`, filling `N=D‚ÇÄ`, the model is saturated-ferromagnetic **iff** the `Œõ‚ÇÄ√óŒõ‚ÇÄ` projection submatrix `(P‚ÇÄ)` is Proof discharge of 11.15 is later work; **Theorem 11.17 discharge STARTED (Issue #4363, PR1 foundation, `GeneralFlatBandOperators.lean` + `GeneralFlatBandGroundAnnihilation.lean`, the latter split out in a build-speed refactor): general-flat-band smeared creation/annihilation operators `√¢‚ÄÝ_{z,œÉ} := ƒà‚ÄÝ_œÉ(Œº_z)` over an arbitrary special basis, full CAR algebra (unified, bilinear `{ƒà_œÉ(œÜ), ƒà‚ÄÝ_œÑ(œà)} = Œ¥_{œÉœÑ}(Œ£œÜœà)¬∑1`, site-dual), Slater states + √¢‚ÄÝ-monomial Fock submodule, single + double site-annihilation peel (eq. 11.3.48 engine), `generalCDownUp` + double-occupancy Gram identity, kinetic operator PSD from hopping PSD (`T = C·¥¥C` Gram-sum), Rayleigh decomposition, no-double-occupancy kill, ground state annihilated by every range-T mode (precise `conj(range T)` / `ƒà_œÉ(star(T¬∑w))` and nonzero-eigenvalue-eigenvector `ƒà_œÉ(≈´)` forms ‚Äî the exact orthonormal-eigenbasis occupation-detection operators), and the spectral `ker T ‚äï range T` classification + completeness `exists_ker_add_range_decomp` ‚Äî the operator-algebraic premise of eq. 11.3.46. **Theorem 11.17 PR2 (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandModeMonomial.lean` + `GeneralFlatBandOccBasis.lean`): general occupation basis over an arbitrary single-particle basis** ‚Äî bilinear creation‚Äìcreation anticommutation `{ƒà‚ÄÝ_œÉ(œÜ),ƒà‚ÄÝ_œÑ(œà)}=0` + nilpotency `(ƒà‚ÄÝ)¬≤=0`; general-basis Fock monomials `ƒà‚ÄÝ_œÉ(e i)`-products on the vacuum spanning `‚ä§` (mode + site creation invariance via the basis `repr`, computational-basis-vector expansion); reorder/permutation-scaling + repeated-mode vanishing; occupation monomials + `generalOccBasis` (a basis of the full Fock space indexed by occupation configs of the `2(M+1)` mode-spin slots, `2^(2(M+1))=finrank`). The Theorem 11.11 occupation-basis machinery, re-developed generically in the basis for instantiation at the spectral eigenbasis of `T`. **Theorem 11.17 PR3 (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandEigenbasis.lean`): spectral-eigenbasis annihilation peel** ‚Äî the orthonormal eigenbasis `T.IsHermitian.eigenvectorBasis` transported to a `Module.Basis` of `Fin(M+1)‚Üí‚ÑÇ` (`eigenbasisAsBasis`), its coordinate orthonormality `Œ£_x conj(e_j(x))e_k(x)=Œ¥_{jk}`, the dual CAR `{ƒà_œÉ(ƒì_j),ƒà‚ÄÝ_œÑ(e_k)}=Œ¥_{jk}Œ¥_{œÉœÑ}¬∑1`, the smeared-annihilator vacuum kill `ƒà_œÉ(œÜ)|vac‚ü©=0`, and the eigenbasis-annihilation peel `ƒà_œÉ(ƒì_j)|qs‚ü© = Œ£·µ¢ (-1)^i¬∑Œ¥_{j,(qs[i]).1}Œ¥_{œÉ,(qs[i]).2}¬∑|qs‚àñi‚ü©` (the occupation-detection engine). **Theorem 11.17 PR4 (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandSpanning.lean`): eq. (11.3.46) Fock spanning (hard direction)** ‚Äî the eigenvalue equation for the transported eigenbasis, the ground-state annihilation `ƒà_œÉ(ƒì_j)Œ¶=0` for every nonzero-eigenvalue mode, the eigenmode number operator `nÃÇ_{j,œÉ}=ƒà‚ÄÝ_œÉ(e_j)ƒà_œÉ(ƒì_j)` with its creation commutation `[nÃÇ,ƒà‚ÄÝ_œÑ(e_k)]=Œ¥_{jk}Œ¥_{œÉœÑ}ƒà‚ÄÝ_œÑ(e_k)`, its diagonality in the occupation basis `nÃÇ_{j,œÉ}occMon(g)=g(j,œÉ)¬∑occMon(g)` (list induction), the **coefficient vanishing** (ground-state occupation-basis coefficient = 0 on any config occupying a nonzero-eigenvalue mode), and the capstone **`flatBand_groundState_mem_flatFockSpan`**: a flat-band ground state lies in the span of the flat-band-supported (zero-eigenvalue) occupation monomials (`IsFlatSupported`). **Theorem 11.17 PR5 (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandFilling.lean`): filling-refined eq. (11.3.46)** ‚Äî the total fermion number operator is diagonal in the occupation basis (`NÃÇ occMon(g)=|occupied(g)|¬∑occMon(g)`, list induction via `[NÃÇ,ƒà‚ÄÝ(w)]=ƒà‚ÄÝ(w)`), so a `D‚ÇÄ`-electron ground state has occupation-basis coefficients supported on the `D‚ÇÄ`-electron configs; combined with the flat-band coefficient vanishing this gives `flatBand_groundState_atFilling_mem_flatFockSpan` (flat-band ground state ‚àà span of flat-supported occupation monomials with exactly `D‚ÇÄ` occupied modes). **Theorem 11.17 PR6 (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandMuTransport.lean`): transport of eq. (11.3.46) to the special basis** ‚Äî the coordinate identity `ker T = span{Œº_z}` (finrank transport via WithLp), the invariance of the `Œº`-Slater Fock submodule `generalFlatBandFockSubmodule Œº` under `ƒà‚ÄÝ_œÉ(w)` for `w ‚àà span{Œº_z}`, and the capstone `flatBand_groundState_mem_generalFlatBandFockSubmodule`: a flat-band ground state at filling lies in the `Œº`-Slater Fock submodule of the special basis (since the flat-supported eigenbasis monomials use only flat eigenvectors, each ‚àà `ker T = span{Œº_z}`). **Theorem 11.17 PR7 in progress (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandSpinConfig.lean`): index-mode number-operator machinery toward eq. (11.3.47)** ‚Äî the site-dual CAR localized on the index set I (`{ƒâ_{z,œÉ},√¢‚ÄÝ_{Œº_{z'},œÑ}}=Œ¥_{œÉœÑ}Œ¥_{zz'}Œº_z(z)`), the index-mode number operator `nÃÇ^Œº_{z,œÉ}=√¢‚ÄÝ_{Œº_z,œÉ}ƒâ_{z,œÉ}` with its creation commutation, its diagonality in the Œº-Slater states (`nÃÇ^Œº_{z,œÉ}|qs‚ü©=Œº_z(z)¬∑count¬∑|qs‚ü©`, list induction), and the **no double occupancy of index modes** `nÃÇ^Œº_{z,‚Üë}nÃÇ^Œº_{z,‚Üì}Œ¶=0` (operator identity = `√¢‚ÄÝ√¢‚ÄÝ¬∑generalCDownUp` + `ƒâ_‚Üìƒâ_‚ÜëŒ¶=0`). **Theorem 11.17 PR8 in progress (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandSpinRep.lean`): tight `I`-mode transport toward eq. (11.3.47)** ‚Äî the special basis `{Œº_z}` extends to a full `Fin(M+1)`-basis `eŒº` (`exists_extended_special_basis`); an `I`-mode `Œº`-Slater state equals a mode monomial over `eŒº` (`generalFlatBandSlaterState_eq_generalModeMonomial`, carrying them into `generalOccBasis eŒº` for linear independence); the tight `I`-mode `Œº`-Slater Fock submodule (`generalFlatBandIModeFockSubmodule`) is invariant under index-mode creation, and a flat-band ground state lies in it (`flatBand_groundState_mem_imode`, resolving the `z‚àâI` subtlety). axiom-free. **The filling embedding is an isometry + the Rayleigh bridge** (`TJFillingRayleighBridge.lean`): `tJFillingEmbedding_conjTranspose_mul_self` (`T·¥¥ T = 1`), `tJFillingExpansion_dotProduct_self` (`‚ü®T c, T c‚ü©=‚ü®c,c‚ü©`), and `rayleighOnVec_tJFillingCompress` (`rayleighOnVec ƒ§_tJ (tJFillingExpansion c) = rayleighOnVec ƒ§_W c`) ‚Äî connecting `groundEnergyAtFilling` to the matrix `ƒ§_W`; axiom-free. **The eigenvector lift is proven** (`TJFillingEigenLift.lean`, `mulVec_tJFillingExpansion_of_compress_eigen`): an eigenvector of `compress(A)` at `E` lifts (when `A` preserves `W`) to an eigenvector of `A` at `E` on `W`, via the projection identity `T T·¥¥ = id` on `W`; plus `tJFillingExpansion_mem_tJFillingWSubmodule`; axiom-free. Remaining for E2-‚â•: apply the matrix A.17 (`exists_joint_su2_energy_eigenstate`) to the min eigenvector of `ƒ§_W` (its hypotheses are the proven compressed su(2)/Hermitian/commute), lift it (this lemma), use odd `Ne` to force `≈ú¬≥=¬Ω` (`TJFillingSpinZDiag.lean`: `≈ú¬≥` is diagonal on filling expansions, `fermionTotalSpinZ_mulVec_tJFillingExpansion`, and the only `≈ú¬≥=0` filling state is `0` for odd `Ne`, `tJFillingExpansion_eq_zero_of_spinZ_mulVec_eq_zero`) ‚üπ `Œº ‚â§ hermitianMinEigenvalue ƒ§_W`, identify `groundEnergyAtFilling = hermitianMinEigenvalue ƒ§_W` via the bridge, completing `groundEnergyAtFilling = Œº`. **E2 is now COMPLETE** (`TJGroundEnergyGe.lean`): `tJ_perronFrobeniusMin_le_hermitianMinEigenvalue` (the W-restricted A.17 application), `tJ_groundEnergyAtFilling_ge_of_sectorMin` (`Œº ‚â§ groundEnergyAtFilling`), and the capstone `tJHamiltonian_groundEnergyAtFilling_eq_perronFrobeniusMin` (`groundEnergyAtFilling = Œº`, with the strictly-positive PF eigenvector `v` at `Œº`) ‚Äî resting only on Theorem A.17 (`exists_joint_su2_energy_eigenstate`, **now discharged** ¬ßA.3.2). **E3a (the SU(2) upper-bound seed, `TJGroundSpinHalfFinrank.lean`) is now COMPLETE**: `tJ_groundSubmodule_spinHalf_finrank_le_one` ‚Äî `finrank ‚ÑÇ (groundSubmoduleAtFilling ƒ§_tJ Ne ‚äì (≈ú¬≥=¬Ω)) ‚â§ 1` ‚Äî via `groundEnergyAtFilling = Œº`, the PF real `finrank ‚â§ 1`, the real‚Üîcomplex eigenspace bridge `matrix_complex_eigenspace_finrank_le_one_of_real`, and the injective `‚ÑÇ`-linear embedding `tJExpansionCoeff‚Çó` (`Œ¶ ‚Ü¶ tJExpansionCoeff Œ¶`, injective by sector-support) of the block into the complex sector eigenspace at `Œº`; supporting lemma `tJ_spinHalf_W_complexSectorEigen` (a block element's coefficient vector is a complex sector-matrix eigenvector at `Œº`); no new axioms. **E3b PR1 (`TJSectorRaise.lean`, toward lifting the PF vector to a highest weight) is COMPLETE**: the single-site raising operator `≈ú‚Å∫_x = ƒâ‚ÄÝ_{x‚Üë}ƒâ_{x‚Üì}` is **sign-free** on the sector basis ‚Äî `fermionSiteSpinPlus_mulVec_tJConfigOf_of_down` (`s x=‚Üì ‚üπ ≈ú‚Å∫_x|Œ¶_s‚ü© = |Œ¶_{s with x‚Ü¶‚Üë}‚ü©` with net Jordan‚ÄìWigner sign `+1`, since the adjacent orbitals `(x‚Üë,x‚Üì)=(2x,2x+1)` with `2x` empty collapse the two strings) + `fermionSiteSpinPlus_mulVec_tJConfigOf_of_not_down` (`s x‚âÝ‚Üì ‚üπ ≈ú‚Å∫_x|Œ¶_s‚ü©=0`) + the config identity `tJConfigOf_update_raise`; fully axiom-free. This Marshall sign-freeness makes the iterated `(≈ú‚Å∫)^m` have nonnegative config coefficients, so a strictly-positive coordinate (from `v q > 0`) gives the non-vanishing `(≈ú‚Å∫)^((Ne-1)/2) Œ¶‚ÇÄ ‚âÝ 0`. **E3b PR2 (`TJRaisingTower.lean`) is COMPLETE**: eigenvalue tracking along the raising tower ‚Äî `fermionTotalSpinZ_mulVec_spinPlusPow` (`≈ú¬≥ (≈ú‚Å∫)^k v = (m+k)(≈ú‚Å∫)^k v`, via `[≈ú¬≥,≈ú‚Å∫]=≈ú‚Å∫`), `tJHamiltonian_mulVec_spinPlusPow` (energy `Œº` preserved, `[ƒ§_tJ,≈ú‚Å∫]=0`), `fermionTotalNumber_mulVec_spinPlusPow` (number `Ne` preserved, `[NÃÇ,≈ú‚Å∫]=0`); so `(≈ú‚Å∫)^k Œ¶‚ÇÄ`, when nonzero, is a fixed-`Ne`, energy-`Œº`, `≈ú¬≥=¬Ω+k` eigenvector; fully axiom-free. **E3b PR3 (`TJRaisingTermination.lean`) is COMPLETE**: the raising tower terminates ‚Äî `fermionTotalDownNumber_mul_fermionTotalSpinPlus` (`[NÃÇ_‚Üì,≈ú‚Å∫]=‚àí≈ú‚Å∫`), `fermionTotalDownNumber_mulVec_spinPlusPow` (`NÃÇ_‚Üì (≈ú‚Å∫)^k v = (m‚àík)(≈ú‚Å∫)^k v`), and `spinPlusPow_succ_eq_zero_of_downNumber` (`NÃÇ_‚Üì v = m v ‚üπ (≈ú‚Å∫)^(m+1) v = 0`, since the `NÃÇ_‚Üì`-eigenvalue `‚àí1` forces `(‚Üìcount+1)¬∑œà(w)=0` per config); fully axiom-free. ground states of `tJExchange` have spin-swap-invariant bond amplitudes ‚Äî toward the degeneracy upper bound; axiom-free); PR3i-2 `TJHalfFillingBondGround.lean` `tJ_ground_bond_mulVec_eq_zero` (a half-filling ground state is annihilated by every adjacent bond: `bond_xy v=0` via the PSD sum `‚ü®v,tJExchange v‚ü©=0` ‚áí each `‚ü®v,bond_xy v‚ü©=0` ‚áí `Œî_xy v=0`; axiom-free); PR3i-3a `TJHalfFillingAmplitude.lean` `tJ_ground_amplitude_swap_invariant` (half-filling ground amplitudes are spin-swap invariant: `v(tJConfigOf s)=v(tJConfigOf(tJSpinSwap s x y))` for adjacent bonds, via filling-basis expansion + the bond half-swap action + `bond_xy v=0`; `tJFillingSwap` sector permutation; axiom-free); PR3i-3b `TJHalfFillingUpCount.lean` `tJ_ground_amplitude_eq_of_same_upCount` (half-filling ground amplitudes depend only on the up-count: equal up-counts ‚üπ equal value-counts ‚üπ adjacent-swap reachable [`adjacentSwapReachable_of_same_counts`] ‚üπ amplitudes equal by reachability induction over the per-bond swap invariance; axiom-free); PR3i-3c `TJHalfFillingDegeneracyUpper.lean` `tJ_halfFilling_groundSubmodule_finrank_le` (**upper bound `finrank G ‚â§ N+2`**: ground states determined by amplitudes constant on the N+2 up-count classes ‚üπ injective `G ‚Ü™ (Fin(N+2)‚Üí‚ÑÇ)`; with the lower bound #4326 ‚üπ `finrank G = N+2`; axiom-free); **PR3-cap `TJHalfFillingMaximalSpin.lean` `tJ_halfFilling_isMaximalSpinMultiplet`** (the half-filling t-J ground subspace is the maximal-spin `(N+2)`-fold multiplet: `IsMaximalSpinMultipletSubmodule N G (N+1)`, assembling `finrank=N+2` with the all-up SU(2) tower spanning `G`; boundary case of Theorem 11.26's t-J side; axiom-free, no A.17); **PR-unify `TJMaximalSpinUnified.lean` `tJ_isMaximalSpinMultiplet_of_le`** (the d=1 t-J ground subspace is the maximal-spin multiplet for ALL odd `Ne ‚â§ K+1`: metallic `Ne<K+1` via `proposition_11_24` + half-filling `Ne=K+1`; the half-filling chain generalized to drop `0<N` so `K=0` is covered; t-J input to Theorem 11.26, rests on A.17 via Prop 11.24); **PR-final: `theorem_11_26` is now a PROVED theorem** (`MetallicFerroModel.lean`, Issue #4314): discharged from `lemma_11_25` (documented strong-coupling-equivalence axiom) + `tJ_isMaximalSpinMultiplet_of_le`; `#print axioms theorem_11_26` = `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound, lemma_11_25]` (A.17's `exists_joint_su2_energy_eigenstate` now discharged ¬ßA.3.2). Tasaki ¬ß11.5.3 metallic-ferromagnetism Theorem 11.26 complete + **`theorem_11_26` (PROVED, Issue #4314)** (d=1, `Ne‚â§K+1=|E|` odd (Tasaki's `N‚â§L`) ‚áí `IsMaximalSpinMultipletSubmodule (3K+2) (groundSubmoduleAtFilling (decHubbardHamiltonian ‚Ä¶) Ne) Ne` for large `t,U` ‚Äî ground `S_tot=Ne/2`, `Ne+1`-fold; metallic when `Ne<K+1`); discharged from `lemma_11_25` (documented axiom) + `tJ_isMaximalSpinMultiplet_of_le`, so the model and Theorem 11.26 are proved modulo `lemma_11_25` (A.17 now discharged); Theorem 11.27 still a documented axiom); **generic single-particle-state operators (`SpinfulVectorOperator.lean`)**: `spinfulCreationFromVector`/`spinfulAnnihilationFromVector` (`ƒà_œÉ(œÜ)=Œ£_x œÜ(x)ƒâ_{x,œÉ}`), shared helper; axiom-free; **¬ß11.5.4 Tanaka‚ÄìTasaki model + Theorem 11.27 (AXIOMATIZED, `TanakaTasakiModel.lean`, Issue #4198) ‚Äî ¬ß11.5/Chapter 11 capstone**: the heavily-decorated lattice `Œõ=E√ó{1,2,3}‚à™I√ó{1,2}` (externals triplicated, internals duplicated), d=1 in `Fin(5K+5)` (`ttExtSite`/`ttIntSite`); special states `ttAlpha`/`ttBeta`/`ttDeltaP`/`ttDeltaI` (eqs. (11.5.19)‚Äì(11.5.22), `√¢` with `1/‚àö(3+4ŒΩ¬≤)`) + ops via `spinfulCreationFromVector`; `ttHopping` (`Œ£_{‚ü®p,q‚ü©}(‚àís √¢‚ÄÝ√¢‚àít bÃÇ‚ÄÝbÃÇ)+u‚ÇÅŒ£bÃÇ‚ÄÝbÃÇ+u‚ÇÇŒ£dÃÇ‚ÄÝdÃÇ`, eq. (11.5.24)) + `ttInteraction` + `ttHamiltonian` (finite); genuine `u‚ÇÇ,U‚Üë‚àû` limit objects `ttDKernel` (`dÃÇŒ¶=0`, via Theorem A.12) + `ttEffectiveHamiltonian` (`u‚ÇÇ,U=‚àû` effective hopping); **`axiom theorem_11_27`** (d=1, `u‚ÇÅ>2(|s|+2|t|)`, `K+1‚â§Ne‚â§2(K+1)` (Tasaki `L^d‚â§N‚â§2L^d`) ‚áí **in the limit** every ground state in `groundSubmoduleAtFilling (ttEffectiveHamiltonian ‚Ä¶) Ne ‚äì ttDKernel` has max spin `S_tot=Ne/2` ‚Äî taken as a genuine limit, not finite thresholds (Tasaki proves it only in the limit, warns finite `u‚ÇÇ,U` is not expected to work); weaker than the multiplet predicate, Tasaki claims only the spin; metallic when `1<Ne/L<2`); model axiom-free, `theorem_11_27` documented axiom ([63])) |

| Appendix A (Tasaki Mathematical Appendices) | The book-order content after Chapter 11; foundations for the deferred Chapter-11 proof discharges (frustration-free A.9/A.10, limit A.11/A.12, Perron‚ÄìFrobenius A.17/A.18 ‚Äî the last largely already in `Math/PerronFrobenius*`/`CollatzWielandt*`). **Complete ‚Äî A.1‚ÄìA.28 all formalized in book order** (Issues [#4205](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/issues/4205) + [#4224](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/issues/4224), both closed; see the **status & axiomatization policy** note below the Roadmap): **Theorem A.1 (Lie product formula)** `e^{A+B} = lim_N (e^{A/N}e^{B/N})^N` **now proved (axiom-free)** (`Math/MatrixAnalysis/LieProduct.lean`, `lieProductFormula` ‚Äî mathlib has only the commuting case): generic `trotterProductFormula` in a complete normed `‚Ñù`-algebra via exponential-series tail bounds (`‚Äñe^X‚àí1‚àíX‚Äñ ‚â§ ‚ÄñX‚Äñ¬≤e^{‚ÄñX‚Äñ}` etc.) + telescoping power estimate (`‚ÄñC‚Åø‚àíD‚Åø‚Äñ ‚â§ n¬∑M^{n‚àí1}¬∑‚ÄñC‚àíD‚Äñ`) + `O(s¬≤)` product comparison + the exact `n`-th-power identity, instantiated for matrices under the scoped operator norm; **Lemmas A.4** (Hermitian `√Ç` is `‚â•0` iff all eigenvalues `‚â•0`), **A.5** (`√Ç,BÃÇ‚â•0 ‚áí √Ç+BÃÇ‚â•0`), **A.6** (`BÃÇ‚ÄÝBÃÇ‚â•0`, and conversely every `√Ç‚â•0` is `ƒà¬≤` for a *unique* PSD square root `ƒà=‚àö√Ç` via `cfc Real.sqrt`, existence+uniqueness) **proved** (axiom-free, `Math/PosSemidef/Basics.lean`). **Theorem A.7** (Weyl monotonicity: Hermitian `√Ç‚â§BÃÇ` ‚áí `i`-th sorted eigenvalue `√Ç.eigenvalues‚ÇÄ i ‚â§ BÃÇ.eigenvalues‚ÇÄ i`) **now proved (axiom-free)** via the Courant‚ÄìFischer block/pigeonhole argument (`Math/MatrixAnalysis/CourantFischer.lean`: spectral Rayleigh-sum `re‚ü®x,Tx‚ü©=‚àëŒª·µ¢‚Äñ‚ü®b·µ¢,x‚ü©‚Äñ¬≤` + block bounds + the top-`(i+1)`/bottom-`(card‚àíi)` eigenspaces meeting by finrank pigeonhole; `EigenvalueMonotone.lean`). **Corollary A.8** (`√Ç‚â§BÃÇ ‚áí Tr e^√Ç ‚â§ Tr e^BÃÇ`, eq A.2.31) **now proved (axiom-free)** (`MatrixAnalysis/TraceExpMonotone.lean`: spectral mapping `Tr e^√Ç = Œ£ e^{a_j}` via `Matrix.exp_conj` + `exp_diagonal` + `trace_mul_cycle`, then A.7 Weyl + `Real.exp` monotone). **Lemmas A.9/A.10** (frustration-free Hamiltonian `ƒ§=Œ£ƒ•‚±º`, `ƒ•‚±º‚â•Œµ‚±º`: A.9 simultaneous eigenstate ‚áí ground state at `Œ£Œµ‚±º`; A.10 converse) **proved** (axiom-free, `FrustrationFree.lean`). **Lemma A.11** (`√Ç‚â•0 ‚àß ‚ü®Œ¶|√Ç|Œ¶‚ü©=0 ‚áí √ÇŒ¶=0`; `√Ç=BÃÇ‚ÄÝBÃÇ ‚áí BÃÇŒ¶=0`; + angular-momentum corollary `ƒ¥¬≤Œ¶=0 ‚áí ƒ¥‚ÅΩ·µÖ‚ÅæŒ¶=0`) **proved** (axiom-free, `PosSemidef/Kernel.lean`, over the existing `RayleighPosSemidefKernel`). **Theorem A.12** (`ƒ§_v=ƒ§‚ÇÄ+vVÃÇ`, `VÃÇ‚â•0`: `v‚Üë‚àû` finite-energy eigenstate families converging to nonzero `Œ¶` satisfy the effective Schr√∂dinger eq `PÃÇ‚ÇÄƒ§‚ÇÄŒ¶=EŒ¶` on `H‚ÇÄ=ker VÃÇ`) **now proved (axiom-free)** (`EffectiveLimit.lean`, weak-form + Tendsto: the paired eigenvalue relation gives `v¬∑‚ü®Œ¶_v|VÃÇ|Œ¶_v‚ü© = E_v‚ÄñŒ¶_v‚Äñ¬≤‚àí‚ü®Œ¶_v|ƒ§‚ÇÄ|Œ¶_v‚ü©`, the converging right side times `v‚Åª¬π‚Üí0` kills the limiting quadratic form, Lemma A.11 kills `VÃÇŒ¶`; pairing with `œà ‚àà ker VÃÇ` removes the `vVÃÇ` term outright and the limit is the weak equation). **Appendix A.2 complete.** **¬ßA.3 angular momentum**: **Lemma A.14** (su(2) ladder) ‚Äî operator identity `ƒ¥‚Åªƒ¥‚Å∫=ƒ¥¬≤‚àíƒ¥¬≥(ƒ¥¬≥+1)` (eq A.3.7) + ladder non-vanishing (`‚àíJ‚â§M<J`, `Œ¶‚âÝ0` ‚áí `ƒ¥‚Å∫Œ¶‚âÝ0`) + raising **membership** (`ƒ¥‚Å∫Œ¶‚ààH_{J,M+1}`, via `[ƒ¥¬≥,ƒ¥‚Å∫]=ƒ¥‚Å∫` + `[ƒ¥¬≤,ƒ¥‚Å∫]=0`) + **lowering** direction (`ƒ¥‚Å∫ƒ¥‚Åª=ƒ¥¬≤‚àíƒ¥¬≥(ƒ¥¬≥‚àí1)` eq A.3.8, `ƒ¥‚ÅªŒ¶‚âÝ0` for `‚àíJ<M‚â§J`, `ƒ¥‚ÅªŒ¶‚ààH_{J,M‚àí1}`) **proved** ‚Äî A.14 fully done (axiom-free, `AngularMomentum/Ladder.lean`). **Lemma A.15 (spin bound, part 1)**: norm identities `‚Äñƒ¥¬±Œ¶‚Äñ¬≤={J(J+1)‚àíM(M¬±1)}‚ÄñŒ¶‚Äñ¬≤` (eq A.3.9, Hermitian ƒ¥^Œ±) `angRaise/angLower_normSq` + `angMom_abs_le_J` (`‚àíJ‚â§M‚â§J`, i.e. `J‚àíM,J+M‚â•0`) **proved** (axiom-free). **Lemma A.15 (integrality)** + **Theorem A.13 (`J = n/2`)**: ladder-termination `raiseIter` (iterated `ƒ¥‚Å∫`) with `raiseIter_eigenspace`/`raiseIter_ne_zero` gives `J‚àíM ‚àà ‚Ñ§‚â•0` (`angMom_sub_mem_nat`); the gauge-reflected `(ƒ¥¬π,‚àíƒ¥¬≤,‚àíƒ¥¬≥)` gives `J+M ‚àà ‚Ñ§‚â•0`, so `2J=(J‚àíM)+(J+M)‚àà‚Ñ§‚â•0`, i.e. `J=n/2` (`angMom_J_eq_half_nat`) **proved** (axiom-free, `AngularMomentum/Quantization.lean`). **Theorem A.16 (SU(2)-multiplet degeneracy)**: for SU(2)-invariant `ƒ§` (`[ƒ§,ƒ¥·µÖ]=0`), a joint energy eigenstate in `H_{J,M0}` yields nonzero same-energy companions in every `H_{J,J‚àík}` (`k‚â§2J`) ‚Äî `ham_su2_multiplet` (energy `E` is ‚â•`(2J+1)`-fold degenerate), proved via raise-to-top + reflected-lowering ladder + `ham_mulVec_raiseIter` (axiom-free, `AngularMomentum/Multiplet.lean`). **Theorem A.17 (spin-0/1-2 sector suffices)**: every energy eigenvalue of an SU(2)-invariant `ƒ§` has an eigenstate with `ƒ¥¬≥=0` or `ƒ¥¬≥=1/2` ‚Äî `ham_eigenstate_spin_zero_or_half` (corollary of A.16+A.13; the lone simultaneous-diagonalization step `exists_joint_su2_energy_eigenstate` is **now proved (axiom-free)** via the generic common eigenvector of two commuting Hermitian operators on an invariant subspace (`Math/CommutingHermitianEigenvector.lean`: `exists_common_eigenvector_of_isHermitian_commute`), the Casimir `ƒ¥¬≤` being PSD and commuting with `ƒ¥¬≥`/`ƒ§`, `AngularMomentum/SpinHalfSector.lean`). **Theorem A.18 (Perron‚ÄìFrobenius, real symmetric)**: a real symmetric `M` with `(c¬∑1‚àíM)` irreducible (off-diag `‚â§0` + connectivity) has a nondegenerate lowest eigenvalue with a strictly positive eigenvector ‚Äî `perronFrobenius_real_symmetric` (reuses the project's Collatz‚ÄìWielandt PF + eigenspace simplicity; the lowest-eigenvalue identification is the variational `|w|`-argument eq A.4.1), proved (axiom-free, `PerronFrobeniusSymmetric.lean`). **Theorems A.19/A.20 (polar + SVD)**: `matrix_polar_decomposition` (`A = W C`, `W` unitary, `C` PSD) and `matrix_singular_value_decomposition` (`A = U D V‚ÄÝ`, `U,V` unitary, `D = diagonal d` with `d ‚â• 0`) ‚Äî **now proved (axiom-free)**: SVD built from the spectral theorem of `A·¥¥A` (`eigenvectorUnitary` `V`, eigenvalues `Œª_i ‚â• 0`, `d_i = ‚àöŒª_i`), with the normalised images `u_i = d_i‚Åª¬π¬∑A v_i` extended to an orthonormal basis (`exists_orthonormalBasis_extension_of_card_eq`) giving `U`, and `A V = U D` columnwise; polar follows as `W = U V·¥¥`, `C = V D V·¥¥` (`MatrixAnalysis/Decomposition.lean`). **Theorems A.21/A.22 (Wigner)**: `wignerAutomorphism_unitary` (linear ‚àó-automorphism of `M(H)` ‚üπ `Œì(√Ç)=√õ‚ÄÝ√Ç√õ`) + `wignerAutomorphism_antiunitary` (antilinear ‚üπ `√õ‚ÄÝ√ÇÃÑ√õ`) + `wignerProjection` (rank-1-projection map preserving `Tr[P P‚Ä≤]` ‚üπ (anti)unitary `VÃÇ`) ‚Äî documented axioms over `Matrix D D ‚ÑÇ` (`WignerTheorem.lean`, Issue #4224). **Definition A.23 + Theorem A.24 (states / Banach‚ÄìAlaoglu)**: `IsState` (weak-‚àó-continuous `œÜ : WeakDual ‚ÑÇ A` with `œÜ 1 = 1`, `0 ‚â§ œÜ(a‚ÄÝa)`) over an abstract unital C*-algebra + `stateSpace_isCompact` (state space weak-‚àó compact, documented axiom) ‚Äî `CStarAlgebra/State.lean`. **Definitions A.25/A.27 + Theorem A.26 (ground states)**: dynamics modelled by a derivation `Œ¥=[ƒ§,¬∑]`; `IsGroundState` (`0 ‚â§ œâ(a‚ÄÝ¬∑Œ¥a)`) + `HasNonzeroGap` (`Œ≥>0`, `œâ(a‚ÄÝŒ¥a) ‚â• Œ≥œâ(a‚ÄÝa)` on `œâ(a)=0`) + `groundState_variational` (ground state ‚ü∫ `œâ(ƒ§_L)` least over states agreeing outside Œõ_L, documented axiom) ‚Äî `CStarAlgebra/GroundState.lean`. **Theorem A.28 (GNS construction)**: `gns_construction` ‚Äî every state `œÅ` on a C*-algebra has a GNS triple `(H_œÅ, œÄ_œÅ, Œ©_œÅ)` (`‚àó`-representation `œÄ : A ‚Üí‚ãÜ‚Çê[‚ÑÇ] (H‚ÜíL H)` + cyclic `Œ©`) with `œÅ(√Ç)=‚ü®Œ©,œÄ(√Ç)Œ©‚ü©` and `{œÄ(√Ç)Œ©}` dense; documented axiom (mathlib has the GNS machinery), `CStarAlgebra/GNS.lean`. **¬ßA.6‚Äì¬ßA.7 (Wigner / states / ground states / GNS) complete ‚Üí Appendix A complete.** Prove-first where mathlib supports, axiomatize-first for the heavy analytic results, strict book order (see the status & axiomatization-policy note below). |

### Appendix A: status and axiomatization policy

Tasaki's **Appendix A is fully formalized in book order (A.1‚ÄìA.28)**, and the
entire Tasaki text up to and including Chapter 11 (¬ß11.5) plus this appendix is
now covered. The appendix splits into two kinds of items:

- **Proved (axiom-free)** ‚Äî the linear-algebra and angular-momentum core that
  `mathlib` supports directly: **A.1** (the Lie product / Trotter formula, proved
  from scratch in a complete normed `‚Ñù`-algebra), **A.12** (the strong-coupling
  effective Hamiltonian limit, by the `v‚Åª¬π`-squeeze + kernel-pairing continuity
  argument), **A.4‚ÄìA.6**
  (positive-semidefinite basics + unique
  PSD square root), **A.7‚ÄìA.8** (Weyl eigenvalue monotonicity via Courant‚ÄìFischer,
  and the trace-exp monotonicity through the spectral mapping for `exp`),
  **A.9‚ÄìA.11** (frustration-free Hamiltonians, energy-form
  kernels), **A.13‚ÄìA.16** (angular-momentum quantization `J = n/2`, the ladder,
  and SU(2)-multiplet degeneracy), **A.17** (spin-0/half sector via the common
  eigenvector of commuting Hermitians), **A.18** (Perron‚ÄìFrobenius for a real
  symmetric matrix, via the project's Collatz‚ÄìWielandt development and the
  variational `|w|`-argument), and **A.19‚ÄìA.20** (polar + singular-value
  decompositions from the spectral theorem).
- **Documented axioms (faithful statements, deferred proofs)** ‚Äî the
  operator-algebraic **A.21‚ÄìA.28** (Wigner's
  theorem, states, Banach‚ÄìAlaoglu, ground states of infinite systems, the GNS
  construction).

**Value judgment / policy.** The documented axioms above are kept as *faithful,
book-order statements* ‚Äî they record exactly what Tasaki proves ‚Äî but they are
**not active proof targets** of this project. They fall into two categories:

- **Operator-algebraic results** (Appendix A.21‚ÄìA.28): the heavy functional-analytic and
operator-algebraic structures (states on the quasi-local C\*-algebra, weak-‚àó
compactness, ground states, GNS, Wigner) belong to a dedicated operator-algebra /
functional-analysis development; such a development may well be carried out
**separately** (for instance contributed to `mathlib`), and these axioms simply
**wait for that implementation**.

- **Perturbation-theoretic results** (e.g., **Lemma 10.1** (Tasaki ¬ß10.1, degenerate
perturbation theory) and singular-perturbation arguments in Chapter 10): the analytic
proofs of weak-coupling continuation and adiabatic following for eigenstate families
are **not undertaken** as an active project goal; such techniques naturally belong to
a separate analytic-perturbation development. Results whose only missing link is a
perturbation-theoretic proof ‚Äî such as the total-spin values in Lieb's repulsive-Hubbard
theorem (Theorem 10.4) ‚Äî are recorded as documented axioms while their companion
finite-coupling combinatorial bounds are proved axiom-free.

- **Book theorems that Tasaki states without proof** (results he quotes from the
external literature rather than proving in the text): **Theorem 10.11** (Kubo‚ÄìKishi
finite-temperature charge/pairing susceptibility bound, Tasaki ¬ß10.2.5, citing
Kubo‚ÄìKishi, *Phys. Rev. B* **41**, 4866 (1990)) and **Theorem 11.13** (Mielke's
flat-band ferromagnetism, `mielke_theorem_11_13`) are recorded as **faithful documented
axioms** on the concrete finite-volume operators (here the Duhamel susceptibilities),
matching the "Tasaki states it without proof" policy ‚Äî the reproving of the cited
external work is not an active project goal.

Accordingly the project's policy is to **axiomatize only the appendix and
perturbation-theory results that Tasaki's formalized main development actually uses**,
to **prove** the remaining ones where `mathlib` provides the tools, and otherwise to
leave a faithful axiom in place rather than invest in large bespoke developments whose
natural home is elsewhere. The `#print axioms` of every theorem in the repository makes
the precise dependency on these documented axioms auditable.

## Formalized theorems

All items below are formally proved with **zero `sorry`**. Full
mathematical statements and proof sketches are in
[`tex/proof-guide.tex`](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/blob/main/tex/proof-guide.tex).

### Single-site Pauli operators

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ¬ß2.1 eq. (2.1.8), p. 15. Cross-checked with Nielsen-Chuang
¬ß2.1.3 Figure 2.2 (pp. 65-66, definitions), Ex. 2.19 (p. 70,
Hermiticity), Ex. 2.41 (p. 78, `(œÉ^Œ±)¬≤ = I` and anticommutation),
Ex. 2.40 (p. 77, commutator, whence the cyclic products).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `pauliX/Y/Z_isHermitian` | `(œÉ^Œ±)‚ÄÝ = œÉ^Œ±` | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |
| `pauliX/Y/Z_mul_self` | `(œÉ^Œ±)¬≤ = I` | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |
| `pauliX_mul_pauliY` etc. | `œÉ^x œÉ^y = i¬∑œÉ^z` (cyclic) | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |
| `pauliX_anticomm_pauliY` etc. | `œÉ^Œ± œÉ^Œ≤ + œÉ^Œ≤ œÉ^Œ± = 0` (Œ± ‚âÝ Œ≤) | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |

### Spin-1/2 rotation operators (Tasaki ¬ß2.1 eq. (2.1.26))

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ¬ß2.1 eq. (2.1.26), p. 17 (closed form) and eq. (2.1.23),
p. 16 (`√õ_{2œÄ} = -1` for half-odd-integer spin).

| `spinHalfRot1/2/3` | `√õ^(Œ±)_Œ∏ := cos(Œ∏/2) ¬∑ 1 - 2i ¬∑ sin(Œ∏/2) ¬∑ ≈ú^(Œ±)` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1/2/3_zero` | `√õ^(Œ±)_0 = 1` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1/2/3_adjoint` | `(√õ^(Œ±)_Œ∏)‚ÄÝ = √õ^(Œ±)_{-Œ∏}` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1/2/3_two_pi` | `√õ^(Œ±)_{2œÄ} = -1` (Tasaki eq. (2.1.23)) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1/2/3_mul` | group law `√õ^(Œ±)_Œ∏ ¬∑ √õ^(Œ±)_œÜ = √õ^(Œ±)_{Œ∏+œÜ}` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1/2/3_unitary` | unitarity `√õ^(Œ±)_Œ∏ ¬∑ (√õ^(Œ±)_Œ∏)‚ÄÝ = 1` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1/2/3_pi` | `√õ^(Œ±)_œÄ = -2i ¬∑ ≈ú^(Œ±)` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1/2/3_pi_sq` | `(√õ^(Œ±)_œÄ)¬≤ = -1` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1_pi_anticomm_spinHalfRot2_pi` (and cyclic) | `{√õ^(Œ±)_œÄ, √õ^(Œ≤)_œÄ} = 0` for `Œ± ‚âÝ Œ≤` (Tasaki (2.1.25)) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1/2/3_pi_conjTranspose` | `(√õ^(Œ±)_œÄ)‚ÄÝ = 2i ¬∑ ≈ú^(Œ±)` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1_pi_mul_spinHalfRot2_pi` (and cyclic) | `√õ^(Œ±)_œÄ ¬∑ √õ^(Œ≤)_œÄ = √õ^(Œ≥)_œÄ` (Tasaki (2.1.29), S=1/2) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1/2/3_pi_conj_spinHalfOp_*` | axis invariance and sign flip at Œ∏=œÄ (Tasaki (2.1.15)/(2.1.21)) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conj_spinHalfOp{2,3,1}` | `(√õ^(Œ±)_Œ∏)‚ÄÝ ≈ú^(Œ≤) √õ^(Œ±)_Œ∏ = cos Œ∏ ¬∑ ≈ú^(Œ≤) - sin Œ∏ ¬∑ ≈ú^(Œ≥)` (Tasaki eq. (2.1.16), even-Œµ cyclic triple) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conj_spinHalfOp{3,1,2}` | `(√õ^(Œ±)_Œ∏)‚ÄÝ ≈ú^(Œ≤) √õ^(Œ±)_Œ∏ = cos Œ∏ ¬∑ ≈ú^(Œ≤) + sin Œ∏ ¬∑ ≈ú^(Œ≥)` (Tasaki eq. (2.1.16), odd-Œµ triple) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conj_spinHalfOp{1,2,3}` | same-axis invariance `(√õ^(Œ±)_Œ∏)‚ÄÝ ≈ú^(Œ±) √õ^(Œ±)_Œ∏ = ≈ú^(Œ±)` (Tasaki eq. (2.1.15)) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot{1,2,3}_half_pi_conj_spinHalfOp_*` | `œÄ/2`-rotation conjugation `(√õ^(Œ±)_{œÄ/2})‚ÄÝ ≈ú^(Œ≤) √õ^(Œ±)_{œÄ/2} = -Œµ^{Œ±Œ≤Œ≥} ≈ú^(Œ≥)` (Tasaki eq. (2.1.22), 6 cases) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot3_eq_exp` | `√õ^(3)_Œ∏ = exp(-iŒ∏ ≈ú^(3))` via `Matrix.exp_diagonal` + Euler (Problem 2.1.b, axis 3) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot3_mul_spinHalfRot2_mulVec_spinHalfUp` | `√õ^(3)_œÜ √õ^(2)_Œ∏ |œà^‚Üë‚ü© = e^{-iœÜ/2} cos(Œ∏/2) |œà^‚Üë‚ü© + e^{iœÜ/2} sin(Œ∏/2) |œà^‚Üì‚ü©` (coherent state, Problem 2.1.d) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot3_mul_spinHalfRot2_mulVec_spinHalfDown` | `√õ^(3)_œÜ √õ^(2)_Œ∏ |œà^‚Üì‚ü© = -e^{-iœÜ/2} sin(Œ∏/2) |œà^‚Üë‚ü© + e^{iœÜ/2} cos(Œ∏/2) |œà^‚Üì‚ü©` (rotation of spin-down, Problem 2.2.c auxiliary) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot3_half_pi_mul_spinHalfRot2_half_pi_mulVec_spinHalfUp` | specialization at Œ∏ = œÜ = œÄ/2 (Problem 2.1.e) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfDotVec` / `spinHalfDotVec_isHermitian` | vector inner product `≈ú ¬∑ v := Œ£_Œ± v_Œ± ≈ú^(Œ±)` and its Hermiticity (cf. (2.1.19)) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot3_commute_spinHalfOp3_smul` | same-axis rotation commutes with `v ¬∑ ≈ú^(3)` (cf. (2.1.20) along axis) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `hadamard` / `hadamard_mul_self` | the Hadamard basis-change matrix `W = (1/‚àö2)¬∑!![1,1;1,-1]` and `W¬∑W = 1` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `hadamard_mul_spinHalfOp1_mul_hadamard` | `W ¬∑ ≈ú^(1) ¬∑ W = ≈ú^(3)` (basis change between œÉ^x and œÉ^z) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `hadamard_mul_spinHalfOp3_mul_hadamard` | `W ¬∑ ≈ú^(3) ¬∑ W = ≈ú^(1)` (inverse basis change) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1_eq_hadamard_conj` | `√õ^(1)_Œ∏ = W ¬∑ √õ^(3)_Œ∏ ¬∑ W` (axis 1 rotation as Hadamard conjugate of axis 3) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1_eq_exp` | `√õ^(1)_Œ∏ = exp(-iŒ∏ ≈ú^(1))` via Hadamard conjugation + `Matrix.exp_conj` (Problem 2.1.b, axis 1) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `yDiag` / `yDiagAdj` / `yDiag_mul_yDiagAdj` / `yDiag_mul_spinHalfOp3_mul_yDiagAdj` | y-axis basis-change unitary `V` with `V¬∑V‚ÄÝ = 1` and `V¬∑≈ú^(3)¬∑V‚ÄÝ = ≈ú^(2)` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot2_eq_yDiag_conj` / `spinHalfRot2_eq_exp` | `√õ^(2)_Œ∏ = V¬∑√õ^(3)_Œ∏¬∑V‚ÄÝ` and `√õ^(2)_Œ∏ = exp(-iŒ∏ ≈ú^(2))` (Problem 2.1.b, axis 2) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |

### 3D rotation matrices `R^(Œ±)_Œ∏` (general Œ∏, Tasaki ¬ß2.1 eq. (2.1.11))

| Lean name | Statement | File |

### Z‚ÇÇ √ó Z‚ÇÇ representation (Tasaki ¬ß2.1 eqs. (2.1.27)-(2.1.34))

The Z‚ÇÇ √ó Z‚ÇÇ structure is proved across files:
- S = 1/2 (projective, eq. (2.1.31)): `spinHalfRot*_pi_sq = -1`, anticommutation, products.
- S = 1 (genuine, eq. (2.1.27)): `spinOnePiRot*_sq = 1`, commutation.

See `Quantum/Z2Z2.lean` for the unified documentation.

### Polynomial-basis decomposition for S = 1 (Tasaki ¬ß2.1 Problem 2.1.a, S = 1)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum
Many-Body Systems*, ¬ß2.1 Problem 2.1.a, p. 15 + solution S.1, p. 493.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `spinOneProj{Plus,Zero,Minus}` | the three diagonal projectors `\|œà^œÉ‚ü©‚ü®œà^œÉ\|` (œÉ ‚àà {+1, 0, -1}) | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneProj{Plus,Zero,Minus}_eq_polynomial` | each diagonal projector equals a polynomial in `≈ú^(3)` (Lagrange interpolation) | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit{01,02,10,12,20,21}` | the six off-diagonal matrix units `\|œà^œÑ‚ü©‚ü®œà^œÉ\|` (œÑ ‚âÝ œÉ) | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit{01,12}_eq_polynomial` | `(1/‚àö2) ≈ú^- ¬∑ P_œÉ` for the two single-step lowering units | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit{10,21}_eq_polynomial` | `(1/‚àö2) ≈ú^+ ¬∑ P_œÉ` for the two single-step raising units | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit02_eq_polynomial` | `(1/2) (≈ú^-)¬≤ ¬∑ P_+` for the double-step lowering unit | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit20_eq_polynomial` | `(1/2) (≈ú^+)¬≤ ¬∑ P_-` for the double-step raising unit | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOne_decomposition` | every 3√ó3 complex matrix is a linear combination of the 9 matrix units (entry-wise); combined with the polynomial expressions above this gives Tasaki Problem 2.1.a for `S = 1` | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |

### Spin-`S` operators (general S ‚â• 0, parameterised by `N = 2S : ‚Ñï`)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body Systems*, ¬ß2.1 Problem 2.1.a (p. 15) and solution S.1 (p. 493).

Generic spin-`S` operators live on `Matrix (Fin (N + 1)) (Fin (N + 1)) ‚ÑÇ`, with `N = 2S : ‚Ñï` (so `N = 1` ‚áî `S = 1/2`, `N = 2` ‚áî `S = 1`). Tracking issue #458 (Tasaki ¬ß2.1 P1d''' general-S generalisation).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `spinSOp3` | `≈ú^(3) := diag(N/2, N/2 - 1, ‚Ä¶, -N/2)` for `N : ‚Ñï` | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus` / `spinSOpMinus` | raising/lowering operators with matrix entries `‚àö(k¬∑(N ‚àí k + 1))` (resp. `‚àö((N ‚àí k)(k + 1))`) on the immediate sub/super-diagonal | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp1` / `spinSOp2` | `≈ú^(1) := (≈ú^+ + ≈ú^-) / 2`, `≈ú^(2) := (≈ú^+ ‚àí ≈ú^-) / (2 i)` | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_apply_top` / `_bottom` | `≈ú^+` annihilates `\|N‚ü©` (highest weight); `≈ú^-` annihilates `\|0‚ü©` (lowest weight) ‚Äî the ladder boundaries | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_commutator_spinSOp{Plus,Minus}` | **Cartan relations** `[≈ú^{(3)}, ≈ú^+] = ≈ú^+` and `[≈ú^{(3)}, ≈ú^-] = -≈ú^-`: `≈ú^¬±` shift the magnetic quantum number by `¬±1`. Proved entry-wise via `Matrix.diagonal_mul` / `mul_diagonal` (Œ≤-2 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Algebra.lean` |
| `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_conjTranspose` / `spinSOp{1,2,3}_isHermitian` | adjointness `(≈ú^+)·¥¥ = ≈ú^-`, `(≈ú^-)·¥¥ = ≈ú^+`, and Hermiticity of `≈ú^{(Œ±)}` for `Œ± ‚àà {1, 2, 3}` (Œ≤-3 of Issue #458). The ladder adjointness follows from the matrix-entry symmetry; `≈ú^{(1)}` and `≈ú^{(2)}` use the `1/2` and `1/(2i)` self-conjugacy facts | `Quantum/SpinS/Hermitian.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj` / `spinSOp3_sub_smul_mul_diagProj` / `_self_mul_diagProj` | the **diagonal projector** `P_k := diag(Œ¥_{i,k})` and the eigenvalue-action lemma `(≈ú^{(3)} ‚àí Œª_j ‚Ä¢ 1) ¬∑ P_k = (Œª_k ‚àí Œª_j) ¬∑ P_k`, with the annihilation specialisation at `j = k` (Œ≤-4 of Issue #458). Foundation for the Lagrange-interpolation polynomial decomposition: each `P_k` will be expressed as `‚àè_{j ‚âÝ k} (≈ú^{(3)} ‚àí Œª_j ‚Ä¢ 1) / (Œª_k ‚àí Œª_j)` in a follow-up PR | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProj.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_mul_diagProj` / `diagProj_mul_spinSOp3` / `spinSOp3_commute_diagProj` | the **eigenvalue equation** `≈ú^{(3)} ¬∑ P_k = Œª_k ¬∑ P_k` (where `Œª_k = (N : ‚ÑÇ)/2 ‚àí k`), with the symmetric `P_k ¬∑ ≈ú^{(3)} = Œª_k ¬∑ P_k` and the resulting commutativity. Both are diagonal-times-diagonal computations (Œ≤-5 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Lagrange.lean` |
| `mul_diagProj_apply` / `diagProj_mul_apply` / `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_mul_diagProj_apply` | **Column/row selection** by the diagonal projector: `(A ¬∑ P_k)[i, j] = A[i, k]` if `j = k` else `0` (and dually for `P_k ¬∑ A`). Specialised to `≈ú^¬±` produces off-diagonal matrix units (Œ≤-6 of Issue #458) ‚Äî the building blocks of the polynomial decomposition theorem | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderProj.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_succ_mul_spinSOpMinus` | **Ladder recursion**: `≈ú^+ ¬∑ P_{k+1} ¬∑ ≈ú^- = (k + 1)(N ‚àí k) ¬∑ P_k`. The triple product collapses to a non-zero scalar multiple of `P_k`. Iterating from the lowest-weight projector `P_N` (itself a polynomial in `≈ú^{(3)}`) yields every diagonal projector as a polynomial in `{1ÃÇ, ≈ú^{(Œ±)}}` (Œ≤-7 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderRecursion.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_first` / `spinSOpMinus_mul_diagProj_last` | **Ladder boundaries**: `≈ú^+ ¬∑ P_0 = 0` (top of ladder) and `≈ú^- ¬∑ P_N = 0` (bottom). The first/last columns of `≈ú^¬±` vanish, so multiplication by `P_{0/N}` (which selects that column) gives `0`. Terminate the recursion at the highest- and lowest-weight projectors (Œ≤-8 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderBoundary.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_isHermitian` / `sum_spinSDiagProj_eq_one` | **Hermiticity** of each `P_k`, and the **resolution of the identity** `Œ£_k P_k = 1`. The latter is the cornerstone for the matrix-unit decomposition: combined with the off-diagonal matrix units (Œ≤-6, Œ≤-7), gives every matrix in `M_{N+1}(‚ÑÇ)` (Œ≤-9 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProjProperties.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_succ_eq_single` / `spinSOpMinus_mul_diagProj_eq_single` | **Off-diagonal matrix-unit decomposition**: `≈ú^+ ¬∑ P_{i+1} = ‚àö((i+1)(N‚àíi)) ¬∑ E_{i, i+1}` and `≈ú^- ¬∑ P_i = ‚àö((N‚àíi)(i+1)) ¬∑ E_{i+1, i}`, where `E_{a, b} := Matrix.single a b 1` is the (a, b) matrix unit (Œ≤-10 of Issue #458). These are the simplest off-diagonal matrix units expressible via `≈ú^¬±` ladder action on diagonal projectors | `Quantum/SpinS/OffDiagUnit.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_sq_eq_diagonal` | `(≈ú^{(3)})¬≤ = diag((N/2 ‚àí i)¬≤)`. Step toward the **Casimir identity** `(≈ú^{(1)})¬≤ + (≈ú^{(2)})¬≤ + (≈ú^{(3)})¬≤ = (N(N+2)/4) ¬∑ 1` for general spin (Œ≤-11 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Op3Square.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_spinSOpMinus_eq_diagonal` | `≈ú^+ ¬∑ ≈ú^- = diag((i + 1)(N ‚àí i))`. The product is diagonal because `≈ú^+[i, l] ¬∑ ≈ú^-[l, j]` is non-zero only when `l = i + 1 = j + 1`, forcing `i = j`. Combined with the analogous `≈ú^- ¬∑ ≈ú^+` and `(≈ú^{(3)})¬≤`, this assembles the Casimir identity (Œ≤-12 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PlusMinusDiag.lean` |
| `spinSOpMinus_mul_spinSOpPlus_eq_diagonal` | `≈ú^- ¬∑ ≈ú^+ = diag(i ¬∑ (N ‚àí i + 1))`. Symmetric to Œ≤-12 with `≈ú^¬±` swapped (Œ≤-13 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/MinusPlusDiag.lean` |
| `spinSOp1_sq_add_spinSOp2_sq` / `spinSOp_total_squared` | **Casimir identity** for general spin-`S`: `(≈ú^{(1)})¬≤ + (≈ú^{(2)})¬≤ + (≈ú^{(3)})¬≤ = (N(N+2)/4) ¬∑ 1`, equivalent to `S(S+1) ¬∑ 1` for `S = N/2`. Proves the irreducible spin-`S` representation has Casimir eigenvalue `S(S+1)` (Schur's lemma). The intermediate identity `(≈ú^{(1)})¬≤ + (≈ú^{(2)})¬≤ = (1/2)(≈ú^+ ¬∑ ≈ú^- + ≈ú^- ¬∑ ≈ú^+)` is proved using `module` (Œ≤-14 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Casimir.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_mulVec_basis` | spin-`S` eigenstate equation `≈ú^{(3)} ¬∑ \|k‚ü© = (N/2 ‚àí k) ¬∑ \|k‚ü©`, where `\|k‚ü© := Pi.single k 1` is the `k`-th unit basis vector of `(Fin (N + 1) ‚Üí ‚ÑÇ)` (Œ≤-15 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Eigenstates.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mulVec_basis` / `spinSOpMinus_mulVec_basis` | **Ladder action on basis vectors**: `≈ú^+ ¬∑ \|k‚ü© = ‚àö(k(N ‚àí k + 1)) ¬∑ \|k ‚àí 1‚ü©` for `k ‚â• 1`, and `≈ú^- ¬∑ \|k‚ü© = ‚àö((N ‚àí k)(k + 1)) ¬∑ \|k + 1‚ü©` for `k ‚â§ N ‚àí 1`. The standard SU(2) ladder relations on the magnetic-quantum-number basis (Œ≤-16 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderStates.lean` |
| `spinSOp_total_squared_mulVec_basis` | **Casimir eigenvalue on basis**: `(≈ú)¬≤ ¬∑ \|k‚ü© = (N(N+2)/4) ¬∑ \|k‚ü©`. Direct consequence of `(≈ú)¬≤ = (N(N+2)/4) ¬∑ 1` (Œ≤-14) ‚Äî every basis vector has the universal Casimir eigenvalue, reflecting that the spin-`S` representation is a single irreducible (Schur) (Œ≤-17 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CasimirEigenvalue.lean` |
| `spinSOp{1,2,3,Plus,Minus}_commute_total_squared` | **Casimir invariance**: each `≈ú^{(Œ±)}` and `≈ú^¬±` commutes with the Casimir `(≈ú)¬≤ = (N(N+2)/4) ¬∑ 1`. Direct consequence of the scalar nature of the Casimir (Œ≤-18 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CasimirInvariance.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_commutator_spinSOpMinus` | **Third Cartan relation**: `[≈ú^+, ≈ú^-] = 2 ¬∑ ≈ú^{(3)}`. Combines Œ≤-12 (`≈ú^+ ¬∑ ≈ú^- = diag((i + 1)(N ‚àí i))`) and Œ≤-13 (`≈ú^- ¬∑ ≈ú^+ = diag(i (N ‚àí i + 1))`); the difference `(i+1)(N-i) ‚àí i(N-i+1) = N ‚àí 2i = 2(N/2 ‚àí i)` matches `2 ¬∑ ≈ú^{(3)}` (Œ≤-19 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Cartan3.lean` |
| `spinSOp1_commutator_spinSOp2` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[≈ú^{(1)}, ≈ú^{(2)}] = i ¬∑ ≈ú^{(3)}`. Derived from the Cartan relations by algebraic manipulation through `≈ú^{(1)} = (≈ú^+ + ≈ú^-)/2` and `≈ú^{(2)} = (≈ú^+ ‚àí ≈ú^-)/(2i)`: `(P+Q)(P-Q) ‚àí (P-Q)(P+Q) = -2[P, Q] = -4 ≈ú^{(3)}`, then scalar simplification `-1/I = I` (Œ≤-20 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator.lean` |
| `spinSOp2_commutator_spinSOp3` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[≈ú^{(2)}, ≈ú^{(3)}] = i ¬∑ ≈ú^{(1)}`. Derived from Cartan Œ≤-2 (`[≈ú^{(3)}, ≈ú^¬±] = ¬±≈ú^¬±`) via `≈ú^{(2)} = (≈ú^+ ‚àí ≈ú^-)/(2i)`: the commutator reduces to `(1/(2i)) (-≈ú^+ ‚àí ≈ú^-) = (-1/(2i)) ¬∑ 2 ≈ú^{(1)} = i ¬∑ ≈ú^{(1)}` (Œ≤-21 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator23.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_commutator_spinSOp1` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[≈ú^{(3)}, ≈ú^{(1)}] = i ¬∑ ≈ú^{(2)}`. Derived from Cartan Œ≤-2 via `≈ú^{(1)} = (≈ú^+ + ≈ú^-)/2`: the commutator reduces to `(1/2) (≈ú^+ ‚àí ≈ú^-) = (1/2) ¬∑ (2i) ¬∑ ≈ú^{(2)} = i ¬∑ ≈ú^{(2)}`. Together with Œ≤-20 and Œ≤-21 this completes the standard SU(2) commutator algebra (Tasaki eq. (2.1.1)) for spin-`S` operators (Œ≤-22 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator31.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_mul_self` / `spinSDiagProj_mul_of_ne` | **Idempotence and orthogonality** of the diagonal projectors `P_k = |k‚ü©‚ü®k|`: `P_k ¬∑ P_k = P_k` and `P_i ¬∑ P_j = 0` for `i ‚âÝ j`. Combined with Œ≤-9 (`‚àë_k P_k = 1`) this gives the spectral decomposition of the identity for `≈ú^{(3)}` (Œ≤-23 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProjOrtho.lean` |
| `aeval_diagonal` | **Polynomial evaluation at a diagonal matrix**: `aeval (Matrix.diagonal v) p = Matrix.diagonal (fun i => p.eval (v i))`. Foundational lemma for the Lagrange-interpolation step (Œ≤-25+) ‚Äî lets us pull a polynomial in `≈ú^{(3)}` (a diagonal matrix) through to its scalar action on each diagonal entry. Proof by `Polynomial.induction_on'` on monomials and addition (Œ≤-24 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/AevalDiagonal.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_eq_lagrange_aeval` | **Lagrange-interpolation formula for `P_k`**: `P_k = aeval (≈ú^{(3)}) (Lagrange.basis Finset.univ (spinSOp3Eigen N) k)`, equivalently `P_k = ‚àè_{j ‚âÝ k} (≈ú^{(3)} ‚àí Œª_j ‚Ä¢ 1)/(Œª_k ‚àí Œª_j)` with `Œª_j = (N : ‚ÑÇ)/2 ‚àí j.val`. Combines Œ≤-24 (`aeval` of diagonal) with mathlib's `Lagrange.eval_basis_self` / `eval_basis_of_ne`. Each diagonal projector is therefore a **polynomial in `≈ú^{(3)}`**, which combined with Œ≤-9 (`‚àë P_k = 1`) gives an explicit polynomial decomposition of `1ÃÇ` (Œ≤-25 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LagrangeFormula.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_eq_one_add_I_smul_two` / `spinSOpMinus_eq_one_sub_I_smul_two` | **Inversion of the Cartesian definition**: `≈ú^+ = ≈ú^{(1)} + i ¬∑ ≈ú^{(2)}` and `≈ú^- = ≈ú^{(1)} ‚àí i ¬∑ ≈ú^{(2)}`. The defining identities `≈ú^{(1)} = (1/2)(≈ú^+ + ≈ú^-)`, `≈ú^{(2)} = (1/(2i))(≈ú^+ ‚àí ≈ú^-)` invert to express the ladder operators as **linear combinations** of the Hermitian Cartesian spin operators (Œ≤-26 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PMAsOneTwo.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_mem_adjoin_spinSOp3` / `spinSDiagProj_mem_adjoin` | **Diagonal projectors live in the algebra generated by the spin operators**: `P_k ‚àà Algebra.adjoin ‚ÑÇ {≈ú^{(3)}}` (and a fortiori `P_k ‚àà Algebra.adjoin ‚ÑÇ {≈ú^{(1)}, ≈ú^{(2)}, ≈ú^{(3)}}`). Promotes the Lagrange-interpolation formula (Œ≤-25) from "polynomial-evaluation" form to "subalgebra-membership" form via `Algebra.adjoin_singleton_eq_range_aeval` (Œ≤-27 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/ProjMemAdjoin.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mem_adjoin` / `spinSOpMinus_mem_adjoin` | **Ladder operators live in `Algebra.adjoin ‚ÑÇ {≈ú^{(Œ±)}}`**: `≈ú^+, ≈ú^- ‚àà Algebra.adjoin ‚ÑÇ {≈ú^{(1)}, ≈ú^{(2)}, ≈ú^{(3)}}`. Direct consequence of Œ≤-26 (`≈ú^¬± = ≈ú^{(1)} ¬± i ¬∑ ≈ú^{(2)}`) and the fact that subalgebras are closed under `‚ÑÇ`-linear combinations (Œ≤-28 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PMMemAdjoin.lean` |
| `single_succ_mem_adjoin` / `single_succ_swap_mem_adjoin` | **Immediate-neighbor matrix units live in `Algebra.adjoin ‚ÑÇ {≈ú^{(Œ±)}}`**: `E_{i, i+1}, E_{i+1, i} ‚àà Algebra.adjoin ‚ÑÇ {≈ú^{(1)}, ≈ú^{(2)}, ≈ú^{(3)}}`. Combines Œ≤-10 (`≈ú^+ ¬∑ P_{i+1} = ‚àö((i+1)(N-i)) ¬∑ E_{i, i+1}`) with Œ≤-27 (`P_k ‚àà adjoin`) and Œ≤-28 (`≈ú^¬± ‚àà adjoin`); the ladder coefficient is non-zero on the valid range and the subalgebra is closed under multiplication and scalar `smul`. Step toward expressing every matrix unit as a polynomial in `{≈ú^{(Œ±)}}` (Œ≤-29 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/NeighborUnitAdjoin.lean` |
| `single_offset_succ_mem_adjoin` / `single_offset_succ_swap_mem_adjoin` | **Arbitrary stride-(k+1) matrix units live in `Algebra.adjoin ‚ÑÇ {≈ú^{(Œ±)}}`**: `E_{i, i+(k+1)}, E_{i+(k+1), i} ‚àà Algebra.adjoin ‚ÑÇ {≈ú^{(Œ±)}}` for any `k`. Induction on `k`: base case is Œ≤-29; inductive step chains via `Matrix.single_mul_single_same` (`E_{i,j} ¬∑ E_{j,k} = E_{i,k}`) (Œ≤-30 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/OffsetUnitAdjoin.lean` |
| `matrix_single_mem_adjoin` | **Every matrix unit `E_{i,j}` lives in `Algebra.adjoin ‚ÑÇ {≈ú^{(Œ±)}}`**. Three-case split: `i = j` reduces to Œ≤-27 via `Matrix.diagonal_single` (`E_{i,i} = P_i`); `i.val < j.val` is Œ≤-30 upper; `j.val < i.val` is Œ≤-30 lower. Last building block before the spanning theorem (Œ≤-31 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllUnitsAdjoin.lean` |
| `matrix_mem_adjoin` / **`spinS_adjoin_eq_top`** | **üéØ Tasaki ¬ß2.1 Problem 2.1.a (P1d''') general-`S`**: every operator on the `(2S+1)`-dimensional spin-`S` Hilbert space `‚ÑÇ^{N+1}` is a polynomial in `{1ÃÇ, ≈ú^{(1)}, ≈ú^{(2)}, ≈ú^{(3)}}`. Equivalently, `Algebra.adjoin ‚ÑÇ {≈ú^{(1)}, ≈ú^{(2)}, ≈ú^{(3)}} = ‚ä§`. Proof: every matrix unit `E_{i,j} ‚àà adjoin` (Œ≤-31), so by the entry-wise decomposition `M = ‚àë_{i,j} M_{i,j} ‚Ä¢ E_{i,j}` (`Matrix.matrix_eq_sum_single`) and the linearity of the subalgebra, every matrix is in the adjoin (Œ≤-32 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/SpanningTheorem.lean` |
| `ManyBodyOpS` / `onSiteS` / `spinSSiteOp{1,2,3,Plus,Minus}` | **Multi-site spin-`S` operator space** indexed by configurations `œÉ : Œõ ‚Üí Fin (N + 1)`, with the site-embedded operator `onSiteS i A` acting as `A` on site `i` and as the identity elsewhere; site-specialised `≈ú_i^{(Œ±)}, ≈ú_i^¬±`. Hermiticity preservation `onSiteS_isHermitian` lifts from single-site to multi-site (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Phase B-Œ≤ Œ≤-3a, Issue #412) | `Quantum/SpinS/MultiSiteCore.lean` + `Quantum/SpinS/MultiSite.lean` |

The combinatorial primitive underlying off-diagonal `œÉ^x_x` / `œÉ^y_x` action (deferred) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `onSite_pauliX_mulVec_basisVec` | basis-state action of the off-diagonal site Pauli: `(onSite x œÉ^x).mulVec |Œ®_œÉ‚ü© = |Œ®_{siteFlipAt œÉ x}‚ü©` (the spin at site `x` is swapped) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `pauliX_eq_indicator` / `onSite_pauliX_mulVec_apply` | closed-form `pauliX a b = if b = 1 - a then 1 else 0`, lifted to `((onSite x œÉ^x).mulVec v) œÑ = v (siteFlipAt œÑ x)` for any state `v` (general extension of the basis-state action) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSign_prod_siteFlipAt` | `‚àè_y Œµ((siteFlipAt œÑ x) y) = -(‚àè_y Œµ(œÑ y))` ‚Äî the per-site flip swaps `Œµ(œÑ x)` with `Œµ(1 - œÑ x) = -Œµ(œÑ x)`, flipping the total sign | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_onSite_pauliX_mulVec` | multi-site sign-flip equivariance for `œÉ^x` (Tasaki ¬ß2.3 (2.3.14) at Œ± = 1): `ŒòÃÇ_tot ((onSite x œÉ^x) v) = (-(onSite x œÉ^x))(ŒòÃÇ_tot v)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_onSite_pauliY_mulVec` | multi-site sign-flip equivariance for `œÉ^y` (Tasaki ¬ß2.3 (2.3.14) at Œ± = 2): `ŒòÃÇ_tot ((onSite x œÉ^y) v) = (-(onSite x œÉ^y))(ŒòÃÇ_tot v)`. The proof handles the per-site `¬±i` factor via `conj(pauliY_sign(1 - s)) = pauliY_sign(s)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_add` / `_smul` / `_real_smul` | multi-spin `ŒòÃÇ_tot` is additive, antilinear in the scalar (`ŒòÃÇ_tot(c ‚Ä¢ v) = conj(c) ‚Ä¢ ŒòÃÇ_tot v`), and real-linear (special case of antilinearity at real `r`) ‚Äî foundational for lifting Pauli-axis equivariance to bilinear / Heisenberg-type Hamiltonian forms | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_onSite_spinHalfOp{1,2,3}_mulVec` | Tasaki ¬ß2.3 (2.3.14) for spin-1/2 ops `≈ú^(Œ±) = œÉ^(Œ±) / 2`: `ŒòÃÇ_tot ((onSite x ≈ú^(Œ±)) v) = (-(onSite x ≈ú^(Œ±)))(ŒòÃÇ_tot v)` for Œ± = 1, 2, 3 ‚Äî direct corollaries of the Pauli versions by scalar (1/2) multiplication | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_spinHalfDot_mulVec` | **Time-reversal invariance of the bilinear `≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_y`** (Tasaki ¬ß2.3): `ŒòÃÇ_tot ((≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_y) v) = (≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_y)(ŒòÃÇ_tot v)` ‚Äî two equivariance `-1` factors cancel; sums per-axis | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec` | **Time-reversal invariance of the Heisenberg Hamiltonian** (Tasaki ¬ß2.3): for real coupling `J` (`conj(J(x,y)) = J(x,y)`), `ŒòÃÇ_tot ((H J) v) = (H J)(ŒòÃÇ_tot v)`. Combines per-bond invariance + ŒòÃÇ_tot antilinearity (J reality) + additivity (over double-sum) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling_conj` / `periodicChainCoupling_conj` | every entry of `openChainCoupling N J` (resp. `periodicChainCoupling N J`) is real (under complex conjugation), since `J : ‚Ñù` makes `(-(J : ‚ÑÇ))` real-valued | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_openChainHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_periodicChainHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_squareLatticeHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_squareTorusHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_cubicLatticeHeisenberg_mulVec` | concrete time-reversal invariance: the open / periodic chain, the 2D open square / torus, and the 3D cubic Heisenberg Hamiltonians all commute with `ŒòÃÇ_tot` for any real coupling `J : ‚Ñù`. Backed by `*Coupling_conj` reality lemmas in `HeisenbergChain.lean` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_basisVec_upDown` / `_basisVec_basisSwap_upDown` | `ŒòÃÇ_tot |‚Üë‚Üì‚ü© = -|‚Üì‚Üë‚ü©` and `ŒòÃÇ_tot |‚Üì‚Üë‚ü© = -|‚Üë‚Üì‚ü©` on `Fin 2` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_singlet` | the two-site spin singlet `|‚Üë‚Üì‚ü© - |‚Üì‚Üë‚ü©` is **time-reversal invariant** (Tasaki ¬ß2.3 / ¬ßA.3): being the SU(2) `S = 0` representation, it survives `ŒòÃÇ_tot` unchanged | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_triplet_zero` | the triplet `m = 0` state `|‚Üë‚Üì‚ü© + |‚Üì‚Üë‚ü©` is **anti-invariant** under `ŒòÃÇ_tot`: `ŒòÃÇ_tot (|‚Üë‚Üì‚ü© + |‚Üì‚Üë‚ü©) = -(|‚Üë‚Üì‚ü© + |‚Üì‚Üë‚ü©)` (the symmetric combination picks up a minus sign from the per-basis-vector flip) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |

### Multi-body operator space (abstract lattice)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ¬ß2.2, pp. 21-26 (tensor-product Hilbert space and site-local
operators). The lattice `Œõ` is an arbitrary finite set with decidable
equality; specializing to `Œõ = Fin N` recovers an `N`-site chain.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `ManyBodyOp Œõ` | `Matrix (Œõ ‚Üí Fin 2) (Œõ ‚Üí Fin 2) ‚ÑÇ` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite i A` | site-embedded operator at `i : Œõ` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_isHermitian` | `A.IsHermitian ‚Üí (onSite i A).IsHermitian` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_add`, `onSite_sub`, `onSite_zero`, `onSite_smul`, `onSite_one` | linearity of the site embedding and `onSite i 1 = 1` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_mul_onSite_of_ne` | distinct-site commutation (Tasaki (2.2.6), `x ‚âÝ y`, S = 1/2) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `basisVec` / `onSite_mulVec_basisVec` | tensor-product basis states and their action under site operators (Tasaki (2.2.1)/(2.2.4)) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_mul_onSite_same` | same-site product `onSite i A ¬∑ onSite i B = onSite i (A ¬∑ B)` (Tasaki (2.2.6), `x = y`) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_commutator_same` | same-site commutator `[onSite i A, onSite i B] = onSite i [A, B]` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.mul_of_commute` | commuting Hermitians multiply Hermitian (generic; relocated to the matrix-analysis layer in PR #4342) | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` |

### Generic matrix-analysis helpers (`Math/MatrixAnalysis/`)

Topic-organized generic linear-algebra facts extracted from the physics files where they had been
re-proved as private helpers (Issue [#4339](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/issues/4339)).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `Matrix.isHermitian_sum` | a finite sum of Hermitian matrices is Hermitian | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.mul_of_commute` | the product of two commuting Hermitian matrices is Hermitian | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_isHermitian` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of pairwise-commuting Hermitian matrices is Hermitian | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_sq_of_sq_one` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of pairwise-commuting involutions is an involution | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_mul_self_of_idempotent` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of pairwise-commuting idempotents is idempotent | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_mulVec_eq_self` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of matrices each fixing `œà` also fixes `œà` | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.trace_im` | the trace of a Hermitian matrix is real | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |
| `Matrix.trace_mul_star_of_isHermitian` | `Tr(A¬∑B)` of two Hermitian matrices is conjugation-invariant | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |

(The two trace helpers above are also listed in the Gibbs-state section where they are consumed; the
canonical definitions live in `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean`.)

These are consumed by the Jordan‚ÄìWigner string / Hubbard hard-core projection layers (PR #4342),
replacing the per-file private copies in `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean`,
`Fermion/JordanWigner/Operators.lean`, and `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean`.

### Horsch‚Äìvon der Linden low-lying states (Tasaki ¬ß3.4, Theorem 3.1)

Start of the Chapters 3‚Äì10 backfill (Issue
[#4485](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/issues/4485); book order, infinite systems in
scope). Tasaki ¬ß3.4, Theorem 3.1, eqs. (3.4.7)‚Äì(3.4.12), pp. 66‚Äì67.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `horsch_vonderLinden_lowLying` | for Hermitian `H` with min eigenvalue `E‚ÇÄ = eigenvalues i‚ÇÄ`, a unit state `Œì` orthogonal to the ground eigenvector with `‚ü®Œì,HŒì‚ü© ‚â§ E‚ÇÄ+Œ¥` yields an energy eigenstate `j ‚âÝ i‚ÇÄ` with `E‚ÇÄ ‚â§ E_j ‚â§ E‚ÇÄ+Œ¥` (a low-lying state; possibly another ground state if degenerate, as Tasaki notes) | `Quantum/HorschVonderLinden.lean` |
| `kaplan_horsch_vonderLinden_order_lower_bound` | **Theorem 3.2, finite-volume core** (¬ß3.4; the thermodynamic double limit 3.4.22 is not formalized): for the field-perturbed ground state `Œ®` of `H ‚àí h¬∑O` (`h>0`) and any trial `Œû`, the order parameter obeys `‚ü®Œû,OŒû‚ü© + (E‚ÇÄ‚àí‚ü®Œû,HŒû‚ü©)/h ‚â§ ‚ü®Œ®,OŒ®‚ü©` (eq. (3.4.21), the variational core; the double limit `L‚Üë‚àû,h‚Üì0` gives the ‚àöq‚ÇÄ SSB bound) | `Quantum/KaplanHorschVonderLinden.lean` |
| `staggeredOrderOpS` / `staggeredOrderOpS_isHermitian` | **Theorem 4.1** (¬ß4.1, Dyson‚ÄìLieb‚ÄìSimon, toward; eq. (4.1.7)): the staggered (N√©el) order operator `√î = Œ£_x Œµ_x ≈ú_x^(3)` (`Œµ_x = ¬±1` sublattice sign) and its Hermiticity (real ¬±1 combination of Hermitian `≈ú_x^(3)`), so `‚ü®Œ¶,√î¬≤Œ¶‚ü©` is a real order parameter. The N√©el-LRO statement needs the actual hypercubic torus + reflection positivity (false for arbitrary bipartite graphs); deferred pending that infrastructure (infinite-volume in scope) | `Quantum/SpinS/DysonLiebSimon.lean` |
| `shastry_no_symmetry_breaking_1d` | **Theorem 4.2** (¬ß4.1, Shastry, AXIOM; eqs. (4.1.9)‚Äì(4.1.10)): absence of SSB in 1D. For the 1D AFM Heisenberg ring under a staggered field `ƒ§_h = Œ£_x ≈ú_x¬∑≈ú_{x+1} ‚àí h¬∑√î_L^(3)`, the per-site staggered order parameter of any normalized ground state vanishes in the iterated limit `lim_{h‚Üì0} lim_{L‚Üë‚àû}` (eq. (4.1.10), Œµ‚ÄìŒ¥ form). Tasaki states it without proof; the deep argument is now being formalized as the **reflection-positivity infrastructure project** (#4777, codex-validated 11-PR plan).
