Defines `ringCoupling`, `ringStaggeredSublattice`, `staggeredFieldChainHamiltonianS`. **RP infra layer 1 (in progress):** `RingBondReflection.lean` ‚Äî even-ring bond reflection `ringReflect n x = 2n‚àí1‚àíx` (involutive, half-swap, staggered-sign flip); `RingReflectionTheta.lean` ‚Äî reflection map `Œ∏(A) œÉ œÑ = conj(A(œÅœÉ)(œÅœÑ))`, an antilinear `*`-automorphism, with the single-site bridge `Œ∏(onSiteS x A) = onSiteS (ringReflect n x) (conj A)`. **RP infra layer 2** (`RingReflectionHamiltonian.lean`): the Hamiltonian Œ∏-decomposition ‚Äî orientation reversal `J(r x)(r y)=J y x`, spin-op conjugation (S¬π/S¬≥ real, S¬≤ imaginary), `Œ∏(≈ú_x¬∑≈ú_y)=≈ú_{r x}¬∑≈ú_{r y}`, `Œ∏(√î_L^(3))=‚àí√î_L^(3)` (staggered-sign flip), `Œ∏(ƒ§)=ƒ§` (reindex + adjacency + dot-comm), and `Œ∏(ƒ§_h)=ƒ§_{‚àíh}` (the reflection symmetry exchanging the two staggered ground states). **RP infra layer 3** (`RingReflectionPositivity.lean`): the left-half subalgebra `SupportedOnLeftS` = `B(H_left) ‚äó I_right` (two conditions: entries vanish off the right-diagonal **and** are independent of the common right-half value) with closure (zero/one/add/smul, left-site `onSiteS`), `Œ∏` maps left-supported to right-supported (`theta_right`), and the reflection-positive functional predicate `ReflectionPositiveFunctionalS` (`0 ‚â§ Re œÜ(Œ∏(A)¬∑A)` for left-supported `A`). **RP infra layer 4** (`RingReflectionTraceCone.lean`): the `Œ≤ = 0` base case ‚Äî `traceFunctional_reflectionPositive` proves the trace functional `X ‚Ü¶ Tr X` is reflection positive, i.e. `0 ‚â§ Re Tr(Œ∏(A)¬∑A)` for every left-supported `A`. The proof collapses `Tr(Œ∏(A)¬∑A) = ‚àë_{œÉ,Œº} Œ∏(A)œÉŒº¬∑AŒºœÉ` to its diagonal (both support conditions force `Œº=œÉ`), then factorizes over the left/right configuration split (`configSplitEquiv`, from `finSumFinEquiv`): the diagonal value `A œÉ œÉ` depends only on the left half, giving `Tr(Œ∏(A)¬∑A) = conj S ¬∑ S = ‚ÄñS‚Äñ¬≤ ‚â• 0` with `S = ‚àë_‚Ñì D ‚Ñì`. This is the infinite-temperature trace cone on which a later layer mounts the Gibbs exponential via Trotter. **RP infra layer 5** (`RingReflectionWeightedCone.lean`): the algebraic completion of the cone ‚Äî `SupportedOnLeftS.mul` (the left-half subalgebra is closed under products), `SupportedOnLeftS.mul_theta_comm` (a left-supported `A` commutes with `Œ∏(B)`, which acts on the right half), and the **weighted trace cone** `weightedTraceFunctional_reflectionPositive` (`X ‚Ü¶ Tr((Œ∏(C)¬∑C)¬∑X)` is reflection positive for left-supported `C`, via `Tr((Œ∏C¬∑C)¬∑(Œ∏A¬∑A)) = Tr(Œ∏(C¬∑A)¬∑(C¬∑A)) ‚â• 0`) plus its nonnegative-finite-combination version `weightedTraceFunctional_reflectionPositive_finsetSum` ‚Äî the cone on which the Trotter/Lie-product factors of the Gibbs exponential will be mounted. **RP infra layer 6** (`RingReflectionGibbsCone.lean`): the reflection-positive trace-weight cone ‚Äî `RPTraceWeightS M` (`X ‚Ü¶ Tr(M¬∑X)` is an RP functional), the cone-representability predicate `RPTraceConeRepS` (nonnegative finite combination `‚àë c·µ¢ Œ∏(C·µ¢)¬∑C·µ¢`) with closure under `one`/`zero`/`add`/`smul_nonneg`/`mul`/`pow`, with the product closure via the **four-operator generalization** `weightGen_mul`: `(Œ∏(A)¬∑B)¬∑(Œ∏(A')¬∑B') = Œ∏(A¬∑A')¬∑(B¬∑B')` (weakest hypotheses: only `B, A'` need be left-supported; `A, B'` unconstrained; specializes to `(Œ∏C¬∑C)(Œ∏D¬∑D) = Œ∏(CD)¬∑(CD)` for the diagonal cone; the off-diagonal `A ‚âÝ B` case closes the field-crossing product of `RPTwoFieldConeRepS.mul`). **Field-dependent crossing cone:** `RPTwoFieldConeRepS n N P` (field-dependent two-field cone representation: a field-independent index `Œπ` with nonnegative weights `c`, field-dependent generators `C i z` (left-supported at each field), such that `P u v = ‚àë·µ¢ c·µ¢ ‚Ä¢ (Œ∏(C i v) ¬∑ C i u)` for every field pair; diagonal `u = v` is a genuine cone; off-diagonal `u ‚âÝ v` is the field-crossing form) with closure under `one`/`zero`/`add`/`smul_nonneg`/`mul`/`pow`/`expSeriesPartialSum` (fieldwise from the field-independent cone operations) and the **field-free bridge** `RPTraceConeRepS.toField` (a constant family `C i z := ƒà i` lifts `RPTraceConeRepS` to `RPTwoFieldConeRepS`, recovering it as the `u = v`, constant-in-field degenerate case ‚Äî not a duplicate). These give the shared field-crossing cone family that the three slots of the two-field reflection Cauchy‚ÄìSchwarz of Tasaki (4.1.51)/(4.1.69) (pp. 89‚Äì93; DLS 1978 ¬ß2‚Äì3) consume. Additional: `RPTraceConeRepS.rpTraceWeight` (representable ‚üπ RP trace weight, from the weighted cone), and `RPTraceWeightS.tendsto` (RP trace weights are closed under limits, by finite-dimensional trace continuity) ‚Äî the cone on which the next layer mounts the interaction Gibbs exponential `exp(t¬∑‚àëc·µ¢Œ∏(C·µ¢)¬∑C·µ¢)` as a limit of cone-representable partial sums (PR #4991). **RP infra layer 7** (`RingReflectionGibbsExp.lean`): `rpInteractionExp_reflectionPositive` ‚Äî for a nonnegative finite interaction `D = t¬∑‚àë·µ¢ c·µ¢ Œ∏(C·µ¢)¬∑C·µ¢` (`t, c·µ¢ ‚â• 0`, `C·µ¢` left-supported), the matrix exponential `exp D` is a reflection-positive trace weight. Each partial sum `‚àë_{k<m}(k!)‚Åª¬πD·µè` of the exponential series is cone-representable (`smul_nonneg`/`add`/`pow`), hence an RP trace weight, and `exp D` is their limit (`expSeries_summable'.hasSum.tendsto_sum_nat`), so `RPTraceWeightS.tendsto` applies. Uses the `L‚àû`-operator-norm Banach structure (`open scoped Matrix.Norms.Operator`), whose topology is the entrywise (Pi) topology ‚Äî the same one underlying the trace continuity, so no topology diamond. **RP infra layer 8** (`RingReflectionExpSupport.lean`): the matrix exponential and the left subalgebra / reflection map ‚Äî `SupportedOnLeftS.exp` (`X` left-supported ‚üπ `exp X` left-supported: the left subalgebra is closed under products/sums/scalars/entrywise-limits via `SupportedOnLeftS.of_tendsto`, and `exp X` is the limit of its partial sums) and `ringReflectionThetaS_exp` (`Œ∏(exp X) = exp(Œ∏ X)`: `Œ∏` is a continuous conjugate-linear `*`-automorphism with real exponential coefficients) ‚Äî the building blocks for the `e^{-Œ≤H_L}` Hamiltonian factor of the full Gibbs reflection-positivity decomposition | `Quantum/SpinS/ShastryNoSSB.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingBondReflection.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTheta.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionHamiltonian.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionPositivity.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTraceCone.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionWeightedCone.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsCone.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsExp.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionExpSupport.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTwoFieldPairing.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionRPDecomposition.lean` |
| `refLeftSum` | **RP infra layer 4b** (`RingReflectionTraceCone.lean`, ¬ß4.1, Shastry base / PR #4977): the left-diagonal coordinate `S(A) = ‚àë_‚Ñì A_{(‚Ñì,‚Ñì),(‚Ñì,‚Ñì)}`, the scalar "square root" of a left-supported operator entering the reflection-positive factorization `Tr(Œ∏(A)¬∑B) = conj(S A)¬∑S B`; fundamental to the genuine bilinear generalization of trace positivity | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTraceCone.lean` |
| `trace_theta_mul_eq_refLeftSum_mul` | **RP infra layer 4b** (`RingReflectionTraceCone.lean`, ¬ß4.1, Shastry base / PR #4977): the **bilinear reflection base identity** `Tr(Œ∏(A)¬∑B) = conj(S A)¬∑S B` for left-supported operators, the genuine bilinear generalization (and proof engine) of trace reflection positivity; the foundation on which the Gibbs / doubled Cauchy‚ÄìSchwarz layer of the Dyson‚ÄìLieb‚ÄìSimon / Shastry argument is built (Tasaki ¬ß4.1, Theorem 4.2, as infrastructure toward its proof) | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTraceCone.lean` |
| `twoField_product_pairing` | **RP infra layer 7b** (`RingReflectionTwoFieldPairing.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2 / PR #4990): the two-field **SWEEP operator identity** ‚Äî the doubled Dyson‚ÄìLieb‚ÄìSimon approximant `(g(x)¬∑Œ∏(g(y))¬∑‚àë·µ¢ w·µ¢¬∑(Œ∏(C·µ¢(y))¬∑C·µ¢(x)))^m` (with left-supported kinetic family `g`, nonnegative weights `w·µ¢ ‚â• 0`, and **FIELD-DEPENDENT** left-supported cone generators `C·µ¢(z)` ‚Äî the reflected pile carrying the `y`-field factor `C·µ¢(y)`, the non-reflected pile the `x`-field factor `C·µ¢(x)`) collapses into a nonnegative reflection pairing `‚àë‚Çñ v‚Çñ¬∑(Œ∏(ùìõ‚Çñ(y))¬∑ùìõ‚Çñ(x))` over the crossing family `Œ∫ = Œπ·µê`, where `v‚Çñ = ‚àè w ‚â• 0` and the pattern-shared family `ùìõ‚Çñ(z)` (left-supported, depending on the field only through `z`) carries the SAME crossing sequence in both piles ‚Äî proved by induction via `sweep_operator_identity` (the single SWEEP step) with the disjoint-support commutation and `Œ∏`'s multiplicativity. The field-free pairing is the constant specialization `C·µ¢(z) := C·µ¢` (Tasaki ¬ß4.1 Theorem 4.2, crossing identity (4.1.65)‚Äì(4.1.69), p. 90). | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTwoFieldPairing.lean` |
| `twoField_product_pairing_trace` | **RP infra layer 7b** (`RingReflectionTwoFieldPairing.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2 / PR #4990): taking the trace of the SWEEP identity `twoField_product_pairing` and applying the bilinear reflection base identity `trace_theta_mul_eq_refLeftSum_mul` termwise yields the weighted ‚Ñì¬≤ Gram form `Tr(U) = ‚àë‚Çñ v‚Çñ ¬∑ conj(refLeftSum ùìõ‚Çñ(y)) ¬∑ refLeftSum ùìõ‚Çñ(x)` (the doubled reflection Cauchy‚ÄìSchwarz pairing consumed by the next PR7b-iii-b/c layer) ‚Äî field-dependent crossing generators (Tasaki ¬ß4.1 Theorem 4.2, (4.1.65)‚Äì(4.1.69), p. 90). | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTwoFieldPairing.lean` |
| `falkBruch_double_commutator` | **Cor 4.3 / Falk‚ÄìBruch** (`FalkBruchDoubleCommutator.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the concrete ground-state Falk‚ÄìBruch inequality ‚Äî for a ground state `Œ¶`, Hermitian `A` and a potential `y` for `AŒ¶` (`(H‚àíE‚ÇÄ)y = AŒ¶`), `2(Re‚ü®Œ¶,A¬≤Œ¶‚ü©)¬≤ ‚â§ Re‚ü®Œ¶,[A,[H,A]]Œ¶‚ü©¬∑Re‚ü®y,AŒ¶‚ü©`; combines the abstract Falk‚ÄìBruch (`K = H‚àíE‚ÇÄ` PSD) with the double-commutator = variational-energy identity | `Quantum/SpinS/FalkBruchDoubleCommutator.lean` |
| `hermitianSubMin_posSemidef` | **Cor 4.3 / Falk‚ÄìBruch** (`HermitianSubMinPosSemidef.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the Hamiltonian shifted to its ground energy `H ‚àí E‚ÇÄ` (`E‚ÇÄ = hermitianMinEigenvalue`) is positive-semidefinite ‚Äî the quadratic form is real (Hermiticity, `isHermitian_dotProduct_mulVec_im_zero`) with nonnegative real part (the variational lower bound); this is the PSD `K` fed into the Falk‚ÄìBruch inequality | `Quantum/SpinS/HermitianSubMinPosSemidef.lean` |
| `falkBruch_of_mulVec_eq` | **Cor 4.3 / Falk‚ÄìBruch** (`FalkBruch.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the abstract ground-state Falk‚ÄìBruch inequality ‚Äî for PSD `K` and `w` in the range of `K` (`K y = w`), `(Re‚ü®w,w‚ü©)¬≤ ‚â§ Re‚ü®w,K w‚ü©¬∑Re‚ü®y,w‚ü©`, an immediate instance of the PSD Cauchy‚ÄìSchwarz (`posSemidef_re_dotProduct_mulVec_sq_le`) with second argument `y`; no spectral theory / pseudo-inverse needed | `Quantum/SpinS/FalkBruch.lean` |
| `double_commutator_ground_state_nonneg` | **Cor 4.3 / Falk‚ÄìBruch** (`DoubleCommutatorNonneg.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the ground-state double commutator `Re‚ü®Œ¶, [A,[H,A]] Œ¶‚ü© ‚â• 0` for Hermitian `A` and a minimal-eigenvalue eigenvector `Œ¶` ‚Äî it equals twice the variational energy of `AŒ¶`, nonnegative since the ground energy is the Rayleigh minimum (`hermitianMinEigenvalue_mul_dotProduct_re_le_rayleighOnVec`); the sign of the Falk‚ÄìBruch numerator | `Quantum/SpinS/DoubleCommutatorNonneg.lean` |
| `double_commutator_ground_state_eq` | **Cor 4.3 / Falk‚ÄìBruch** (`DoubleCommutatorVariational.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): for Hermitian `H`, `A` and a ground state `HŒ¶ = E‚ÇÄŒ¶` (`E‚ÇÄ` real), the f-sum-rule oscillator strength equals twice the variational energy of `AŒ¶` ‚Äî `‚ü®Œ¶, [A,[H,A]] Œ¶‚ü© = 2‚ü®AŒ¶, H AŒ¶‚ü© ‚àí 2E‚ÇÄ‚ü®AŒ¶, AŒ¶‚ü©` (Hermitian-shift); the denominator of the ground-state Falk‚ÄìBruch inequality | `Quantum/SpinS/DoubleCommutatorVariational.lean` |
| `spinSDot_double_commutator_onSiteS_spinSOp3` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`SingleBondDoubleCommutator.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the per-bond double commutator is the negated transverse bond energy `[≈ú_a^{(3)}, [≈ú_a¬∑≈ú_b, ≈ú_a^{(3)}]] = ‚àí(≈ú_a^{(1)}≈ú_b^{(1)} + ≈ú_a^{(2)}≈ú_b^{(2)})` (the f-sum-rule integrand / oscillator strength of the staggered mode), bounded by the bond energy | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleBondDoubleCommutator.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_commutator_onSiteS` | **Cor 4.3 / IR bound** (`HeisenbergSpinSOp3Commutator.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the Heisenberg Hamiltonian commutator distributes over bonds `[ƒ§_J, ≈ú_z^{(3)}] = Œ£_x Œ£_y J_{xy} [≈ú_x¬∑≈ú_y, ≈ú_z^{(3)}]`; with the per-bond case analysis this reduces the spin-current divergence `[ƒ§, ≈ú_z^{(3)}]` to the two bonds incident to `z` | `Quantum/SpinS/HeisenbergSpinSOp3Commutator.lean` |
| `spinSDot_commutator_onSiteS_spinSOp3` / `_right` | **Cor 4.3 / IR bound** (`SingleBondSpinSOp3Commutator.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the single-bond spin current `[≈ú_a¬∑≈ú_b, ≈ú_a^{(3)}] = i(≈ú_a^{(1)} ≈ú_b^{(2)} ‚àí ≈ú_a^{(2)} ≈ú_b^{(1)})` for `a ‚âÝ b` (only the transverse bond components fail to commute with `≈ú_a^{(3)}`, via the cyclic SU(2) commutators; `_right` is the symmetric right-endpoint companion `[≈ú_a¬∑≈ú_b, ≈ú_b^{(3)}]`, and `_eq_zero_of_ne` is the off-bond vanishing `[≈ú_x¬∑≈ú_y, ≈ú_z^{(3)}] = 0` for `z ‚àâ {x,y}`); the building block of `[ƒ§, ≈ú_z^{(3)}]` and the double commutator in the infrared / f-sum-rule bound | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleBondSpinSOp3Commutator.lean` |
| `expectation_abs_le_manyBodyOperatorNormS` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`ExpectationNormBound.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the general norm-to-expectation bound ‚Äî for any `M` and a normalized state `Œ¶` (`star Œ¶ ‚¨ù·µ• Œ¶ = 1`), `|‚ü®Œ¶, M Œ¶‚ü©.re| ‚â§ ‚ÄñM‚Äñ` (normalized-state form of `abs_re_dotProduct_mulVec_le_norm_mul`); converts operator-norm bounds into expectation bounds in the Falk‚ÄìBruch argument | `Quantum/SpinS/ExpectationNormBound.lean` |
| `transverseBondEnergy_expectation_abs_le` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`TransverseBondEnergyExpectation.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): in a normalized state `Œ¶`, the transverse bond energy expectation `|‚ü®Œ¶, (≈ú_a^{(1)}≈ú_b^{(1)} + ≈ú_a^{(2)}≈ú_b^{(2)}) Œ¶‚ü©.re| ‚â§ 2N¬≤` (the operator-norm bound + the norm‚Üíexpectation bridge `abs_re_dotProduct_mulVec_le_norm_mul` + `‚ÄñŒ¶‚Äñ = 1`); summed over the `O(L)` adjacent bonds bounds the oscillator strength by `O(L)` | `Quantum/SpinS/TransverseBondEnergyExpectation.lean` |
| `spinSDot_commutator_onSiteS_spinSOp3_norm_le` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`SingleBondCommutatorNorm.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the single-bond spin current `[≈ú_a¬∑≈ú_b, ≈ú_a^{(3)}]` has operator norm `‚â§ 2N¬≤` (`‚Äñi‚Äñ = 1` + per-site norm bounds + submultiplicativity); the `O(1)` bound per bond, summed over `z`'s two incident bonds, bounds `‚Äñ[ƒ§, ≈ú_z^{(3)}]‚Äñ` by `O(1)` | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleBondCommutatorNorm.lean` |
| `transverseBondEnergy_manyBodyOperatorNormS_le` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`TransverseBondEnergyNorm.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the transverse bond energy (per-bond oscillator strength `= ‚àí[≈ú_a^{(3)},[≈ú_a¬∑≈ú_b,≈ú_a^{(3)}]]`) has operator norm `‚Äñ≈ú_a^{(1)}≈ú_b^{(1)} + ≈ú_a^{(2)}≈ú_b^{(2)}‚Äñ ‚â§ 2N¬≤` (per-site norm bound `‚Äñ≈ú^{(Œ±)}‚Äñ ‚â§ N` + submultiplicativity); summed over the `O(L)` adjacent bonds this bounds the oscillator strength by `O(L)` | `Quantum/SpinS/TransverseBondEnergyNorm.lean` |
| `pair_double_commutator_eq_zero_of_ne` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`PairCommutatorVanish.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): off-pair vanishing `[≈ú_x^{(3)}, [ƒ§, ≈ú_z^{(3)}]] = 0` for `x ‚àâ {z‚àí1, z, z+1}` ‚Äî since `[ƒ§, ≈ú_z^{(3)}]` is supported on `z`'s two incident bonds, `≈ú_x^{(3)}` commutes with it (Commute lemmas); hence the oscillator-strength double sum has only `O(L)` nonzero terms (three per `z`) | `Quantum/SpinS/PairCommutatorVanish.lean` |
| `no_long_range_order_1d_of_susceptibility` | **Cor 4.3 / conditional reduction** (`NoLongRangeOrderConditional.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the exact `Œµ`‚Äì`Œ¥` statement of Corollary 4.3 *modulo the susceptibility bound* ‚Äî if there is `C ‚â• 0` such that every normalized ground state of the zero-field ring has a potential `y` for `√îŒ¶` with `Re‚ü®y,√îŒ¶‚ü© ‚â§ C¬∑L`, then for every `Œµ > 0` there is `L‚ÇÄ` beyond which every normalized ground state has `\|‚ü®Œ¶,√î¬≤Œ¶‚ü©.re/L¬≤\| < Œµ` (assembling the `O(L)` oscillator bound + susceptibility reduction + ground-state bridge + an Archimedean `Œµ`‚Äì`Œ¥`). This isolates the unconditional Cor 4.3 to the reflection-positivity infrared bound `Re‚ü®y,√îŒ¶‚ü© ‚â§ C¬∑L`; the axiom `no_long_range_order_1d` remains until that bound is proven (PR #4848) | `Quantum/SpinS/NoLongRangeOrderConditional.lean` |
| `groundState_mulVec_eq_hermitianMinEigenvalue` | **Cor 4.3 / ground-state bridge** (`HermitianGroundStateEigenvalue.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): a ground-state eigenvalue equals the minimum eigenvalue ‚Äî for a Hermitian `H` and a normalized eigenvector `Œ¶` (`HŒ¶ = E‚ÇÄ‚Ä¢Œ¶`) whose `E‚ÇÄ` has minimal real part over all eigenpairs, `HŒ¶ = (hermitianMinEigenvalue H)‚Ä¢Œ¶` (`E‚ÇÄ.im = 0` by Hermiticity; `E‚ÇÄ.re` squeezed between the variational lower bound and the minimizing eigenvector); bridges the "energy-minimizing eigenpair" axiom hypothesis to the Falk‚ÄìBruch `hermitianMinEigenvalue` interface (PR #4847) | `Quantum/SpinS/HermitianGroundStateEigenvalue.lean` |
| `staggeredOrder_sq_le_susceptibility` | **Cor 4.3 / Falk‚ÄìBruch** (`StaggeredOrderSusceptibility.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the squared staggered order parameter is bounded by `O(L)` times the susceptibility ‚Äî for a ground state `Œ¶` of the zero-field ring (eigenvalue `hermitianMinEigenvalue`) and a potential `y` for `√îŒ¶` (`(ƒ§‚àíE‚ÇÄ)y = √îŒ¶`), `2(Re‚ü®Œ¶,√î¬≤Œ¶‚ü©)¬≤ ‚â§ 12N¬≥¬∑L¬∑Re‚ü®y,√îŒ¶‚ü©` (Falk‚ÄìBruch `2‚ü®√î¬≤‚ü©¬≤‚â§osc¬∑œá` with the `O(L)` oscillator bound `osc‚â§12N¬≥L` and `œá=Re‚ü®y,√îŒ¶‚ü©‚â•0` PSD); reduces Cor 4.3 to the susceptibility bound `œá ‚â§ C¬∑L` (PR #4846) | `Quantum/SpinS/StaggeredOrderSusceptibility.lean` |
| `oscillatorStrength_abs_le` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`OscillatorStrengthBound.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the f-sum-rule oscillator strength is `O(L)` ‚Äî in a normalized state `Œ¶`, `|‚ü®Œ¶, [√î, [ƒ§, √î]] Œ¶‚ü©.re| ‚â§ 12N¬≥¬∑L` (assembling the double-sum form, the off-pair vanishing restricting to three nonzero `z` per `x`, the uniform per-pair norm `‚â§ 4N¬≥`, and the norm‚Üíexpectation bridge); the `O(L)` numerator of the ground-state Falk‚ÄìBruch bound (PR #4845) | `Quantum/SpinS/OscillatorStrengthBound.lean` |
| `pair_double_commutator_norm_le` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`PairCommutatorNorm.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): each summand of the oscillator strength is bounded uniformly in `L` ‚Äî `‚Äñ[≈ú_x^{(3)}, [ƒ§, ≈ú_z^{(3)}]]‚Äñ ‚â§ 4N¬≥` (commutator norm `‚â§ 2‚Äñ≈ú^{(3)}‚Äñ¬∑‚Äñ[ƒ§, ≈ú_z^{(3)}]‚Äñ` with `‚Äñ≈ú^{(3)}‚Äñ ‚â§ N/2` and `‚Äñ[ƒ§, ≈ú_z^{(3)}]‚Äñ ‚â§ 4N¬≤`); combined with off-pair vanishing gives `O(L)` | `Quantum/SpinS/PairCommutatorNorm.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_commutator_onSiteS_spinSOp3_norm_le` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`RingHamiltonianCommutatorNorm.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): since `[ƒ§, ≈ú_z^{(3)}]` localizes to `z`'s two incident bonds, its operator norm is `O(1)` ‚Äî `‚Äñ[ƒ§, ≈ú_z^{(3)}]‚Äñ ‚â§ 4N¬≤` (sum of the two single-bond spin-current norms `‚â§ 2N¬≤` each); the length-independent locality making the oscillator strength `O(L)` | `Quantum/SpinS/RingHamiltonianCommutatorNorm.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_commutator_onSiteS_spinSOp3_closed` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`RingHamiltonianCommutatorClosed.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): closed form of the spin-current divergence on a ring `L ‚â• 2` ‚Äî `[ƒ§, ≈ú_z^{(3)}] = [≈ú_z¬∑≈ú_{z+1}, ≈ú_z^{(3)}] + [≈ú_{z‚àí1}¬∑≈ú_z, ≈ú_z^{(3)}]` (the bond sum collapses to `z`'s two incident bonds via `Finset.sum_pair`, every other bond commuting); plus `finRotate_ne_self` | `Quantum/SpinS/RingHamiltonianCommutatorClosed.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_commutator_onSiteS_spinSOp3` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`RingHamiltonianCommutatorBondSum.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the ring Hamiltonian commutator with `≈ú_z^{(3)}` as a bond sum `[ƒ§, ≈ú_z^{(3)}] = Œ£_x [≈ú_x¬∑≈ú_{x+1}, ≈ú_z^{(3)}]` (bond-sum form + commutator distribution); since each bond commutator vanishes off `z`, this localizes the spin-current divergence to `z`'s two incident bonds | `Quantum/SpinS/RingHamiltonianCommutatorBondSum.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_eq_bondSum_general` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`RingBondSumGeneral.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the ring Heisenberg Hamiltonian on `Fin L` of any length as a bond sum `ƒ§ = Œ£_x ≈ú_x¬∑≈ú_{finRotate L x}` (directed coupling collapses the defining double sum); the form used to localize `[ƒ§, ≈ú_z^{(3)}]` to the two bonds incident to `z` | `Quantum/SpinS/RingBondSumGeneral.lean` |
| `staggeredOrderOpS_double_commutator_dotProduct` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`OscillatorStrengthForm.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the oscillator strength as a double sum of two-site expectations `‚ü®Œ¶,[√î,[ƒ§,√î]]Œ¶‚ü© = Œ£_x Œ£_z Œµ_x Œµ_z ‚ü®Œ¶,[≈ú_x^{(3)},[ƒ§,≈ú_z^{(3)}]]Œ¶‚ü©` (mulVec/dotProduct linearity over the operator double sum), whose summands vanish off adjacent sites ‚Äî the `O(L)` oscillator strength | `Quantum/SpinS/OscillatorStrengthForm.lean` |
| `staggeredOrderOpS_double_commutator` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`StaggeredOrderDoubleCommutator.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the f-sum-rule double commutator as a double site sum `[√î,[ƒ§,√î]] = Œ£_x Œ£_z Œµ_x Œµ_z [≈ú_x^{(3)},[ƒ§,≈ú_z^{(3)}]]` (order-operator commutator distribution applied twice; `_commutator'` reversed variant), whose summands vanish off adjacent sites so the apparent `O(L¬≤)` collapses to `O(L)` | `Quantum/SpinS/StaggeredOrderDoubleCommutator.lean` |
| `staggeredOrderOpS_commutator` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`StaggeredOrderCommutator.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the staggered order operator commutator distributes over sites `[√î, C] = Œ£_x Œµ_x [≈ú_x^{(3)}, C]`; applied twice it expands the f-sum-rule double commutator `[√î,[ƒ§,√î]]` into a double site sum, whose terms vanish off adjacent sites (reducing the naive `O(L¬≤)` count to `O(L)`) | `Quantum/SpinS/StaggeredOrderCommutator.lean` |
| `staggeredOrderOpS_sq_dotProduct` | **Cor 4.3 bridge** (`StaggeredOrderSquareForm.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the ground-state quadratic-form expansion `‚ü®Œ¶, √î¬≤ Œ¶‚ü© = Œ£_x Œ£_y Œµ_x Œµ_y ‚ü®Œ¶, ≈ú_x^{(3)} ≈ú_y^{(3)} Œ¶‚ü©`, rewriting the order parameter in the exact form `star Œ¶ ‚¨ù·µ• (√î¬≤).mulVec Œ¶` of the Corollary through the two-point functions the RP machinery bounds | `Quantum/SpinS/StaggeredOrderSquareForm.lean` |
| `staggeredOrderOpS_sq_eq_sum` | **Cor 4.3 bridge** (`StaggeredOrderSquare.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the staggered order operator squared expands as the two-point double sum `√î¬≤ = Œ£_x Œ£_y Œµ_x Œµ_y ≈ú_x^{(3)} ≈ú_y^{(3)}` ‚Äî the bridge from the order parameter to the two-point functions bounded by the reflection-positivity / infrared machinery (the squared order parameter per `L¬≤` is controlled by the total two-point sum, only `O(L)` in one dimension) | `Quantum/SpinS/StaggeredOrderSquare.lean` |
| `rpTraceWeight_cauchySchwarz_abs` | **RP infra layer 40** (`RingReflectionCauchySchwarzSqrt.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the square-root (usable) form of the RP Cauchy‚ÄìSchwarz ‚Äî `|Re Tr(M¬∑Œ∏A¬∑B)| ‚â§ ‚àö(Re Tr(M¬∑Œ∏A¬∑A))¬∑‚àö(Re Tr(M¬∑Œ∏B¬∑B))` for a reflection-invariant RP trace weight `M` and left-supported `A,B`, by taking roots in the chessboard inequality (diagonals nonnegative); the form iterated in the DLS spreading argument | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionCauchySchwarzSqrt.lean` |
| `ringTranslateConj` / `chessboard_ringTranslateConj_of` | **RP infra layer 39** (`RingReflectionChessboardTransport.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): conjugation by the ring translation `œÑ(X) = T‚ÄÝ¬∑X¬∑T` is a trace-preserving `*`-homomorphism (`ringTranslateConj_mul`/`_trace`), so any chessboard estimate transports unchanged to the translation-conjugated data `(œÑM, œÑ(Œ∏A), œÑB)` (`chessboard_ringTranslateConj_of`) ‚Äî the weight `œÑM` is the Marshall gauge relative to the shifted reflection plane; carries the single-axis chessboard to every bond toward Gaussian domination | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionChessboardTransport.lean` |
| `chainTranslation_conj_gibbs` / `thermalAverageReS_chainTranslation` | **RP infra layer 38** (`RingTranslationGibbs.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): unlike the half-system gauge, the physical ring Heisenberg Hamiltonian is translation invariant (`chainTranslation_conj_heisenbergHamiltonianS`), so the physical Gibbs operator `exp(‚àíŒ≤¬∑ƒ§)` is translation invariant (`chainTranslation_conj_gibbs`) and every physical thermal average is translation covariant `‚ü®T‚ÄÝAT‚ü© = ‚ü®A‚ü©` (`thermalAverageReS_chainTranslation`); the thermal-state translation symmetry for the momentum-space infrared analysis | `Quantum/SpinS/RingTranslationGibbs.lean` |
| `chainTranslation_conj_ringReflectionThetaS_onSiteS` / `_spinSDot` | **RP infra layer 37** (`RingReflectionTranslation.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): translation covariance of the fixed-axis reflection map `Œ∏` ‚Äî conjugating `Œ∏(onSiteS x A)` (resp. `Œ∏(≈ú_x¬∑≈ú_y)`) by the ring translation `chainTranslationOp` shifts the reflection axis, sending the sites to `finRotate (ringReflect x)` (resp. both endpoints); the foundation for reflections across every bond, needed to spread the single-axis chessboard toward DLS Gaussian domination | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTranslation.lean` |
| `ringDLSGibbs_reflection_step` | **RP infra layer 36** (`RingReflectionGibbsChessboard.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the concrete chessboard inequality for the gauged ring Gibbs weight ‚Äî combining its reflection positivity (Gibbs capstone) and reflection invariance (`Œ∏M=M`), for `Œ≤ ‚â• 0` and left-supported `A,B`, `(Re Tr(M¬∑Œ∏A¬∑B))¬≤ ‚â§ Re Tr(M¬∑Œ∏A¬∑A)¬∑Re Tr(M¬∑Œ∏B¬∑B)` with `M = exp(‚àíŒ≤¬∑toHamiltonian)`; the DLS factorization estimate for the thermal state of the physical ring antiferromagnet | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsChessboard.lean` |
| `ringReflectionThetaS_toHamiltonian` / `_ringDLSGibbs` | **RP infra layer 35** (`RingReflectionThetaInvariance.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the gauged DLS Hamiltonian is reflection-invariant `Œ∏(toHamiltonian) = toHamiltonian` (the `H_L + Œ∏(H_L)` part is swapped to itself; each crossing bond `Œ∏(crossBondInteractionS x) = crossBondInteractionS x` since `≈ú_x^Œ±` commutes with its right-half reflection `Œ∏(≈ú_x^Œ±)`), hence the gauged ring Gibbs weight `exp(‚àíŒ≤¬∑toHamiltonian)` is reflection-invariant ‚Äî supplying the `Œ∏M = M` hypothesis for the chessboard/real-diagonal lemmas | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionThetaInvariance.lean` |
| `rpForm_re_symm` / `rpTraceWeight_reflection_step` | **RP infra layer 34** (`RingReflectionChessboard.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): for a reflection-invariant RP trace weight `M` (`Œ∏M=M`) and left-supported `A,B`, the off-diagonal form value is symmetric `Re Tr(M¬∑Œ∏A¬∑B) = Re Tr(M¬∑Œ∏B¬∑A)` (since `A` commutes with the right-half `Œ∏B`); feeding this into the Cauchy‚ÄìSchwarz discriminant collapses the cross term, giving the chessboard bound `(Re Tr(M¬∑Œ∏A¬∑B))¬≤ ‚â§ Re Tr(M¬∑Œ∏A¬∑A)¬∑Re Tr(M¬∑Œ∏B¬∑B)` ‚Äî the DLS factorization step underlying Gaussian domination | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionChessboard.lean` |
| `ringReflectionThetaS_trace` / `rpForm_diag_im_zero` | **RP infra layer 33** (`RingReflectionTraceReality.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the reflection map conjugates the trace `Tr(Œ∏X) = conj(Tr X)` (reindex the diagonal by the involutive site reflection), so for a reflection-invariant weight `M` (`Œ∏M = M`) and left-supported `A` the RP-form diagonal `Tr(M¬∑Œ∏A¬∑A)` is real (`Œ∏¬≤=id`, `*`-homomorphism, `A` commutes with `Œ∏A`); together with the Cauchy‚ÄìSchwarz this makes `(A,B)‚Ü¶Tr(M¬∑Œ∏A¬∑B)` a genuine positive-semidefinite form with real diagonal | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTraceReality.lean` |
| `rpTraceWeight_cauchySchwarz` | **RP infra layer 32** (`RingReflectionCauchySchwarz.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): a reflection-positive trace weight `M` makes `(A,B) ‚Ü¶ Tr(M¬∑Œ∏A¬∑B)` a positive-semidefinite sesquilinear form on the left subalgebra; expanding `0 ‚â§ Re Tr(M¬∑Œ∏(A‚àítB)¬∑(A‚àítB))` as a nonnegative real quadratic in `t` and taking its discriminant (`discrim_le_zero`) gives `(Re Tr(M¬∑Œ∏A¬∑B) + Re Tr(M¬∑Œ∏B¬∑A))¬≤ ‚â§ 4¬∑Re Tr(M¬∑Œ∏A¬∑A)¬∑Re Tr(M¬∑Œ∏B¬∑B)` ‚Äî the workhorse for the DLS infrared bound | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionCauchySchwarz.lean` |
| `thermalAverageReS_toHamiltonian_eq` | **RP infra layer 31** (`RingReflectionThermalTransfer.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the right-half gauge is a similarity transform, so it leaves the partition function invariant (`thermalPartitionFnS_toHamiltonian_eq`) and transports thermal averages ‚Äî the physical ring Heisenberg canonical average of `A` equals the reflection-positive DLS canonical average of `rightGauge ¬∑ A ¬∑ rightGaugeInv`; the bridge carrying RP bounds back to the physical ring antiferromagnet | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionThermalTransfer.lean` |
| `exp_neg_smul_toHamiltonian_eq` | **RP infra layer 30** (`RingReflectionGibbsGauge.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): conjugation by the right-half gauge commutes with the matrix exponential (`Matrix.exp_units_conj`), so the physical ring Heisenberg Gibbs operator `exp(‚àíŒ≤ ƒ§)` and the reflection-positive DLS Gibbs operator `exp(‚àíŒ≤ ¬∑ ringDLSDecomposition.toHamiltonian)` are unitarily equivalent ‚Äî physical thermal averages become DLS thermal averages of gauge-transformed observables | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsGauge.lean` |
| `rightGauge_conj_ringHamiltonian` | **RP infra layer 29** (`RingReflectionGaugeAssembly.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the right-half DLS/Marshall gauge conjugates the ring Heisenberg Hamiltonian into the reflection-positive DLS Hamiltonian ‚Äî `rightGauge ¬∑ heisenbergHamiltonianS (ringCoupling (2n)) ¬∑ rightGaugeInv = ringDLSDecomposition.toHamiltonian` (bond Hamiltonians fixed, the two crossing bonds become the negated gauged crossing interactions); the unitary equivalence bridging the physical ring antiferromagnet to its reflection-positive form | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGaugeAssembly.lean` |
| `rightGauge_conj_ringLeftHamiltonian` / `_theta_*` | **RP infra layer 28** (`RingReflectionNonCrossConj.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): summing the bond-invariance lemmas, the right-half gauge fixes both the left bond Hamiltonian (`rightGauge_conj_ringLeftHamiltonian`) and the reflected right bond Hamiltonian `Œ∏(ringLeftHamiltonian)` (`rightGauge_conj_theta_ringLeftHamiltonian`) ‚Äî only the two crossing bonds are affected | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionNonCrossConj.lean` |
| `rightGauge_conj_crossBond` | **RP infra layer 27** (`RingReflectionCrossConj.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the gauge image of a crossing bond is the negated gauged crossing interaction. For a left site `x < n` (reflection `r x` a right site), `rightGauge ¬∑ (≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_{r x}) ¬∑ rightGaugeInv = ‚àícrossBondInteractionS x` ‚Äî the left endpoint untouched, the right endpoint gauged (flipping `≈ú^1`/`≈ú^3` signs), matching `crossBondInteractionS_eq`. Both ring crossing bonds `(n‚àí1,n)` and `(2n‚àí1,0)` are of this form | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionCrossConj.lean` |
| `rightGauge_conj_spinSDot_left`/`_right` | **RP infra layer 26** (`RingReflectionBondConj.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the right-half gauge fixes every non-crossing bond. `rightGauge_conj_spinSDot_left` (left bond, both sites `< n`: untouched) and `rightGauge_conj_spinSDot_right` (right bond, both sites gauged: the `≈ú^1`/`≈ú^3` sign flips cancel in the product, `≈ú^2` unchanged). Only the two crossing bonds acquire the DLS sign flip. Helpers `rightGauge_conj_onSiteS_left`/`_right` | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionBondConj.lean` |
| `rightGauge_conj_mul` (gauge homomorphism) | **RP infra layer 25** (`RingReflectionGaugeConj.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): conjugation `X ‚Ü¶ rightGauge¬∑X¬∑rightGaugeInv` by the right-half DLS/Marshall gauge is an algebra homomorphism ‚Äî `rightGauge_conj_mul` (over products, via `rightGaugeInv_mul_rightGauge`), `rightGauge_conj_add`, `rightGauge_conj_smul`, `rightGauge_conj_sum`. With `rightGauge_conj_onSiteS` these compute the gauge image of any operator built from single-site operators (the ring Hamiltonian in particular) | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGaugeConj.lean` |
| `AxisTwoPiRotS` / `rightGauge_conj_onSiteS` | **RP infra layer 24** (`RingReflectionGauge.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the right-half DLS/Marshall gauge. `manyBodyTensorS_conj_onSiteS_dep` ‚Äî site-dependent tensor conjugation `(‚äó_i W_i)(onSiteS z A)(‚äó_i Winv_i) = onSiteS z (W_z¬∑A¬∑Winv_z)` (for `W_i¬∑Winv_i = 1`). `AxisTwoPiRotS` ‚Äî the single-site œÄ-rotation around axis 2 as an interface (following `AxisSwapUnitaryS`; fields `U ≈ú^1 U‚Åª¬π = ‚àí≈ú^1`, `U ≈ú^2 U‚Åª¬π = ≈ú^2`, `U ≈ú^3 U‚Åª¬π = ‚àí≈ú^3`, non-vacuous at spin-1/2). `rightGauge`/`rightGaugeInv` ‚Äî the gauge unitary applying `U` on the right sites only; `rightGaugeInv_mul_rightGauge` (invertible) and `rightGauge_conj_onSiteS` (conjugation acts as `U¬∑A¬∑U‚Åª¬π` on right sites, trivially on left). The interface is made **non-vacuous at spin-1/2** by `axisTwoPiRotSpinHalf` (the explicit `spinHalfRot2 œÄ`). The gauge bridging the ungauged ring Hamiltonian to its reflection-positive form | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGauge.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_ungauged_dls` | **RP infra layer 23** (`RingReflectionUngaugedDLS.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the ungauged DLS split of the ring Heisenberg Hamiltonian. Combining the bond split (#4798) with `ringRightBondSum = Œ∏(ringLeftHamiltonian)` (#4799): `heisenbergHamiltonianS (ringCoupling (2n)) = ringLeftHamiltonian + Œ∏(ringLeftHamiltonian) + (≈ú_{n‚àí1}¬∑≈ú_n + ≈ú_{2n‚àí1}¬∑≈ú_0)`. The crossing term is the *ungauged* AFM crossing bond, differing from the gauged `crossBondInteractionS` (with `≈ú¬≤` sign flipped) by the DLS/Marshall gauge; bridging the two via the gauge unitary is the next step | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionUngaugedDLS.lean` |
| `ringRightBondSum_eq_theta` | **RP infra layer 22** (`RingReflectionRightEqTheta.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the right bond Hamiltonian equals the reflection of the left bond Hamiltonian. Reindexing by the bijection `x ‚Ü¶ r(x+1)` (reflection of the cyclic successor `ringBondSucc`, shown bijective `= ¬∑+1` via `Equiv.addRight`) and using `spinSDot` symmetry, `ringRightBondSum = Œ∏(ringLeftHamiltonian)` (`Fintype.sum_bijective`). This identifies the right part of the bond split with the `Œ∏(H_L)` term of the DLS decomposition; combined with the bond split (#4798), `heisenbergHamiltonianS (ringCoupling (2n)) = ringLeftHamiltonian + Œ∏(ringLeftHamiltonian) + (ungauged crossing bonds)` | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionRightEqTheta.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_bond_split` | **RP infra layer 21** (`RingReflectionBondSplit.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the ring Heisenberg Hamiltonian split into left, right, and crossing bonds. The bonds `(x, x+1)` of `Fin (2n)` fall into four disjoint classes by `x.val` (left `x ‚â§ n‚àí2`, crossing `x = n‚àí1`, right `n ‚â§ x ‚â§ 2n‚àí2`, crossing `x = 2n‚àí1`); summing the bond form over this partition (`bondTerm_four_way` per-bond decomposition + `Finset.sum_add_distrib` + `sum_ite_val_eq`) gives `heisenbergHamiltonianS (ringCoupling (2n)) = ringLeftHamiltonian + ringRightBondSum + ≈ú_{n‚àí1}¬∑≈ú_n + ≈ú_{2n‚àí1}¬∑≈ú_0`. Identifying `ringRightBondSum` with `Œ∏(ringLeftHamiltonian)` and the crossing bonds with the gauged interaction is the next step | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionBondSplit.lean` |
| `ringDLSDecomposition_gibbs_rpTraceWeight` | **RP infra layer 20** (`RingReflectionConcreteGibbs.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the concrete gauged ring antiferromagnet has a reflection-positive Gibbs weight. Instantiating the ring DLS decomposition with the concrete left part `H_L = ringLeftHamiltonian` (`ringDLSDecomposition`), `ringDLSDecomposition_toHamiltonian` gives the gauged ring Hamiltonian `H = ringLeftHamiltonian + Œ∏(ringLeftHamiltonian) ‚àí (crossBondInteractionS 0 + crossBondInteractionS (n‚àí1))`, and `ringDLSDecomposition_gibbs_rpTraceWeight` concludes (via the Gibbs capstone) that `exp(‚àíŒ≤¬∑H)` is a reflection-positive trace weight (`Œ≤ ‚â• 0`). Identification of `H` with the physical ungauged Hamiltonian (bond-split assembly + gauge unitary) is the next step | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionConcreteGibbs.lean` |
| `ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian` / `ringBondSquareLinField` / `ringBondSquareConst` / `ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian_eq` / `ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian_isHermitian` | **Bond-square field Hamiltonian foundation** (`RingReflectionBondSquareField.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1 (4.1.48), book p.86, bond-square field Hamiltonian / PR #4987): the faithful quadratic bond-square field Hamiltonian and its reduction to the linear-core form. Tasaki's field Hamiltonian couples the staggered field `f_x = (‚àí1)À£ h_x` **quadratically** inside the bond-square `¬Ω{≈ú¬≥‚Çì + ≈ú¬≥_y ‚àí f_x ‚àí f_y}¬≤`; this square form is the faithful object. The equivalent linear-core form `ringFieldHamiltonian n N (ringBondSquareLinField n h) + (ringBondSquareConst n h : ‚ÑÇ) ‚Ä¢ 1` is a **proved identity `(‚òÖ)` `ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian_eq`**, obtained by expanding the square, completing the isotropic dot product with the `≈ú¬≥‚Çì≈ú¬≥_y` cross term, cancelling the single-ion terms via successor reindexing, and regrouping into linear and scalar parts. The staggered-field per-site coefficient `ringBondSquareLinField n h z = ‚àí(f_z + f_{z+1}) ‚àí (f_{z‚àí1} + f_z)` (the two incident ring-bond field sums, Tasaki `bÃÉ`-field (4.1.38), book p.84). The scalar constant `ringBondSquareConst n h = ¬Ω Œ£_bonds (f_x + f_{x+1})¬≤` is nonnegative (deferred; C(h)‚â•0 is consumed in later PR-BS3+ where the collapse inequality and uniform-field bound are applied) and repairs the reflection-bound inequality direction. The bond-square Hamiltonian is Hermitian (`ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian_isHermitian`) as the sum of the Hermitian linear-core part and the real scalar constant. This is PR-BS1 (bond-square route) toward the reflection-positivity infrastructure for Theorem 4.2. | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionBondSquareField.lean` |
| `ringBondSquareFieldPartitionRe` / `ringBondSquareFieldPartitionRe_eq_scaled` | **Bond-square field partition function and scaled reduction** (`RingReflectionBondSquareFieldPartition.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1 (4.1.49), book p.86, bond-square partition function / PR #4988): the canonical partition function `Z^{BS}_Œ≤(h) = Re Tr exp(‚àíŒ≤¬∑ƒ§_h)` of the bond-square field Hamiltonian `ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian` (Tasaki (4.1.48), book p.86). The crux is the **scalar-shift reduction `(‚òÖ‚òÖ)` `ringBondSquareFieldPartitionRe_eq_scaled`**: since the reduction identity `(‚òÖ)` (`ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian_eq`, PR-BS1) decomposes `ƒ§_h = ƒ§(kOf h) + C(h)¬∑1` where `C(h)¬∑1` is a real scalar central element commuting with `ƒ§(kOf h)`, the matrix exponential splits: `exp(‚àíŒ≤¬∑ƒ§_h) = exp(‚àíŒ≤¬∑ƒ§(kOf h)) ¬∑ exp(‚àíŒ≤C(h)¬∑1)`, and the scalar exponential `exp(‚àíŒ≤C(h)¬∑1)` evaluates to `e^{‚àíŒ≤C(h)}¬∑1` (via `Matrix.exp_diagonal`). The real scalar `e^{‚àíŒ≤C(h)}` pulls out of both the trace (`Matrix.trace_smul`) and the real part (`Complex.re_ofReal_mul`), yielding `Z^{BS}_Œ≤(h) = e^{‚àíŒ≤C(h)} ¬∑ Z^{repo}_Œ≤(kOf h)` (Tasaki ¬ß4.1 (4.1.49), book p.86), the finite-`Œ≤` form of the uniform-field bound. This equation lets later PR-BS3+ reuse the single-field symmetries of `ringFieldPartitionRe` for the bond-square partition function without redefining `Z` on a field map. This is PR-BS2 of the bond-square route toward the reflection-positivity infrastructure for Theorem 4.2. | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionBondSquareFieldPartition.lean` |
| `ringBondSquareConst_const` / `ringBondSquareLinField_const` / `ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian_const` / `ringBondSquareFieldPartitionRe_const` | **Constant-field collapse** (`RingReflectionBondSquareField.lean` / `RingReflectionBondSquareFieldPartition.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1 footnote 10 (4.1.49), book pp. 84‚Äì86, constant-field collapse / PR #4989): at a constant field `h_x ‚â° c`, the staggered field on any ring bond sums to zero `f_x + f_{x+1} = (‚àí1)^x c + (‚àí1)^{x+1} c = 0` via the even-ring staggered sign relation `(‚àí1)^{x+1} = ‚àí(‚àí1)^x` (private lemma: `neg_one_pow_ringBondSucc`); consequently both the bond-square constant `C(h^const) = 0` (`ringBondSquareConst_const`) and the bond-square linear field `kOf(h^const) = 0` (`ringBondSquareLinField_const`) vanish (each per-site coefficient is the sum of two incident bond cancellations, proven via private `ringBondSquareStagField_const_bond_cancel`). The **operator collapse** `ƒ§(h^const) = ringFieldHamiltonian n N 0` (`ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian_const`) follows from the reduction identity `(‚òÖ)` and the two vanishings: the bond-square Hamiltonian at a constant field collapses to the field-free linear-core form `ƒ§‚ÇÄ` (Tasaki ¬ß4.1 (4.1.49), book p.86, finite-`N` form of `E_GS(h^const) = E_GS(0,‚Ä¶,0)`). The **partition-function collapse** `Z^{BS}_Œ≤(h^const) = Z^{repo}_Œ≤(0)` (`ringBondSquareFieldPartitionRe_const`) is immediate from the operator collapse: both partition functions are the real part of the Gibbs trace of the same Hamiltonian `ringFieldHamiltonian n N 0`, and the scalar exponential `e^{‚àíŒ≤C(h^const)} = e^0 = 1` vanishes from the `(‚òÖ‚òÖ)` reduction (finite-`Œ≤` form of `E_GS(h^const) = E_GS(0,‚Ä¶,0)`, consumed by the uniform-field Gaussian-domination bound PR-BS10+). This is PR-BS3 of the bond-square route toward the reflection-positivity infrastructure for Theorem 4.2. | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionBondSquareField.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionBondSquareFieldPartition.lean` |
| `ringBondSquareLeftFieldHamiltonian` / `ringBondSquareLeftFieldHamiltonian_supportedOnLeft` / `ringBondSquareCrossingGen` / `ringBondSquareCrossingGen_supportedOnLeft` / `ringBondSquareFieldCrossing` / `ringBondSquareFieldCrossing_twoFieldConeRep` / `ringBondSquareTwoFieldWeight` / `ringBondSquareTwoFieldWeight_self` / `ringBondSquareTwoFieldWeight_isLimit` | **Bond-square DLS decomposition and two-field weight** (`RingReflectionBondSquareTwoFieldWeight.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1 (4.1.65)‚Äì(4.1.69), book p. 90, bond-square DLS structure and Trotter limit / PR #4992, H_L corrected PR #4994): the left-half Hamiltonian, field-dependent crossing generators, and the doubled Gibbs weight for the bond-square model, all in the gauge spin basis. **Left half**: `ringBondSquareLeftFieldHamiltonian n N a` is the intra-left bond terms `+≈ú¬π≈ú¬π + ≈ú¬≤≈ú¬≤ + ¬Ω(≈ú¬≥‚Çì + ≈ú¬≥_y ‚àí a_x ‚àí a_y)¬≤`, the single-ion `‚àí(≈ú¬≥)¬≤`, and the boundary half-square `¬Ω(≈ú¬≥‚Çì ‚àí a_x)¬≤` (in repo DLS physical Heisenberg frame; sign corrected from TÃÇ-form transcription via the `(‚àí1)À£` of `TÃÇ¬≥ = (‚àí1)À£≈ú¬≥` flipping Œ±=1 to `+≈ú¬π≈ú¬π` and converting longitudinal difference to sum-form; left-supported, proved by `ringBondSquareLeftFieldHamiltonian_supportedOnLeft`; exact physical coefficients deferred to PR-BS8; PR #4994). **Crossing generators**: `ringBondSquareCrossingGen n N p z` is field-free on kinetic slots `Œ± = 0,1` and carries a bare central scalar shift `‚àíz_x` on the longitudinal slot `Œ± = 2` (Tasaki (4.1.69), book p. 90), left-supported (`ringBondSquareCrossingGen_supportedOnLeft`). **Two-field crossing**: `ringBondSquareFieldCrossing n N a b` is the field-dependent interaction `‚àë_c Œ∏(C_c(b))¬∑C_c(a)` (with reflected `b` on the right, non-reflected `a` on the left), instantiating the `RPTwoFieldConeRepS` shape (`ringBondSquareFieldCrossing_twoFieldConeRep`). **Two-field weight**: `ringBondSquareTwoFieldWeight n N Œ≤ a b` is the doubled Gibbs operator `exp(‚àíŒ≤¬∑(H_L(a) + Œ∏(H_L(b)) ‚àí crossing))` with independent left and right fields; its diagonal collapse to a single field (`ringBondSquareTwoFieldWeight_self`) recovers the symmetric-field DLS form; its Trotter-limit representation (`ringBondSquareTwoFieldWeight_isLimit`, via `lieProductFormula`) decomposes as `(exp(‚àí(Œ≤/m)H_L(a)) ¬∑ Œ∏(exp(‚àí(Œ≤/m)H_L(b))) ¬∑ exp((Œ≤/m)¬∑crossing))^m` converging as `m ‚Üí ‚àû` (mirroring the linear `ringTwoFieldWeight_isLimit` with the two-field crossing dependence). The physical coefficients and field identification are deferred to PR-BS8 (the DLS form is Hamiltonian definition). This is PR-BS6 of the bond-square route toward the reflection-positivity infrastructure for Theorem 4.2. | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionBondSquareTwoFieldWeight.lean` |
| `ringBondSquareTwoFieldWeight_reflection_cauchySchwarz` | **Bond-square reflection Cauchy‚ÄìSchwarz capstone** (`RingReflectionTwoFieldCauchySchwarz.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1 (4.1.51), book p. 86, bond-square finite-Œ≤ reflection Cauchy‚ÄìSchwarz / PR #4993): the **bond-square variant of Tasaki's reflection Cauchy‚ÄìSchwarz inequality** ‚Äî `(Re Tr W^{BS}(a,b))¬≤ ‚â§ Re Tr W^{BS}(a,a) ¬∑ Re Tr W^{BS}(b,b)` where `W^{BS}(a,b) = ringBondSquareTwoFieldWeight n N Œ≤ a b`. The proof structure mirrors the linear-field version (`ringTwoFieldWeight_reflection_cauchySchwarz`, PR #4982) via nested double limit (DLS Trotter), with the finite `(m,r)` Cauchy‚ÄìSchwarz upgraded by the inner limit `r ‚Üí ‚àû` (exponential cone closure) and outer limit `m ‚Üí ‚àû` (Trotter convergence); both limits preserve the inequality via `cauchySchwarz_of_tendsto`. **Simpler than linear case**: the bond-square crossing is genuinely field-dependent (via the crossing generators instantiating `RPTwoFieldConeRepS`), so the `(b,b)` slot in the Trotter representation directly carries the correct field-dependent crossing from the isLimit statement ‚Äî no `interaction_eq` rewrite or new sign lemma is required (contrast with the linear case where the field-independent crossing necessitates `interaction_eq` unification of the (b,b) slot). The `0 ‚â§ Œ≤` hypothesis ensures cone positivity of `(Œ≤/m)D` where `D` is the bond-square crossing interaction. This capstone completes the bond-square reflection Cauchy‚ÄìSchwarz layer toward Theorem 4.2 (the bond-square Gaussian-domination sub-arc). | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTwoFieldCauchySchwarz.lean` |
| `ringBondSquareBondTermOf` / `ringBondSquareLeftBondSum` / `ringBondSquareRightBondSum` / `ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian_eq_bondTermOf_sum` / `ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian_ungauged_dls` / `ringBondSquareLeftBondSum_eq_leftCouplingBulk` | **Bond-square ungauged DLS split** (`RingReflectionBondSquareUngaugedDLS.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1 (4.1.48)/(4.1.69), book pp.86,90, ungauged bond-square DLS decomposition / PR #4995): the physical bond-square field Hamiltonian reorganised without expanding any square into four-way directed-bond classification, mirroring the linear ungauged DLS split (`heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_ungauged_dls`). **Per-bond term** `ringBondSquareBondTermOf n N f x`: the summand `≈ú¬π‚Çì≈ú¬π_{x+1} + ≈ú¬≤‚Çì≈ú¬≤_{x+1} + ¬Ω(≈ú¬≥‚Çì + ≈ú¬≥_{x+1} ‚àí f_x ‚àí f_{x+1})¬≤` at the staggered-field bare form `f` (no gauging). **Directed sums**: `ringBondSquareLeftBondSum` (intra-left bonds `x+1 < n`), `ringBondSquareRightBondSum` (intra-right bonds `n ‚â§ x ‚àß x+1 < 2n`) ‚Äî the four-way partition via `ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian_eq_bondTermOf_sum` and the auxiliary private `sum_four_way_split`. **Main theorem** `ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian_ungauged_dls`: `ƒ§(h) = intra-left + intra-right + crossing(n‚àí1) + crossing(2n‚àí1) ‚àí single-ion`, the bare-field ungauged form before PR-BS8a-ii gauge conjugation into the DLS crux `H_L(a) + Œ∏(H_L(b)) ‚àí crossing(a,b)`. **Bridge** `ringBondSquareLeftBondSum_eq_leftCouplingBulk`: the directed intra-left sum equals the merged `ringBondSquareLeftFieldHamiltonian`'s bulk double sum via `ringLeftCoupling`, the bond-square analogue of `ringLeftHamiltonian_eq_leftBondSum` (PR-RP infra 18), aligning the ungauged split with the DLS left half. Staggered-field visibility: `ringBondSquareStagField` (per-site coefficient `(‚àí1)^x h_x`, originally defined private in PR-BS1, `RingReflectionBondSquareField.lean`) is de-privatized in this PR (PR-BS8a-i, #4995) for this split's bond reorganisation. The gauge crux, physical-field identification, and reflection step are deferred to PR-BS8a-ii and PR-BS8b. This is PR-BS8a-i of the bond-square route toward the reflection-positivity infrastructure for Theorem 4.2. | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionBondSquareUngaugedDLS.lean` |
| `physBondSquareFieldOf` / `ringBondSquareStagField_physBondSquareFieldOf` / `rightGauge_conj_ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian` / `rightGauge_conj_sub` / `rightGauge_conj_ringBondSquareBondTermOf_left` / `rightGauge_conj_ringBondSquareLeftBondSum` / `rightGauge_conj_ringBondSquareSingleIon` / `rightGauge_conj_ringBondSquareRightBondSum` | **Bond-square right-half gauge crux (G)** (`RingReflectionBondSquareGaugeCrux.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1 (4.1.48)/(4.1.65)‚Äì(4.1.69), book pp.86,90, bond-square gauge conjugation and DLS crux / PR #4996): conjugating the ungauged bond-square field Hamiltonian (BS8a-i) by the right-half Marshall gauge into the two-field DLS operator `H_L(a) + Œ∏(H_L(b)) ‚àí crossing(a,b)`. The crux input is the **staggered wrapper** `physBondSquareFieldOf n a b z = (‚àí1)·∂ª ¬∑ physFieldOf n a b z`, a `(Fin (2n) ‚Üí ‚Ñù)` carrying the spin-basis physical field whose bare (TÃÇ-basis) split is the linear `physFieldOf a b`; feeding it into the bond-square Hamiltonian cancels the physical Hamiltonian's internal `(‚àí1)·∂ª` staggering via `((‚àí1)·∂ª)¬≤ = 1` (**W1** `ringBondSquareStagField_physBondSquareFieldOf`), so the effective field inside the square is the bare `physFieldOf a b`. The gauge distributes by algebra-homomorphism laws with **no square expanded** in the bulk: intra-left bonds are gauge-fixed to `H_L(a)` bulk; intra-right bonds reindex to `Œ∏(H_L(b))` bulk (the right-half double-sign cancellation); only the two `O(1)` crossing bonds are completed `¬Ω(A‚àíB)¬≤ = ¬ΩA¬≤ + ¬ΩB¬≤ ‚àí AB` into boundary half-squares and the field crossing `‚àíringBondSquareFieldCrossing a b`. The single-ion term splits left/right, right half reindexing to `Œ∏` of the left. Assembling gives the **gauge crux (G)** `rightGauge_conj_ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian`, on which PR-BS8b builds the physical-field identification and the one reflection step. Helper lemmas distribute conjugation over difference/sum/product with localized gauge action. **De-privatised** `sum_right_eq_sum_reflect_left` from `RingReflectionFieldPartition.lean` (reindexing the single-ion right half via reflection bijection, statement and proof unchanged, visibility only to avoid duplicate reindex lemma). This is PR-BS8a-ii of the bond-square route toward the reflection-positivity infrastructure for Theorem 4.2. | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionBondSquareGaugeCrux.lean` |
| `ringLeftFieldHamiltonian` / `ringLeftFieldHamiltonian_supportedOnLeft` | **Gaussian-domination layer 1** (`RingReflectionFieldWeight.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the field-augmented left Hamiltonian. For the Dyson‚ÄìLieb‚ÄìSimon Gaussian-domination bound, the first infrastructure piece augments the left-half DLS part `H_L` with a diagonal single-site field `Œ£_{x < n} (a x) ¬∑ ≈ú_x^{(3)}`, yielding `ringLeftFieldHamiltonian n N a = ringLeftHamiltonian + ‚àë_{x<n} (a x : ‚ÑÇ) ‚Ä¢ onSiteS x (spinSOp3 N)` (still left-supported); `ringLeftFieldHamiltonian_supportedOnLeft` proves left-supportedness (sum of left-supported terms) ‚Äî the field-shifted left part `Lfield(a)` of Tasaki eq. (4.1.9). This is the foundation for the symmetric-field Gibbs weight and the Gaussian-domination RP cone. | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionFieldWeight.lean` |
| `ringFieldDLSDecomposition` | **Gaussian-domination layer 2** (`RingReflectionFieldWeight.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): field-augmented DLS decomposition. The ring crossing reflection-positive decomposition instantiated with the field-augmented left part `ringLeftFieldHamiltonian`: `ringFieldDLSDecomposition n N a = ringCrossingRPDecomposition (ringLeftFieldHamiltonian n N a) (ringLeftFieldHamiltonian_supportedOnLeft n N a)`. Its reconstructed Hamiltonian is the symmetric-field ring Hamiltonian `Lfield(a) + Œ∏(Lfield(a)) ‚àí crossing`, the DLS form for the uniform-field Gaussian-domination bound `Z_Œ≤(h) ‚â§ Z_Œ≤(0)` (Tasaki ¬ß4.1, eqs. (4.1.49)‚Äì(4.1.51), p. 86). | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionFieldWeight.lean` |
| `ringReflectionThetaS_ringFieldDLSDecomposition_toHamiltonian` / `ringReflectionThetaS_ringDLSFieldWeightSym` | **Gaussian-domination layer 3** (`RingReflectionFieldWeight.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): reflection invariance of the field-augmented Hamiltonian and weight. The field-augmented DLS Hamiltonian is reflection invariant `Œ∏(toHamiltonian) = toHamiltonian` (the `Lfield + Œ∏(Lfield)` part is swapped by the involution `Œ∏`; each crossing-bond interaction is `Œ∏`-fixed); consequently the symmetric-field Gibbs weight `ringDLSFieldWeightSym n N Œ≤ a = exp(‚àíŒ≤ ¬∑ (ringFieldDLSDecomposition n N a).toHamiltonian)` is reflection-invariant `Œ∏(M) = M` ‚Äî supplies the `Œ∏ M = M` hypothesis for the chessboard/real-diagonal reflection-positivity lemmas in the Gaussian-domination bound. | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionFieldWeight.lean` |
| `ringDLSFieldWeightSym` / `ringDLSFieldWeightSym_rpTraceWeight` | **Gaussian-domination layer 4** (`RingReflectionFieldWeight.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): symmetric-field Gibbs weight and reflection positivity. For `Œ≤ ‚â• 0`, the symmetric-field Gibbs weight `ringDLSFieldWeightSym n N Œ≤ a = exp(‚àíŒ≤ ¬∑ H_{sym})` (with `H_{sym} = (ringFieldDLSDecomposition n N a).toHamiltonian` the symmetric-field ring Hamiltonian) is a reflection-positive trace weight ‚Äî direct consequence of the Gibbs capstone `RPDecomposition.gibbs_rpTraceWeight` applied to the field-augmented decomposition. This finite-Œ≤ field-shifted weight is the Fourier-insertion target consumed by the later Gaussian-domination PRs and is the first sound piece of the finite-Œ≤ Gaussian-domination sub-arc. | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionFieldWeight.lean` |
| `ringTwoFieldWeight` / `ringTwoFieldWeight_self` / `ringTwoFieldWeight_isLimit` | **Gaussian-domination layer 5** (`RingReflectionTwoFieldWeight.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, PR #4976, toward Theorem 4.2): the two-field ("doubled") Gibbs weight and Trotter representation. Decoupling the symmetric-field Gibbs weight into independent left and right halves, the doubled weight `W(a,b) := exp(‚àíŒ≤¬∑(Lfield(a) + Œ∏(Lfield(b)) ‚àí D))` (where `D = (ringFieldDLSDecomposition a).interaction`) carries independent fields `a` on the left and `b` on the right (transported by reflection `Œ∏`). The left part `Lfield(a)` is left-supported and `Œ∏(Lfield(b))` is right-supported, so they commute ‚Äî the kinetic factor splits by `exp_add_of_commute`, giving the Dyson‚ÄìLieb‚ÄìSimon Trotter approximant `(E_G ¬∑ Œ∏(E_{G'}) ¬∑ E_D)^m` with exponential factors merging via `ringReflectionThetaS_exp_mul_theta_exp` (asymmetric kinetic merge, placed in `RingReflectionKineticConeRep.lean` to avoid a norm-instance diamond). The Lie-product limit representation `ringTwoFieldWeight_isLimit` converges the Trotter sequence to `W(a,b)`. The diagonal case `ringTwoFieldWeight_self` recovers the symmetric-field weight of PR7a. This layer feeds the reflection Cauchy‚ÄìSchwarz stage (PR7b-iii) for Gaussian domination (eq. (4.1.51), p. 86). | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTwoFieldWeight.lean` |
| `ringReflectionThetaS_exp_mul_theta_exp` | **Gaussian-domination layer 5b** (`RingReflectionKineticConeRep.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, PR #4976, toward Theorem 4.2): asymmetric kinetic merge for the two-field weight. For left-supported `X` and `Y`, the product `exp X ¬∑ Œ∏(exp Y)` merges into `exp(X + Œ∏ Y)` (the left-supported `exp X` and right-supported `Œ∏(exp Y)` commute by disjoint supports). This is the two-field analogue of `ringReflectionThetaS_exp_add_eq` with independent left and right generators; placed here rather than in `RingReflectionTwoFieldWeight.lean` to avoid importing `MatrixExponential` and creating a norm-instance diamond. Consumed by `ringTwoFieldWeight_isLimit` as the kinetic-factor building block of the Lie-product convergence. | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionKineticConeRep.lean` |
| `ringTwoFieldWeight_reflection_cauchySchwarz` | **RP capstone layer 7b-iii-c** (`RingReflectionTwoFieldCauchySchwarz.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1 Theorem 4.2, finite-Œ≤ reflection Cauchy‚ÄìSchwarz / PR #4982): the **two-field reflection Cauchy‚ÄìSchwarz on the doubled Gibbs weight** ‚Äî the finite-Œ≤ matrix form of Tasaki's reflection bound (4.1.51), p. 86 (proof pp. 89‚Äì93; DLS 1978 ¬ß2‚Äì3). For `0 ‚â§ Œ≤`, `(Re Tr W(a,b))¬≤ ‚â§ Re Tr W(a,a) ¬∑ Re Tr W(b,b)` where `W(a,b) = ringTwoFieldWeight n N Œ≤ a b`. Proved by a **genuinely nested double limit** (DLS Trotter): the finite `(m,r)` Cauchy‚ÄìSchwarz `twoField_product_pairing_cauchySchwarz` (PR #4979, iii-b, crossing is a literal finite cone `S_r = ‚àë_{k<r}(k!)‚Åª¬πP·µè`) is lifted by the **inner limit `r ‚Üí ‚àû`** (exp-of-cone via `twoField_pairing_cauchySchwarz_exp`, cone partial sums converge to `exp P`) to the per-`m` approximant `T_m = (g_m ¬∑ Œ∏(g_m) ¬∑ exp((Œ≤/m)D_a))^m` satisfying the same inequality, then by the **outer limit `m ‚Üí ‚àû`** (Trotter via `ringTwoFieldWeight_isLimit`, PR #4976) from `T_m ‚Üí W(a,b)` to the capstone. Both limits reuse the abstract limit-preserving Cauchy‚ÄìSchwarz `cauchySchwarz_of_tendsto` (via `le_of_tendsto_of_tendsto'` on pointwise `‚â§` under `Tendsto.pow`/`mul`); the field-free crossing interaction (via `ringFieldDLSDecomposition_interaction_eq`) aligns the `(b,b)` slot where `isLimit(b,b)` carries `D_b` while the per-`m` fact carries `D_a`; the hypothesis `0 ‚â§ Œ≤` ensures cone positivity (`(Œ≤/m)D ‚â• 0`) of the crossing factor. Helpers: `cauchySchwarz_of_tendsto` (limit-preserving CS, reused for both limits), `ringFieldDLSDecomposition_interaction_eq` (field-independence of crossing), `twoField_pairing_cauchySchwarz_exp` (per-`m` CS with exponential crossing). This theorem **completes the reflection Cauchy‚ÄìSchwarz layer toward Theorem 4.2** (the DLS / Shastry Gaussian-domination bound for long-range order). | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTwoFieldCauchySchwarz.lean` |
| `ringFieldPartitionRe` / `ringFieldPartitionRe_physFieldOf` / `ringTwoFieldWeight_self_trace_re_nonneg` / `ringFieldPartitionRe_reflection_step` | **RP capstone layer 7c** (`RingReflectionFieldPartition.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1 eqs. (4.1.47)‚Äì(4.1.51), pp. 85‚Äì86, physical field partition identification / PR #4983): identify the physical per-site field partition function `Z_Œ≤(h) := Re Tr exp(‚àíŒ≤¬∑ƒ§_field(h))` with the traced doubled Gibbs weight, yielding the **one-reflection-step finite-Œ≤ bound** `Z_Œ≤(h)¬≤ ‚â§ Z_Œ≤(h_L)¬∑Z_Œ≤(h_R)` (the finite-Œ≤ form of Tasaki's reflection bound (4.1.51)). The crux: the field-splitting map `physFieldOf n a b : Fin (2n) ‚Üí ‚Ñù`, defined as `if z < n then a_z else ‚àíb(r z)`, realizes `h` as the physical field that reproduces the doubled weight under the right-half Marshall gauge `U`: conjugating `ƒ§_field(physFieldOf a b)` by `U` gives `L_field(a) + Œ∏(L_field(b)) ‚àí D` (crux lemma `AxisTwoPiRotS.rightGauge_conj_ringFieldHamiltonian` via `Gauge.lean:56`), so the two sources of sign ‚Äî the gauge's `‚àí≈ú¬≥` on the right and `physFieldOf`'s `‚àíb(r z)` ‚Äî **cancel** (no new sign lemma needed, purely definitional from the split). Staggered-field sanity check (`physFieldOf_self`): the uniform staggered field `h_x = (‚àí1)À£ h‚ÇÄ` decomposes as `b_x = a_x`, leaving `W(a,a)` on the diagonal (gauge-invariant, hence trace-equal identification). Nonnegativity (`ringTwoFieldWeight_self_trace_re_nonneg`): the diagonal weight `W(a,a)` is an RP trace weight (via `ringDLSFieldWeightSym_rpTraceWeight`), so by the RP cone property applied to `A = 1` (`SupportedOnLeftS.one`), `0 ‚â§ Re Tr W(a,a)`. Identification: `Z_Œ≤(physFieldOf a b) = Re Tr W(a,b)` (gauge invariance of trace + matrix exponential conjugation). One reflection step: setting `b_x := ‚àíh(r x)` and applying the identification three times ‚Äî at `(h,b)`, `(h,h)`, `(b,b)` ‚Äî combined with `ringTwoFieldWeight_reflection_cauchySchwarz` (PR #4982), yields `Z_Œ≤(h)¬≤ ‚â§ Z_Œ≤(physFieldOf n h h)¬∑Z_Œ≤(physFieldOf n b b)`, where `physFieldOf n h h` and `physFieldOf n b b` are exactly the reflected copies `h_L` and `h_R` from (4.1.50). This is the **finite-Œ≤ partition-function version of (4.1.51)**; the `Œ≤ ‚Üí ‚àû` limit gives the ground-state reflection bound (PR7d/e, out of scope here). | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionFieldPartition.lean` |
| `ringFieldPartitionRe_translate` / `ringFieldPartitionRe_neg` / `ringFieldPartitionRe_pos` | **RP capstone layer 7d-i** (`RingReflectionFieldPartitionSymmetry.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1 eqs. (4.1.49)‚Äì(4.1.52), pp. 85‚Äì86, field partition symmetries / PR #4984): the **three structural symmetries of the physical field partition function** `Z_Œ≤(h) = ringFieldPartitionRe n N Œ≤ h` consumed by the `‚àílog Z` symmetrisation step (PR7d-ii/iii). **(A) Translation invariance** `ringFieldPartitionRe_translate`: `Z_Œ≤(h) = Z_Œ≤(h ‚àò finRotate)` ‚Äî the ring Hamiltonian is translation invariant (`chainTranslation_conj_heisenbergHamiltonianS`), so conjugation by the unitary ring translation carries `ƒ§_field(h‚àòfinRotate)` to `ƒ§_field(h)` and the trace is invariant. **(B) Spin-flip invariance** `ringFieldPartitionRe_neg`: `Z_Œ≤(‚àíh) = Z_Œ≤(h)` ‚Äî a global axis-2 œÄ-rotation `fullGauge` (the `AxisTwoPiRotS.U` on every site) preserves the Heisenberg dot product (`fullGauge_conj_spinSDot`) while flipping the field `Œ£_z h_z ≈ú¬≥_z ‚Ü¶ Œ£_z (‚àíh_z) ≈ú¬≥_z`, so `Z_Œ≤(‚àíh) = Z_Œ≤(h)` by trace invariance. **(C) Strict positivity** `ringFieldPartitionRe_pos`: `0 < Z_Œ≤(h)` ‚Äî the field Hamiltonian is Hermitian (`ringFieldHamiltonian_isHermitian`), so `exp(‚àíŒ≤¬∑ƒ§_field(h))` is positive definite (`Matrix.posDef_exp_of_isHermitian`), hence its trace is strictly positive; this is the genuine domain condition of the `‚àílog Z` step. The period-2 staggered relabel `P h z = (‚àí1)^z h z` (PR7d-ii) has period 2, so a single one-step shift produces a global sign: both **A and B are required for cyclicity** of the averaged potential in the classical chessboard lemma (Lemma 4.5, Tasaki pp. 87‚Äì88). | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionFieldPartitionSymmetry.lean` |
| `Matrix.posDef_exp_of_isHermitian` | **Math helper** (generic finite-dim linear algebra, `Math/PosSemidef/ExpPosDef.lean`): the exponential of a Hermitian complex matrix is positive definite. For Hermitian `A`, `exp A` is positive definite: writing `M = exp(A/2)` (Hermitian + invertible), the split `exp A = M·¥¥ ¬∑ M` (via the commuting-exponent addition law `exp(A/2 + A/2) = M¬∑M`) is positive definite by `Matrix.PosDef.conjTranspose_mul_self`. This avoids the continuous-functional-calculus route (which causes deterministic typeclass-resolution timeouts). **Load-bearing strict positivity** behind `Z_Œ≤(h) = Tr exp(‚àíŒ≤H) > 0` for Hermitian Hamiltonians (consumed by `ringFieldPartitionRe_pos` in the `‚àílog Z` step) | `Math/PosSemidef/ExpPosDef.lean` |
| `ringReflectionThetaS_ringLeftHamiltonian` | **RP infra layer 19** (`RingReflectionRightBondSum.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the reflected left bond Hamiltonian is the right bond Hamiltonian. Applying `Œ∏` to the left bond sum reflects each left bond `(x,x+1)` to a right bond `(r x, r(x+1)) = (2n‚àí1‚àíx, 2n‚àí2‚àíx)`: `Œ∏(ringLeftHamiltonian) = ‚àë_x [if x+1 < n then ≈ú_{r x}¬∑≈ú_{r(x+1)} else 0]` (via `ringReflectionThetaS_sum` + `ringReflectionThetaS_spinSDot`). This is the right part `Œ∏(H_L)` of the ring DLS decomposition | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionRightBondSum.lean` |
| `ringLeftHamiltonian_eq_leftBondSum` | **RP infra layer 18** (`RingReflectionLeftBondSum.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the left-half bond Hamiltonian as a sum over left bonds. Collapsing the left coupling's double sum, `ringLeftHamiltonian n N = ‚àë_x [if x+1 < n then ≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_{x+1} else 0]` ‚Äî only the bonds `(x, x+1)` entirely inside `{0,‚Ä¶,n‚àí1}` survive (helpers `ringLeftCoupling_succ_of_lt`, `ringLeftCoupling_eq_zero_of_ne`, `ringLeftCoupling_eq_zero_of_not_lt`). The left part of the ring DLS decomposition over its actual bonds | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionLeftBondSum.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_eq_bondSum` | **RP infra layer 17** (`RingReflectionBondSum.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the ring Heisenberg Hamiltonian as a sum over nearest-neighbor bonds. The directed coupling `ringCoupling (2n)` is `1` exactly on successor pairs `(x, x+1 mod 2n)`, so the double sum collapses (`Finset.sum_eq_single`) to `heisenbergHamiltonianS (ringCoupling (2n)) N = ‚àë_x ≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_{ringBondSucc x}` (`ringBondSucc x = x+1 mod 2n`). This bond form is the starting point for the left/right/crossing bond split | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionBondSum.lean` |
| `ringLeftHamiltonian_supportedOnLeft` | **RP infra layer 16** (`RingReflectionLeftHamiltonian.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the left part `H_L` of the ring DLS decomposition is left-supported. `SupportedOnLeftS.sum` (left-supportedness closed under finite sums); `heisenbergHamiltonianS_supportedOnLeft` (a Heisenberg Hamiltonian whose coupling `J x y` vanishes unless both sites are in the left half is left-supported, being `‚àë_{x,y} J x y ¬∑ ≈ú_x¬∑≈ú_y` with each surviving term left-supported); `ringLeftCoupling` (the ring nearest-neighbor coupling restricted to left-half sites); and `ringLeftHamiltonian = heisenbergHamiltonianS (ringLeftCoupling n) N`, the concrete `H_L` that feeds `ringCrossingRPDecomposition`. The remaining bond-split match to the gauged ring Hamiltonian and the unitary ungauge are the next step | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionLeftHamiltonian.lean` |
| `ring_gibbs_rpTraceWeight` (ring instance) | **RP infra layer 15** (`RingReflectionRingInstance.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the DLS decomposition of the gauged ring antiferromagnet. The two crossing bonds of `Fin (2n)` are `(n‚àí1,n)` and `(2n‚àí1,0)`, reflected from left sites `n‚àí1` and `0`; `ringCrossingRPDecomposition` packages (for an arbitrary left-supported `H_L`) the `RPDecomposition` whose crossing bonds are the single-site spin operators at sites `0` and `n‚àí1`, with `ringCrossingRPDecomposition_interaction` identifying its interaction as `crossBondInteractionS 0 + crossBondInteractionS (n‚àí1)` and `ringCrossingRPDecomposition_toHamiltonian` giving `toHamiltonian = H_L + Œ∏(H_L) ‚àí (crossing)`. Then `ring_gibbs_rpTraceWeight` concludes (via the Gibbs capstone) that the gauged ring Gibbs weight `exp(‚àíŒ≤¬∑(H_L+Œ∏(H_L)‚àícrossing))` is a reflection-positive trace weight (`Œ≤ ‚â• 0`). Also `spinSDot_supportedOnLeft` (a left-half bond is left-supported). The concrete left-bond `H_L` and the unitary equivalence to `heisenbergHamiltonianS (ringCoupling (2n))` are the next step | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionRingInstance.lean` |
| `RPDecomposition.gibbs_rpTraceWeight` (**Gibbs RP capstone**) | **RP infra layer 14** (`RingReflectionGibbsCapstone.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): **full Gibbs reflection positivity** ‚Äî for a reflection-positive decomposition `H = H_L + Œ∏(H_L) ‚àí D` (`RPDecomposition`) and `Œ≤ ‚â• 0`, the Gibbs weight `exp(‚àíŒ≤¬∑H)` is a reflection-positive trace weight. The Dyson‚ÄìLieb‚ÄìSimon Trotter assembly: `e^{‚àíŒ≤H} = e^{A+B}` (`A = ‚àíŒ≤¬∑(H_L+Œ∏H_L)`, `B = Œ≤¬∑D`), via the Lie product formula `e^{A+B} = lim_m (e^{A/m}¬∑e^{B/m})^m` (`LieProduct.lieProductFormula`); each factor is consumed by an accumulating RP trace weight ‚Äî the kinetic factor `e^{A/m} = exp(Y+Œ∏Y)` is cone-representable (`coneRep_exp_add` + `mul_coneRep_right`) and the interaction factor `e^{B/m}` is the exp of a cone-representable operator (`mul_exp_coneRep_right`) ‚Äî then induction over the power and `RPTraceWeightS.tendsto`. Helpers: `realSmul_add_theta`, `RPDecomposition.interaction_coneRep`. This is the reflection positivity at the heart of the DLS / Shastry argument | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsCapstone.lean` |
| `RPTraceWeightS.mul_exp_coneRep_right` | **RP infra layer 13** (`RingReflectionMulExpConeRep.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): an RP trace weight absorbs the exponential of a cone-representable operator. If `M` is RP and `P` is `RPTraceConeRepS`, then `M ¬∑ exp P` is RP ‚Äî the partial sums `‚àë_{k<m}(k!)‚Åª¬πP·µè` are cone-representable (`pow`/`smul_nonneg`/`add`), so each `M¬∑(partial sum)` is RP (`mul_coneRep_right`), and `M¬∑exp P` is their limit (continuity of left multiplication + `RPTraceWeightS.tendsto`). This consumes the interaction Gibbs factor `e^{Œ≤D/m}` in the Trotter expansion | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionMulExpConeRep.lean` |
| `coneRep_exp_add` (kinetic factor) | **RP infra layer 12** (`RingReflectionKineticConeRep.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the kinetic Gibbs factor is cone-representable. For left-supported `X`, `exp(X + Œ∏ X) = exp X ¬∑ Œ∏(exp X) = Œ∏(exp X) ¬∑ exp X` (`ringReflectionThetaS_exp_add_eq`, via `exp_add_of_commute` since `X` and `Œ∏(X)` commute, `Œ∏(exp X) = exp(Œ∏ X)`, and `mul_theta_comm`); the right-hand side is a single cone generator `Œ∏(L)¬∑L` (`L = exp X` left-supported), so `coneRep_exp_add` gives `RPTraceConeRepS (exp(X + Œ∏ X))`. This is the kinetic building block (`e^{‚àíŒ≤H‚ÇÄ/m}`) the Trotter expansion of `e^{‚àíŒ≤H}` consumes | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionKineticConeRep.lean` |
| `RPTraceWeightS.mul_coneRep_right` (cone closure) | **RP infra layer 11** (`RingReflectionRPWeightCone.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the reflection-positive trace weights form a convex cone ‚Äî `RPTraceWeightS.zero`/`one` (the identity is RP, the `Œ≤=0` trace cone), `add`, `smul_nonneg`, `sum` (closed under finite sums) ‚Äî and `RPTraceWeightS.mul_coneRep_right`: an RP trace weight times a cone-representable operator (`RPTraceConeRepS`) is again RP (since `M¬∑‚àëc·µ¢Œ∏(C·µ¢)C·µ¢ = ‚àëc·µ¢(M¬∑Œ∏(C·µ¢)C·µ¢)`, each RP by the cone-generator closure, summed). This is the algebraic engine that accumulates reflection positivity factor-by-factor in the Trotter expansion of `e^{‚àíŒ≤H}` | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionRPWeightCone.lean` |
| `RPTraceWeightS.mul_weightGen_right` / `RPTraceWeightS.weightGen_mul_left` | **RP infra layer 10** (`RingReflectionGibbsRP.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): closure of reflection-positive trace weights under cone generators `Œ∏(L)¬∑L` (`L` left-supported). The naive "conjugation" closure `M ‚Ü¶ L¬∑M¬∑Œ∏(L)` is **false** (the homomorphism order `Œ∏(LA)=Œ∏(L)Œ∏(A)` clashes with the test operator `B=AL`, and left-supported operators do not commute); the correct closure multiplies by a cone generator: `M¬∑(Œ∏(L)¬∑L)` is RP (substitute the test operator `A ‚Ü¶ L¬∑A`, using `L¬∑Œ∏(A)=Œ∏(A)¬∑L` and `Œ∏(L)¬∑Œ∏(A)=Œ∏(L¬∑A)`) and `(Œ∏(L)¬∑L)¬∑M` is RP (substitute `A ‚Ü¶ A¬∑L`, via trace cyclicity). These are the kinetic-factor building blocks of the full Dyson‚ÄìLieb‚ÄìSimon Gibbs reflection positivity | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsRP.lean` |
| `crossBondInteractionS` / `RPDecomposition` | **RP infra layer 9** (`RingReflectionRPDecomposition.lean`, Tasaki ¬ß4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the DLS decomposition data and the crossing-bond interaction. `crossBondInteractionS x = Œ£_Œ± ≈ú_x^Œ±¬∑Œ∏(≈ú_x^Œ±)` (left site `x`), with the **gauge identity** `crossBondInteractionS x = ≈ú_x^1 ≈ú_{r x}^1 ‚àí ≈ú_x^2 ≈ú_{r x}^2 + ≈ú_x^3 ≈ú_{r x}^3` (the lone `‚àí` on the `≈ú^2` term is the sign flipped by the DLS/Marshall gauge ‚Äî a œÄ-rotation around axis 2 on the right half ‚Äî making all three components contribute the same negative reflection-positive sign). Each `onSiteS x ≈ú^Œ±` (`x<n`) is left-supported; `crossBondInteractionS_exp_rpTraceWeight` shows `exp(t¬∑crossBondInteractionS x)` (`t‚â•0`) is a reflection-positive trace weight (via `mul_theta_comm` + the interaction-exponential cone). The abstract `RPDecomposition` structure (`H_L + Œ∏(H_L) ‚àí Œ£_b C_b¬∑Œ∏(C_b)`, all `C_b` left-supported) packages the DLS form, with `interaction_exp_rpTraceWeight` giving the Gibbs interaction factor's reflection positivity | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionRPDecomposition.lean` |
| `no_long_range_order_1d` | **Corollary 4.3** (¬ß4.1, AXIOM; eq. (4.1.11)): absence of LRO in 1D. For the zero-field 1D AFM Heisenberg ring, the squared staggered order parameter per site vanishes in the thermodynamic limit `lim_{L‚Üë‚àû} ‚ü®Œ¶_GS\|(√î_L^(3)/L)¬≤\|Œ¶_GS‚ü© = 0` (Œµ‚ÄìŒ¥ form). In the book a corollary of Thm 4.2 + Thm 3.2 + MLM; runs through the deferred double-limit form of Thm 3.2, so recorded as a documented axiom (in scope) | `Quantum/SpinS/ShastryNoSSB.lean` |
| `staggeredCasimirOpS` / `shenQiuTian_ferrimagnetic_lro` | **Theorem 4.4** (¬ß4.1, Shen‚ÄìQiu‚ÄìTian, PROVED axiom-free; eqs. (4.1.12)‚Äì(4.1.13)): ferrimagnetic LRO on an asymmetric bipartite lattice. The SU(2)-invariant squared staggered operator `(√î_Œõ)¬≤ = Œ£_{x,y} Œµ_x Œµ_y ≈ú_x¬∑≈ú_y`, and the bound `S¬≤(\|A\|‚àí\|B\|)¬≤ ‚â§ ‚ü®Œ¶_GS\|(√î_Œõ)¬≤\|Œ¶_GS‚ü©` for any normalized ground state of the connected bipartite AFM model (same hypotheses as Thm 2.3). **PROVED axiom-free** (Issues #4604/#4617 CLOSED; book proof = chain (4.1.16): (4.1.15) cross-term + Lieb‚ÄìMattis Thm 2.3 total spin + Casimir). PR1 (#4605) formalizes the **operator-algebra core** in `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROComponentAlgebra.lean`: `staggeredTransverseCasimirOpS` (transverse part of `(√î_Œõ)¬≤`), `(√î_Œõ)¬≤ = transverse + (√î_Œõ^(3))¬≤`, the PSD drop `‚ü®transverse‚ü© ‚â§ ‚ü®(√î_Œõ)¬≤‚ü©`, `(≈ú_tot^(Œ±))¬≤ = Œ£_{x,y} ≈ú_x^(Œ±)≈ú_y^(Œ±)`, the Casimir identity `Œ£_{x,y}(≈ú_x^(1)≈ú_y^(1)+≈ú_x^(2)≈ú_y^(2)) = (≈ú_tot)¬≤ ‚àí (≈ú_tot^(3))¬≤`, and the M=0 reduction `‚ü®(≈ú_tot)¬≤‚ü© = ‚ü®transverse‚ü©`. PR2 (#4606) adds the **cross-term inequality (4.1.15)** in `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROCrossTermCore.lean` (transverse correlation + ladder positivity + per-pair sign) + `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROCrossTerm.lean` (the summed cross-term inequality, split for build speed): the transverse correlation `‚ü®Œ¶,T_xy Œ¶‚ü©` (`T_xy = ¬Ω(≈ú_x^+≈ú_y^- + ≈ú_x^-≈ú_y^+)`), its non-positivity for cross-sublattice pairs on a Marshall-positive sector ground state (`marshallSignS¬∑c`, `c>0`), and the summed dominance `‚ü®Œ¶,(Œ£_{x,y}T_xy)Œ¶‚ü©.re ‚â§ ‚ü®Œ¶,staggeredTransverseCasimirOpS Œ¶‚ü©.re`. PR3 (#4607) adds the **total-spin value** in `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROTotalSpin.lean` (coupling-agnostic via a `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` hypothesis): `exists_centered_groundState_predictedCasimir_of_tasaki23` produces a centered (`≈ú¬≥_tot Œ¶‚Å∞=0`) Marshall ground state with `(≈ú_tot)¬≤ Œ¶‚Å∞ = S_tot(S_tot+1)¬∑Œ¶‚Å∞` (`S_tot=(\|A\|‚àí\|B\|)N/2`), reusing `tasaki23_pf_groundState_casimir_eq_predicted_sector` + the extremal-Casimir machinery. PR4 (#4608) proves the **capstone** `ferrimagnetic_lro_completeBipartite_centered` in `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROCapstone.lean`: assembling chain (4.1.16) on the centered ground state, `(N/2)¬≤(\|A\|‚àí\|B\|)¬≤¬∑‚ü®Œ¶‚Å∞,Œ¶‚Å∞‚ü©.re ‚â§ ‚ü®Œ¶‚Å∞,(√î_Œõ)¬≤Œ¶‚Å∞‚ü©.re` (existence form, coupling-agnostic via `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3`; sorry-free, axiom-clean, independent of the `shenQiuTian_ferrimagnetic_lro` axiom; that axiom is now itself proved in UniversalFinal). For build speed the proof's private Rayleigh-ratio helpers and the per-sector oriented bound `staggeredCasimir_weightComponent_bound_oriented` (now module-public: `S_tot¬≤¬∑‚ÄñŒ¶_M‚Äñ¬≤ ‚â§ ‚ü®Œ¶_M,(√î_Œõ)¬≤Œ¶_M‚ü©.re` for each magnetization weight component `Œ¶_M` of a ground state) live in the companion `‚Ä¶UniversalFinalCore.lean`, with the sum assembly + the public `shenQiuTian_ferrimagnetic_lro` kept in `‚Ä¶UniversalFinal.lean`. | `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROUniversalFinal.lean` + `‚Ä¶UniversalFinalCore.lean` + `FerrimagneticLRO.lean` + `‚Ä¶ComponentAlgebra.lean` + `‚Ä¶CrossTerm.lean` + `‚Ä¶TotalSpin.lean` + `‚Ä¶TotalSpinCore.lean` + `‚Ä¶Capstone.lean` + `‚Ä¶Universal.lean` |
| `raiseLowerReachableS_of_connected` | **Connected-graph spin-config reachability** (¬ß2.5, PROVED; Issue #4609, prereq for the connected Marshall‚ÄìLieb‚ÄìMattis extension / Thm 4.4 axiom): on a connected graph `G`, any two equal-magnetization configs are raise/lower reachable ‚Äî `G.Connected ‚Üí magSumS œÉ = magSumS œÉ' ‚Üí RaiseLowerReachableS G œÉ œÉ'`. Discharged axiom-free by strong induction on the `configDistS` mismatch (surplus/deficit sites + connected-graph walk) with overflow-safe single-quantum path transport (`transportOne`; at each edge push the quantum forward when the next vertex has room, else recurse first to make room). Generalizes the complete-bipartite `raiseLowerReachableS_bipartiteCompleteGraph` to arbitrary connected graphs ‚Äî the combinatorial core of extending Theorem 2.3 to connected couplings (PR #4610). **PR2 (#4611)** lifts this to the dressed-matrix per-sector Perron‚ÄìFrobenius positivity for a general connected bipartite `G` in `Quantum/SpinS/ConnectedDressedPF.lean`: `exists_matrixPow_pos_of_magConfigS_connected` (`‚àÉ k, 0 < (shiftedDressedSReMatrixOnMagSector A J N c M)^k œÉ' œÉ`) from `G.Connected` + `hGbip` + edge-positivity `hJ_pos_G : G.Adj x y ‚Üí 0 < (J x y).re` (not complete-bipartite positivity), via the graph-agnostic edge-local witness `neg_dressedHeisenbergSReMatrix_apply_pos_of_raiseLowerStepS_witness` + the connected reachability ‚Äî generalizing `exists_matrixPow_pos_of_magConfigS_bipartite`. **PR3 (#4612)** assembles **Theorem 2.3 itself for connected couplings**: `isIrreducible_shiftedDressedSReMatrixOnMagSector_connected` (ConnectedSectorIrreducible.lean) + 20 `_of_irreducible` chain variants parameterizing the PF-consuming Marshall‚ÄìLieb‚ÄìMattis chain by the irreducibility result (graph-agnostic) + the capstone `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_data_of_connected` (ConnectedTheorem23Core.lean, ConnectedTheorem23.lean): the per-magnetization-sector Marshall-positive ground states + global minimality of Theorem 2.3 hold for ANY connected bipartite `J` (`G.Connected` + `hGbip` + edge-positivity `hJ_pos_G`), not just complete-bipartite ‚Äî the `hOutside`/lower-bound side reuses `tasaki23_general_hOutside`/`tasaki23_eigenvalue_ge_common` (which need only `hJ_nn`).
