This is the connected-coupling extension of Marshall‚ÄìLieb‚ÄìMattis (Lieb‚ÄìMattis 1962). **PR4 (#4613)** assembles the **connected-coupling ferrimagnetic LRO bound (existence form)** `ferrimagnetic_lro_connected_centered` (ConnectedFerrimagneticLRO.lean): for any connected bipartite AFM coupling (`G.Connected` + `hGbip` + `hJ_pos_G`), there is a centered ground state `Œ¶‚Å∞` with `(N/2)¬≤(\|A\|‚àí\|B\|)¬≤¬∑‚ü®Œ¶‚Å∞,Œ¶‚Å∞‚ü©.re ‚â§ ‚ü®Œ¶‚Å∞,(√î_Œõ)¬≤Œ¶‚Å∞‚ü©.re` ‚Äî Tasaki's chain (4.1.16) for the genuine connected (non-complete) coupling, built from the connected Theorem 2.3 data + connected irreducibility Casimir value + the PR1/PR2 operator-algebra (all graph-agnostic). **PR5 (#4614)** proves the **`SU(2)` invariance of `(√î_Œõ)¬≤`** in `Quantum/SpinS/StaggeredCasimirSU2Invariance.lean`: `staggeredCasimirOpS_commute_totalSpinSOp{1,2,3}` + `‚Ä¶OpPlus`/`‚Ä¶OpMinus` (the squared staggered order operator commutes with every total-spin operator), immediate from the per-pair `spinSDot_commutator_totalSpinSOp*` vanishing ‚Äî ingredient (a) of the universal-form transfer. **PR6 (#4615)** proves the **core of ingredient (b)** in `Quantum/SpinS/SU2ExpectationLadderInvariant.lean`: `su2_expectationRatioRe_ladder_invariant` ‚Äî for an SU(2)-invariant operator `O` (commuting with `≈ú^¬±_tot`) and a joint `≈ú¬≥_tot`/Casimir eigenvector `v`, the real expectation ratio `‚ü®w,Ow‚ü©.re/‚ü®w,w‚ü©.re` is unchanged under lowering `w ‚Ü¶ ≈ú‚Åª_tot v` (both numerator and denominator scale by the same `≈ú‚Å∫≈ú‚Åª = Casimir ‚àí (≈ú¬≥)¬≤ + ≈ú¬≥` eigenvalue). This is the ladder step that makes `(√î_Œõ)¬≤`'s expectation constant across the spin-`S_tot` multiplet; **PR7 (#4616)** iterates it to `(≈ú‚Åª_tot)^k` (`su2_expectationRatioRe_ladder_iterate_invariant`, `Quantum/SpinS/SU2ExpectationLadderIterated.lean`), so every member of the lowering tower shares the centered ratio. The **stated** `shenQiuTian_ferrimagnetic_lro` axiom (universal over *all* ground states) now remains pending only the final assembly of ingredient (b): the ground-state weight decomposition + the **ground-eigenspace classification** (any global-min eigenvector's nonzero `≈ú¬≥`-weight components lie in the admissible sectors ‚Äî needs strict energy separation, the hard remaining gate) (#4604). **PR8 (#4618)** adds step 1 of that dimension bound: `heisenbergHamiltonianSMatrixOnMagSector_finrank_le_one_of_marshall_positive_connected` (`Quantum/SpinS/ConnectedSectorFinrankLeOne.lean`) ‚Äî connected-coupling per-sector ground-state uniqueness (`finrank ‚â§ 1`), the connected-irreducibility mirror of the complete-graph lemma (Issue #4617). **PR9 (#4619)** adds step 3: `weightPreserving_expectation_eq_sum_sector` (`Quantum/SpinS/WeightPreservingExpectationSum.lean`) ‚Äî for any operator commuting with `≈ú¬≥_tot`, `‚ü®Œ¶,OŒ¶‚ü© = Œ£_M ‚ü®Œ¶_M,OŒ¶_M‚ü©` over the `≈ú¬≥`-weight sector components (cross terms vanish by `≈ú¬≥_tot`-eigenvalue orthogonality; no Hermiticity of `O` needed), the block-diagonal decomposition for `(√î_Œõ)¬≤`'s expectation. **PR10 (#4620)** clears the hard gate (step 2): `tasaki23_strict_hOutside_of_connected` (`Quantum/SpinS/StrictHOutsideFerrimagnetic.lean`; for build speed the per-sector ferrimagnetic foundation `tasaki23_strict_hOutside_ferrimagnetic` and its Casimir-obstruction helpers live in the companion `‚Ä¶StrictHOutsideFerrimagneticCore.lean`) ‚Äî **strict** energy separation, `Œº < ŒºM` for any non-admissible sector `M ‚àâ [min¬∑N, max¬∑N]` (the connected ferrimagnetic generalization of the balanced `tasaki23_strict_hOutside_of_card_eq_zero_casimir_ladder_obstruction`, via the Casimir equality-obstruction: ladder a hypothetical non-admissible Œº-eigenvector to the admissible band edge, pull back the predicted Casimir `S_tot(S_tot+1)` through the ladder, contradict the abs Casimir lower bound `|center‚àíM|(|center‚àíM|+1) > S_tot(S_tot+1)`). This is the strict Lieb‚ÄìMattis level ordering for the connected AFM Heisenberg ‚Äî the last gate; only the final weight-decomposition assembly remains to discharge `shenQiuTian_ferrimagnetic_lro` (#4617). **PR11 (#4621)** adds the universal-form assembly infra in `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROUniversal.lean`: `chain_bound_marshall_sector` (Tasaki's chain (4.1.16) at an arbitrary-weight Marshall sector vector: `(Œ≥ ‚àí m¬≤)‚Äñw‚Äñ¬≤ ‚â§ ‚ü®w,(√î_Œõ)¬≤w‚ü©.re`), `star_dotProduct_self_eq_sum_sector` (`‚ÄñŒ¶‚Äñ¬≤ = Œ£_M ‚ÄñŒ¶_M‚Äñ¬≤`), `heisenbergHamiltonianS_magSectorProjection_eigen` (a weight component of an H-eigenvector is an H-eigenvector), the diagonal-shift `c` existence, and the global-flip / weight-commute helpers. The remaining step to remove the axiom is the SU(2) Rayleigh-ratio constancy across the spin-`S_tot` multiplet (transfer the per-sector bound from a near-central admissible sector to all sectors via the merged iterated ladder-invariance) (#4617) | `Quantum/SpinS/ConnectedRaiseLower.lean` + `‚Ä¶ConnectedDressedPF.lean` + `‚Ä¶ConnectedSectorIrreducible.lean` + `‚Ä¶ConnectedTheorem23Core.lean` + `‚Ä¶ConnectedTheorem23.lean` + `‚Ä¶ConnectedFerrimagneticLRO.lean` + `‚Ä¶StaggeredCasimirSU2Invariance.lean` + `‚Ä¶SU2ExpectationLadderInvariant.lean` + `‚Ä¶SU2ExpectationLadderIterated.lean` + `‚Ä¶ConnectedSectorFinrankLeOne.lean` + `‚Ä¶WeightPreservingExpectationSum.lean` + `‚Ä¶StrictHOutsideFerrimagnetic.lean` + `‚Ä¶StrictHOutsideFerrimagneticCore.lean` + `‚Ä¶FerrimagneticLROUniversal.lean` |
| `reflectionPositivity_averaging` | **Lemma 4.5** (¬ß4.1.2, PROVED; eqs. (4.1.55)‚Äì(4.1.57)): the abstract reflection-positivity averaging inequality used in the proof of Thm 4.1. For `F : (Fin 2n ‚Üí V) ‚Üí ‚Ñù` (arbitrary `V`) invariant under the cyclic shift and satisfying the reflection bound `F f ‚â• ¬Ω(F(reflectLeft f)+F(reflectRight f))`, one has `(1/2n)¬∑Œ£_j F(j‚Ü¶f j) ‚â§ F f`. Discharged axiom-free via Tasaki's `G_min=0` collapse argument: minimise `G f = F f ‚àí (2n)‚Åª¬πŒ£_j F(const f_j)` over the finite carrier `{f‚àòœÉ}`; at a minimiser the reflection bound is an equality, and a leading-run doubling collapses the minimiser to a constant string where `G = 0` (PR #4598) | `Math/ReflectionPositivityAveraging.lean` |
| `HypercubicTorus` / `torusNNCoupling` / `staggeredRaisingOpS` / `tower_lowLying_energy_bound` | **Theorem 4.6** (¬ß4.2.2, Anderson's tower, **now PROVED ‚Äî axiom discharged**, PR #4769/#4773; eqs. (4.2.2)‚Äì(4.2.4)): low-lying tower states. Builds the d-dim hypercubic torus model `Fin d ‚Üí ZMod L` (`L^d` sites, NN coupling, parity sublattice) + staggered raising/lowering order operators `√î_L^¬± = Œ£_x Œµ_x ≈ú_x^¬±`. The two-sided tower states `towerState M = (√î_L^{sgn M})^{\|M\|} Œ¶_GS` (`M : ‚Ñ§`, raising for M‚â•0 / lowering for M<0) satisfy `‚ü®œà_M,ƒ§œà_M‚ü©/‚ü®œà_M,œà_M‚ü© ‚â§ E_GS + C‚ÇÇM¬≤/L^d` for `\|M\| ‚â§ C‚ÇÅL^{d/2}` (Rayleigh-ratio form, scale-invariant). **Conditional on LRO** (`q‚ÇÄ>0` premise: `‚ü®(√î_L/L^d)¬≤‚ü© = ‚ü®√î¬≤‚ü©/(‚ü®Œ¶,Œ¶‚ü©¬∑(L^d)¬≤) ‚â• q‚ÇÄ`, eq. 4.1.7), so vacuous in d=1 per Cor 4.3. **Proof** (axiom-clean, `#print axioms` = propext/Classical.choice/Quot.sound): the variational double-commutator bound (‚òÖ, `tower_numerator_double_commutator_le`) reduces the gap to `‚ü®Œ¶,[(√¥‚Åª)^M,[ƒ§,(√¥‚Å∫)^M]]Œ¶‚ü©`; this numerator is bounded `‚â§ M¬≤(A/V¬∑P_M + B M¬≤/V¬≤¬∑P_M)` via Lemma R2 (split-independent renormalized commutator estimate `r2_split_independent`) + locality decay of `dÃÇ=[√¥‚Å∫,[ƒ§,√¥‚Åª]]` and `[ƒ§,√¥‚Å∫]` + moment-factor‚Üí`P_M` conversions; the denominator `‚Äñ(√¥¬±)^M Œ¶‚Äñ¬≤ ‚â• ¬ΩP_M` (Lemma R1, balanced-word `orderWord_balanced_re_close`); the LRO recursion `2q‚ÇÄP_n ‚â§ P_{n+1}` (`phatMoment_succ_two_q0_le`) needs the **total-spin-singlet** premise `≈ú¬≥Œ¶=≈ú¬πŒ¶=0` (added to `IsAndersonTowerConstants`; faithful ‚Äî the bipartite AFM GS is the unique singlet by MLM Thm 2.3). `M<0` via Hermitian conjugation; constants `C‚ÇÅ=min(‚àö(q‚ÇÄ/6N),‚àö(2^d)‚àö(2q‚ÇÄ)/16N)`, `C‚ÇÇ=288dN‚Å¥/q‚ÇÄ+576C‚ÇÅ¬≤dN¬≥(1+1/‚àö(2q‚ÇÄ))/q‚ÇÄ`; `N=0` vacuous (order op = 0) | `Quantum/SpinS/AndersonTower.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/AndersonTowerTheorem46.lean` |
| `tower_lowLying_eigenstates` | **Corollary 4.7** (¬ß4.2.2, **now PROVED ‚Äî axiom discharged**, PR #4775; eq. (4.2.7)): the tower of low-lying energy **eigenstates**. For a total-spin-singlet GS and each `M ‚âÝ 0` with `\|M\| ‚â§ C‚ÇÅL^{d/2}` (nonzero tower state), ‚àÉ an ƒ§-eigenstate `Œ®_M` in the `≈ú_tot^(3)` sector `M` with `E_GS < E_M ‚â§ E_GS + C‚ÇÇM¬≤/L^d`. **Proof** (axiom-clean): `Œ®_M` = the minimum-energy eigenstate of `ƒ§` restricted to the magnetization sector of `towerState M Œ¶` (`heisenbergHamiltonianS_magSector_min_eigenvector`, lifting the restricted-Hermitian min eigenvector via `magSectorEmbedding`); its energy `‚â§ Rayleigh(towerState)` (`tower_sectorMin_mul_le`, variational + norm/energy embedding bridges) `‚â§ E_GS + C‚ÇÇM¬≤/L^d` (Theorem 4.6); strict gap from the **ground-sector-exclusion** premise (every ground eigenstate is a singlet) since `Œ®_M` sits in sector `M ‚âÝ 0`. Distinct `M` ‚Üí distinct sectors ‚Üí `O(L^{d/2})` distinct low-lying eigenstates (Anderson tower). Conditional on LRO (vacuous in d=1) | `Quantum/SpinS/AndersonTowerEigenstates.lean` |

(4.2.10)‚Äì(4.2.11)): the Tanaka full-symmetry-breaking state `Œû_{(1,0,0)} = (1/‚àö2)((√î_L^(1))^M Œ¶/‚Äñ¬∑‚Äñ + (√î_L^(1))^{M+1} Œ¶/‚Äñ¬∑‚Äñ)` (each term separately normalized via `unitNormalize`/`vecNormSqRe`; Œ±=1 staggered op `staggeredOrderOp1S`) is low-lying: `‚ü®Œû,ƒ§Œû‚ü©/‚ü®Œû,Œû‚ü© ‚â§ E_GS + C‚ÇÇ{M+1}¬≤/L^d` for `M>0`, `M+1 ‚â§ C‚ÇÅL^{d/2}` (`‚àÉ C‚ÇÅ C‚ÇÇ, IsAndersonTowerConstants ‚àß IsTanakaSSBConstants`, one pair via `C‚ÇÅ=min`/`C‚ÇÇ=max` merge of the Thm 4.6 constants). **Proof**: scale-invariance drop (eq. 4.2.70) ‚Üí per-tower-term Rayleigh bound from the double-commutator numerator (`tanaka_numerator_bound`, eq. 4.2.71) over the balanced-word denominator (`orderSum_pow_two_denom_lower`, eq. 4.2.67), collapsed by the **central-binomial (Pascal) cancellation** `2¬∑C(2(k-1),k-1) ‚â§ C(2k,k)` and the LRO moment step `2q‚ÇÄP_{k-1}‚â§P_k`; the two orthogonal parity-opposite tower terms (cross-term = 0, eq. 4.2.69) averaged via `tanakaSSBState_expectationRatioRe_le`. Conditional on LRO (vacuous in d=1) | `Quantum/SpinS/AndersonTowerTanakaCapstone.lean` |
| `tanakaSSB_full_symmetry_breaking` / `IsTanakaFullSSBConstants` | **Theorem 4.9** (¬ß4.2.2, Tasaki, **PROVED** #4967 PR5; eqs. (4.2.12)‚Äì(4.2.15)/(4.2.56)/footnote 21): the Tanaka state exhibits **full SU(2) symmetry breaking** in the (1,0,0) direction. The former axiom is now a **proved theorem** for the **explicit** slowly-diverging sequence `M(L) := ‚åäL^{d/4}‚åã` (`Tendsto M atTop atTop`, with `M+1 ‚â§ C‚ÇÅL^{d/2}` and `M¬≤/V ‚Üí 0` both eventually). Proof: expand into the `2^{2(m+1)}` order words, drop unbalanced words via singlet charge-selection, leaving `C(2(m+1),m+1)` balanced words, each pinched by the swap-chain kernel `orderWord_balanced_re_close_step` **(retaining the `(m+1)¬≤ N/V` prefactor‚Äînot collapsing to crude `¬Ω P`)**; sum per-word deviations. | `tasaki_4_22_magnetization_vanishes` / `tasaki_4_22_exponential_clustering` | **Theorem 4.22** (¬ß4.4.1, AXIOMS; eqs. (4.4.1)‚Äì(4.4.6)): the 1D Heisenberg model is **disordered at any nonzero temperature**. New finite-temperature framework: Gibbs operator `thermalGibbsOpS Œ≤ H = e^{‚àíŒ≤H}`, partition `thermalPartitionFnS`, real canonical average `thermalAverageReS Œ≤ H A = Re Tr[A e^{‚àíŒ≤H}]/Re Tr[e^{‚àíŒ≤H}]` (eq. 4.4.3); field Hamiltonians `heisenbergFieldHamiltonianS ferro` covering ferro (4.4.1, uniform field) and AFM (4.4.2, staggered field); torus embedding `torusEmbed`, `‚Ñì¬π` distance `intL1Dist`, even-side sequence `evenSide n=2(n+1)`. **(4.4.5)** `tasaki_4_22_magnetization_vanishes`: `lim_{h‚Üì0} lim_{L‚Üë‚àû} ‚ü®≈ú_x^(3)‚ü©_{Œ≤,h}^L=0` (no SSB; order of limits essential), stated soundly per footnote 41 (Œµ‚ÄìŒ¥ with inner liminf-subsequence `‚àÉn‚ÇÄ ‚àÄn‚â•n‚ÇÄ ‚àÉm‚â•n, |mag|<Œµ`). **(4.4.6)** `tasaki_4_22_exponential_clustering`: ‚àÉ Œæ(Œ≤),C(Œ≤)‚àà(0,‚àû) with `|‚ü®≈ú_x^(Œ±)≈ú_y^(Œ±)‚ü©_{Œ≤,0}^‚àû| ‚â§ C exp(‚àí|x‚àíy|/Œæ)`, bounding the concrete `liminf` correlation `infiniteVolSpinCorrLiminf` | `Quantum/SpinS/HeisenbergEquilibrium.lean` |
| `tasaki_4_23_high_temperature_disorder` | **Theorem 4.23** (¬ß4.4.1, AXIOM; eqs. (4.4.7)‚Äì(4.4.8)): the Heisenberg model in `d ‚â• 2` is **disordered at sufficiently high temperature**. Reuses the Theorem 4.22 finite-temperature framework at general `d` (`hd : 2 ‚â§ d`): there exists `Œ≤‚ÇÄ ‚àà (0,‚àû)` (depending only on `d` and `S`, **not** on the ferro/AFM flag ‚Äî `ferro` is universally quantified under `‚àÉ Œ≤‚ÇÄ`) such that for every `Œ≤ ‚àà [0, Œ≤‚ÇÄ]`, **(4.4.7)** the magnetization vanishes `lim_{h‚Üì0} lim_{L‚Üë‚àû} ‚ü®≈ú_x^(3)‚ü©_{Œ≤,h}^L = 0` (same Œµ‚ÄìŒ¥ / liminf-subsequence form) and **(4.4.8)** ‚àÉ Œæ(Œ≤),C(Œ≤)‚àà(0,‚àû) with `|‚ü®≈ú_x^(Œ±)≈ú_y^(Œ±)‚ü©_{Œ≤,0}^‚àû| ‚â§ C exp(‚àí|x‚àíy|/Œæ)`. | `improved_hohenberg_mermin_wagner` | **Theorem 4.24** (¬ß4.4.3, AXIOM; eqs. (4.4.21)‚Äì(4.4.22)): the **improved Hohenberg‚ÄìMermin‚ÄìWagner theorem** ‚Äî the 2D Heisenberg model exhibits no magnetic ordering of any type at any nonzero temperature. New `generalizedFieldHamiltonianS J Œæ` (eq. 4.4.21): `J Œ£ ≈ú¬∑≈ú ‚àí h Œ£_x Œ£_Œ± (Œæ_x^Œ±) ≈ú_x^(Œ±)` (field `h_x = h Œæ_x`) with `J ‚àà {‚àí1,+1}` and an **arbitrary fixed field-direction family** `Œæ` (`\|Œæ_x\| ‚â§ 1`), generalizing the uniform/staggered fields; `finiteVolMagnetizationGenS` is its finite-volume Gibbs magnetization `‚ü®≈ú_x^(Œ±)‚ü©_{Œ≤,h}^L`. For `d=2`, any `Œ≤ ‚àà [0,‚àû)`, every `J=¬±1`, every bounded `Œæ`, every component `Œ±=1,2,3`, and every `x ‚àà ‚Ñ§¬≤`: `lim_{h‚Üì0} lim_{L‚Üë‚àû} ‚ü®≈ú_x^(Œ±)‚ü©_{Œ≤,h}^L = 0` (4.4.22). Per footnote 48 the inner limit is a **limsup**, so the sound form is **eventual** (`‚àÉn‚ÇÄ ‚àÄn‚â•n‚ÇÄ`, all large even volumes) ‚Äî stronger than the liminf-subsequence form of 4.22/4.23. (4.4.23)‚Äì(4.4.24)): the **McBryan‚ÄìSpencer / Koma‚ÄìTasaki power-law bound** ‚Äî in `d=2` at `h=0` the finite-volume two-point correlation `finiteVolSpinCorrS` decays at least as a power law: there is an `L`-independent positive **decreasing** exponent `Œ∑(Œ≤)` with `\|‚ü®≈ú_x^(Œ±)≈ú_y^(Œ±)‚ü©_{Œ≤,0}^L\| ‚â§ 2S¬≤ \|x‚àíy\|^{‚àíŒ∑(Œ≤)}` (`Real.rpow`) for every `Œ±=1,2,3`, `Œ≤‚àà[0,‚àû)`, and every pair of **distinct** sites with `0 < \|x‚àíy\| < L/2`. The distinctness `0<\|x‚àíy\|` is **required for soundness** (`Real.rpow 0 (‚àíŒ∑)=0` in Lean, so the bound would be false at `x=y` where the self-correlation is positive). `Œ∑(Œ≤) ‚âÉ (16CS¬≤Œ≤)^{‚àí1}` for large `Œ≤`; the same `Œ∑` is bound before `ferro` (works for both signs). Weaker than the conjectured exponential decay but already excludes 2D long-range order | `Quantum/SpinS/HeisenbergEquilibrium.lean` |
| `theorem_4_26_staggered_lro` | **Theorem 4.26** (¬ß4.4.4, AXIOM; eq. (4.4.52)): the **Dyson‚ÄìLieb‚ÄìSimon theorem** ‚Äî genuine N√©el long-range order at sufficiently low temperature in `d ‚â• 3`. New per-axis staggered order operator `staggeredOrderOpAxisS Œ± A N` (`Œ±=0/1/2 ‚Üí √î^(1)/√î^(2)/√î^(3)`). For the AFM Heisenberg model with `d ‚â• 3` (`hd : 3 ‚â§ d`) and any `S = N/2` with `N ‚â• 1` (`[NeZero N]`): there exist `Œ≤‚ÇÄ ‚àà (0,‚àû)` and `q(Œ≤)` with `q(Œ≤) > 0` for `Œ≤ > Œ≤‚ÇÄ` such that `‚ü®(√î_L^(Œ±)/L^d)¬≤‚ü©_{Œ≤,0}^L = ‚ü®(√î_L^(Œ±))¬≤‚ü©_{Œ≤,0}^L / (L^d)¬≤ ‚â• q(Œ≤)` for every `Œ±=1,2,3`, every `Œ≤ > Œ≤‚ÇÄ`, and all sufficiently large even volumes (the squared operator `√î_L^(Œ±)/L^d` gives the `(L^d)¬≤` normalization ‚Äî the intensive LRO density, as in ¬ß4.1/¬ß4.2). `[NeZero N]` is **essential for soundness** (at `N=0` the order operator vanishes, contradicting `q(Œ≤)>0`). Proof: reflection positivity (DLS; `d=3,S=1/2` via Kennedy‚ÄìLieb‚ÄìShastry). The ferromagnetic LRO is only conjectured (RP fails) | `Quantum/SpinS/HeisenbergEquilibrium.lean` |
| `theorem_4_27_griffiths_koma_tasaki_ssb` | **Theorem 4.27** (¬ß4.4.4, AXIOM; eq. (4.4.53)): the **Griffiths / Koma‚ÄìTasaki theorem** ‚Äî under the same conditions as Theorem 4.26 (AFM, `d ‚â• 3`, `N ‚â• 1` `[NeZero N]`), low-temperature LRO is accompanied by genuine SSB. With the staggered field (eq. 4.4.2), `lim_{h‚Üì0} lim_{L‚Üë‚àû} ‚ü®√î_L^(3)‚ü©_{Œ≤,h}^L / L^d ‚â• ‚àö(3 q(Œ≤))` for `Œ≤ > Œ≤‚ÇÄ`. The axiom bundles the Theorem 4.26 LRO bound (4.4.52) and the SSB bound (4.4.53) under one **shared `‚àÉŒ≤‚ÇÄ ‚àÉq`**, making the "same q" dependence explicit; the `‚àö3` has the same origin as Theorem 4.11. Double limit in Œµ‚ÄìŒ¥ form (outer `h‚Üì0`, inner liminf as eventual lower bound). Proof: Koma‚ÄìTasaki (extending Griffiths) | `Quantum/SpinS/HeisenbergEquilibrium.lean` |

### Bose‚ÄìEinstein condensation of hard-core bosons (Tasaki ¬ß5.1‚Äì¬ß5.2)

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `xyHamiltonianS` / `tasaki_5_1_xy_odlro_half_filling` | **Theorem 5.1** (¬ß5.1‚Äì¬ß5.2, AXIOM; eqs. (5.1.1)‚Äì(5.2.5)): **off-diagonal long-range order (ODLRO) of hard-core bosons at half filling**. Hard-core bosons (`u‚Üë‚àû` limit of the bosonic Hubbard model, eq. 5.1.4) are **equivalent to the S=1/2 XY model** (eq. 5.1.5) via the identification `√¢_x‚ÄÝ‚Üî(‚àí1)^x ≈ú_x^+`, `nÃÇ_x‚Üî≈ú_x^(3)+¬Ω`, `NÃÇ‚Üî≈ú_tot^(3)+L^d/2` (eqs. 5.1.6‚Äì5.1.7); half filling `œÅ=¬Ω` ‚Üî the `≈ú_tot^(3)=0` sector. `xyHamiltonianS d L` = the XY Hamiltonian (XXZ at Œª=D=0, N=1). The bosonic order operators `√î_L^(1,2)` (eqs. 5.2.2/5.2.4) are the staggered XY-plane spin order operators. For `d ‚â• 2` (`hd : 2 ‚â§ d`) and half filling, ODLRO holds with `q‚ÇÄ > 0` (depending only on `d`): every ground state `Œ¶_GS` (eigenvector + min-energy, `Œ¶‚âÝ0`, `≈ú_tot^(3) Œ¶=0`) of `xyHamiltonianS` satisfies `‚ü®(√î_L^(Œ±))¬≤‚ü©/‚ü®Œ¶,Œ¶‚ü©/(L^d)¬≤ ‚â• q‚ÇÄ` for Œ±=1,2 (`Œ±:Fin 3, Œ±‚âÝ2`) and all large even `L` (eq. 5.2.5; the squared operator `√î_L^(Œ±)/L^d` gives the `(L^d)¬≤` denominator, the intensive ODLRO density). Half filling is essential; the ground state shows LRO **without** SSB (eq. 5.2.8). Proof: reflection positivity (Kennedy‚ÄìLieb‚ÄìShastry, Kubo‚ÄìKishi) | `Quantum/SpinS/BoseEinsteinCondensate.lean` |
| `xyChemicalPotentialHamiltonianS` / `IsBECTowerConstants` / `tasaki_5_2_bec_tower` | **Theorem 5.2** (¬ß5.3, AXIOM; eqs. (5.3.2)‚Äì(5.3.4)): **low-lying tower states of hard-core bosons**. The chemical-potential boson Hamiltonian `ƒ§_Œº = ƒ§ ‚àí Œº NÃÇ` (spin form `2¬∑xyHamiltonianS ‚àí Œº¬∑totalSpinSOp3`, since `ƒ§ = 2 ƒ§_XY` and `NÃÇ ‚Üî ≈ú_tot^(3)+L^d/2`, eq. 5.3.2; the factor 2 keeps the documented `Œº` equal to Tasaki's chemical potential, half filling `Œº=0`). Given a ground state `Œ¶_GS` of `ƒ§_Œº` exhibiting ODLRO with `q‚ÇÄ > 0` (Theorem 5.1), there are `C‚ÇÅ,C‚ÇÇ > 0` (depending on `d`, the density selected by `Œº`, and `q‚ÇÄ` ‚Äî `Œº` is a parameter of `IsBECTowerConstants`, so the constants are per-density) such that the bosonic tower states `Œì_M = (√î_L^{sgn M})^{\|M\|} Œ¶_GS` (`\|M\| ‚â§ C‚ÇÅL^{d/2}`) are **nonvanishing and** low-lying ‚Äî both stated as conclusions (faithful to 5.2, which asserts `Œì_M` is nonvanishing) ‚Äî with the **cubic** energy increment `towerState ‚âÝ 0 ‚àß ‚ü®Œì_M,ƒ§_Œº Œì_M‚ü©/‚ü®Œì_M,Œì_M‚ü© ‚â§ E‚ÇÄ + C‚ÇÇ\|M\|¬≥/L^d` (eq. 5.3.4; cubic, vs the quadratic `M¬≤` of the Anderson-tower Theorem 4.6). Conditional on ODLRO (`q‚ÇÄ>0`); PÃÇ_hc = identity for spin-¬Ω. Proof: Koma‚ÄìTasaki | `Quantum/SpinS/BoseEinsteinCondensate.lean` |
| `becCoherentState` / `IsBECCoherentSSBConstants` / `tasaki_5_3_bec_u1_ssb` | **Theorem 5.3** (¬ß5.3, AXIOM; eqs. (5.3.5)‚Äì(5.3.8)): **U(1) symmetry-breaking states of hard-core bosons** ‚Äî the BEC counterpart of the Tanaka Theorem 4.9. The phase-`Œ∏` **coherent state** `Œû_Œ∏ = (2M_max+1)^{‚àí1/2} Œ£_{M=‚àíM_max}^{M_max} e^{‚àíiMŒ∏} Œì_M` (eq. 5.3.5; `Œì_M` = normalized tower state), with a slow window `M_max(L)` (`IsSlowBECWindow`: monotone, ‚Üí ‚àû, `‚â§ C‚ÇÅL^{d/2}` eventually). For every `Œ∏` and realizing ground-state family (eventual GS of `ƒ§_Œº` with ODLRO `q‚ÇÄ`, nonvanishing tower states over `\|M\| ‚â§ C‚ÇÅL^{d/2}`), **there exists a sufficiently slowly diverging** `M_max` (existential, matching Tasaki's "if `M_max` diverges not too rapidly" and Theorem 4.9's `‚àÉ M`) for which `Œû_Œ∏` fully breaks U(1): **(5.3.7)** `‚ü®√î_L^(1)‚ü©/L^d ‚Üí m\*cosŒ∏`, `‚ü®√î_L^(2)‚ü©/L^d ‚Üí m\*sinŒ∏`; **(5.3.8)** `‚ü®(√î_L^(Œ±))¬≤‚ü©/(L^d)¬≤ ‚Üí (m\*cosŒ∏)¬≤/(m\*sinŒ∏)¬≤`; **(5.3.6)** the complex moments (`expectationRatioComplex`) `‚ü®√î_L^¬±‚ü©/L^d ‚Üí m\*e^{¬±iŒ∏}`. The order parameter obeys `m\* ‚â• ‚àö(2q‚ÇÄ)` (U(1) `‚àö2`, vs SU(2) `‚àö3` of Theorem 4.11). Limits in the sound eventual-`Œµ` form (footnote 9). Conditional on ODLRO; `Œº` parametrizes density. Proof: Koma‚ÄìTasaki | `Quantum/SpinS/BoseEinsteinCondensate.lean` |
| `CoupledSite` / `coupledHamiltonian` / `coupledCrossCorrelation` / `tasaki_5_4_coupled_bec_ssb` | **Theorem 5.4** (¬ß5.5, AXIOM; eqs. (5.5.1)‚Äì(5.5.6)): **symmetry breaking in coupled BEC**. The coupled two-species lattice `CoupledSite = HypercubicTorus √ó Bool` (`(x,false)=a`, `(x,true)=b`); total Hamiltonian `ƒ§_tot^Œµ = ƒ§_a + ƒ§_b + Œµ ƒ§_tunnel` (`coupledHamiltonian`, eq. 5.5.1): the two uncoupled boson copies `ƒ§_a+ƒ§_b = 2(ƒ§_XY,a+ƒ§_XY,b)` (the boson factor `ƒ§=2ƒ§_XY`, consistent with `xyChemicalPotentialHamiltonianS`, so `Œµ` is the textbook tunneling strength; via `sameSpeciesNNCoupling`) + the tunneling term `tunnelHamiltonian œÜ = ‚àíŒ£_x (e^{iœÜ} ≈ú^+_a ≈ú^‚àí_b + e^{‚àíiœÜ} ≈ú^‚àí_a ≈ú^+_b)` (eq. 5.5.3). Total `2N` particles = doubled half filling `≈ú_tot^(3)=0`. Assuming single-system ODLRO (`hODLRO`: the uncoupled XY ground states satisfy the Theorem 5.1 bound with `q‚ÇÄ`, tying `q‚ÇÄ` to genuine ODLRO), the unique coupled ground state `Œ¶^Œµ` develops a definite relative U(1) phase: ‚àÉ `mÃÉ ‚â• ‚àö(2q‚ÇÄ)` with `lim_{Œµ‚Üì0} lim_{L‚Üë‚àû} ‚ü®Œ¶^Œµ, √¢‚ÄÝ_{(x,a)}√¢_{(x,b)} Œ¶^Œµ‚ü©/‚ü®Œ¶^Œµ,Œ¶^Œµ‚ü© = mÃÉ¬≤e^{‚àíiœÜ}` (eq. 5.5.5; `coupledCrossCorrelation = ≈ú^+_{(x,a)}≈ú^‚àí_{(x,b)}`) **and** the conjugate `‚ü®Œ¶^Œµ, √¢_{(x,a)}√¢‚ÄÝ_{(x,b)} Œ¶^Œµ‚ü©/‚ü®¬∑‚ü© = mÃÉ¬≤e^{+iœÜ}` (eq. 5.5.6; `coupledCrossCorrelationConj = ≈ú^‚àí_{(x,a)}≈ú^+_{(x,b)}`). Double limit in eventual-`Œµ'` form (outer `Œµ‚Üì0`, inner `L‚Üë‚àû`); GS family given (unique by MLM). The two condensates are coherently coupled (entangled). Proof: Koma‚ÄìTasaki. **Completes Tasaki Chapter 5** | `Quantum/SpinS/BoseEinsteinCondensate.lean` |

**PROVED**, axiom-free, in `LiebSchultzMattisProof.lean`): `‚ü®Œ¶_LSM,ƒ§Œ¶_LSM‚ü©/‚ü®Œ¶_LSM,Œ¶_LSM‚ü© ‚àí E_GS ‚â§ 8œÄ¬≤S¬≤/L` for any `S` ‚Äî via the per-bond symmetrised twist identity `√õ‚ÄÝ(≈ú_x¬∑≈ú_y)√õ + √õ(≈ú_x¬∑≈ú_y)√õ‚ÄÝ ‚àí 2(≈ú_x¬∑≈ú_y) = (2cos(Œ∏_x‚àíŒ∏_y)‚àí2)¬∑XY_{xy}` (the longitudinal `≈ú¬≥≈ú¬≥` part and the imaginary current cancel in the `¬±Œ∏` average), `cos(Œ∏_x‚àíŒ∏_y)=cos(2œÄ/L)` on every bond, `|‚ü®XY_b‚ü©.re| ‚â§ 2S¬≤‚ÄñŒ¶‚Äñ¬≤`, and `1‚àícos(2œÄ/L) ‚â§ (2œÄ/L)¬≤/2` summed over the `L` bonds; the generic Courant‚ÄìFischer second-eigenvalue bound `hermitian_second_eigenvalue_variational` (`HermitianSecondEigenvalue.lean`) with the existence of an eigenvalue above the ground line (`hermitian_exists_eigenvalue_gt`, `HermitianGapExists.lean`) defines `E‚ÇÅ`, and Lemma 6.1 (trial energy) + Lemma 6.2 (orthogonality) bound `E‚ÇÅ ‚â§ E‚ÇÄ+8œÄ¬≤S¬≤/L` | `Quantum/SpinS/LiebSchultzMattis.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/LiebSchultzMattisRingGap.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/LiebSchultzMattisProof.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/LiebSchultzMattisOrthogonality.lean` |

(6.3.7)‚Äì(6.3.9)): the **S=1 chain on the hidden-AFM subspace `H_HAF`**. **`tasaki_prop_6_5_hhaf_spin_one` is now a proved theorem** (formerly a documented axiom; `#print axioms` ‚Üí `propext`/`Classical.choice`/`Quot.sound`): the final assembly `tasaki_prop_6_5_hhaf_spin_one` bundles, for even `L>0`, a ground state `Œ¶ = hhafSubspaceEmbedding` of the balanced Perron‚ÄìFrobenius eigenvector with (i) projection-fixedness + nonzero + eigenvector at `E = hhafMinEnergy`, (ii) minimality over `hhafRealSpectrum`, (iii) **uniqueness** (`hhafRestrictedMatrix_ground_finrank_le_one`: the restricted ground eigenspace is one-dimensional ‚Äî at `E < ‚àí2` the single-`¬±` block is vacated since its eigenvalues are `‚â• ‚àí2`, and the balanced block contributes the 1-D PF ground state via the injective restriction `R` into the balanced block eigenspace), lifted to the operator via `hhafSubspaceEmbedding_of_projFixed` + `hhafRestrictedMatrix_mulVec_of_projFixed_eig` + `finrank_le_one_iff`, (iv) the positive gap (`exists_hhaf_positive_gap`), and (v) exponential decay (`hhaf_correlation_exp_decay_exists`). For the spin-1 chain (`N=2`, configs `Fin L ‚Üí Fin 3`, `œÉ_x=0/1/2 ‚Ü¶ +1/0/‚àí1`), a config has **complete hidden AFM order** (`IsHiddenAFMConfig`, via `IsPM`/`InCyclicOpen`/`IsNextPM`) when the nonzero (`¬±`) spins strictly alternate around the ring with arbitrary `0`-spins between (eq. 6.3.9). `H_HAF` = span of those basis states; `hhafProjection` (diagonal projection), `hhafRestrictedChainHamiltonianS = P_HAF¬∑ƒ§¬∑P_HAF`, `hhafRealSpectrum` (eigenvalues with `H_HAF` eigenvectors). For even `L>0`, the restricted chain has a **unique ground state**, a **finite gap** (`gap>0` in the `H_HAF`-restricted spectrum), and **exponentially decaying** correlation `\|chainCorrelation\| ‚â§ C e^{‚àíd(x,y)/Œæ}` (`ringDist`) ‚Äî so the Haldane conjecture for `S=1` holds rigorously within this artificial subspace. Proof: path-integral / 2D classical stat-mech. **Spectral foundations now proved** (infrastructure for the now-proved theorem, Issue #4718): `hhafProjection_isHermitian` (P_HAF Hermitian), `hhafProjection_mul_self` (P_HAF idempotent), `afmHeisenbergChainHamiltonianS_isHermitian` (defined in `HaldaneConjecture.lean`), `hhafRestrictedChainHamiltonianS_isHermitian` (compressed `P_HAF¬∑ƒ§¬∑P_HAF` Hermitian). **Ground-energy existence now proved**: `hhafConfig` (hidden-AFM config subtype), `hhafRestrictedMatrix` (H_HAF√óH_HAF submatrix, Hermitian), `hhafSubspaceEmbedding` (zero-extension) with the compression‚Äìembedding intertwining `P_HAF¬∑ƒ§¬∑P_HAF ‚àò emb = emb ‚àò restMat`, and `hhafMinEnergy`/`hhafMinEnergy_mem_realSpectrum` (the minimal restricted eigenvalue is a genuine `hhafRealSpectrum` member with a nonzero H_HAF ground state), and `exists_hhaf_min_real_eigenvalue` (it is `‚â§` every restricted eigenvalue, so a genuine restricted **ground energy** with minimality). **Per-L exp-decay** proved (`exp_decay_envelope_of_finite`/`hhaf_correlation_exp_decay_exists`): on the finite ring any state's correlation admits `|‚ü®≈ú_x¬∑≈ú_y‚ü©| ‚â§ C e^{‚àíd/Œæ}` (Œæ=1, C=Œ£|corr|e^{d}) ‚Äî the decay clause of Prop 6.5 (now all clauses proved). **Max-energy infra** added: `hhafRestrictedMatrix_eigenvalue_mem_realSpectrum` (every restricted-matrix eigenvalue embeds into `hhafRealSpectrum`), `hhafMaxEnergy` + `hhafMaxEnergy_mem_realSpectrum`; **spectral completeness/finiteness** (`hhafRealSpectrum_restrict` reverse bridge, `hhafRealSpectrum_subset_eigenvalue_range` via mathlib `spectrum_real_eq_range_eigenvalues`, `hhafRealSpectrum_finite`) ‚Äî the restricted spectrum IS the finite restricted-matrix eigenvalue set. **Diagonal bounds**: new general `HermitianVariationalUpperBound` (max-Rayleigh) + `hhafMinEnergy_le_diag`/`diag_le_hhafMaxEnergy` give `hhafMinEnergy ‚â§ (restMat œÉ œÉ).re ‚â§ hhafMaxEnergy` via Rayleigh at the basis vector (toward non-scalarity for the gap). **Non-scalarity PROVED**: `hhafMinEnergy_lt_hhafMaxEnergy` (even L‚â•2) via all-zero config (diag=0) and domain-wall config œÉ=[0,2,1,‚Ä¶,1] (HAF, diag<0); general formula `hhaf_diag_eq_succ_sum` (diag = Œ£_x (1-œÉx)(1-œÉ_{x+1})). **Positive gap PROVED**: `exists_hhaf_positive_gap` (even L>0) ‚Äî first-excited eigenvalue E‚ÇÅ = (eigenvalues image filtered above min).min with E‚ÇÅ ‚àà spectrum, E‚ÇÅ > minEig (gap>0), and minimal among spectrum elements above minEig (via completeness). These foundations feed the now-discharged uniqueness clause. **PF real-form foundation** started: `hhafRestrictedMatrix_im_zero` (entries real), `hhafRestrictedMatrixReal` (real symmetric form) + `hhafRestrictedMatrixReal_ofReal`/`_isSymm` ‚Äî the entry point for the Perron-Frobenius uniqueness route (Marshall gauge (now built: `ringSublattice`, `hhafDressedMatrix`, `hhafDressedMatrix_eq` = Œµ(œÉ)Œµ(œÑ)¬∑M_real via the dressed-Heisenberg machinery) + **symmetrized-coupling infra** for the off-diagonal sign step (`heisenbergHamiltonianS_coupling_swap`: the Heisenberg Hamiltonian is invariant under transposing the coupling, via `spinSDot_comm`; `ringCouplingSym` = `ringCoupling + ringCoupling·µÄ` with `_symm`/`_im_zero`/`_re_nonneg`; `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCouplingSym`: `H(ringCouplingSym) = 2¬∑H(ringCoupling)`, so the directed ring Hamiltonian equals ¬Ω the symmetric-coupling one ‚Äî letting the symmetric-coupling off-diagonal nonpositivity lemma apply) ‚Äî **off-diagonal nonpositivity now PROVED**: `ringCouplingSym_bipartite` (even ring `L` is bipartite w.r.t. `ringSublattice`: for even `L`, `(a+1)%L` flips parity, so equal-parity sites are never adjacent ‚Äî via `Nat.mod_mod_of_dvd`), `dressedHeisenbergS_ringCoupling_re_eq_half` (dressed directed = ¬Ω dressed symmetric), and `hhafDressedMatrix_offdiag_nonpos` (even `L`: `hhafDressedMatrix œÉ œÑ ‚â§ 0` for `œÉ ‚âÝ œÑ`, via the symmetric-coupling nonpositivity lemma) ‚Äî the diagonal `¬±1` Marshall gauge makes the AFM off-diagonals sign-definite. **Symmetry + nonneg shift now PROVED**: `hhafDressedMatrix_isSymm` (the dressed matrix is symmetric), `hhafDressedMatrix_diag_eq` (diagonal = real restricted diagonal, since the Marshall sign squares to `1`), `hhafDressedMatrix_diag_le_hhafMaxEnergy` (diagonal ‚â§ max energy), and `hhafShifted_entry_nonneg` (for `c ‚â• hhafMaxEnergy` and even `L`, the shifted matrix `c¬∑I ‚àí M` has nonnegative entries ‚Äî the nonnegativity hypothesis of `perronFrobenius_real_symmetric`). **Irreducibility started** (Issue #4732, G√≥mez-Santos kink ergodicity): `hhafShifted_pos_of_ladderStep` ‚Äî two hidden-AFM configs differing by one adjacent raise/lower (ladder) move on a bipartite bond have a strictly positive shifted entry `(c¬∑I ‚àí M) œÑ œÉ > 0` (reusing `dressedHeisenbergS_apply_re_neg_of_raiseLowerStepS_witness` on `ringCouplingSym` + the ¬Ω scaling), the single-edge positivity for the Perron‚ÄìFrobenius reachability argument. **Ring bond graph** added: `hhafRingGraph` (cyclic nearest-neighbour graph on `Fin L`), with `hhafRingGraph_adj_sublattice_ne` (bipartite w.r.t. `ringSublattice` for even `L`) and `ringCouplingSym_re_pos_of_ringGraph_adj` (the symmetrized coupling is strictly positive on every edge) ‚Äî the bond-graph data feeding the per-step positivity. **HAF reachability + walk‚Üípower** added: `RaiseLowerStepSHhaf` / `RaiseLowerReachableSHhaf` (single HAF-preserving ladder move / its reflexive-transitive closure), `hhafShifted_pos_of_stepHhaf` (each step gives a strictly positive shifted entry), and `exists_matrixPow_apply_pos_of_hhafReachable` (HAF-reachability ‚áí some power `(c¬∑I ‚àí M)^k` is strictly positive ‚Äî the walk‚Üípower bridge, mirroring the magnetization-sector version). **Kink-reduction setup** added: `hhafCanonical` (the all-`0`-spin configuration, base case), `pmCount` (number of `¬±` spins, the induction measure), `pmCount_eq_zero_iff` (no `¬±` spins ‚ü∫ canonical). **Slide move** added: `slidePM` (move the spin at `a` onto a cyclic neighbour `b`, leaving a `0` at `a`), with `slidePM_apply` (its pointwise values) and `slidePM_isRaiseLowerStep` (it is a single raise/lower ladder step on the ring-graph bond `{a, b}` when `a` carries a `¬±` spin and `b` carries a `0`). **Slide preserves hidden-AFM order PROVEN** (the kink-ergodicity core): `slidePM_isHiddenAFM` ‚Äî sliding a `¬±` spin onto its `0`-carrying cyclic successor keeps the strict `+,‚àí,‚Ä¶` alternation, proved by transferring each `IsNextPM` pair of the moved configuration back to one of `œÉ` (relabelling `b ‚Ü¶ a`) via the open-arc decomposition lemmas `inCyclicOpen_succ_left_imp` / `inCyclicOpen_succ_right_imp` / `notInCyclicOpen_succ` (each an `omega` calculation after splitting the cyclic wrap). **Slide lifted to a subtype step** (`slidePM_isRaiseLowerStepSHhaf`) and the **annihilation move** added: `annihPM` (set adjacent `a, b` both to `0`), `annihPM_apply`, and `annihPM_isRaiseLowerStep` (it is a single ladder step when `a, b` carry opposite `¬±` spins). **Annihilation preserves hidden-AFM order PROVEN**: `annihPM_isHiddenAFM` ‚Äî removing an adjacent opposite-sign `+,‚àí` pair keeps the alternation (each `IsNextPM` pair of the annihilated config either avoids the removed pair, giving an `IsNextPM` pair of `œÉ`, or straddles it, where the chain `x, a, b, y` forces opposite endpoint signs), via the sub-arc lemmas `inCyclicOpen_succ_iff_mem` / `inCyclicOpen_sub_left` / `inCyclicOpen_sub_right` / `notInCyclicOpen_succ_right` / `notInCyclicOpen_pred_left`. **Move-count behaviour** added: `annihPM_isRaiseLowerStepSHhaf` (annihilation as a subtype step), `slidePM_pmCount_card` (sliding preserves the `¬±` count), `annihPM_pmCount_card_lt` (annihilation strictly decreases it). **CONNECTIVITY PROVEN** (the kink-ergodicity theorem): the reduction measure `hhafS = Œ£_{¬± spins}(L ‚àí x)` strictly decreases under a slide (`slidePM_hhafS_lt`, non-wrapping successor) and an annihilation (`annihPM_hhafS_lt`); `hhaf_single_step` produces, for any balanced (even-`pmCount`) non-canonical config, a single HAF ladder move to another balanced config of smaller measure (first `¬±` spin at `p ‚â§ L‚àí2`, sliding past a `0` or annihilating an adjacent opposite `¬±`); `hhaf_reachable_to_canonical` / `hhaf_reachable_canonical` (strong induction on `hhafS`) then show **every balanced hidden-AFM configuration is HAF-reachable from the canonical all-`0` configuration** (with `RaiseLowerReachableSHhaf_symm` for the reverse). **Design note**: the *full* HAF matrix is reducible ‚Äî a single-`¬±` config (`pmCount = 1`, magnetization `¬±1`) is vacuously hidden-AFM but unreachable from `canonical` (the moves conserve magnetization), so irreducibility holds only within the balanced (charge-`0`) sector; this is the sector containing the antiferromagnetic ground state. **Balanced-sector block irreducibility PROVEN**: `hhafConfig0` (the even-`pmCount` subtype) with `hhafShiftedMatrix0` (the `c¬∑I ‚àí M` block), `hhaf_reachable_to_canonical0` / `RaiseLowerReachableSHhaf0_symm` (balanced-sector connectivity lifted to the subtype), `exists_matrixPow_apply_pos_of_hhafReachable0` (subtype walk‚Üípower), and `hhafShiftedMatrix0_isIrreducible` ‚Äî for even `L` and shift `c > hhafMaxEnergy`, the balanced-sector shifted matrix is Perron‚ÄìFrobenius irreducible (`isIrreducible_iff_exists_pow_pos`: nonnegative + entrywise-positive power, with the diagonal handled by the strict shift `c > hhafMaxEnergy`). **Balanced-sector unique ground state PROVEN**: `hhafDressedMatrix0` (the dressed block on `hhafConfig0`), `hhafDressedMatrix0_isSymm` (submatrix of a symmetric matrix), `hhafShiftedMatrix0_eq` (`hhafShiftedMatrix0 = c¬∑I ‚àí M‚ÇÄ`, the identity restricting since `Subtype.val` is injective), and `hhafDressedMatrix0_ground_finrank_le_one` ‚Äî applying `perronFrobenius_real_symmetric` to the balanced block yields a strictly positive lowest eigenvector with a **one-dimensional** ground eigenspace: the unique ground state of the AFM chain *within the balanced (charge-`0`) sector* of `H_HAF`. **Marshall-gauge transfer DONE**: `hhafRestrictedMatrixReal0` (undressed real balanced block), `hhafMarshallDiag0` (the `¬±1` Marshall sign diagonal, squaring to `1`), `hhafDressedMatrix0_eq_conj` (`M‚ÇÄ = Œò¬∑M_real‚ÇÄ¬∑Œò`), and `hhafRestrictedMatrixReal0_ground_finrank_le_one` ‚Äî transferring the **full Perron‚ÄìFrobenius data** (nonzero lowest eigenvector `Œòv`, ground minimality, and `finrank ‚â§ 1`) through the Marshall similarity (the generic `matrix_similar_eigenspace_finrank_eq`, now generalised from `‚ÑÇ` to any field), so the **undressed** real balanced block also has a one-dimensional ground eigenspace with an explicit minimal eigenvector. Remaining for the *global* ground-state uniqueness: the inter-sector energy ordering (balanced sector strictly lowest, a Marshall‚ÄìLieb‚ÄìMattis statement within `H_HAF`). **N√©el witness** added (toward that ordering): `hhafNeel` (the maximally-AFM configuration `+1` on even sites, `‚àí1` on odd), `hhafNeel_isHiddenAFM` (it is hidden-AFM on an even ring ‚Äî all sites are `¬±`, every `IsNextPM` pair is cyclically adjacent hence opposite parity, via `inCyclicOpen_succ_mem_of_ne`), `hhafNeel_pmCount` (`¬±`-count `= L`), and `hhafNeelConfig0` (the N√©el configuration as a balanced charge-`0` configuration). **Ground energy ‚â§ ‚àíL PROVEN**: `hhafNeel_diag` (the N√©el diagonal energy is `‚àíL`, every nearest-neighbour bond antiferromagnetic `(+1)(‚àí1) = ‚àí1`) and `hhafMinEnergy_le_neg_L` (the global restricted ground energy `hhafMinEnergy ‚â§ ‚àíL`, via the Rayleigh quotient `hhafMinEnergy_le_diag` at the N√©el state) ‚Äî the first half of the inter-sector ordering (the balanced sector reaches energy `‚àíL`; the remaining half bounds the magnetization-`¬±1` single-`¬±` sectors above this). Toward that bound: `hhaf_diag_eq_zero_of_pmCount_one` ‚Äî a single-`¬±` configuration has **vanishing diagonal energy** (every nearest-neighbour bond has a `0`-spin endpoint since only one site is `¬±`), the Gershgorin-disc centre for the magnetization-`¬±1` sectors. `spinSDot_re_abs_le_one_raising_lowering` / `spinSDot_re_abs_le_one_lowering_raising` ‚Äî the spin-`1` ladder off-diagonal amplitude `|‚ü®œÉ'|≈ú_x¬∑≈ú_y|œÉ‚ü©| ‚â§ 1` (each `‚àö` factor `‚â§ ‚àö2` for `N = 2`), the Gershgorin-disc radius ingredient. `hhafDressedMatrix_abs_le_ringCouplingSym` ‚Äî for two hidden-AFM configs related by a single ladder move on a bond `{x, y}`, the dressed off-diagonal entry satisfies `|M œÉ œÑ| ‚â§ (ringCouplingSym x y).re` (the Marshall signs are `¬±1`, the Heisenberg element is `ringCouplingSym ¬∑ spinSDot`, and the ladder amplitude is `‚â§ 1`). **Magnetization-`¬±1` sector min `‚â• ‚àí2` PROVEN** (codex insight: the cyclic `IsHiddenAFMConfig` forbids odd `pmCount > 1`, so the only HAF sectors are balanced `Sz=0` (even `pmCount`) and single-`¬±` `Sz=¬±1` (`pmCount = 1`) ‚Äî there are **no higher-`Sz` sectors**, dissolving the inter-sector Lieb‚ÄìMattis wall): `hhafConfigPM1` (the `pmCount = 1` subtype) with `hhafDressedMatrixPM1` (dressed block), `hhafPM1Site` (the unique `¬±` site, computed via `Finset.min'` so `Finset.image` reductions stay light), `hhafPM1_coupling_imp` (a nonzero off-diagonal forces `œÑ` to be the slide `slidePM` of `œÉ`'s `¬±` spin to its new site, sharing the `¬±` value by magnetization conservation `magEigenvalueS_eq_iff`), `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_offdiag_le` (per-entry bound `‚â§ ringCouplingSym`), `hhafPM1Site_injOn_support` (the `¬±`-site map is injective on the coupling support), `ringCouplingSym_re_row_sum` (`‚àë_t (ringCouplingSym s t).re = 2`, the two incident directed ring bonds), the generic `sum_comp_le_sum_univ_of_injOn` (injective-on-support reindex avoiding a heavy concrete `Finset.univ`), and `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_rowSum_le_two` ‚üπ `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_eigenvalue_ge` (Gershgorin `eigenvalue_mem_ball`, diagonal `0` + row sum `‚â§ 2`: every single-`¬±` eigenvalue `‚â• ‚àí2`) ‚Äî the single-kink tight-binding lower bound placing the `Sz = ¬±1` sectors above the balanced ground energy (`‚â§ ‚àíL ‚â§ ‚àí2`). **Balanced ground energy `< ‚àíL` STRICT PROVEN** (`hhafDressedMatrix0_ground_lt_neg_L`): the Perron‚ÄìFrobenius lowest eigenvector `v > 0` paired with the N√©el row gives `Œº¬∑v(N√©el) = ‚àíL¬∑v(N√©el) + Œ£_{œÑ‚âÝN√©el} M‚ÇÄ(N√©el,œÑ) v(œÑ)`; every off-diagonal term is `‚â§ 0` (`hhafDressedMatrix0_offdiag_nonpos`) and the annihilation neighbour (`hhafNeel_step_neighbor`, annihilating the adjacent opposite-`¬±` pair at sites `0,1`) is strictly `< 0`, so via the generic row inequality `row_sum_mul_lt_diag_mul_of_offdiag_nonpos_exists_neg` and `hhafDressedMatrix0_Neel_diag` (`M‚ÇÄ(N√©el,N√©el) = ‚àíL`) we get `Œº¬∑v(N√©el) < ‚àíL¬∑v(N√©el)`, hence `Œº < ‚àíL`. Since `‚àíL ‚â§ ‚àí2`, the balanced sector lies **strictly** below the magnetization-`¬±1` sectors with **no `L=2` tie** ‚Äî the inter-sector ordering is complete. **Sector classification PROVEN** (`hhaf_pmCount_eq_one_or_even`): every hidden-AFM configuration has `pmCount = 1` or even `pmCount` ‚Äî an odd count `‚â• 3` is impossible. Proof: the `¬±` sites read in increasing index order are pairwise `IsNextPM` (`hhaf_isNextPM_consecutive` ‚Äî no `¬±` spin lies strictly between index-adjacent `¬±` sites, via `orderEmbOfFin`), so their signs strictly alternate (`hhaf_pm_alternates`: the `i`-th `¬±` value is the `0`-th when `i` is even and its complement `2 - ¬∑` when odd, `pm_flip`); and the maximal/minimal `¬±` sites are also `IsNextPM` (`hhaf_isNextPM_wrap` ‚Äî the cyclic wrap arc holds no `¬±` spin), which would force the first and last `¬±` signs to *differ* while the linear alternation makes them *equal* at odd count ‚Äî contradiction. The magnetization corollaries `hhaf_magSumS_eq_L_of_even` (even `pmCount ‚üπ magSumS = L`, via `magSumS`-invariance under HAF ladder moves `magSumS_eq_of_hhafReachable` + reachability from canonical) and `hhaf_magSumS_ne_L_of_pmCount_one` (single-`¬±` ‚üπ `magSumS = L ¬± 1 ‚âÝ L`) identify the sectors: balanced `= Sz 0`, single-`¬±` `= Sz ¬±1`, with no higher sectors ‚Äî the cross-sector vanishing (magnetization conservation) needed to split a ground eigenvector into balanced + single-`¬±` blocks. **Magnetization-block structure of the restricted matrix PROVEN**: `hhafRestrictedMatrix_eq_zero_of_magSumS_ne` (entries between different-`magSumS` configurations vanish ‚Äî magnetization conservation); the generic slice `hhafRestrictedMatrixSlice P` (submatrix on `{œÉ // P œÉ}`) with `hhafRestrictedMatrixSlice_mulVec_of_full_eigen` (a full eigenvector restricted to a block-closed `P`-slice is a `P`-slice eigenvector with the same eigenvalue); and the two block-closures `hhafRestrictedMatrix_pmCount_one_block_closed` / `hhafRestrictedMatrix_even_block_closed` (via the classification: a single-`¬±` config couples only to single-`¬±`, an even config only to even) ‚Äî the block-diagonal decomposition into the balanced and single-`¬±` blocks, the structural input for the ground-eigenspace `finrank ‚â§ 1`. **Per-block spectral bounds PROVEN**: the complex blocks are the real blocks cast to `‚ÑÇ` (`hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrix0_eq_map` / `hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrixPM1_eq_map`, entries real); `hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrix0_eigenspace_finrank_le_one` ‚Äî the **complex balanced block ground eigenspace is one-dimensional** (real‚Üícomplex bridge `matrix_complex_eigenspace_finrank_le_one_of_real` from the real balanced Perron‚ÄìFrobenius `finrank ‚â§ 1`); and `hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrixPM1_eigenvalue_ge` ‚Äî the **complex single-`¬±` block has all eigenvalues `‚â• ‚àí2`** (an eigenvalue is real, its re/im part is a real-block eigenvector, transferred to a dressed-block eigenvector via the Marshall conjugation `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_eq_conj` where the Gershgorin bound `‚â• ‚àí2` applies). These are the per-block spectral inputs: at the ground energy `E < ‚àí2` the single-`¬±` block is vacated and the balanced block contributes the unique ground state. **Reverse block restriction PROVEN**: `hhafSliceExtend P` (zero-extension of a `P`-slice vector to all of `hhafConfig`) with `hhafSliceExtend_ne_zero` and `hhafRestrictedMatrix_mulVec_hhafSliceExtend` (if there is no coupling *into* a `P`-slice from its complement, the zero-extension of a `P`-slice eigenvector is a full eigenvector at the same eigenvalue) ‚Äî the reverse counterpart of the forward slice restriction, used to lift the balanced Perron‚ÄìFrobenius eigenvector to a full restricted-matrix eigenvector (so the balanced ground energy is in the restricted spectrum). (The Perron‚ÄìFrobenius uniqueness development ‚Äî kink moves through the Marshall transfer, the magnetization-`¬±1` Gershgorin bound, and the strict balanced ground energy ‚Äî lives in the companion `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniqueness.lean`, with its combinatorial kink-move/balanced-sector core split off into `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniquenessCore.lean` for build speed.) | `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrder.lean` + `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniqueness.lean` + `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniquenessCore.lean` |

### The AKLT model (Tasaki ¬ß7.1)

(7.1.1)‚Äì(7.1.2)): the Affleck‚ÄìKennedy‚ÄìLieb‚ÄìTasaki `S=1` chain. `akltHamiltonianS L = Œ£_x {≈ú_x¬∑≈ú_{x+1} + ‚Öì(≈ú_x¬∑≈ú_{x+1})¬≤}` (eq. 7.1.1, PBC ring, SU(2)-invariant; the biquadratic term puts it outside Marshall‚ÄìLieb‚ÄìMattis). For all sufficiently large chains (`L = n+1`, any parity), `aklt_theorem_7_1` gives: a **unique ground state** `Œ¶` (= the valence-bond-solid VBS state; nonzero eigenvector at `E‚ÇÄ`, every ground eigenvector ‚àù Œ¶); a **spectral gap** `‚â• ŒîE‚ÇÄ` for a positive `L`-independent `ŒîE‚ÇÄ` (`IsPositiveSpectralGap`); and the **exact correlation** `‚ü®Œ¶,≈ú_x¬∑≈ú_y Œ¶‚ü©/‚ü®Œ¶,Œ¶‚ü© ‚Üí 4(‚àí3)^{‚àí\|x‚àíy\|}` (eq. 7.1.2, sign-alternating exponential decay) for fixed sites with `\|x‚àíy\| ‚â• 1`, as `L‚Üë‚àû` (`chainSite` embedding, eventual-Œµ form). `ŒîE‚ÇÄ` and `Œ¶` quantified outermost. On the quasi-local C*-algebra, a state `œâ` is a ground state iff `IsGroundState œâ Œ¥` (Def A.25: `œâ(√Ç‚ÄÝ[ƒ§,√Ç])‚â•0`) and has a nonzero gap iff `HasNonzeroGap œâ Œ¥ Œ≥` (Def A.27, `Œ≥>0`). For the 1D `InfiniteSpinSystem` with the AKLT dynamics `Œ¥` (tied by the uninterpreted marker `IsAKLTChainDynamics`), `aklt_theorem_7_2` gives `‚àÉ œâ`, `IsState œâ`, `IsGroundState œâ Œ¥`, **unique among states** (`‚àÄœâ', IsState œâ' ‚Üí IsGroundState œâ' Œ¥ ‚Üí œâ'=œâ`), and `‚àÉŒ≥, HasNonzeroGap œâ Œ¥ Œ≥`. The unique GS is the `L‚Üë‚àû` limit of the VBS state. Proof: Matsui (uniqueness) on AKLT; documented operator-algebra axiom (per the C*-algebra policy) | `Quantum/SpinS/AKLTInfiniteChain.lean` |

(7.1.4)): the AKLT gap is **stable** under small local perturbations. `perturbedAKLTHamiltonianS L Œµ v = akltHamiltonianS L + Œµ Œ£_x vÃÇ_x` (eq. 7.1.4); `IsAKLTPerturbation L r v‚ÇÄ v` bundles: each `vÃÇ_x` Hermitian, `r`-local (`IsLocalRangeR`), bounded (`manyBodyOperatorNormS ‚â§ v‚ÇÄ`), and translation-covariant (`IsTranslationCovariant`, an uninterpreted marker for `vÃÇ_x = TÃÇ^x vÃÇ_o TÃÇ^{‚àíx}`). For range `r` bounded by `v‚ÇÄ`, ‚àÉ `Œµ‚ÇÄ>0` such that for `|Œµ|<Œµ‚ÇÄ` there are `ŒîE,C,Œæ>0` **L-independent** with: for any `L ‚â• 3` and any such `v`, the perturbed AKLT chain has a **unique ground state** (`IsUniqueChainGroundState`), a **gap ‚â• ŒîE** (`IsPositiveSpectralGap`), and **exponential decay of the connected correlation** `|connectedChainCorrelation| ‚â§ C e^{‚àíd(x,y)/Œæ}` (`ringDist`). The **connected/truncated** correlation `‚ü®≈ú_x¬∑≈ú_y‚ü© ‚àí Œ£_Œ±‚ü®≈ú_x^(Œ±)‚ü©‚ü®≈ú_y^(Œ±)‚ü©` is used since a symmetry-breaking perturbation can give nonzero one-point functions (the raw correlation need not decay); `L ‚â• 3` excludes the degenerate small rings. The gapful AKLT phase is stable. Proof: cluster expansion (Yarotsky) | `Quantum/SpinS/AKLTStability.lean` |
| `bondSpin2ProjectionS` / `aklt_bond_term_eq_bondSpin2Projection` / `tasaki_lemma_7_4` | **¬ß7.1.3 Lemma 7.4** (local VBS ground-state characterization; eqs. (7.1.5), (7.1.19)‚Äì(7.1.20)): the bond projection onto total spin 2 and the singlet form. `bondSpin2ProjectionS x y = ¬Ω(≈ú_x¬∑≈ú_y) + ‚Öô(≈ú_x¬∑≈ú_y)¬≤ + ‚Öì¬∑1` is the projection `PÃÇ‚ÇÇ[≈ú_x+≈ú_y]` onto the bond total-spin-2 subspace. `aklt_bond_term_eq_bondSpin2Projection` (**PROVED**, eq. 7.1.5) records the affine identity `≈ú_x¬∑≈ú_y + ‚Öì(≈ú_x¬∑≈ú_y)¬≤ = 2 PÃÇ‚ÇÇ[≈ú_x+≈ú_y] ‚àí ‚Öî`, so the AKLT local term penalizes only the spin-2 component of each bond. `tasaki_lemma_7_4` (**AXIOM**, eqs. 7.1.19‚Äì7.1.20): for `1 < L` (so the bond `{x, ringSucc x}` is genuinely two-site), a state `Œ¶` is annihilated by the bond projection, `PÃÇ‚ÇÇ[≈ú_x+≈ú_{x+1}] Œ¶ = 0`, **iff** `Œ¶` has the valence-bond-solid singlet-tensor form on that bond (`IsVBSGroundForm`, an uninterpreted marker for the duplicated-`S=¬Ω` / Schwinger-boson singlet construction not yet formalized). The AKLT ground space = states annihilated by every bond projection = the VBS state (Kennedy‚ÄìLieb‚ÄìTasaki). Proof: spin-`{0,1,2}` decomposition + duplicated-chain singlet construction (KLT 1988) | `Quantum/SpinS/AKLTBondProjection.lean` |

(7.2.6)‚Äì(7.2.8)): the AKLT VBS ground state has positive string order. `spinSStringPhaseS1 = diag(‚àí1,1,‚àí1)` is the single-site string phase `exp(iœÄ≈ú^z)` for `S=1` (entry `(‚àí1)^{k+1}`). `stringOperatorS x y` is the diagonal string operator `exp(iœÄ Œ£_{x<z<y} ≈ú_z^{(3)})` (phase `‚àè_{x<z<y}(‚àí1)^{œÉ_z+1}`). `stringCorrelationS x y Œ¶ = ‚àí‚ü®Œ¶\|≈ú_x^{(3)} exp(iœÄŒ£≈ú^{(3)}) ≈ú_y^{(3)}\|Œ¶‚ü©` (= `S^{(3)}_{x,y}`, eq. 7.2.6, with the leading minus; the raw expectation is negative, ‚Üí `‚àí4/9` for VBS, so the minus makes it positive, and `O_string^{(3)} = lim lim S^{(3)}_{x,y}`). `aklt_string_order_7_2_8` (**AXIOM**, eq. 7.2.8): for the AKLT VBS ground-state family (`IsAKLTVBSGroundStateFamily`, an uninterpreted marker), the den Nijs‚ÄìRommelse string order parameter `O_string^{(3)}(Œ¶_VBS) = lim_{d‚Üë‚àû} lim_{L‚Üë‚àû} stringCorrelationS = 4/9 > 0` (explicit double-Œµ form over ordered linear windows `0 < x < y < L` with separation `d = y‚àíx`, inner `L‚Üë‚àû` first), so the VBS state has hidden AFM order in all three directions (SU(2)) while N√©el order vanishes ‚Äî the hallmark of the Haldane phase. (7.2.36), (7.2.41)‚Äì(7.2.42)): the AKLT VBS state as an injective matrix product state. `MPSMatrices D N = Fin(N+1)‚ÜíD√óD` matrices `A^œÉ`; `mpsTransferMatrix A` is the D¬≤√óD¬≤ transfer matrix `√É_{(Œ±,Œ≤),(Œ±',Œ≤')} = Œ£_œÉ conj(A^œÉ_{Œ±,Œ±'}) A^œÉ_{Œ≤,Œ≤'}` (eq. 7.2.42); `orderedProd A œÉs` the ordered product `A^{œÉ_1}‚ãØA^{œÉ_‚Ñì}`; `mpsProductsSpanAt A ‚Ñì` (products span all D√óD matrices); `IsMPSNormalized A Œª` (eq. 7.2.41, `Œ£_œÉ A^œÉ(A^œÉ)‚ÄÝ = ŒªI`, `Œª>0`); `HasPrimitiveTransferSpectrum A Œª` (**concrete**: `Œª ‚àà spectrum`, eigenspace `finrank = 1`, every other eigenvalue `‚ÄñŒº‚Äñ < Œª`); `IsInjectiveMPS` bundles normalization + (i)+(ii)+(iii). `mps_theorem_7_5` (**AXIOM**): for normalized `A` (`0<D`), the three conditions (i) `MPSSpansEventually` ‚Üî (ii) `MPSSpansForAllLarge` ‚Üî (iii) `HasPrimitiveTransferSpectrum` are equivalent (Perron‚ÄìFrobenius for the CP transfer map). `mps_theorem_7_6` (**AXIOM**, Fannes‚ÄìNachtergaele‚ÄìWerner, unproven in book): two injective collections generating the same MPS (`GeneratesSameMPS` marker) are related by a **pure unitary gauge** `A^œÉ = U B^œÉ U‚ÄÝ` (`U` unitary, eq. 7.2.44; no extra scalar ‚Äî a rescaling would change the length-`L` amplitudes by its `L`-th power) ‚Äî the MPS representation is essentially unique, underlying SPT-phase classification (¬ß8.3.4). (8.3.1)‚Äì(8.3.4)): the Haldane phase as an SPT phase. `guWenHamiltonianS L D B = anisotropicChainHamiltonianS L D + B Œ£_x ≈ú_x^{(1)}` (eq. 8.3.4, S=1 chain + magnetic field B in 1-dir, breaks Z‚ÇÇ√óZ‚ÇÇ‚ÜíZ‚ÇÇ; B=0 ‚áí anisotropic 8.1.1). `tasaki_oshikawa_8_3_3` (**AXIOM**, eq. 8.3.3): the spin-`S` VBS string order `O_string^{(Œ±)}` is `>0` for odd `S`, `=0` for even `S` (even/odd-`S` distinction); `tasaki_vbs_edge_degeneracy` (**AXIOM**): the spin-`S` AKLT open chain has `(S+1)¬≤`-fold edge degeneracy. **SPT definition** (`def : Prop`, NOT axiom): `HamiltonianPath` (continuous `‚Ñù‚Üí` Hamiltonian, each short-range gapped-unique via `IsShortRangeGappedUniqueGS` marker); `ContinuouslyConnected`/`SymmetryConnected sym` (path with all `ƒ§_s` having the symmetry `sym`); `IsTrivialPhase sym H` = symmetry-connected to a product state (`IsProductStateHamiltonian`); `IsSPTPhase sym H` = short-range gapped-unique + `sym H` + ¬¨trivial. (8.3.6)‚Äì(8.3.10)): the Haldane phase is protected by any of three symmetries ‚Äî (S1) Z‚ÇÇ√óZ‚ÇÇ (`IsZ2Z2Invariant`), (S2) time-reversal (`IsTimeReversalInvariant` marker), (S3) bond-centered inversion (`IsBondInversionInvariant` marker). `vbsInversionParityS L S` (marker, ‚Ñ§) + `tasaki_vbs_inversion_parity` (**AXIOM**): `√õ_inv|Œ¶_VBS^S‚ü© = (‚àí1)^{L¬∑S}|Œ¶_VBS^S‚ü©` ‚Äî odd `L¬∑S` ‚üπ odd parity ‚üπ Z‚ÇÇ obstruction to connecting to the trivial state. `IsSpinSVBSNontrivialSPT S` (marker) + `tasaki_spt_classification` (**AXIOM**): the spin-`S` VBS is a nontrivial SPT phase **iff `S` is odd** (even `S` ‚áí trivial). `entanglementEntropyS` (marker, eqs. 8.3.7‚Äì8.3.8): the bipartite entanglement entropy `‚àíŒ£ p_j log p_j` from the Schmidt decomposition. ¬ß8.3.3 is heuristic; precise indices come in ¬ß8.3.4 (MPS) / ¬ß8.3.6 (Ogata) | `Quantum/SpinS/SPTTopologicalIndex.lean` |

(8.3.42)‚Äì(8.3.47)): the precise MPS invariant. A protecting symmetry `G` acts on the bond space by a projective representation with phase function (2-cocycle) `œÜ : G‚ÜíG‚Üí‚Ñù` (`IsProjectiveRep` marker); it is **trivial** (`IsTrivialProjectiveRep` marker) iff `œÜ` is a coboundary (eq. 8.3.43) ‚Äî the cohomology class is the SPT index. `SymmetricInjectiveMPSExists G œÜ` (marker): an injective MPS invariant up to phase under `VÃÇ(g)`. `tasaki_theorem_8_7` (**AXIOM**): symmetric injective MPS ‚üπ trivial projective rep. For half-odd-integer spin (`N` odd), `z2z2Spin_nontrivial_of_odd` (**AXIOM**, eq. 2.1.31): the Z‚ÇÇ√óZ‚ÇÇ rep is nontrivial. `tasaki_corollary_8_5` (**PROVED**, contrapositive of Thm 8.7): for `N` odd there is **no** Z‚ÇÇ√óZ‚ÇÇ-invariant injective MPS ‚Äî the matrix-product Lieb‚ÄìSchultz‚ÄìMattis no-go | `Quantum/SpinS/SPTMatrixProductIndex.lean` |

For **half-odd-integer** spin (`S=N/2`, `N` odd) with a short-ranged translation-invariant Hamiltonian (`HasShortRangeHamiltonianS`/`IsTranslationInvariantS` markers) having **either** Z‚ÇÇ√óZ‚ÇÇ **or** time-reversal symmetry (`IsZ2Z2SymmetricS ‚à® IsTimeReversalSymmetricS`), the ground state can **never** be both unique and gapped: `tasaki_theorem_8_6` (**AXIOM**) `‚üπ ¬¨HasUniqueGappedGroundStateS H`. (8.3.55)): the existence of a topological phase transition. `sptInterpolatingHamiltonianS L s = s¬∑akltHamiltonianS L + (1‚àís)¬∑Œ£_x (≈ú_x^{(3)})¬≤` (eq. 8.3.55) linearly connects the trivial Hamiltonian (8.1.2, `s=0`, large-`D` phase) to the AKLT/VBS Hamiltonian (7.1.1, `s=1`, Haldane phase), keeping all three protecting symmetries. `tasaki_theorem_8_8` (**AXIOM**): the model's infinite-volume limit undergoes a phase transition ‚Äî ‚àÉ intermediate `s‚àà(0,1)` at which the family `L‚Ü¶ƒ§_s` is **either** gapless (`IsGaplessInInfiniteVolume`), **or** has multiple ground states (`HasMultipleGroundStatesInInfiniteVolume`), **or** has a local-expectation discontinuity (`HasLocalExpectationDiscontinuityInInfiniteVolume`) ‚Äî markers on the `L‚Üë‚àû` family. Proving topological phase transitions resists standard methods; Tasaki settled it via an index theorem | `Quantum/SpinS/SPTPhaseTransition.lean` |
| `toricCodeHamiltonianS` / `tasaki_theorem_8_9` | **¬ß8.4 Topological order: Kitaev's toric code** (Theorem 8.9, Bravyi‚ÄìHastings‚ÄìMichalakis; eqs. (8.4.1), (8.4.19)): a gapped phase whose degeneracy is protected by topology, not symmetry. Qubits live on the edges `ToricEdge L = (ZMod L √ó ZMod L) √ó Fin 2` of an `L√óL` torus (`2L¬≤` edges); `starEdges`/`plaquetteEdges` are the 4-edge stars `A_v`/plaquettes `B_p`; `sigmaZS`/`sigmaXS` = `œÉ^z`/`œÉ^x`. `toricCodeHamiltonianS L = ‚àíŒ£_v ‚àè_{A_v} œÉ^z ‚àí Œ£_p ‚àè_{B_p} œÉ^x` (eq. 8.4.1, commuting stabilizers, gap ‚â• 2, 4-fold degenerate on the torus). `perturbedToricHamiltonianS L Œµ V = ƒ§_tc + Œµ Œ£_x V_x` (eq. 8.4.19). `tasaki_theorem_8_9` (**AXIOM**): for range-`r` perturbations there are **L-independent** `Œµ‚ÇÄ,Œî>0` such that for `|Œµ|‚â§Œµ‚ÇÄ`, all `L‚â•2` (nontrivial torus), and any local perturbation (`IsToricPerturbation`), the perturbed Hamiltonian has **four near-degenerate ground states** separated by `Œî` (`HasFourNearlyDegenerateGroundStates`) ‚Äî topological 4-fold degeneracy stable under arbitrary perturbation, no symmetry needed. Lattice + stabilizers + both Hamiltonians concrete | `Quantum/SpinS/ToricCode.lean` |

(2.4.9), p. 33, up to normalisation). Proof via Nat induction using the Cartan ladder `[≈útot^(3), ≈útot^-] = -≈útot^-` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |

| `singlet_mem_magnetizationSubspace_zero` / `triplet_zero_mem_magnetizationSubspace_zero` | singlet `|‚Üë‚Üì‚ü© - |‚Üì‚Üë‚ü©` and triplet-`m=0` state `|‚Üë‚Üì‚ü© + |‚Üì‚Üë‚ü©` lie in `H_0` (Tasaki ¬ßA.3 decomposition at the `M = 0` sector) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |

| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_neelChainState_mem_magnetizationSubspace_zero` | for any coupling `J`, `H ¬∑ |Œ¶_N√©el‚ü©` again lies in `H_0` ‚Äî immediate corollary of SU(2) invariance applied to the N√©el state. | `spinHalfDot_mulVec_neelChainState_adjacent` | Tasaki ¬ß2.5 eq. (2.5.3) per-bond action: for every adjacent pair `(i, i+1)` of the chain `Fin (2 * K)`, `≈ú_‚ü®i‚ü© ¬∑ ≈ú_‚ü®i+1‚ü© ¬∑ |Œ¶_N√©el‚ü© = (1/2) ¬∑ |swap_{i,i+1} Œ¶_N√©el‚ü© - (1/4) ¬∑ |Œ¶_N√©el‚ü©` (antiparallel case, parity-derived) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_neelChainState_wrap` | wrap-around bond `(2K + 1, 0)` action on the periodic chain `Fin (2 * (K + 1))`: same `(1/2) swap - (1/4) N√©el` decomposition as the open-bond case (parities `1` and `0` differ since the cycle length is even). | `neelSquareConfig_magnetization_zero` / `neelSquareState_mem_magnetizationSubspace_zero` | the 2D N√©el configuration has total magnetisation `0` and the corresponding state lies in the `≈ú_tot^(3) = 0` eigenspace `H_0`. | `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_neelSquareState_one_one` | concrete `K = L = 1` (2√ó2 = 4-site) instance: `ŒòÃÇ_tot (neelSquareState 1 1) = basisVec (flipConfig (neelSquareConfig 1 1))` (the equal up/down counts make `(-1)^|A| = 1`, so no overall sign) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |

| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_neelSquareState` | general-`K, L` 2D checkerboard: `ŒòÃÇ_tot (neelSquareState K L) = basisVec (flipConfig (neelSquareConfig K L))` (no sign because `(-1)^(2KL) = 1`). | `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_neelCubicState` | general-`K, L, M` 3D cubic checkerboard: `ŒòÃÇ_tot (neelCubicState K L M) = basisVec (flipConfig (neelCubicConfig K L M))` (no sign because `(-1)^(4KLM) = 1`). | `sum_mul_basisVec` / `basisVec_sum_mul` | selector-sum identities `‚àë œÑ, f œÑ ¬∑ basisVec œÉ œÑ = f œÉ` (and the symmetric form), the workhorses for inner-product computations on the spin Hilbert space | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `basisVec_inner` | basis-vector orthonormality `‚àë œÑ, basisVec œÉ œÑ ¬∑ basisVec œÅ œÑ = if œÅ = œÉ then 1 else 0`. Real bilinear pairing (no complex conjugation needed since `basisVec` values are 0 or 1) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `basisSwap_ne_self` | `œÉ x ‚âÝ œÉ y ‚Üí basisSwap œÉ x y ‚âÝ œÉ` (the swap of an antiparallel pair changes the configuration). Useful for orthogonality computations on swapped states | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |

| `neelChainState_inner_basisVec_basisSwap_adjacent_eq_zero` | the N√©el chain state is orthogonal to the swapped basis vector at any adjacent (antiparallel) bond: `‚àë œÑ, Œ¶_N√©el(œÑ) ¬∑ basisVec(swap)(œÑ) = 0`. | `neelChainState_inner_spinHalfDot_adjacent_eq_neg_one_quarter` | the per-adjacent-bond expectation `‚ü®Œ¶_N√©el, ≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_y ¬∑ Œ¶_N√©el‚ü© = -1/4` (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 (2.5.4) ingredient at S = 1/2). | `inner_basisVec_spinHalfDot_basisVec_antiparallel` | generic lemma: for any antiparallel `(x, y)` configuration `œÉ`, `‚ü®basisVec œÉ, ≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_y ¬∑ basisVec œÉ‚ü© = -1/4`. The 1-line foundation for every N√©el-bond expectation | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `inner_basisVec_spinHalfDot_basisVec_parallel` | parallel companion: for `œÉ x = œÉ y` (and `x ‚âÝ y`), `‚ü®basisVec œÉ, ≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_y ¬∑ basisVec œÉ‚ü© = +1/4`. Both basis vectors at the parallel pair are eigenvectors of `≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_y` (eigenvalue `+1/4`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `neelChainState_inner_spinHalfDot_parallel_eq_one_quarter` | N√©el chain same-sublattice (parallel) bond expectation `+1/4`: for any `x ‚âÝ y` with `x.val % 2 = y.val % 2` (e.g., `(0, 2)`, `(1, 3)`), `‚ü®Œ¶_N√©el, ≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_y ¬∑ Œ¶_N√©el‚ü© = +1/4` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `onSite_spinHalfOp3_mul_onSite_spinHalfOp3_mulVec_basisVec` | `(≈ú^(3)_x ¬∑ ≈ú^(3)_y) ¬∑ basisVec œÉ = (spinHalfSign œÉ x ¬∑ spinHalfSign œÉ y) ¬∑ basisVec œÉ`: every basis vector is an eigenvector of the diagonal `≈ú^z¬∑≈ú^z` correlator. | `spinHalfSign_mul_antiparallel` | for antiparallel `s ‚âÝ t : Fin 2`, `spinHalfSign s ¬∑ spinHalfSign t = -(1/4)`. Made public in PR #332 to power the generic `inner_neelStateOf_szsz_neelStateOf_antiparallel = -(1/4)` N√©el correlator | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `inner_basisVec_spinHalfDot_sub_szsz_basisVec_antiparallel` | generic off-diagonal correlator: for any antiparallel `(x, y)` configuration `œÉ`, `‚ü®basisVec œÉ, (≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_y - ≈ú^(3)_x ¬∑ ≈ú^(3)_y) ¬∑ basisVec œÉ‚ü© = 0`. | `neelChainState_inner_off_diagonal_correlator_adjacent_eq_zero` | the per-adjacent-bond off-diagonal correlator on the N√©el chain vanishes: `‚ü®Œ¶_N√©el, (≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_y - ≈ú^(3)_x ¬∑ ≈ú^(3)_y) ¬∑ Œ¶_N√©el‚ü© = 0`. Direct application of the generic helper | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelChainState_inner_szsz_adjacent_eq_neg_one_quarter` | per-adjacent-bond `≈ú^(3)_x ¬∑ ≈ú^(3)_y` correlation on the N√©el chain: `-1/4`. | `neelChainState_inner_szsz_wrap_eq_neg_one_quarter` | 1D N√©el periodic chain: per-wrap-bond `≈ú^(3)_x ¬∑ ≈ú^(3)_y` correlation `-1/4` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelSquareState_inner_szsz_{horizontal,vertical}_{adjacent,wrap}_eq_neg_one_quarter` | 2D N√©el: per-bond `≈ú^(3)¬∑≈ú^(3)` correlation `-1/4` for every horizontal / vertical adjacent and wrap bond | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelCubicState_inner_szsz_{x,y,z}_{adjacent,wrap}_eq_neg_one_quarter` | 3D N√©el: per-bond `≈ú^(3)¬∑≈ú^(3)` correlation `-1/4` for every x / y / z adjacent and wrap bond. Completes the `≈ú^z¬∑≈ú^z` correlation coverage parity with the full `≈ú¬∑≈ú` family from #273 | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelChainState_inner_spinHalfDot_wrap_eq_neg_one_quarter` | 1D wrap-bond expectation `-1/4` on the periodic N√©el chain `Fin (2(K+1))` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |

| `neelCubicState_inner_spinHalfDot_{x,y,z}_adjacent_eq_neg_one_quarter` / `_{x,y,z}_wrap_` | 3D N√©el: per-bond expectation `-1/4` for every x / y / z adjacent and wrap bond. | `neelChainState_energy_expectation_K1` | `K = 1` (2-site) open-chain Heisenberg energy expectation `‚ü®Œ¶_N√©el, H_open ¬∑ Œ¶_N√©el‚ü© = J/2`. | `neelConfigOf` / `neelStateOf` | generic graph-centric N√©el state from a sublattice indicator `A : V ‚Üí Bool`: `neelConfigOf A x := if A x then ‚Üë else ‚Üì` and `neelStateOf A := basisVec (neelConfigOf A)`. The chain / 2D / 3D `neelXyzConfig` / `neelXyzState` definitions are bridged via `_eq_neelConfigOf` / `_eq_neelStateOf`. Tasaki ¬ß2.5 eq. (2.5.2) graph-centric form | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_neelStateOf_antiparallel` | generic per-bond `≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_y` action on the canonical N√©el state: for any `x ‚âÝ y` with `A x ‚âÝ A y`, `≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_y ¬∑ Œ¶_N√©el(A) = (1/2) ¬∑ |swap_{x, y} Œ¶_N√©el(A)‚ü© - (1/4) ¬∑ Œ¶_N√©el(A)`. Tasaki ¬ß2.5 eq. (2.5.3) graph-centric form. The chain / 2D / 3D `_adjacent` / `_wrap` bond actions are 1-line corollaries via the `_eq_neelStateOf` bridges | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `inner_neelStateOf_spinHalfDot_neelStateOf_antiparallel` | generic per-bond `≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_y` expectation on the canonical N√©el state: for any `x ‚âÝ y` with `A x ‚âÝ A y`, `‚ü®Œ¶_N√©el(A), ≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_y ¬∑ Œ¶_N√©el(A)‚ü© = -(1/4)`. Tasaki ¬ß2.5 (2.5.4) ingredient (graph-centric form). The chain / 2D / 3D `_eq_neg_one_quarter` companions reduce to this via the `_eq_neelStateOf` bridges | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `inner_neelStateOf_szsz_neelStateOf_antiparallel` | generic per-bond `≈ú^z_x ¬∑ ≈ú^z_y` correlation on the canonical N√©el state: for any `A x ‚âÝ A y`, `‚ü®Œ¶_N√©el(A), ≈ú^z_x ¬∑ ≈ú^z_y ¬∑ Œ¶_N√©el(A)‚ü© = -(1/4)`. Diagonal half of Tasaki ¬ß2.5 (2.5.4) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf` | generic graph-centric Marshall sign `‚àè_{x ‚àà A} (-1)^(œÉ x)` for any finite vertex type `V`, sublattice indicator `A : V ‚Üí Bool`, and configuration `œÉ : V ‚Üí Fin 2`. Aligns with the project-wide graph-centric design (CLAUDE.local.md) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf_const_zero` | for any sublattice indicator `A`, the all-up Marshall sign is `marshallSignOf A (const 0) = 1`. Generic counterpart of `marshallSignChainConfig_const_zero` etc.; those are now 1-line corollaries via the `_eq_marshallSignOf` bridges | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig` / `marshallSignChainConfig_neelChainConfig` | the Marshall sign `(-1)^(N_A^‚Üì)` for spin-1/2 configurations on the parity-coloured chain `Fin (2K)`, encoded as `‚àè_{x even} (-1)^(œÉ x)`; specialisation to the N√©el configuration gives sign `+1` (no down spins on sublattice `A`). Foundational definition for the Marshall basis change underpinning the Marshall-Lieb-Mattis theorem (Tasaki ¬ß2.5). **Deprecated** as of 2026-04-22 in favour of the generic `marshallSignOf` (the chain / 2D / 3D Marshall sign defs are kept for backward compatibility but new code should prefer the generic form) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_eq_marshallSignOf` / `_square_` / `_cubic_` | the chain / 2D / 3D parity-coloured Marshall signs are precisely `marshallSignOf` instantiated at the corresponding parity colouring | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignSquareConfig` / `marshallSignSquareConfig_neelSquareConfig` | 2D analogue: Marshall sign `‚àè_{(i,j) with i+j even} (-1)^(œÉ (i,j))` on `Fin (2K) √ó Fin (2L)`; equals `+1` on the 2D checkerboard N√©el configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignCubicConfig` / `marshallSignCubicConfig_neelCubicConfig` | 3D analogue: Marshall sign `‚àè_{((i,j),k) with i+j+k even} (-1)^(œÉ ((i,j),k))` on `(Fin (2K) √ó Fin (2L)) √ó Fin (2M)`; equals `+1` on the 3D cubic checkerboard N√©el configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_const_zero` / `_const_one` | Marshall sign on the all-up / all-down chain configurations: `marshallSignChainConfig K (const 0) = 1` and `marshallSignChainConfig K (const 1) = (-1)^K` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignSquareConfig_const_zero` / `_const_one` | 2D Marshall sign on the all-up / all-down checkerboard: both equal `+1` (the all-down case has `2KL` down spins on `A`, so `(-1)^(2KL) = 1`) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignCubicConfig_const_zero` / `_const_one` | 3D Marshall sign on the all-up / all-down cubic: both equal `+1` (the all-down case has `4KLM` down spins on `A`, so `(-1)^(4KLM) = 1`) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_flipConfig` | Marshall sign under the global spin-flip on the chain: `marshallSignChainConfig K (flipConfig œÉ) = (-1)^K ¬∑ marshallSignChainConfig K œÉ`. Each of the K even-indexed sites contributes `-1`. Proof: `Finset.prod_mul_distrib` + helper `prod_alternating_neg_one` collapses the contributing factor product to `(-1)^K`, with the per-site identity `(-1)^((1-s).val) = (-1)¬∑(-1)^(s.val)` closed by `fin_cases` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignSquareConfig_flipConfig` / `marshallSignCubicConfig_flipConfig` | 2D / 3D Marshall sign invariant under the global spin-flip (the contributing factor product `(-1)^(2KL)` resp. `(-1)^(4KLM)` equals `+1` for all K, L, M) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallChainState` / `_neelChainConfig` | Marshall-rotated chain basis state `marshallSignChainConfig K œÉ ‚Ä¢ basisVec œÉ`; specialisation at the N√©el configuration coincides with `neelChainState K` (since the Marshall sign of the N√©el state is `+1`) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSquareState` / `_neelSquareConfig` | 2D Marshall-rotated checkerboard state; coincides with `neelSquareState K L` at the N√©el configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallCubicState` / `_neelCubicConfig` | 3D cubic Marshall-rotated checkerboard state; coincides with `neelCubicState K L M` at the N√©el configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_flipConfig_neelChainConfig` / `_square_` / `_cubic_` | Marshall sign on the *flipped* N√©el configuration: `(-1)^K` (1D), `+1` (2D), `+1` (3D) ‚Äî direct compositions of `_flipConfig` and `_neelChainConfig` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallChainState_flipConfig_eq_timeReversalSpinHalfMulti` / `_square_` / `_cubic_` | the Marshall-rotated *flipped* N√©el state coincides with the time-reversed N√©el state in 1D, 2D, 3D ‚Äî both sides equal the same explicit `(-1)^K` (1D) or `+1` (2D, 3D) scaled basis vector. Establishes a direct bridge between the Marshall basis change (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 / Marshall-Lieb-Mattis) and the time-reversal operator (Tasaki ¬ß2.3) on the N√©el ground-state ansatz | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallDressedBasis A œÉ` | **Marshall-dressed standard basis state** `:= marshallSignOf A œÉ ‚Ä¢ basisVec œÉ` on a generic finite vertex type `V` with sublattice indicator `A : V ‚Üí Bool` (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 eq. (2.5.8), p. 41). The dressing produces a basis in which the spin-1/2 antiferromagnetic Heisenberg Hamiltonian on a connected bipartite graph has all off-diagonal matrix elements `‚â§ 0` (Marshall sign trick), the input to the Perron‚ÄìFrobenius proof of the MLM theorem | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallDressedBasis_self` / `_of_ne` / `_apply` | pointwise rules: `Œ®ÃÉ^œÉ œÉ = marshallSignOf A œÉ`; `Œ®ÃÉ^œÉ œÑ = 0` for `œÑ ‚âÝ œÉ`; explicit `Œ®ÃÉ^œÉ œÑ = marshallSignOf A œÉ ¬∑ basisVec œÉ œÑ` | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf_sq_eq_one` | each factor of `marshallSignOf` is `¬±1`, so the sign squares to `1`: `(marshallSignOf A œÉ)¬≤ = 1` | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallDressedBasis_inner` | orthonormality of the Marshall-dressed basis under the real bilinear pairing: `Œ£_œÑ Œ®ÃÉ^œÉ œÑ ¬∑ Œ®ÃÉ^œÅ œÑ = if œÅ = œÉ then 1 else 0` (combines `basisVec_inner` with `marshallSignOf_sq_eq_one`) | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallDressedBasis_mem_magnetizationSubspace` / `_zero` | the dressed basis state lies in the same magnetisation-`M` subspace `H_M = H_{œÉÃÑ/2}` as the underlying `basisVec œÉ` (Tasaki eq. (2.2.10)); the `_zero` specialisation places it in `H_0` when `Œ£_x œÉ_x = 0` | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_apply_im_eq_zero` | every matrix entry of the two-site spin product `≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_y` is **real**: `((spinHalfDot x y) œÉ œÉ').im = 0` for all `x, y, œÉ, œÉ'`. Case analysis on `x = y` / parallel / antiparallel via the existing `spinHalfDot_mulVec_basisVec_{parallel,antiparallel}` action lemmas. **Property (i) ingredient** for the Marshall‚ÄìLieb‚ÄìMattis theorem (Tasaki ¬ß2.5, p. 41) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Realness.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_apply_im_eq_zero` | for **real** coupling `J : Œõ ‚Üí Œõ ‚Üí ‚ÑÇ` (`(J x y).im = 0` for all `x, y`), every matrix entry of the Heisenberg Hamiltonian `H = Œ£_{x,y} J(x,y) ¬∑ spinHalfDot x y` is real: `((heisenbergHamiltonian J) œÉ œÉ').im = 0`. ‚Ñù-linearity + `spinHalfDot_apply_im_eq_zero` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Realness.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf_im_eq_zero` | the Marshall sign `marshallSignOf A œÉ` is **real**: `(marshallSignOf A œÉ).im = 0`. | `dot_marshallDressed_heisenbergHamiltonian_marshallDressed_im_eq_zero` | **MLM Property (i)**: for real coupling `J`, the dressed Heisenberg bilinear pairing `Œ£_œÑ \|Œ®ÃÉ^œÉ‚ü© œÑ ¬∑ (H ¬∑ \|Œ®ÃÉ^{œÉ'}‚ü©) œÑ` is real (Tasaki ¬ß2.5, p. 41 in the proof of Theorem 2.2). | `dot_marshallDressed_mulVec_marshallDressed_eq` | for any operator `M`, the dressed bilinear pairing factorises: `Œ£_œÑ \|Œ®ÃÉ^œÉ‚ü© œÑ ¬∑ (M ¬∑ \|Œ®ÃÉ^{œÉ'}‚ü©) œÑ = marshallSignOf A œÉ ¬∑ marshallSignOf A œÉ' ¬∑ M œÉ œÉ'`. Generalises the inner-product computation used in Property (i) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MarshallSignTrick.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf_mul_marshallSignOf_basisSwap_of_bipartite_antiparallel` | **Marshall sign relation**: for a bond `{x, y}` crossing the bipartition (`A x ‚âÝ A y`) with `œÉ` antiparallel at `{x, y}` (`œÉ x ‚âÝ œÉ y`), `marshallSignOf A œÉ * marshallSignOf A (basisSwap œÉ x y) = -1`. The combined product over `Œõ` of pairwise factors collapses: outside `{x, y}` each pairwise factor is `(¬±1)¬≤ = 1`; at the unique site in `A ‚à© {x, y}` the pair contributes `(-1)^(œÉ x + œÉ y) = -1` since `œÉ x ‚âÝ œÉ y`; the other site of `{x, y}` lies outside `A` and contributes `1` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MarshallSignTrick.lean` |
| `bond_dressed_contribution_re_nonpos` | per-bond non-positivity: for `œÉ ‚âÝ œÉ'` and any bond `(x, y)` with real non-negative `J(x, y)` supported on bipartite bonds, the contribution `marshallSignOf A œÉ ¬∑ marshallSignOf A œÉ' ¬∑ J(x,y) ¬∑ (spinHalfDot x y) œÉ œÉ'` to the dressed off-diagonal element has non-positive real part. Case analysis on `(spinHalfDot x y) œÉ œÉ'` (zero off-diagonal except at `œÉ = basisSwap œÉ' x y`, antiparallel œÉ', `x ‚âÝ y`) combined with the Marshall sign relation | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MarshallSignTrick.lean` |
| `dot_marshallDressed_heisenbergHamiltonian_marshallDressed_re_nonpos_of_ne` | **MLM Property (ii)** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5, p. 41): for real non-negative `J` supported on bipartite bonds and `œÉ ‚âÝ œÉ'`, the dressed off-diagonal Heisenberg pairing `Œ£_œÑ \|Œ®ÃÉ^œÉ‚ü© œÑ ¬∑ (H ¬∑ \|Œ®ÃÉ^{œÉ'}‚ü©) œÑ` has non-positive real part. Sum bond-by-bond using `bond_dressed_contribution_re_nonpos`. | `SwapStep`, `SwapReachable` | one-step swap relation `œÉ ‚Ü¶ basisSwap œÉ x y` along a graph edge `(x, y)` with `œÉ x ‚âÝ œÉ y`; reflexive transitive closure for multi-step reachability | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Connectivity.lean` |
| `swapReachable_of_walk_of_ne` | for any `G`-walk from `x` to `y` and `œÉ x ‚âÝ œÉ y`, `SwapReachable G œÉ (basisSwap œÉ x y)`. Walk induction with case analysis on `œÉ z` at intermediate vertex (Tasaki p. 41 "Proof of Property (iii)" Lemma) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Connectivity.lean` |
| `swapReachable_of_reachable_of_ne` / `_preconnected_` | for any `x, y` reachable in `G` (or any `x, y` if `G` preconnected) with `œÉ x ‚âÝ œÉ y`, the swap is reachable. **MLM Property (iii) ingredient** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 p. 41) ‚Äî combined with iteration over the magnetisation-difference, gives Perron‚ÄìFrobenius irreducibility on `H_M` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Connectivity.lean` |
| `H‚ÇÄIndex Œõ` | index type `{œÉ : Œõ ‚Üí Fin 2 // magnetization Œõ œÉ = 0}` for the zero-magnetisation subspace `H_0`; `Fintype` and `DecidableEq` instances | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0` | real-valued matrix on `H‚ÇÄIndex Œõ` with entries `Re (marshallSignOf A œÉ ¬∑ marshallSignOf A œÑ ¬∑ (H_J)_{œÉ,œÑ})` ‚Äî the matrix to which Tasaki's Perron‚ÄìFrobenius proof of MLM applies | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0_isSymm` | the matrix is **symmetric** for real symmetric `J` (Hermiticity of Heisenberg + realness of entries) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0_offdiag_nonpos` | **off-diagonal entries are non-positive** for real non-negative bipartite `J` and distinct `œÉ ‚âÝ œÑ`, packaged from PR Œ±-3's Property (ii) via `dot_marshallDressed_mulVec_marshallDressed_eq` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `magnetization_basisSwap` | `basisSwap œÉ x y` preserves total magnetisation. Key ingredient for Tasaki ¬ß2.5 p. 42 Proposition (equal-magnetisation reachability) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetization.lean` |
| `disagreementSet` / `configDist` | the set / count of sites where `œÉ` and `œÉ'` disagree; `configDist_eq_zero_iff` characterises configuration equality | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `exists_swap_pair_of_eq_magnetization` | for `œÉ ‚âÝ œÉ'` with equal magnetisation, there exist sites `x` (with `œÉ x = 0, œÉ' x = 1`) and `y` (with `œÉ y = 1, œÉ' y = 0`). Pigeonhole/cardinality argument: the `(0, 1)`-disagreement and `(1, 0)`-disagreement sets have equal cardinality from magnetisation equality, and the disagreement set is non-empty for `œÉ ‚âÝ œÉ'` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `configDist_basisSwap_lt` | swapping at sites `x ‚àà D01`, `y ‚àà D10` strictly decreases the configuration distance to `œÉ'`. The disagreement set strictly shrinks (`x` newly agrees with `œÉ'` after swap) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `swapReachable_of_eq_magnetization` | **Tasaki ¬ß2.5 p. 42 Proposition**: any two configurations `œÉ`, `œÉ'` with the same total magnetisation are connected by a chain of single-edge bond swaps, on a connected graph. Strong induction on `configDist`, reducing by `‚â• 2` per step via the swap pair from `exists_swap_pair_of_eq_magnetization`. **Final ingredient** for Perron‚ÄìFrobenius irreducibility on `H_M` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted` | the shifted matrix `B := c¬∑I ‚àí M` on `H‚ÇÄIndex Œõ`. Used as input to Perron‚ÄìFrobenius: `B` is symmetric, has non-negative off-diagonal (sign flip of `M`'s non-positive off-diagonal), and non-negative diagonal when `c ‚â• M œÉ œÉ` for all `œÉ`. The maximum eigenvalue of `B` corresponds to the minimum eigenvalue of `M` (the H_0 ground state of the AFM Heisenberg) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Shifted.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isSymm` / `_nonneg` (`_offdiag_nonneg`, `_diag_nonneg`) | symmetry and (off-diagonal / full) non-negativity of `B` under the appropriate hypotheses on `J` and `c` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Shifted.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_apply_basisSwap` | the off-diagonal matrix entry `(spinHalfDot x y) œÉ (basisSwap œÉ x y) = 1/2` for `x ‚âÝ y` and antiparallel `œÉ_x ‚âÝ œÉ_y`. | `basisSwap_basisSwap_ne_self_of_ne_bond` | combinatorial helper: for `x ‚âÝ y`, `œÉ_x ‚âÝ œÉ_y`, and `(u, v) ‚àâ {(x, y), (y, x)}`, the configuration `basisSwap (basisSwap œÉ x y) u v ‚âÝ œÉ`. Site analysis: `œÉ` and `œÉ' = basisSwap œÉ x y` differ at exactly `{x, y}`, so for the iterated swap to return to `œÉ`, the swap sites `{u, v}` must coincide with `{x, y}`. | `spinHalfDot_apply_basisSwap_off_bond_eq_zero` | for `œÉ' = basisSwap œÉ x y` (with `x ‚âÝ y`, `œÉ_x ‚âÝ œÉ_y`) and any `(u, v) ‚àâ {(x, y), (y, x)}`, the matrix entry `(spinHalfDot u v) œÉ œÉ' = 0`. Three cases: `u = v` (diagonal), `u ‚âÝ v` parallel œÉ' (constant action), `u ‚âÝ v` antiparallel + off-bond (combinatorial helper) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SpinDotOffBond.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_apply_basisSwap` | the Heisenberg matrix entry on swap-related configurations: `(heisenbergHamiltonian J) œÉ (basisSwap œÉ x y) = (J x y + J y x) / 2`. | `dressed_pairing_basisSwap_eq` / `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0_apply_basisSwap` | the dressed Heisenberg matrix entry on swap-related H_0 configurations: complex-level value `-J(x, y)` (Marshall sign trick √ó Heisenberg formula √ó symmetric `J`), real-part value `-(J x y).re`. | `dressedHeisenbergShifted_pos_of_basisSwap` | strict positivity `0 < B œÉ œÑ` on swap-related H_0 configurations with positive symmetric bipartite `J`.
