Single-step strict positivity for Perron‚ÄìFrobenius irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0ShiftedSwap.lean` |
| `matrix_pow_succ_pos_of_path` | generic matrix-power positivity from a positive path: for non-negative matrix `B` and a path `p_0, ..., p_{k+1}` with `B(p_i, p_{i+1}) > 0` on every consecutive pair, `(B^(k+1))(p_0)(p_{k+1}) > 0`. Induction on `k` using `pow_succ` + `Finset.sum_pos'`. Used to lift single-step swap positivity (Œ±-5j) to multi-step matrix-power positivity for PF irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MatrixPowPath.lean` |
| `matrix_pow_succ_pos_of_pow_pos_step` | one-step extension: if `(B^m) œÉ œÑ > 0` and `B œÑ œÅ > 0` for non-negative `B`, then `(B^(m+1)) œÉ œÅ > 0`. Inductive building block for ReflTransGen-style matrix-power lifting | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MatrixPowExtend.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_pow_pos_of_swapReachable` | for `œÉ : H‚ÇÄIndex Œõ` and any `Œæ` with `Relation.ReflTransGen (SwapStep G) œÉ.val Œæ`, there exists `m` with `(B^m) œÉ ‚ü®Œæ, h_mag‚ü© > 0`. Induction on `ReflTransGen`: refl gives `m = 0`, tail extends by one swap using Œ±-5j (single-step swap positivity) and Œ±-5l (one-step matrix-power extension). Key bridge from combinatorial reachability to PF irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0ShiftedReachable.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isIrreducible` | **`B = c ¬∑ I ‚àí M` is irreducible** on `H_0` for connected bipartite `G` with positive symmetric real coupling supported on `G`-edges and shift constant `c > M œÉ œÉ` (strict). Cases on `œÉ = œÑ` (use diagonal positivity) vs `œÉ ‚âÝ œÑ` (use Œ±-5c reachability + Œ±-5m matrix-power lift). Final input for Perron‚ÄìFrobenius application | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0ShiftedIrreducible.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isHermitian` | the shifted matrix is Hermitian (= symmetric for real matrices). Wraps `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isSymm` (PR Œ±-5d) into the IsHermitian form needed by Perron‚ÄìFrobenius | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0PFApplication.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_exists_pos_eigenvec_max` / `_pos_eigenvec_unique` | **Perron‚ÄìFrobenius applied to `B = c ¬∑ I ‚àí M` on `H_0`**: existence of a strictly positive eigenvector `v` for some real eigenvalue `Œº`, and uniqueness up to positive scalar. Translating back to `M`, `v` is the eigenvector for the **minimum** eigenvalue (the H_0 ground state of the AFM Heisenberg). This is the matrix-level Tasaki (2.5.4): the H_0 ground-state expansion `Œ£_œÉ c_œÉ \|Œ®ÃÉ^œÉ‚ü©` with `c_œÉ = v œÉ > 0` is unique up to positive scalar | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0PFApplication.lean` |
| `bipartiteCoupling` / `heisenbergToyHamiltonian` | the Tasaki ¬ß2.5 p. 40 toy Hamiltonian setup: `bipartiteCoupling A x y := if A x ‚âÝ A y then 1 else 0` (the unnormalised bipartite coupling), and `heisenbergToyHamiltonian A := heisenbergHamiltonian (bipartiteCoupling A)`. Real symmetric, non-negative, supported on bipartite bonds, positive on inter-sublattice pairs. Hermitian. Used in subsequent PRs to derive `S_tot = 0` for the AFM Heisenberg ground state via inner-product comparison | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonian.lean` |
| `bipartiteGraphFromA` | the complete bipartite graph on `Œõ` from sublattice indicator `A : Œõ ‚Üí Bool`: vertices `x, y` are adjacent iff `A x ‚âÝ A y`. The natural bond graph for the toy Hamiltonian (every edge of `bipartiteCoupling A` is a `bipartiteGraphFromA A`-edge and vice versa) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/BipartiteGraph.lean` |
| `bipartiteGraphFromA_preconnected` | `bipartiteGraphFromA A` is preconnected when both sublattices are non-empty. Cases on `A x = A y` (length-2 path via opposite sublattice) vs `A x ‚âÝ A y` (direct edge). Provides the `G.Preconnected` hypothesis needed for MLM application to the toy Hamiltonian | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/BipartiteGraph.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_toy_exists_pos_eigenvec_max` / `_pos_eigenvec_unique` | **Matrix-level Tasaki (2.5.4) for the toy Hamiltonian**: the shifted toy matrix `B_toy = c ¬∑ I ‚àí M_toy` (under both-sublattices-nonempty + diagonal-shift hypothesis) has a unique-up-to-positive-scalar strictly positive eigenvector. Specialises Œ±-5o to the toy via Œ±-6a + Œ±-6b | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyPF.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfOp1/2/3` | sublattice spin operators `≈ú_A^(Œ±) := Œ£_{x ‚àà A} onSite x ≈ú^(Œ±)` for `Œ± ‚àà {1, 2, 3}`. Foundation for the Casimir identity `ƒ§_toy = (1/(2|Œõ|))((≈ú_tot)¬≤ ‚àí (≈ú_A)¬≤ ‚àí (≈ú_B)¬≤)` (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 (2.5.11)) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinCore.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinSOp1/2/3` / `totalSpinSOp{1,2,3}_eq_sublattice_sum` | **Spin-`S` analogues**: `≈ú_A^(Œ±) := Œ£_{x : A x} onSiteS x (spinSOp_Œ± N)` for `Œ± ‚àà {1, 2, 3}`, with decomposition `≈ú_tot^(Œ±) = ≈ú_A^(Œ±) + ≈ú_¬¨A^(Œ±)`. | `sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_isHermitian` | Spin-`S` sublattice operator Hermiticity (Œ≥-4 step 2). Sum of Hermitian summands is Hermitian | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1043) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS` / `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_isHermitian` | spin-`S` sublattice Casimir `(≈ú_A)¬≤ := Œ£_Œ± (≈ú_A^(Œ±))¬≤` plus Hermiticity. Foundation for the Casimir identity in Tasaki ¬ß2.5 (2.5.11) at general spin-S (Œ≥-4 step 3) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1044) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_cross_commute` | spin-`S` cross-sublattice same-axis commutativity `Commute (≈ú_A^(Œ±)) (≈ú_¬¨A^(Œ±))` for Œ± ‚àà {1, 2, 3}. Sites in A vs ¬¨A are distinct, so per-site operators commute via `onSiteS_commute_of_ne` (Œ≥-4 step 4) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1045) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOpGeneric_cross_commute` / `sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_cross_commute_op{1,2,3}` | spin-`S` mixed-axes cross-sublattice commutativity: `Commute (≈ú_A^(Œ±)) (≈ú_¬¨A^(Œ≤))` for any Œ±, Œ≤ ‚àà {1, 2, 3}. | `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_cross_commute` | spin-`S` sublattice Casimir cross-commute: `Commute (≈ú_A)¬≤ (≈ú_¬¨A)¬≤`. | `sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_commutator_sublatticeSpinSOp{2,3,1}` | spin-`S` sublattice SU(2) algebra: `[≈ú_A^Œ±, ≈ú_A^Œ≤] = i Œµ^Œ±Œ≤Œ≥ ≈ú_A^Œ≥`. | `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_commutator_sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}` / `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_commute_sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}` | spin-`S` sublattice Casimir self-invariance: `[(≈ú_A)¬≤, ≈ú_A^(Œ±)] = 0`, equivalently `Commute (≈ú_A)¬≤ ≈ú_A^(Œ±)`. | `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_commute_totalSpinSOp{1,2,3}` | spin-`S` sublattice Casimir vs total spin axis-Œ±: `Commute (≈ú_A)¬≤ ≈ú_tot^(Œ±)` for Œ± ‚àà {1, 2, 3}. | `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_commute_totalSpinSSquared` | spin-`S` sublattice Casimir vs total Casimir: `Commute (≈ú_A)¬≤ (≈ú_tot)¬≤`. | `sublatticeSpinSDot` / `sublatticeSpinSDot_def` / `sublatticeSpinSDot_eq_sum_sum` / `sublatticeSpinSDot_complement_isHermitian` | spin-`S` cross-sublattice spin dot product `≈ú_A ¬∑ ≈ú_B := Œ£_Œ± ≈ú_A^(Œ±) ≈ú_B^(Œ±)`, definitional unfolding, bilinear expansion `≈ú_A ¬∑ ≈ú_B = Œ£_{x : A x} Œ£_{y : B y} ≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_y`, and Hermiticity for `B = ¬¨A` (each axis-Œ± summand is a product of two commuting Hermitian operators) (Œ≥-4 step 13) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpinDot.lean` (PR #1054) |
| `sublatticeSpinSDot_complement_comm` / `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_eq_sublatticeSpinSDot_sum` / `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_eq_two_sublatticeSpinSDot` | spin-`S` toy Hamiltonian decomposes as oriented cross-sublattice spin dot products: `ƒ§_toy_S = ≈ú_A ¬∑ ≈ú_¬¨A + ≈ú_¬¨A ¬∑ ≈ú_A`, and via cross-sublattice symmetry the closed form `ƒ§_toy_S = 2 ‚Ä¢ ≈ú_A ¬∑ ≈ú_¬¨A`. | `totalSpinSSquared_eq_sublattice_casimir` / `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_eq_casimir_diff` | **spin-`S` Casimir identity** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 (2.5.11)): `(≈ú_tot)¬≤ = (≈ú_A)¬≤ + 2 ‚Ä¢ (≈ú_A ¬∑ ≈ú_¬¨A) + (≈ú_¬¨A)¬≤` (per-axis `(a + b)¬≤ = a¬≤ + 2ab + b¬≤` via cross-commute), and the closed form (without `1/\|Œõ\|`) `ƒ§_toy_S = (≈ú_tot)¬≤ ‚àí (≈ú_A)¬≤ ‚àí (≈ú_¬¨A)¬≤` (Œ≥-4 step 15) | `Quantum/SpinS/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` (PR #1056) |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_commute_totalSpinSSquared` / `_commute_sublatticeSpinSquaredS` / `_complement` | spin-`S` toy Hamiltonian commutes with the three Casimirs `(≈ú_tot)¬≤`, `(≈ú_A)¬≤`, `(≈ú_¬¨A)¬≤`. | `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_mulVec_of_jointCasimirEigenvector` / `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_via_joint` | **spin-`S` toy Hamiltonian eigenvalue on simultaneous Casimir eigenvectors**: for `v` a joint eigenvector of `(≈ú_tot)¬≤`, `(≈ú_A)¬≤`, `(≈ú_¬¨A)¬≤` at eigenvalues `Œ±, Œ≤_A, Œ≤_B`, `ƒ§_toy_S ¬∑ v = (Œ± ‚àí Œ≤_A ‚àí Œ≤_B) ‚Ä¢ v` and `‚ü®v, ƒ§_toy_S v‚ü© = (Œ± ‚àí Œ≤_A ‚àí Œ≤_B) ¬∑ ‚ü®v, v‚ü©`. | `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_jointCasimirEigenspace_le_eigenspace` | **subspace-level form of PR #2774**: the meet of the three Casimir eigenspaces at `(Œ±, Œ≤_A, Œ≤_B)` is contained in the `ƒ§_toy_S`-eigenspace at `(Œ± ‚àí Œ≤_A ‚àí Œ≤_B)`, i.e. `eigenspace(≈ú_tot¬≤)_Œ± ‚äì eigenspace(≈ú_A¬≤)_Œ≤_A ‚äì eigenspace(≈ú_¬¨A¬≤)_Œ≤_B ‚â§ eigenspace(ƒ§_toy_S)_{Œ±‚àíŒ≤_A‚àíŒ≤_B}`. | `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_eq_total_minus_sublattice_casimirs` | **N√©el toy-Hamiltonian expectation via Casimir decomposition**: `‚ü®Œ¶_N√©el \| ƒ§_toy_S \| Œ¶_N√©el‚ü© = ‚ü®Œ¶_N√©el \| (≈ú_tot)¬≤ \| Œ¶_N√©el‚ü© ‚àí s_A(s_A+1) ¬∑ ‚ü®Œ¶_N√©el,Œ¶_N√©el‚ü© ‚àí s_B(s_B+1) ¬∑ ‚ü®Œ¶_N√©el,Œ¶_N√©el‚ü©`, where `s_A = \|A\|¬∑N/2, s_B = \|¬¨A\|¬∑N/2`. Uses PR #1056's closed form `ƒ§_toy_S = (≈ú_tot)¬≤ ‚àí (≈ú_A)¬≤ ‚àí (≈ú_¬¨A)¬≤` and PR #1067's sublattice-Casimir-on-N√©el eigenvector identities. Bridges PR #2774's joint-Casimir framework to the existing direct expectation `‚àí\|A\|¬∑\|¬¨A\|¬∑N¬≤/2` (PR #1070); useful for subsequent Œ≥-4 variational comparisons against the predicted toy-Hamiltonian ground state (PR #2776, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelToyHamiltonianViaCasimir.lean`) |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_apply_diag_neel_complement` / `neelStateOfS_complement_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation` | **complement-orientation N√©el toy-Hamiltonian expectation**: `‚ü®Œ¶_N√©el(¬¨A) \| ƒ§_toy_S(A) \| Œ¶_N√©el(¬¨A)‚ü© = ‚àí\|A\|¬∑\|¬¨A\|¬∑N¬≤/2`. Same value as the original-orientation expectation (PR #1093): the toy Hamiltonian is sublattice-swap invariant (via `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_eq_two_sublatticeSpinSDot` + `sublatticeSpinSDot_complement_comm`), and the complement N√©el basis configuration produces the same per-bond `m_x ¬∑ m_y = -N¬≤/4` contribution. Completes the orientation-pair (`Œ¶_N√©el(A)` for `\|A\| ‚â• \|¬¨A\|`, `Œ¶_N√©el(¬¨A)` for `\|¬¨A\| ‚â• \|A\|`) at the same toy-Hamiltonian value (PR #2777, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelToyComplementExpectation.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted` / `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_eq_simplified` / `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted` / `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_le_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_eigenspace` | **predicted toy-Hamiltonian ground state for the bipartite \|A\| ‚â• \|¬¨A\| case** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3): definition `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted A N := (s_A ‚àí s_B)(s_A ‚àí s_B + 1) ‚àí s_A(s_A+1) ‚àí s_B(s_B+1)` with `s_A = \|A\|¬∑N/2, s_B = \|¬¨A\|¬∑N/2`; simplification to `‚àí\|A\|¬∑\|¬¨A\|¬∑N¬≤/2 ‚àí \|¬¨A\|¬∑N` via ring; definition `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted A N` as the joint eigenspace at the three target eigenvalues (sublattice Casimirs at maximum, total Casimir at the triangle-inequality minimum); subspace inclusion `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted ‚â§ eigenspace(ƒ§_toy_S)_{bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted}` via PR #2775's joint-Casimir-eigenspace inclusion. The minimality of `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted` as the actual `ƒ§_toy_S` ground-state eigenvalue (variational lower bound + sector non-emptiness) is the next Œ≥-4 milestone (PR #2778, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergy.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_eq_zero_of_cardNotA_zero` / `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_eq_balanced_form_of_card_eq` | **structural properties of `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted`**: (i) saturated edge case `\|¬¨A\| = 0` ‚Üí energy 0 (trivial empty-cross-bond limit); (ii) balanced edge case `\|A\| = \|¬¨A\|` ‚Üí energy collapses to `‚àí2 s_A(s_A+1)`, the anti-ferromagnetic-saturation value (PR #2779, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyProperties.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_sub_complement` | **complement-orientation gap identity**: `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted A N ‚àí bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted (¬¨A) N = (\|A\| ‚àí \|¬¨A\|)¬∑N`. Quantifies the orientation-asymmetry of the signed-difference formula in PR #2778: the true Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3 prediction is `min(E(A), E(¬¨A))`, achieved at the orientation `\|A\| ‚â• \|¬¨A\|` (or the complement orientation). Direct ring computation from PR #2779's simplified form via `(¬¨¬¨A) = A` at the filter level (PR #2780, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyComplement.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm` / `_eq_predicted_of_cardNotA_le_cardA` / `_eq_complement_predicted_of_cardA_le_cardNotA` / `_complement` | **sublattice-swap symmetric predicted min energy**: `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A N := ‚àí\|A\|¬∑\|¬¨A\|¬∑N¬≤/2 ‚àí min(\|A\|, \|¬¨A\|)¬∑N`, the true Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3 prediction independent of orientation. Bridges: equals `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted A N` when `\|¬¨A\| ‚â§ \|A\|`, equals `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted (¬¨A) N` when `\|A\| ‚â§ \|¬¨A\|`. Sublattice-swap invariant: `Symm (¬¨A) N = Symm A N` via `Nat.min_comm` + `(¬¨¬¨A) = A` (PR #2781, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergySymm.lean`) |
| `allAlignedStateS_zero_mem_bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_of_cardNotA_zero` | **non-emptiness of the predicted GS subspace in the saturated edge case**: for `\|¬¨A\| = 0`, the all-up state `|œÉ_‚ä§‚ü©` is in `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted A N`. This is the first concrete realisation of PR #2778's predicted ground-state subspace. Three eigenvector witnesses: `(≈ú_tot)¬≤ ¬∑ \|œÉ_‚ä§‚ü© = s_A(s_A+1) ¬∑ \|œÉ_‚ä§‚ü©` (PR #878 with `\|V\| = \|A\|`), `(≈ú_A)¬≤ ¬∑ \|œÉ_‚ä§‚ü© = s_A(s_A+1) ¬∑ \|œÉ_‚ä§‚ü©` (PR #1067), `(≈ú_¬¨A)¬≤ ¬∑ \|œÉ_‚ä§‚ü© = 0 ¬∑ \|œÉ_‚ä§‚ü©` (PR #1067 with `\|¬¨A\| = 0` collapsing the eigenvalue formula) (PR #2782, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyGroundStateSaturated.lean`) |
| `allAlignedStateS_last_mem_bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_of_cardNotA_zero` | **all-down version of PR #2782**: for `\|¬¨A\| = 0`, the all-down state `\|œÉ_‚ä•‚ü©` is also in `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted A N`. Mirror of PR #2782 using `_last` variants (`totalSpinSSquared_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_last_eigenvalue` PR #879, `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_last` PR #1067-mirror). Together with PR #2782, gives two distinct elements of the predicted GS subspace (for `N ‚â• 1`, where `\|œÉ_‚ä§‚ü© ‚âÝ \|œÉ_‚ä•‚ü©`; for `N = 0` the spin-`0` case collapses to a 1-dim space) ‚Äî and via the saturated-ferromagnet ladder, the entire (2 s_A + 1)-dim irrep is contained for any `N` (PR #2783, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyGroundStateSaturatedLast.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_eq_zero_of_card_zero` / `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_eq_zero_of_card_zero` / `sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_eq_totalSpinSOp{1,2,3}_of_cardNotA_zero` / `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_eq_totalSpinSSquared_of_cardNotA_zero` | **sublattice ‚Üî total spin identities at the saturated edge case**: when `\|¬¨A\| = 0`, the complement sublattice `¬¨A` is empty, so `≈ú_¬¨A^(Œ±) = 0` and `≈ú_¬¨A¬≤ = 0`, and the `A`-sublattice operators coincide with the total operators: `≈ú_A^(Œ±) = ≈ú_tot^(Œ±)` for `Œ± ‚àà {1,2,3}` and `≈ú_A¬≤ = ≈ú_tot¬≤`. Foundation for the dim-bound on the predicted GS subspace in the saturated case: `eigenspace(≈ú_tot¬≤)_Œ± ‚äÜ bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted A N` whenever `\|¬¨A\| = 0` and `Œ± = s_A(s_A+1)` (PR #2784, file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeEqTotalOfCardZero.lean`) |
| `totalSpinSSquaredEigenspace_le_bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_of_cardNotA_zero` | **eigenspace bridge for the saturated case**: when `\|¬¨A\| = 0`, `eigenspace((≈ú_tot)¬≤)_{s_A(s_A+1)} ‚äÜ bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted A N`. Proof: any `(≈ú_tot)¬≤`-eigenvector at `s_A(s_A+1)` automatically satisfies all three joint Casimir eigenspace conditions, since (i) `(≈ú_tot)¬≤` eigenvalue matches the predicted target `(s_A‚àí0)(s_A‚àí0+1)`, (ii) `≈ú_A¬≤ = ≈ú_tot¬≤` (PR #2784) gives the `(≈ú_A)¬≤` condition for free, (iii) `≈ú_¬¨A¬≤ = 0` trivially. Combined with PR #2768's `saturatedFerromagnetJointEigenspace = span(ladderIterateUp)` and finrank `2 m_max + 1`, this yields the dimension lower bound `finrank(predicted GS) ‚â• 2 m_max + 1 = 2 s_A + 1` at the saturated edge case, matching the Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3 prediction `2 S_tot + 1` (PR #2785, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyGSEigenspaceBridge.lean`) |
| `saturatedFerromagnetJointEigenspace_le_bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_of_cardNotA_zero` | **¬ß2.4 ‚Üî Œ≥-4 bridge at saturated case**: when `\|¬¨A\| = 0`, `saturatedFerromagnetJointEigenspace J N ‚äÜ bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted A N` for any choice of coupling `J`. Direct corollary of PR #2785's eigenspace bridge applied to the `(≈ú_tot)¬≤`-eigenspace component of the saturated FM joint eigenspace, with the identification `m_max = s_A` (forced by `\|V\| = \|A\|`). With PR #2768 (joint = span ladder) and PR #2769 (finrank = 2 m_max + 1), this gives the dimension lower bound `finrank(predicted GS subspace) ‚â• 2 m_max + 1 = 2 s_A + 1` (PR #2786, file `Quantum/SpinS/SaturatedFMJointLePredictedGS.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_finrank_ge_succ_card_mul_N_of_cardNotA_zero` | **predicted GS subspace dim ‚â• 2 m_max + 1 at saturated case**: when `\|¬¨A\| = 0`, `\|V\|¬∑N + 1 ‚â§ finrank(bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted A N)`. Direct corollary of PR #2786's bridge + PR #2769's `saturatedFerromagnetJointEigenspace_finrank_eq` via `Submodule.finrank_mono`. Realises the Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3 degeneracy `2 S_tot + 1` lower bound at the saturated edge case, where `S_tot = m_max = \|V\|¬∑S` (PR #2787, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyGSFinrankSaturated.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_complement_finrank_ge_succ_card_mul_N_of_cardA_zero` | **opposite saturated case**: when `\|A\| = 0`, the **complement-orientation** predicted GS subspace `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted (¬¨A) N` has `finrank ‚â• \|V\|¬∑N + 1`. Application of PR #2787 to the flipped sublattice, reducing `\|¬¨¬¨A\| = 0` to the original `\|A\| = 0` via filter congruence. Note: with `\|A\| = 0`, the original-orientation predicted GS subspace would target a different (≈ú_tot)¬≤ eigenvalue (signed-difference goes negative); the complement orientation gives the natural choice (PR #2788, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyGSFinrankOppositeSaturated.lean`) |

| `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_norm_eq_zero_iff_card_eq` | **norm-zero characterisation**: at `N ‚â• 1`, `‚ÄñbipartiteImbalanceWeight A N‚Äñ = 0 ‚Üî \|A\| = \|¬¨A\|`. Direct from `norm_eq_zero` + PR #2854 (PR #2855, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteImbalanceWeightNormEqZero.lean`) |
| `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_re_pos_of_cardA_gt_cardNotA_of_N_pos` / `_re_neg_of_cardA_lt_cardNotA_of_N_pos` | **strict sign of the imbalance-weight real part** (at `N ‚â• 1`): `\|A\| > \|¬¨A\|` gives `(bipartiteImbalanceWeight A N).re > 0`; `\|A\| < \|¬¨A\|` gives `< 0`. Direct from PR #2773's bridge + PR #2826's norm formula. Quantitative connection from the N√©el variational input to Theorem 2.3's predicted spin (PR #2857, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelStateMagNormEqPredicted.lean`) |
| `magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS_complement_norm_eq` | **complement N√©el-state magnetization norm = same predicted spin**: `‚ÄñmagEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS (¬¨A) N)‚Äñ = \|\|A\| ‚àí \|¬¨A\|\|¬∑N/2`. Confirms orientation independence of Theorem 2.3 (PR #2858, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelStateComplementMagNormEqPredicted.lean`) |
| `magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS_norm_eq_mMax_of_cardNotA_zero` / `_of_cardA_zero` | **N√©el-state mag norm at saturated edges = `m_max`**: at `\|¬¨A\| = 0` or `\|A\| = 0`, `‚ÄñmagEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS A N)‚Äñ = \|Œõ\|¬∑N/2 = m_max`. | `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_norm_eq_re_of_cardNotA_le_cardA` | **norm = real part when `\|A\| ‚â• \|¬¨A\|`**: `‚ÄñbipartiteImbalanceWeight A N‚Äñ = (bipartiteImbalanceWeight A N).re`. Both terms bounded via AM-GM (`\|A\|¬∑\|¬¨A\| ‚â§ (\|Œõ\|/2)¬≤`) and `min(\|A\|, \|¬¨A\|) ‚â§ \|Œõ\|/2` (PR #2875, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergySymmNormLeBound.lean`) |

Direct from Œ≥-4 step 224 + Œ≥-4 step 230 + `ring`. The complement N√©el sits at the opposite `≈ú_tot^(3)` eigenvalue (Œ≥-4 step 231) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1278) |
| `magnetization_neelConfigOf_complement_eq_neg` | **Spin-`1/2` negation relation**: `magnetization Œõ (neelConfigOf (¬¨A)) = ‚àímagnetization Œõ (neelConfigOf A)`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of Œ≥-4 step 231; direct from existing `magnetization_neelConfigOf{,_complement}` + `ring` (Œ≥-4 step 232) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1279) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfSquared_complement_eq` | **Spin-`1/2` Casimir is sublattice-swap invariant on N√©el**: `<Œ¶_N√©el(¬¨A)\|(≈ú_tot)¬≤\|Œ¶_N√©el(¬¨A)> = <Œ¶_N√©el(A)\|(≈ú_tot)¬≤\|Œ¶_N√©el(A)>`. Direct from Œ≥-4 step 227 (Casimir = `(M/2)¬≤+\|Œõ\|/2`) + Œ≥-4 step 232 (`M(¬¨A) = ‚àíM(A)`): `(M)¬≤` is sign-flip invariant (Œ≥-4 step 233) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1280) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSSquared_complement_eq` | **Spin-`S` Casimir is sublattice-swap invariant on N√©el**: `<Œ¶_N√©el(¬¨A)\|(≈ú_tot)¬≤\|Œ¶_N√©el(¬¨A)> = <Œ¶_N√©el(A)\|(≈ú_tot)¬≤\|Œ¶_N√©el(A)>`. Direct from Œ≥-4 step 218 + 221 + 231 + `ring`: `magEigenvalueS¬≤` is sign-flip invariant; `Œ£_x m_x¬≤` evaluates to `\|V\|¬∑(N/2)¬≤` for both A and ¬¨A. Spin-`S` mirror of Œ≥-4 step 233 (Œ≥-4 step 234) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1281) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp3_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`S` `(≈ú_tot^(3))¬≤` is sublattice-swap invariant on N√©el**: `<Œ¶_N√©el(¬¨A)\|(≈ú_tot^(3))¬≤\|Œ¶_N√©el(¬¨A)> = <Œ¶_N√©el(A)\|(≈ú_tot^(3))¬≤\|Œ¶_N√©el(A)>`. Direct from Œ≥-4 step 205 + 231 + `ring`: squared eigenvalue is sign-flip invariant (Œ≥-4 step 235) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1282) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp3_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`1/2` `(≈ú_tot^(3))¬≤` is sublattice-swap invariant on N√©el**: `<Œ¶_N√©el(¬¨A)\|(≈ú_tot^(3))¬≤\|Œ¶_N√©el(¬¨A)> = <Œ¶_N√©el(A)\|(≈ú_tot^(3))¬≤\|Œ¶_N√©el(A)>`. Direct from Œ≥-4 step 204 + 232 + `ring`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of Œ≥-4 step 235 (Œ≥-4 step 236) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1283) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp12_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`S` transverse Casimir `(≈ú^1)¬≤+(≈ú^2)¬≤` is sublattice-swap invariant on N√©el**: equal expectations on `Œ¶_N√©el(A)` and `Œ¶_N√©el(¬¨A)`. Both reduce to `\|V\|¬∑N/2` via Œ≥-4 step 220 + 221 (Œ≥-4 step 237) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1284) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp12_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`1/2` transverse Casimir invariance on N√©el**: both expectations equal `\|Œõ\|/2`. Direct from Œ≥-4 step 219 (œÉ-independent constant). Spin-`1/2` mirror of Œ≥-4 step 237 (Œ≥-4 step 238) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1285) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp3_complement_eq_neg` | **Spin-`S` linear `≈ú_tot^(3)` negates under sublattice swap on N√©el**: `<Œ¶_N√©el(¬¨A)\|≈ú_tot^(3)\|Œ¶_N√©el(¬¨A)> = ‚àí<Œ¶_N√©el(A)\|≈ú_tot^(3)\|Œ¶_N√©el(A)>`. Direct from Œ≥-4 step 206 + 231 (Œ≥-4 step 239) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1286) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp3_complement_eq_neg` | **Spin-`1/2` linear `≈ú_tot^(3)` negates under sublattice swap on N√©el**. Direct from Œ≥-4 step 207 + 232 + `ring`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of Œ≥-4 step 239 (Œ≥-4 step 240) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1287) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp1/2_expectation` | **Spin-`S` N√©el transverse linear expectations vanish**: `<Œ¶_N√©el\|≈ú_tot^(1,2)\|Œ¶_N√©el> = 0`. Trivial corollary of Œ≥-4 step 214 applied to `œÉ = neelConfigOfS A N` (Œ≥-4 step 241). Together with Œ≥-4 step 91 (`<≈ú_tot^(3)> = magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS`), gives the complete per-axis profile `(0, 0, (\|A\|‚àí\|¬¨A\|)¬∑N/2)` | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1288) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp1/2_expectation` | **Spin-`1/2` N√©el transverse linear expectations vanish**: same as Œ≥-4 step 241 for spin-`1/2`, derived from Œ≥-4 step 215 (Œ≥-4 step 242) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1289) |

The single-cluster Problem 2.5.a modules below are imported from the
build root again after the 2026-05-30 orphan-module sweep had removed
the earlier implementation.  The restored concrete tip is
`Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean`, and the
minimum-eigenvalue bridge in `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianMin.lean`
keeps the abstract Hamiltonian, energy hygiene, concrete
dimer/trimer/quartet/pentamer eigenvalue formulas, and variational
upper/lower-bound consumers live in the default Lean build
(PR #4032; PR #4033; PR #4034; PR #4035; PR #4036).

| `singleClusterHamiltonianS` | **Single-cluster (star-graph) Heisenberg Hamiltonian** (Tasaki Problem 2.5.a, p. 38): `H = Œ£_{j=1}^z ≈ú_0 ¬∑ ≈ú_j` on `Fin (z + 1)` with central vertex `0` and `z` leaves. Ground-state energy `‚àíS(1 + zS)` (Œ≥-5 step 243) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1290) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_isHermitian` | **Hermiticity** of the single-cluster Heisenberg Hamiltonian. Sum of Hermitian `spinSDot 0 j N` over `j ‚àà univ.erase 0` (Œ≥-5 step 244) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1291) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_zero_z` | **Edge case `z = 0`**: `singleClusterHamiltonianS 0 N = 0` since `univ.erase 0 = ‚àÖ` in `Fin 1`. Tasaki's formula `‚àíS(1 + zS)` is intended for `z ‚â• 1` (Œ≥-5 step 245) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1292) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allUp_expectation` | **All-up expectation**: `<Œ¶_‚ä§\|H\|Œ¶_‚ä§> = z¬∑(N/2)¬≤` for the single-cluster Hamiltonian. Far above Tasaki's GS energy `‚àíS(1+zS)` since all-up is in the maximum-`s_tot` Casimir sector, not the minimum (Œ≥-5 step 246) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1293) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allAligned_expectation` | **AllAligned-c expectation**: `<Œ¶_aligned(c)\|H\|Œ¶_aligned(c)> = z¬∑(N/2 ‚àí c.val)¬≤`. Generalises Œ≥-5 step 246 (the `c = 0` case). For `c = Fin.last N` (all-down) gives same `z¬∑(N/2)¬≤` (Œ≥-5 step 247) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1294) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allDown_expectation` | **All-down expectation**: `<Œ¶_‚ä•\|H\|Œ¶_‚ä•> = z¬∑(N/2)¬≤`. Direct specialisation of Œ≥-5 step 247 at `c = Fin.last N` (Œ≥-5 step 248) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1295) |
| `leafSpinSOp1/2/3` | **Leaf-spin operators on `Fin (z+1)`**: `≈ú_R^(Œ±) = Œ£_{j ‚âÝ 0} onSiteS j ≈ú^(Œ±)` for axes `Œ± = 1, 2, 3`. Sum over leaves of single-site `≈ú^(Œ±)` (excludes central vertex `0`). Building blocks for the upcoming Casimir decomposition `H = (≈ú_tot¬≤ ‚àí ≈ú_0¬≤ ‚àí ≈ú_R¬≤)/2` (Œ≥-5 step 249) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1296) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eq_dot_leaves` | **`≈ú_0 ¬∑ ≈ú_R` decomposition**: `H = Œ£_Œ± onSiteS 0 (≈ú^(Œ±)) ¬∑ leafSpinSOp_Œ±`. Direct distribution of left multiplication over the sum `Œ£_j (A * B_j) = A * (Œ£_j B_j)` via `Finset.mul_sum` (Œ≥-5 step 250) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1297) |
| `totalSpinSOp1/2/3_eq_onSite_zero_add_leafSpinSOp1/2/3` | **Total = central + leaves** (per axis): `totalSpinSOp_Œ± (Fin (z+1)) N = onSiteS 0 (≈ú^(Œ±)) + leafSpinSOp_Œ± z N`. Direct from `Finset.sum_erase_add` + `add_comm` (Œ≥-5 step 251) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1298) |
| `leafSpinSSquared` | **Leaf-spin Casimir**: `≈ú_R¬≤ := (≈ú_R^(1))¬≤ + (≈ú_R^(2))¬≤ + (≈ú_R^(3))¬≤`, total-spin-squared restricted to leaves of `Fin (z+1)` (Œ≥-5 step 252) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1299) |
| `onSiteS_zero_commute_leafSpinSOp1/2/3` | **Center-leaf commutativity**: `Commute (onSiteS 0 ≈ú^(Œ±)) (leafSpinSOp_Œ± z N)`. Direct from `Commute.sum_right` + `onSiteS_commute_of_ne` (disjoint sites) (Œ≥-5 step 253) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1300) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_mul_eq_casimir_diff` | **Casimir decomposition**: `2¬∑H = (≈ú_tot)¬≤ ‚àí ≈ú_0¬≤ ‚àí ≈ú_R¬≤` for the single-cluster Hamiltonian, where `≈ú_0¬≤ = spinSDot 0 0 N` and `≈ú_R¬≤ = leafSpinSSquared z N`. Proof: expand `Œ£_Œ± totalSpinSOp_Œ±¬≤ = Œ£_Œ± (onSite 0 + leaf_Œ±)¬≤` via Œ≥-5 step 251 + `(a+b)¬≤ = a¬≤ + 2ab + b¬≤` (using Œ≥-5 step 253 commutativity) + Œ≥-5 step 250 (cross term identification) + `noncomm_ring` (Œ≥-5 step 254) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1301) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_smul_eq_casimir_diff` | **‚ÑÇ-smul Casimir form**: `(2 : ‚ÑÇ) ‚Ä¢ H = (≈ú_tot)¬≤ ‚àí ≈ú_0¬≤ ‚àí ≈ú_R¬≤`. Direct corollary of Œ≥-5 step 254 via `two_mul + two_smul` (Œ≥-5 step 255) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1302) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_mul_expectation` | **Casimir decomposition expectation form**: `2 ¬∑ <v\|H\|v> = <v\|Stot¬≤\|v> ‚àí <v\|S0¬≤\|v> ‚àí <v\|SR¬≤\|v>` for any `v`. Direct corollary of Œ≥-5 step 255 + linearity of `dotProduct + mulVec` over `smul` and subtraction (Œ≥-5 step 256) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1303) |
| `spinSDot_self_expectation_general` | **Single-site Casimir expectation**: `<v\|spinSDot x x N\|v> = (N(N+2)/4) ¬∑ <v\|v>` for any `v`. Direct from `spinSDot_self` (= scalar matrix) + linearity. Used to evaluate the `S0¬≤` term in the Casimir decomposition (Œ≥-5 step 257) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1304) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_mul_expectation_simplified` | **Simplified Casimir expectation**: `2 ¬∑ <v\|H\|v> = <v\|Stot¬≤\|v> ‚àí (N(N+2)/4)¬∑<v\|v> ‚àí <v\|SR¬≤\|v>`. Combines Œ≥-5 step 256 + 257; the `S0¬≤` term is now an explicit scalar multiple of the norm-squared (Œ≥-5 step 258) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1305) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_of_joint_casimir_eigenvec` | **H eigenvalue from joint Casimir eigenvalues**: if `Stot¬≤¬∑v = Œ±¬∑v` and `SR¬≤¬∑v = Œ≤¬∑v`, then `H¬∑v = (Œ± ‚àí N(N+2)/4 ‚àí Œ≤)/2 ¬∑ v`. Reduces the GS-energy problem to enumerating admissible `(Œ±, Œ≤)` joint eigenvalues (Œ≥-5 step 259) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1306) |
| `spinSDot_self_mulVec_eq_smul` | **Single-site Casimir as scalar action**: `spinSDot x x N ¬∑ v = (N(N+2)/4) ‚Ä¢ v` for any `v`. Direct from `spinSDot_self` (= scalar matrix). Standalone helper extracted from Œ≥-5 step 259 (Œ≥-5 step 260) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1307) |
| `leafSpinSOp1/2/3_zero_z` and `leafSpinSSquared_zero_z` | **Edge case `z=0`** (single vertex, no leaves): `leafSpinSOp_Œ± 0 N = 0` and `leafSpinSSquared 0 N = 0` since `univ.erase 0 = ‚àÖ` in `Fin 1` (Œ≥-5 step 261) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1308) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_eq_sum_spinSDot` | **`leafSpinSSquared` as double sum**: `leafSpinSSquared z N = Œ£_{j,k ‚àà univ.erase 0} spinSDot j k N`. Mirrors `totalSpinSSquared_eq_sum_spinSDot` for the leaf-only Casimir (Œ≥-5 step 262) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1309) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_allUp_expectation` | **All-up expectation**: `<Œ¶_‚ä§\|leafSpinSSquared z N\|Œ¶_‚ä§> = (zN/2)¬∑(zN/2 + 1) = s_R(s_R+1)` where `s_R = z¬∑(N/2)` is max leaf spin. Computed by rearranging Œ≥-5 step 254 (Casimir decomposition) and applying closed forms for `<Stot¬≤>`, `<S0¬≤>`, `<H>` (Œ≥-5 step 263) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1310) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` | **Eigenvector form on allUp**: `H ¬∑ \|Œ¶_‚ä§‚ü© = z¬∑(N/2)¬≤ ¬∑ \|Œ¶_‚ä§‚ü©`. Proof: each leaf-pair `spinSDot 0 j` at `j ‚âÝ 0` gives `(N¬≤/4)¬∑\|Œ¶_‚ä§‚ü©` (via `spinSDot_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero_of_ne`); sum over `z` leaves (Œ≥-5 step 264) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1311) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` | **Eigenvector form** of `≈ú_R¬≤` on allUp: `≈ú_R¬≤ ¬∑ \|Œ¶_‚ä§‚ü© = (zN/2)¬∑(zN/2+1) ¬∑ \|Œ¶_‚ä§‚ü©`. Witnesses that `\|Œ¶_‚ä§‚ü©` is in the maximum-leaf-spin Casimir sector `s_R = zN/2`. Combined with Œ≥-5 step 264 + existing `Stot¬≤` eigenvector identity, confirms `\|Œ¶_‚ä§‚ü©` is a joint eigenstate of `H, Stot¬≤, ≈ú_0¬≤, ≈ú_R¬≤`. Proof: rearrange Œ≥-5 step 255 + apply (Œ≥-5 step 265) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1312) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_last` | **Eigenvector form on allDown**: `H ¬∑ \|Œ¶_‚ä•‚ü© = z¬∑(N/2)¬≤ ¬∑ \|Œ¶_‚ä•‚ü©`. AllDown mirror of Œ≥-5 step 264; same eigenvalue (spin-flip symmetry) (Œ≥-5 step 266) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1313) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_last` | **Eigenvector form** of `≈ú_R¬≤` on allDown: `≈ú_R¬≤ ¬∑ \|Œ¶_‚ä•‚ü© = (zN/2)¬∑(zN/2+1) ¬∑ \|Œ¶_‚ä•‚ü©`. AllDown mirror of Œ≥-5 step 265; both `\|Œ¶_‚ä§‚ü©` and `\|Œ¶_‚ä•‚ü©` lie in the maximum-leaf-spin Casimir sector `s_R = zN/2`, differing only by total `≈ú_tot^(3)` magnetization (Œ≥-5 step 267) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1314) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_at_gs_casimir_sector` | **GS-sector eigenvalue specialization**: if `v` is a joint eigenvector of `≈ú_tot¬≤` at `((z‚àí1)N/2)((z‚àí1)N/2+1)` and `≈ú_R¬≤` at `(zN/2)(zN/2+1)`, then `H ¬∑ v = ‚àí(N/2)¬∑(zN/2+1) ¬∑ v = ‚àíS(1+zS) ¬∑ v`. Specialization of Œ≥-5 step 259 to the ground-state sector predicted by Tasaki Problem 2.5.a (Œ≥-5 step 268) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1315) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_at_max_casimir_sector` | **Max-Casimir-sector eigenvalue specialization**: if `v` is a joint eigenvector of `≈ú_tot¬≤` at `((z+1)N/2)((z+1)N/2+1)` and `≈ú_R¬≤` at `(zN/2)(zN/2+1)`, then `H ¬∑ v = z¬∑(N/2)¬≤ ¬∑ v = zS¬≤ ¬∑ v`. Maximum Casimir sector containing `\|Œ¶_‚ä§‚ü©, \|Œ¶_‚ä•‚ü©` (Œ≥-5 step 269) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1316) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS` / `singleClusterHamiltonianS_mulVec_eq_gs_energy_smul` | **Predicted ground-state energy** (Tasaki Problem 2.5.a target): `singleClusterGSEnergyS z N := ‚àí(N/2)¬∑(zN/2+1) = ‚àíS(1+zS)`, with named eigenvalue identity `H ¬∑ v = singleClusterGSEnergyS z N ‚Ä¢ v` at the GS Casimir sector (Œ≥-5 step 270) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1317) |
| `singleClusterMaxEnergyS` / `singleClusterHamiltonianS_mulVec_eq_max_energy_smul` | **Maximum Casimir-sector energy**: `singleClusterMaxEnergyS z N := z¬∑(N/2)¬≤ = zS¬≤`, with named eigenvalue identity `H ¬∑ v = singleClusterMaxEnergyS z N ‚Ä¢ v` at the max Casimir sector containing `\|Œ¶_‚ä§‚ü©, \|Œ¶_‚ä•‚ü©` (Œ≥-5 step 271) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1318) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_re_le_zero` / `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_re_nonneg` | **Energy real-part sign hygiene**: `Re(GS) ‚â§ 0` (AFM physical bound) and `0 ‚â§ Re(Max)` (extremal aligned-state eigenvalue is non-negative) (Œ≥-5 step 272) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1319) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_re_le_singleClusterMaxEnergyS_re` | **Energy ordering**: `Re(GS) ‚â§ Re(Max)`. Consistency check between Œ≥-5 steps 268 and 269 (Œ≥-5 step 273) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1320) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_im_zero` / `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_im_zero` | **Energies are real**: imaginary parts of both named single-cluster energies vanish (Œ≥-5 step 274) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1321) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_one_eq` / `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_one_eq` | **Dimer (z=1) closed forms**: `singleClusterGSEnergyS 1 N = ‚àí(N/2)(N/2+1) = ‚àíS(S+1)` (canonical singlet eigenvalue of `≈ú_0¬∑≈ú_1`); `singleClusterMaxEnergyS 1 N = (N/2)¬≤ = S¬≤` (canonical triplet eigenvalue) (Œ≥-5 step 275) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1322) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_zero_right` / `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_zero_right` / `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_zero_left` | **Trivial edge cases**: `GS z 0 = 0` (spin-0), `Max z 0 = 0` (spin-0), `Max 0 N = 0` (single-site, no leaves) (Œ≥-5 step 276) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1323) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_re_eq` / `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_re_eq` | **Explicit `.re` forms** as `‚Ñù` values: `Re(GS) = -(N/2)(zN/2+1)`, `Re(Max) = z¬∑N¬≤/4`. Useful for downstream real comparisons (Œ≥-5 step 278) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1325) |
| `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_sub_singleClusterGSEnergyS` | **GS-Max energy gap**: `Max ‚àí GS = (N/2)(zN+1) = S(2zS+1)`. Closed-form difference between the two named eigenvalues (Œ≥-5 step 280) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1327) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_re_lt_singleClusterMaxEnergyS_re_of_pos` | **Strict gap for `N ‚â• 1`**: `Re(GS) < Re(Max)` whenever spin is non-trivial (Œ≥-5 step 281) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1328) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_re_neg_of_pos` / `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_re_pos_of_pos` | **Strict sign**: `Re(GS) < 0` for `N ‚â• 1`; `0 < Re(Max)` for `z ‚â• 1, N ‚â• 1`. Strengthens Œ≥-5 step 272 (Œ≥-5 step 283) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1330) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_one` | **Single-leaf reduction**: `leafSpinSSquared 1 N = spinSDot 1 1 N` on `Fin 2`. Single-leaf leaf-Casimir collapses to single-site Casimir (Œ≥-5 step 285) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean` (PR #1332) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_one_mulVec` / `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_dimer` | **Dimer eigenvalue from Stot¬≤ alone**: at z=1, `leafSpinSSquared` acts as the scalar `N(N+2)/4`, so any Stot¬≤ eigenvector is auto a joint eigenvector with `H ¬∑ v = ((Œ± ‚àí N(N+2)/2) / 2) ‚Ä¢ v` (Œ≥-5 step 286) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean` (PR #1333) |

| `exists_eigenvector_in_invariant_submodule` | **Invariant-submodule eigenvector bridge**: over an algebraically closed field, any non-zero finite-dimensional submodule invariant under an endomorphism contains a non-zero eigenvector of that endomorphism. The proof restricts the endomorphism to the submodule and applies Mathlib's finite-dimensional eigenvalue-existence theorem. This is the linear-algebra bridge for the next Problem 2.5.a joint total/leaf Casimir sector-decomposition step after magnetization extraction (Œ≥-5 step 332) | `Math/InvariantSubmoduleEigenvector.lean` (PR #4041) |
| `exists_joint_eigenvector_in_invariant_submodule` | **Joint invariant-submodule eigenvector bridge**: over an algebraically closed field, any non-zero finite-dimensional submodule invariant under two commuting endomorphisms contains a non-zero simultaneous eigenvector for both. The proof first extracts an eigenvector for the first endomorphism, then applies the one-operator bridge to the non-zero intersection of the original submodule with that eigenspace, using commutativity to prove invariance under the second endomorphism. This packages the next linear-algebra step for extracting total/leaf Casimir sector data inside the single-cluster magnetization/Hamiltonian component (Œ≥-5 step 333) | `Math/InvariantSubmoduleEigenvector.lean` (PR #4042) |
| `singleCluster_totalSpinSSquared_commute_leafSpinSSquared` / `singleClusterHamiltonianS_commute_totalSpinSSquared` / `singleClusterHamiltonianS_commute_leafSpinSSquared` / `singleClusterHamiltonianMagEigenspaceSubmodule` / `singleCluster_totalSpinSSquared_mulVecLin_commute_leafSpinSSquared_mulVecLin` / `singleClusterHamiltonianMagEigenspaceSubmodule_totalSpinSSquared_invariant` / `singleClusterHamiltonianMagEigenspaceSubmodule_leafSpinSSquared_invariant` / `exists_joint_casimir_eigenvector_in_singleClusterHamiltonianMagEigenspaceSubmodule` | **Single-cluster Casimir invariance bridge**: the Casimir decomposition `(2 : ‚ÑÇ) ‚Ä¢ H = (≈ú_tot)¬≤ - ≈ú_0¬≤ - ≈ú_R¬≤`, the scalar central-site Casimir, and the total/leaf sublattice-Casimir commutation theorem show that the single-cluster Hamiltonian commutes with both the total and leaf Casimirs. Consequently the Hamiltonian eigenspace intersected with a fixed magnetization subspace is invariant under both Casimirs, and any non-zero such submodule contains a non-zero joint total/leaf Casimir eigenvector when the caller supplies the explicit algebraically closed field hypothesis `[IsAlgClosed ‚ÑÇ]` (Œ≥-5 step 334) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterCasimirInvariance.lean` (PR #4043) |
| `singleCluster_global_eigenvalue_lower_of_exists_joint_casimir_energy_lower` / `singleClusterGSEnergyS_re_le_hermitianMinEigenvalue_of_exists_joint_casimir_energy_lower` / `singleClusterHamiltonianS_hermitianMinEigenvalue_eq_gs_of_gs_sector_and_exists_joint_lower` / `singleClusterHamiltonianS_minEigenvalue_eq_gs_of_exists_gs_sector_and_exists_joint_lower` | **Same-energy joint-Casimir lower bridge**: the lower-bound callback no longer needs the original non-zero real-energy `H`-eigenvector to be a joint total/leaf Casimir eigenvector. | `singleCluster_exists_joint_casimir_energy_lower_of_mag_component_joint_lower` / `singleClusterGSEnergyS_re_le_hermitianMinEigenvalue_of_mag_component_joint_lower` / `singleClusterHamiltonianS_minEigenvalue_eq_gs_of_gs_sector_and_mag_joint_lower` / `singleClusterHamiltonianS_minEigenvalue_eq_gs_of_exists_gs_sector_and_mag_joint_lower` | **Single-cluster joint-Casimir witness bridge**: magnetization extraction turns any non-zero real-energy Hamiltonian eigenvector into a non-zero same-energy magnetization component. This proves the corresponding Hamiltonian/magnetization submodule is non-zero; the invariant-submodule Casimir theorem then supplies a non-zero same-energy joint total/leaf Casimir eigenvector in that submodule. A remaining Casimir-energy lower callback on such extracted joint vectors therefore yields the same-energy witness callback from PR #4044, plus Hermitian-min lower and conditional equality wrappers under the explicit `[IsAlgClosed ‚ÑÇ]` hypothesis (Œ≥-5 step 336) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterJointCasimirWitness.lean` (PR #4045) |

Reindexing `M = m_max ‚àí magSumS œÉ` as `M = k ‚àí m_max` lets the existing coupled leaf/center sector-energy theorem supply the Casimir-energy lower callback required by PR #4045. | `Matrix.isHermitian_sum` / `rayleighOnVec_sum_matrix` / `sum_lower_bounds_le_hermitianMinEigenvalue_sum` / `add_lower_bounds_le_hermitianMinEigenvalue_add` / `tasaki25b_local_cluster_sum_lower_bound` / `tasaki25b_local_cluster_sum_lower_bound_closed_form` | **Problem 2.5.b sum lower-bound bridge**: packages Tasaki's Lemma A.5 argument for finite-dimensional Hermitian matrices. If each local Hamiltonian has a lower bound `Œµ x`, then the Hermitian minimum eigenvalue of the sum is at least `Œ£ x, Œµ x`. The local-cluster wrappers specialize this to the Problem 2.5.a star-cluster energies, yielding the abstract lower bound `E_GS ‚â• -Œ£_{x‚ààA} S(1 + degree(x) S)` with `S = N/2` once a graph Hamiltonian is decomposed into centered local cluster terms (Œ≥-6 step 339). The generic "a finite sum of Hermitian matrices is Hermitian" helper has been extracted as `Matrix.isHermitian_sum` into `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` (PR #4341), replacing the per-file private copies in `Quantum/IsingChain.lean`, `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean`, and `Quantum/SpinS/TotalSpin.lean` | `Quantum/SpinS/HermitianMinEigenvalueSumLower.lean` (PR #4048), `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` (PR #4341) |
| `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_isHermitian` / `heisenbergHamiltonianOnGraphS_one_eq_sum_graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS` / `heisenbergHamiltonianOnGraphS_half_eq_sum_filter_graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS` | **Problem 2.5.b graph-local decomposition**: defines the same-Hilbert-space local star Hamiltonian `h_x = Œ£_{y‚ààN_G(x)} ≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_y`. The unit-coupling graph Hamiltonian is `Œ£_x h_x` under the repository's ordered-pair convention, and on a bipartite graph the half-coupling Hamiltonian is the one-sided sum `Œ£_{x‚ààA} h_x`, using the pair swap and `spinSDot_comm`. This pins the coefficient convention needed before applying the Problem 2.5.a local-cluster lower bounds (Œ≥-6 step 340) | `Quantum/SpinS/HeisenbergGraphLocal.lean` (PR #4050) |
| `siteConfigEquiv` / `siteConfigEquiv_apply` / `siteConfigEquiv_symm_apply` / `reindex_onSiteS_siteEquiv` / `reindex_spinSDot_siteEquiv` / `transportedSingleClusterHamiltonianS` / `transportedSingleClusterHamiltonianS_eq_sum` / `optionClusterHamiltonianS` / `singleClusterOptionEquiv` / `sum_fin_erase_zero_eq_sum_succ` / `transportedSingleClusterHamiltonianS_option_eq` | **Problem 2.5.b single-cluster transport bridge**: transports many-body spin operators along an equivalence of site types. A transported `singleClusterHamiltonianS z N` is the sum of dot products between the transported center and leaves; in particular, the canonical `Fin (s.card + 1) ‚âÉ Option s` equivalence turns the abstract single-cluster Hamiltonian into the option-star Hamiltonian with center `none` and leaves `some y`. This isolates the reindexing part of the remaining graph-local-star comparison (Œ≥-6 step 341) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterTransport.lean` (PR #4051) |
| `graphLocalStarConfig` / `graphLocalStarConfig_center` / `graphLocalStarConfig_neighbor` / `graphLocalStarConfig_outside` / `graphLocalStarConfig_agree_off_pair_of_option_agree` / `option_agree_of_graphLocalStarConfig_agree_off_pair` / `spinSDot_graphLocalStarConfig_eq_option` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_apply_eq_zero_of_outside_diff` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_block_eq_optionClusterHamiltonianS` | **Problem 2.5.b graph-local star block bridge**: fixes an outside configuration and embeds option-star configurations into the full graph Hilbert space. The graph-local star has zero matrix entries between configurations with different outside restrictions, and each fixed-outside block is exactly the option-star Hamiltonian on `Option (G.neighborFinset x)`. This supplies the same-Hilbert-space block comparison needed before turning the Problem 2.5.a local-cluster bound into a full graph-local lower bound (Œ≥-6 step 342) | `Quantum/SpinS/GraphLocalStarBlockCore.lean` (embedding + matrix-entry comparison) + `Quantum/SpinS/GraphLocalStarBlock.lean` (block identity, split for build speed) (PR #4052) |
| `graphLocalOutsideSite` / `graphLocalOutsideSite_fintype` / `graphLocalOutsideSite_decidableEq` / `graphLocalOutsideConfigExtend` / `graphLocalProductConfig` / `graphLocalConfigEquiv` / `graphLocalConfigEquiv_apply_none` / `graphLocalConfigEquiv_apply_some` / `graphLocalConfigEquiv_apply_outside` / `graphLocalConfigEquiv_symm_apply` / `graphLocalProductConfig_outside` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_product` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_product_isHermitian` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_product_apply_of_outside_eq` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_product_apply_of_outside_ne` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_product_mulVec` / `matrix_mulVec_reindex_comp_symm` / `dotProduct_comp_equiv_symm` / `rayleighOnVec_reindex_comp_symm` / `dotProduct_product_re_eq_sum_blocks` / `rayleighOnVec_graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_product` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_product_rayleigh_lower` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_product_minEigenvalue_lower` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_rayleigh_lower` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_minEigenvalue_lower` | **Problem 2.5.b graph-local product lower-bound bridge**: reindexes a full graph configuration around `x` as an option-star configuration paired with an outside assignment. In these product coordinates the graph-local star is block diagonal in the outside coordinate, each diagonal block is the option-star Hamiltonian, and the Rayleigh numerator and squared norm decompose as sums over outside blocks. Reindex-invariance lemmas transfer the result back to the original same-Hilbert-space graph-local star. Therefore any common Rayleigh lower bound for the option-star blocks gives the same Hermitian minimum-eigenvalue lower bound for the original graph-local star (Œ≥-6 step 343) | `Quantum/SpinS/GraphLocalStarLowerBoundCore.lean` (product coordinates + product cluster Hamiltonian with Hermiticity / outside-block apply) + `Quantum/SpinS/GraphLocalStarLowerBound.lean` (product mulVec + reindexing + Rayleigh decomposition over outside blocks + lower bound, split for build speed) (PR #4053) |
| `transportedSingleClusterHamiltonianS_isHermitian` / `optionClusterHamiltonianS_isHermitian` / `dotProduct_comp_equiv` / `rayleighOnVec_reindex_comp` / `optionClusterHamiltonianS_rayleigh_lower_singleClusterGSEnergy` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_minEigenvalue_lower_singleClusterGSEnergy` / `tasaki25b_graphLocalCluster_sum_lower_bound` / `tasaki25b_heisenbergHamiltonianOnGraphS_half_lower_bound` | **Problem 2.5.b option-star and graph-local sum wrapper**: transports the Problem 2.5.a minimum-eigenvalue formula to the canonical option-star Hamiltonian by Rayleigh reindexing. Under `[IsAlgClosed ‚ÑÇ]` and the local positive-degree hypothesis `1 ‚â§ (G.neighborFinset x).card`, this gives the single-cluster ground-energy lower bound for the same-Hilbert-space graph-local star. The finite-sum wrapper then bounds a chosen family of graph-local stars, and the bipartite half-coupling theorem applies the bound directly to `heisenbergHamiltonianOnGraphS G (1 / 2) N = Œ£_{x‚ààA} h_x` for one side of a bipartition (Œ≥-6 step 344) | `Quantum/SpinS/GraphLocalStarSumWrapper.lean` (PR #4054) |
| `tasaki25b_graphLocalCluster_sum_lower_bound_closed_form` / `tasaki25b_graphLocalCluster_sum_lower_bound_degree_closed_form` / `tasaki25b_heisenbergHamiltonianOnGraphS_half_lower_bound_closed_form` / `tasaki25b_heisenbergHamiltonianOnGraphS_half_lower_bound_degree_closed_form` | **Problem 2.5.b closed-form and degree wrappers**: rewrites the graph-local star and half-coupling graph-Hamiltonian lower bounds using the explicit Problem 2.5.a formula `Re E_GS(z) = -(N/2)(zN/2+1)`. The degree-spelled variants replace `(G.neighborFinset x).card` by `G.degree x`, matching Tasaki's graph-theoretic statement while preserving the necessary positive-degree hypothesis for centers on the chosen side (Œ≥-6 step 345) | `Quantum/SpinS/GraphLocalStarSumWrapper.lean` (PR #4055) |
| `totalSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}_eq_sublattice_sum` | total spin decomposition: `≈ú_tot^(Œ±) = ≈ú_A^(Œ±) + ≈ú_¬¨A^(Œ±)` for `Œ± ‚àà {1, 2, 3}`. Direct from the partition `Œõ = A ‚à™ ¬¨A` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinCore.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared` / `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_isHermitian` | sublattice spin Casimir: `(≈ú_A)¬≤ := Œ£_Œ± (≈ú_A^(Œ±))¬≤`. Hermitian (each `(≈ú_A^(Œ±))¬≤` is the square of a Hermitian operator). Foundation for the Casimir identity `ƒ§_toy = (1/(2|Œõ|))((≈ú_tot)¬≤ ‚àí (≈ú_A)¬≤ ‚àí (≈ú_B)¬≤)` (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 (2.5.11)) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinCore.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfOpGeneric_cross_commute` / `sublatticeSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}_cross_commute_op{1,2,3}` | mixed-axes cross-sublattice commutativity: `Commute (≈ú_A^(Œ±)) (≈ú_¬¨A^(Œ≤))` for any axes `Œ±, Œ≤ ‚àà {1, 2, 3}`. Generic helper expresses this for arbitrary single-site operators `S, T`; the six mixed-axis specialisations follow as one-line corollaries | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinCore.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_cross_commute` | the two sublattice Casimirs commute: `Commute (≈ú_A)¬≤ (≈ú_¬¨A)¬≤`. Each pairwise component `Commute ((≈ú_A^(Œ±))¬≤) ((≈ú_¬¨A^(Œ≤))¬≤)` follows from the mixed-axes cross-commute by chaining `Commute.mul_left` / `mul_right`. Sets up the joint eigenbasis of `(≈ú_tot)¬≤`, `(≈ú_A)¬≤`, `(≈ú_¬¨A)¬≤` for the toy-Hamiltonian eigenvalue analysis | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinCore.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}_commutator_sublatticeSpinHalfOp{2,3,1}` | **Sublattice SU(2) algebra**: `[≈ú_A^(Œ±), ≈ú_A^(Œ≤)] = i Œµ^Œ±Œ≤Œ≥ ≈ú_A^(Œ≥)` for each cyclic axis triple. Generic helper `sublatticeSpin_commutator_general` lifts the single-site commutator `[SŒ±, SŒ≤] = i ‚Ä¢ SŒ≥` to the sublattice sum (off-diagonal pairs vanish by `onSite_mul_onSite_of_ne`; diagonal contributes `if A x then i ‚Ä¢ onSite x SŒ≥ else 0`) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinCore.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_commute_sublatticeSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}` | **Sublattice Casimir self-invariance**: `Commute (≈ú_A)¬≤ (≈ú_A^(Œ±))` for each axis. Standard SU(2) Casimir argument applied at the sublattice level: per-axis Leibniz rule `[X¬≤, C] = X[X,C] + [X,C]X` combined with the sublattice SU(2) algebra. Together with cross-commute, gives `Commute (≈ú_A)¬≤ (≈ú_tot^(Œ±))`, hence `Commute (≈ú_A)¬≤ (≈ú_tot)¬≤` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinCore.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_commute_sublatticeSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}_complement` / `_totalSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}` / `_totalSpinHalfSquared` | `(≈ú_A)¬≤` commutes with each `≈ú_¬¨A^(Œ±)` (`Commute.mul_left` over the mixed-axes cross-commute), with each `≈ú_tot^(Œ±) = ≈ú_A^(Œ±) + ≈ú_¬¨A^(Œ±)`, and with `(≈ú_tot)¬≤ = Œ£_Œ± (≈ú_tot^(Œ±))¬≤`. Provides the **third pairwise commutativity** needed for the joint eigenbasis of `(≈ú_tot)¬≤`, `(≈ú_A)¬≤`, `(≈ú_¬¨A)¬≤` (the first two are Œ±-6r self-invariance and Œ±-6o inter-Casimir cross-commute) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinCore.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinDot` / `sublatticeSpinDot_complement_isHermitian` | cross-sublattice spin dot product: `≈ú_A ¬∑ ≈ú_B := Œ£_Œ± ≈ú_A^(Œ±) ≈ú_B^(Œ±)`. `≈ú_A ¬∑ ≈ú_¬¨A` is Hermitian (each summand is the product of two commuting Hermitian operators). Bilinear expansion `sublatticeSpinDot_eq_sum_sum`: `≈ú_A ¬∑ ≈ú_B = Œ£_{x : A x} Œ£_{y : B y} ≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_y` connects the operator-level Casimir form to the bond-level Heisenberg expression in the toy Hamiltonian (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 (2.5.10)) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinDot.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_eq_sum_dot` / `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_basisVec_const` / `_all_up` / `_all_down` / `_of_const_on` | `(≈ú_A)¬≤ = Œ£_{x ‚àà A} Œ£_{y ‚àà A} ≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_y` (specialisation `B = A` of the bilinear expansion), and the **maximum-spin Casimir eigenvalue on the all-aligned state**: `(≈ú_A)¬≤ ¬∑ \|s s ‚Ä¶ s‚ü© = (\|A\|¬∑(\|A\|+2)/4) ¬∑ \|s s ‚Ä¶ s‚ü©` for any `s : Fin 2`. Generalised form `_of_const_on`: any `\|œÉ‚ü©` with `œÉ` **constant on `A`** is an eigenvector with eigenvalue `\|A\|¬∑(\|A\|+2)/4` (relevant for N√©el-style states which are constant on each sublattice but not globally) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_eq_sublatticeSpinDot_sum` | the MLM toy Hamiltonian decomposes as an oriented cross-sublattice spin dot product: `ƒ§_toy = ≈ú_A ¬∑ ≈ú_¬¨A + ≈ú_¬¨A ¬∑ ≈ú_A`. Bridges the bipartite-bond sum (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 (2.5.10)) to the operator-level Casimir form (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 (2.5.11)) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinDot_complement_comm` / `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_eq_two_sublatticeSpinDot` | cross-sublattice symmetry: `≈ú_A ¬∑ ≈ú_¬¨A = ≈ú_¬¨A ¬∑ ≈ú_A` (each axis pair commutes by `sublatticeSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}_cross_commute`), giving the closed form `ƒ§_toy = 2 ‚Ä¢ ≈ú_A ¬∑ ≈ú_¬¨A` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_eq_sublattice_casimir` / `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_eq_casimir_diff` | **Casimir identity** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 (2.5.11)): `(≈ú_tot)¬≤ = (≈ú_A)¬≤ + 2 ‚Ä¢ (≈ú_A ¬∑ ≈ú_¬¨A) + (≈ú_¬¨A)¬≤` (per-axis `(a + b)¬≤ = a¬≤ + 2ab + b¬≤` via cross-commute), and the closed form (without `1/|Œõ|`) `ƒ§_toy = (≈ú_tot)¬≤ ‚àí (≈ú_A)¬≤ ‚àí (≈ú_¬¨A)¬≤` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_commute_totalSpinHalfSquared` | the toy Hamiltonian commutes with `(≈ú_tot)¬≤` (specialisation of `heisenbergHamiltonian_commute_totalSpinHalfSquared`). The standard fact used to project the toy ground state onto a fixed `(≈ú_tot)¬≤` eigenspace, underpinning the `S_tot = 0` selection of the toy ground state | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_commute_sublatticeSpinHalfSquared` / `_complement` | the toy Hamiltonian commutes with `(≈ú_A)¬≤` and with `(≈ú_¬¨A)¬≤`. Direct consequences of the closed form `ƒ§_toy = (≈ú_tot)¬≤ ‚àí (≈ú_A)¬≤ ‚àí (≈ú_¬¨A)¬≤` and the three pairwise Casimir commutativities (PRs Œ±-6o, Œ±-6s, self-commute trivially). Together with Œ±-6p, gives all four Casimir-style commutators of `ƒ§_toy`, the prerequisite for the joint eigenbasis analysis on which the `S_tot = 0` selection rests | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_const` / `_simplified` | explicit eigenvalue of `ƒ§_toy` on the all-aligned state: the Casimir-difference form `\|Œõ\|¬∑(\|Œõ\|+2)/4 ‚àí \|A\|¬∑(\|A\|+2)/4 ‚àí \|¬¨A\|¬∑(\|¬¨A\|+2)/4` algebraically simplifies via `\|Œõ\| = \|A\| + \|¬¨A\|` to the **product form** `\|A\|¬∑\|¬¨A\|/2`. The eigenvalue is non-negative for any bipartite lattice and strictly positive when both sublattices are non-empty | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_neelStateOf` / `_complement_mulVec_neelStateOf` | sublattice Casimir eigenvalues on the **N√©el state** `Œ¶_N√©el(A) := basisVec (neelConfigOf A)`: `(≈ú_A)¬≤ ¬∑ \|Œ¶_N√©el(A)‚ü© = (\|A\|¬∑(\|A\|+2)/4) ¬∑ \|Œ¶_N√©el(A)‚ü©` and `(≈ú_¬¨A)¬≤ ¬∑ \|Œ¶_N√©el(A)‚ü© = (\|¬¨A\|¬∑(\|¬¨A\|+2)/4) ¬∑ \|Œ¶_N√©el(A)‚ü©`. Direct corollaries of `_of_const_on` since the N√©el configuration is constant on each sublattice (`œÉ x = 0` on `A`, `œÉ x = 1` on `¬¨A`); the N√©el state is simultaneously a `(≈ú_A)¬≤` and `(≈ú_¬¨A)¬≤` eigenvector at maximum-spin eigenvalues | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelCore.lean` |
| `mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_commute_of_mem` | generic preservation: any operator `A` with `Commute A (≈útot^(3))` maps each magnetisation sector `H_M` to itself ‚Äî operator-level form of Tasaki ¬ß2.2 (2.2.10), p. 22 block-diagonalisation | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | Casimir specialisation: `≈útot¬≤` preserves every `H_M` (since `[≈útot¬≤, ≈útot^(3)] = 0`) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | for any `J : Œõ ‚Üí Œõ ‚Üí ‚ÑÇ` and `M : ‚ÑÇ`, `v ‚àà H_M` implies `H ¬∑ v ‚àà H_M` ‚Äî the operator-level statement that any Heisenberg Hamiltonian block-diagonalises against Tasaki ¬ß2.2 (2.2.10), p. 22 magnetisation-sector decomposition (consequence of SU(2) invariance `[H, ≈útot^(3)] = 0`) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOpMinus_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | for any `M : ‚ÑÇ`, `v ‚àà H_M` implies `≈útot^- ¬∑ v ‚àà H_{M-1}` ‚Äî the standard SU(2) lowering ladder action via the Cartan relation `[≈útot^(3), ≈útot^-] = -≈útot^-` | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOpPlus_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | for any `M : ‚ÑÇ`, `v ‚àà H_M` implies `≈útot^+ ¬∑ v ‚àà H_{M+1}` ‚Äî the SU(2) raising ladder action via `[≈útot^(3), ≈útot^+] = +≈útot^+` | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_two_site` | for `Œõ = Fin 2` and any `Œ∏`, the global rotation factors as `onSite 0 (√õ^(Œ±)_Œ∏) * onSite 1 (√õ^(Œ±)_Œ∏)` (general-Œ∏ extension of Problem 2.2.b) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `onSite_exp_eq_exp_onSite` | `onSite x (exp A) = exp (onSite x A)` ‚Äî bridge between single-site and many-body matrix exponential. Local Frobenius normed structure + `LinearMap.continuous_of_finiteDimensional` + `NormedSpace.map_exp` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_eq_exp` | Tasaki eq. (2.2.11): `√õ^(Œ±)_Œ∏_tot = exp(-iŒ∏ ≈ú_tot^(Œ±))`. | `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_commute_of_commute` | Tasaki ¬ß2.2 (2.2.12) ‚Üí (2.2.13): `Commute A (≈ú_tot^(Œ±)) ‚Üí Commute A (√õ^(Œ±)_Œ∏_tot)`. Generic operator version, follows from `Commute.exp_right` after rewriting `√õ` via `_eq_exp` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp{Plus,Minus}_exp_commute_of_commute` | ladder version: `Commute A (≈ú^¬±_tot) ‚Üí Commute A (exp(c ‚Ä¢ ≈ú^¬±_tot))` for any `c : ‚ÑÇ` (useful for U(1) symmetry) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conjTranspose_mul_self` | `(√õ^(Œ±)_Œ∏_tot)·¥¥ * √õ^(Œ±)_Œ∏_tot = 1` (unitarity). Derived from `exp_mem_unitary_of_mem_skewAdjoint` after recognizing `-iŒ∏ ≈ú_tot^(Œ±)` as skew-adjoint | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conj_eq_self_of_commute` | Tasaki eq. (2.2.13) finite form: `Commute A (≈ú_tot^(Œ±)) ‚Üí (√õ^(Œ±)_Œ∏_tot)·¥¥ * A * √õ^(Œ±)_Œ∏_tot = A`. Combines `_commute_of_commute` with unitarity | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |

### Two-site spin inner product (Tasaki ¬ß2.2 eq. (2.2.16))

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ¬ß2.2 eq. (2.2.16), p. 24.

| `spinHalfDot x y` | `≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_y := Œ£_{Œ±} onSite x ≈ú^(Œ±) ¬∑ onSite y ≈ú^(Œ±)` | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_eq_plus_minus` | `≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_y = (1/2)(≈ú_x^+ ≈ú_y^- + ≈ú_x^- ≈ú_y^+) + ≈ú_x^(3) ≈ú_y^(3)` (Tasaki (2.2.16)) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |

| `spinHalfDot_commutator_totalSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}` | `[≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_y, ≈ú_tot^(Œ±)] = 0` for Œ± ‚àà {1, 2, 3} (SU(2) invariance, Tasaki (2.2.17)) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |

| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_basisVec_both_up/down` | `≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_y |‚Üë‚Üë‚ü© = (1/4) |‚Üë‚Üë‚ü©`, `≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_y |‚Üì‚Üì‚ü© = (1/4) |‚Üì‚Üì‚ü©` (Tasaki (2.2.19) triplet `m = ¬±1`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
