| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_basisVec_antiparallel` | `≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_y |œÉ‚ü© = (1/2) |swap œÉ‚ü© - (1/4) |œÉ‚ü©` when `œÉ x ‚âÝ œÉ y` (anti-parallel case) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_singlet` | singlet eigenvalue `≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_y (|œÉ‚ü© - |swap œÉ‚ü©) = -(3/4) (|œÉ‚ü© - |swap œÉ‚ü©)` (Tasaki (2.2.19) singlet `S = 0`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_triplet_anti` | triplet `m = 0` eigenvalue `≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_y (|œÉ‚ü© + |swap œÉ‚ü©) = (1/4) (|œÉ‚ü© + |swap œÉ‚ü©)` (Tasaki (2.2.19) triplet `m = 0`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian` | `H = Œ£_{x,y} J(x,y) ≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_y` (general Heisenberg-type Hamiltonian) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_commutator_totalSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}` | `[H, ≈ú_tot^(Œ±)] = 0` for all axes (Tasaki (2.2.13) SU(2) invariance) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_commutator_totalSpinHalfOp{Plus,Minus}` | `[H, ≈ú^¬±_tot] = 0` (ladder form of SU(2) invariance) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_commute_totalSpinHalfSquared` | `Commute H ≈útot¬≤` ‚Äî the Casimir operator-level form of SU(2) invariance (consequence of `[H, ≈útot^Œ±] = 0` for each Œ±, via `Commute.mul_right` and `.add_right`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_preserves_totalSpinHalfSquared_eigenvalue` | if `≈útot¬≤ ¬∑ v = S ¬∑ v` then `≈útot¬≤ ¬∑ (H ¬∑ v) = S ¬∑ (H ¬∑ v)` ‚Äî operator-level simultaneous diagonalisation of `H` and the SU(2) Casimir | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfOpPlus_mul_pauliZ` / `pauliZ_mul_spinHalfOpPlus` | `œÉ^+ ¬∑ œÉ^z = -œÉ^+` and `œÉ^z ¬∑ œÉ^+ = œÉ^+` ‚Äî the (anti)commutation at the single-site Pauli algebra level, used for the Jordan-Wigner cross-site CAR | `Quantum/SpinHalfBasis.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_basisVec_const` | `≈ú_tot¬≤ |s s ‚Ä¶ s‚ü© = (N(N+2)/4) |s s ‚Ä¶ s‚ü©` for any constant `s : Fin 2` (Casimir eigenvalue at maximum total spin `S = N/2`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_basisVec_all_up/down` | specializations of the above to `s = 0` (all-up) and `s = 1` (all-down) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOp{Minus,Plus}_pow_basisVec_const` | for any `s : Fin 2` and `k : ‚Ñï`, `≈útot¬≤ ¬∑ (≈útot^‚àì)^k ¬∑ |s‚Ä¶s‚ü© = (|Œõ|¬∑(|Œõ|+2)/4) ¬∑ (≈útot^‚àì)^k ¬∑ |s‚Ä¶s‚ü©` ‚Äî the iterated ladder iterates remain in the maximum-total-spin SU(2) representation `S = Smax = |Œõ|/2` (Casimir invariance, Tasaki ¬ß2.4) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_const` | `H |s‚Ä¶s‚ü© = (Œ£_{x,y} J(x,y)¬∑(if x=y then 3/4 else 1/4)) ¬∑ |s‚Ä¶s‚ü©` ‚Äî every Heisenberg-type Hamiltonian acts on a uniformly-aligned basis state as a scalar; bilinear-sum lift of Tasaki ¬ß2.4 eq. (2.4.5), p. 32 (`-≈ú_x¬∑≈ú_y |Œ¶‚Üë‚ü© = -S¬≤ |Œ¶‚Üë‚ü©` for `S = 1/2`, `x ‚âÝ y`), with the diagonal `S(S+1) = 3/4` contribution recorded explicitly | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_all_up/down` | specialisations of the above to `s = 0` (all-up) / `s = 1` (all-down) ‚Äî the eigenvector property of the fully-polarised states; ground-state status (Tasaki's `E_GS = -|B|¬∑S¬≤`) requires extra ferromagnetic structure on `J` and is not asserted here | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpPlus_basisVec_const` | `H ¬∑ (≈ú_tot^+ ¬∑ |s‚Ä¶s‚ü©) = c_J ¬∑ (≈ú_tot^+ ¬∑ |s‚Ä¶s‚ü©)` ‚Äî `≈ú_tot^+` preserves the H-eigenvalue on a constant-spin basis state (corollary of SU(2) invariance, Tasaki ¬ß2.4 (2.4.7), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpMinus_basisVec_const` | same with `≈ú_tot^-` ‚Äî the canonical lowering ladder Tasaki uses to enumerate the ferromagnetic ground states `|Œ¶_M‚ü©` (eq. (2.4.9), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpMinus_pow_basisVec_const` | iterated form: for any constant `s : Fin 2` and any `k : ‚Ñï`, `H ¬∑ ((≈ú_tot^-)^k ¬∑ |s‚Ä¶s‚ü©) = c_J ¬∑ ((≈ú_tot^-)^k ¬∑ |s‚Ä¶s‚ü©)`; specialised at `s = 0` this gives the unnormalised Tasaki ¬ß2.4 (2.4.9), p. 33 ‚Äî every iterate `(≈ú_tot^-)^k ¬∑ |Œ¶‚Üë‚ü©` lies in the same H-eigenspace as `|Œ¶‚Üë‚ü©` | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpPlus_pow_basisVec_const` | companion iterated form for `≈ú_tot^+`: for any constant `s : Fin 2` and any `k : ‚Ñï`, `H ¬∑ ((≈ú_tot^+)^k ¬∑ |s‚Ä¶s‚ü©) = c_J ¬∑ ((≈ú_tot^+)^k ¬∑ |s‚Ä¶s‚ü©)` (corollary of SU(2) invariance, Tasaki ¬ß2.4 (2.4.7), iterated) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_commute_totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}` | for any `J` and `Œ∏ : ‚Ñù`, `H` commutes with the global rotation `√õ^(Œ±)_Œ∏ = exp(-iŒ∏ ≈ú_tot^Œ±)` (composes `heisenbergHamiltonian_commutator_totalSpinHalfOp{Œ±}` with `totalSpinHalfRot{Œ±}_commute_of_commute`; the operator-level form of Tasaki ¬ß2.4 (2.4.7), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_basisVec_const` | for any `J`, `Œ∏`, and constant `s : Fin 2`, `H ¬∑ (√õ^(Œ±)_Œ∏ ¬∑ |s‚Ä¶s‚ü©) = c_J ¬∑ (√õ^(Œ±)_Œ∏ ¬∑ |s‚Ä¶s‚ü©)` ‚Äî the rotated (single-axis) constant-spin state shares the H-eigenvalue (Tasaki ¬ß2.4 (2.4.7), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfRot32_basisVec_const` | for any `J`, `Œ∏`, `œï`, and constant `s : Fin 2`, `H ¬∑ (√õ^(3)_œï ¬∑ √õ^(2)_Œ∏ ¬∑ |s‚Ä¶s‚ü©) = c_J ¬∑ (√õ^(3)_œï ¬∑ √õ^(2)_Œ∏ ¬∑ |s‚Ä¶s‚ü©)` ‚Äî the two-step spin-coherent state of Tasaki eq. (2.4.6) (`|Œû_Œ∏,œï‚ü©` for `s = 0`) is an H-eigenvector with the same eigenvalue as the constant configuration (Tasaki eq. (2.4.7), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_two_site_singlet` | `≈ú_tot¬≤ (|‚Üë‚Üì‚ü© - |‚Üì‚Üë‚ü©) = 0` for `Œõ = Fin 2` (singlet, `S = 0`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_two_site_triplet_zero` | `≈ú_tot¬≤ (|‚Üë‚Üì‚ü© + |‚Üì‚Üë‚ü©) = 2(|‚Üë‚Üì‚ü© + |‚Üì‚Üë‚ü©)` for `Œõ = Fin 2` (triplet `m = 0`, `S = 1`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp3_mulVec_two_site_singlet` | the two-site singlet has zero `≈ú_tot^(3)` magnetization | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `onSite_commutator_totalOnSite` | `[onSite x SŒ±, Œ£_z onSite z SŒ≤] = onSite x [SŒ±, SŒ≤]` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |

### One-dimensional open-chain quantum Ising

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ¬ß3.3 eq. (3.3.1), p. 55 (transverse-field Ising on an open
chain). Our formalization uses the Pauli convention `œÉ = 2¬∑S` and an
explicit bond coupling `J`.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `quantumIsingHamiltonian N J h` | `H = -J Œ£ œÉ^z_i œÉ^z_{i+1} - h Œ£ œÉ^x_i` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingHamiltonian_isHermitian` | `H` is Hermitian for real `J`, `h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `spinZDot x y` | the Ising bond operator `œÉ^z_x ¬∑ œÉ^z_y` (generic in `Œõ`) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `spinZDot_isHermitian` | each bond operator is Hermitian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianGeneric (J : Œõ ‚Üí Œõ ‚Üí ‚ÑÇ) (h : ‚ÑÇ)` | **graph-friendly** Ising Hamiltonian `Œ£ J(x,y) œÉ^z_x œÉ^z_y ‚àí h Œ£ œÉ^x_x` for any finite `Œõ` and pairwise coupling `J`; specialises to chain / graph / lattice via the choice of `J` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianGeneric_isHermitian` | Hermitian for entry-wise real `J` and real `h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianOnGraph G J h` | graph wrapper: `isingHamiltonianGeneric (couplingOf G J) h`; double-sum convention matches Heisenberg-on-graph | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianOnGraph_isHermitian` | Hermitian for real `J, h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingGibbsStateOnGraph G Œ≤ J h` | Gibbs state of the graph-built Ising Hamiltonian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingGibbsStateOnGraph_isHermitian` / `isingGibbsStateOnGraph_commute_hamiltonian` | Hermiticity (real `J, h`) and commute with the Hamiltonian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.sum_pathGraph_forward` / `sum_pathGraph_backward` / `sum_pathGraph_adj` | sum-decomposition helpers for `pathGraph (N+1)` adjacency: `Œ£_{x,y}` over ordered adjacent pairs = `Œ£_{i:Fin N} (f i.cs i.s + f i.s i.cs)` | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `pathGraphParityColoring` / `pathGraph_isBipartite` | parity-based 2-colouring of `pathGraph (N + 1)` (`i ‚Ü¶ i.val % 2`) and the corresponding `IsBipartite` proof. Underpins the N√©el state (`Quantum/NeelState.lean`) and the Marshall-Lieb-Mattis theorem (Tasaki ¬ß2.5) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `cycleGraphEvenParityColoring` / `cycleGraph_even_isBipartite` | parity-based 2-colouring of the even cycle `cycleGraph (2 * K + 2)` and the corresponding `IsBipartite` proof. Wrap-around case `(2K+1) + 1 ‚â° 0` still flips parity because the cycle length is even (odd cycles are not bipartite) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `quantumIsingHamiltonian_eq_isingHamiltonianGeneric` | **generic-N bridge**: `quantumIsingHamiltonian N J h = isingHamiltonianGeneric (couplingOf (pathGraph (N+1)) (-J/2)) h`. The proof itself is the robust regression test | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |

### Testing infrastructure

| Lean name / location | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `LatticeSystem/Tests/Sanity.lean` | sanity-check `example` blocks for concrete small-N instances (Pauli arithmetic, spin-1/2 ladder actions, Heisenberg / Ising Hermiticity on small chains, graph-centric bridge identities) |
| decide-based property tests (in `Tests/Sanity.lean`) | universally-quantified properties verified by `decide` on small finite types (graph adjacency symmetry / irreflexivity / connectivity on `pathGraph n` and `cycleGraph n` for small `n`); real proofs, no `sorry` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState Œ≤ J h N` | `gibbsState Œ≤ (quantumIsingHamiltonian N J h)` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_isHermitian` | the Ising-chain Gibbs state `œÅ_Œ≤` is Hermitian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_commute_hamiltonian` | `[œÅ_Œ≤, H_Ising] = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_zero` | infinite-temperature closed form `‚ü®A‚ü©_0 = (1/dim) ¬∑ Tr A` (independent of `J, h`) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_im_of_isHermitian` | for Hermitian `O`, `(‚ü®O‚ü©_Œ≤).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_commutator_hamiltonian` | conservation `‚ü®[H_Ising, A]‚ü©_Œ≤ = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_im` | `(‚ü®H_Ising‚ü©_Œ≤).im = 0` (energy expectation is real) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_mul_hamiltonian_im` | for Hermitian `O`, `(‚ü®H_Ising ¬∑ O‚ü©_Œ≤).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_sq_im` | `(‚ü®H_Ising^2‚ü©_Œ≤).im = 0` (energy-squared expectation real) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_pow_im` | `(‚ü®H_Ising^n‚ü©_Œ≤).im = 0` for any `n : ‚Ñï` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_anticommutator_im` | for Hermitian `A, B`, `(‚ü®A¬∑B + B¬∑A‚ü©_Œ≤).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_commutator_re` | for Hermitian `A, B`, `(‚ü®A¬∑B ‚àí B¬∑A‚ü©_Œ≤).re = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsHamiltonianVariance_im` | `(Var_Œ≤(H_Ising)).im = 0` (energy variance real) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsing_partitionFn_im` | `(partitionFn Œ≤ H_Ising).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_ofReal_re_eq` | for Hermitian `O`, `((‚ü®O‚ü©_Œ≤).re : ‚ÑÇ) = ‚ü®O‚ü©_Œ≤` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_pow_trace` | `Tr(œÅ_Œ≤^n) = Z(nŒ≤) / Z(Œ≤)^n` for the Ising Hamiltonian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_self_eq` | `‚ü®H_Ising‚ü©_Œ≤ = -J ¬∑ ‚àë ‚ü®œÉ^z œÉ^z‚ü©_Œ≤ + (-h) ¬∑ ‚àë ‚ü®œÉ^x‚ü©_Œ≤` (energy as bond + transverse-field decomposition) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |

### Gibbs state (Tasaki ¬ß3.3)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ¬ß3.3.

All theorems in this module are fully proved with **zero `sorry`**.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `gibbsExp Œ≤ H` | `exp(-Œ≤H) := Matrix.exp (-Œ≤ ‚Ä¢ H)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_isHermitian` | `exp(-Œ≤H)` is Hermitian (when `H` is Hermitian) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_zero` | `exp(-0¬∑H) = 1` (Tasaki ¬ß3.3, pp. 75‚Äì78) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_add` | `exp(-(Œ≤‚ÇÅ+Œ≤‚ÇÇ)H) = exp(-Œ≤‚ÇÅH) ¬∑ exp(-Œ≤‚ÇÇH)` (one-parameter group) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_add_of_commute_hamiltonians` | `exp(-Œ≤(H‚ÇÅ+H‚ÇÇ)) = exp(-Œ≤H‚ÇÅ) ¬∑ exp(-Œ≤H‚ÇÇ)` for commuting `H‚ÇÅ, H‚ÇÇ` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_neg_mul_self` | `exp(Œ≤H) ¬∑ exp(-Œ≤H) = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_self_mul_neg` | `exp(-Œ≤H) ¬∑ exp(Œ≤H) = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_isUnit` | `exp(-Œ≤H)` is invertible | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_ne_zero` | `exp(-Œ≤H) ‚âÝ 0` (corollary of `gibbsExp_isUnit`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_ne_zero` | `œÅ_Œ≤ ‚âÝ 0` when `Z(Œ≤) ‚âÝ 0` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_inv` | `(œÅ_Œ≤)‚Åª¬π = Z(Œ≤) ¬∑ e^{Œ≤H}` when `Z(Œ≤) ‚âÝ 0` (general matrix inverse, generalises `gibbsState_zero_inv`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_smul_gibbsState_eq_gibbsExp` | `Z(Œ≤) ¬∑ œÅ_Œ≤ = e^{-Œ≤H}` when `Z(Œ≤) ‚âÝ 0` (canonical rescaled identity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_mul_gibbsExpectation_eq` | `Z(Œ≤) ¬∑ ‚ü®A‚ü©_Œ≤ = Tr(e^{-Œ≤H} ¬∑ A)` when `Z(Œ≤) ‚âÝ 0` (canonical unnormalised expectation) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_natCast_mul` | `exp(-(n¬∑Œ≤)H) = (exp(-Œ≤H))^n` for `n : ‚Ñï` (exact discrete semigroup identity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_two_mul` | `exp(-(2Œ≤)H) = exp(-Œ≤H) ¬∑ exp(-Œ≤H)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_inv` | `(exp(-Œ≤H))‚Åª¬π = exp(Œ≤H)` (matrix inverse made explicit) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_intCast_mul` | `exp(-(n¬∑Œ≤)H) = (exp(-Œ≤H))^n` for `n : ‚Ñ§` (integer-power extension) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn Œ≤ H` | `Z := Matrix.trace (exp(-Œ≤H))` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_zero` | `Z(0) = Fintype.card (Œõ ‚Üí Fin 2)` (dimension of the Hilbert space) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_zero_ne_zero` | `Z(0) ‚âÝ 0` (concrete sorry-free proof that the partition function is nonzero at Œ≤ = 0) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.trace_im` | for any Hermitian `A : Matrix n n ‚ÑÇ`, `A.trace.im = 0` (generic helper; relocated to the matrix-analysis layer in PR #4344) | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |
| `partitionFn_im_of_isHermitian` | for Hermitian `H`, `(partitionFn Œ≤ H).im = 0` (Z is real) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_mul_self_trace` | `Tr(œÅ_Œ≤¬≤) = Z(2Œ≤) / Z(Œ≤)¬≤` (purity / R√©nyi-2 entropy precursor) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_pow_trace` | `Tr(œÅ_Œ≤^n) = Z(nŒ≤) / Z(Œ≤)^n` for any `n : ‚Ñï` (R√©nyi-n entropy precursor) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_zero` | `œÅ_0 = (1/dim) ¬∑ I` (maximally mixed state at infinite temperature) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_zero_inv` | `œÅ_0‚Åª¬π = dim ¬∑ I` (matrix inverse at Œ≤ = 0) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_zero` | `‚ü®A‚ü©_0 = (1/dim) ¬∑ Tr A` (high-temperature limit) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState Œ≤ H` | `œÅ := (1/Z) ‚Ä¢ exp(-Œ≤H)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_trace` | `Tr(œÅ) = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_isHermitian` | `œÅ` is Hermitian | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation Œ≤ H O` | `‚ü®O‚ü© := Matrix.trace (œÅ * O)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_one` | `‚ü®1‚ü© = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_add` | `‚ü®O‚ÇÅ + O‚ÇÇ‚ü© = ‚ü®O‚ÇÅ‚ü© + ‚ü®O‚ÇÇ‚ü©` (linearity in observable) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_smul` | `‚ü®c ¬∑ O‚ü© = c ¬∑ ‚ü®O‚ü©` (scalar linearity, `c : ‚ÑÇ`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_neg` | `‚ü®-O‚ü© = -‚ü®O‚ü©` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_sub` | `‚ü®A - B‚ü© = ‚ü®A‚ü© - ‚ü®B‚ü©` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_sum` | `‚ü®‚àë i ‚àà s, f i‚ü© = ‚àë i ‚àà s, ‚ü®f i‚ü©` (finite-sum linearity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_commute_hamiltonian` | `[exp(-Œ≤H), H] = 0` (Tasaki ¬ß3.3, p. 80) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_commute_hamiltonian` | `[œÅ_Œ≤, H] = 0`, i.e. `œÅ_Œ≤` is stationary under the dynamics generated by `H` (Tasaki ¬ß3.3, p. 80) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `Matrix.trace_mul_star_of_isHermitian` | `star (Tr(A ¬∑ B)) = Tr(A ¬∑ B)` for Hermitian `A, B : Matrix n n ‚ÑÇ` (algebraic core, Gibbs-independent; relocated to the matrix-analysis layer in PR #4344) | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_star_of_isHermitian` | `star ‚ü®O‚ü©_Œ≤ = ‚ü®O‚ü©_Œ≤` for Hermitian `H`, `O` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_im_of_isHermitian` | `(‚ü®O‚ü©_Œ≤).im = 0` for Hermitian `H`, `O` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_ofReal_re_eq_of_isHermitian` | `((‚ü®O‚ü©_Œ≤).re : ‚ÑÇ) = ‚ü®O‚ü©_Œ≤` for Hermitian `H`, `O` (real-cast equality) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_mul_hamiltonian_comm` | `‚ü®H ¬∑ A‚ü©_Œ≤ = ‚ü®A ¬∑ H‚ü©_Œ≤` for any `A` (Tasaki ¬ß3.3, p. 80) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_mul_comm_of_commute_hamiltonian` | for any conserved `A` (`[A, H] = 0`), `‚ü®A ¬∑ O‚ü©_Œ≤ = ‚ü®O ¬∑ A‚ü©_Œ≤` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_commutator_eq_zero_of_commute_hamiltonian` | for any conserved `A`, `‚ü®A ¬∑ O ‚àí O ¬∑ A‚ü©_Œ≤ = 0` (selection rule) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_commutator_hamiltonian` | `‚ü®[H, A]‚ü©_Œ≤ = 0` (conservation law) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_im` | `(‚ü®H‚ü©_Œ≤).im = 0` for Hermitian `H` (real energy expectation) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_sq_im_of_isHermitian` | `(‚ü®O ¬∑ O‚ü©_Œ≤).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, O` (second-moment realness, variance precursor) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_pow_im_of_isHermitian` | `(‚ü®O^n‚ü©_Œ≤).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, O`, any `n : ‚Ñï` (all natural-power moments real) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance Œ≤ H O` | `Var_Œ≤(O) := ‚ü®O ¬∑ O‚ü©_Œ≤ ‚àí ‚ü®O‚ü©_Œ≤¬≤` (canonical-ensemble variance) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_eq` | unfolding lemma for `gibbsVariance` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_im_of_isHermitian` | `(Var_Œ≤(O)).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, O` (variance is real) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_zero` | at Œ≤ = 0, `Var_0(O) = (1/dim) ¬∑ Tr(O¬≤) ‚àí ((1/dim) ¬∑ Tr O)¬≤` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_eq_centered_sq` | `Var_Œ≤(O) = ‚ü®(O ‚àí ‚ü®O‚ü©_Œ≤ ¬∑ 1) ¬∑ (O ‚àí ‚ü®O‚ü©_Œ≤ ¬∑ 1)‚ü©_Œ≤` (centered-square form, `Z ‚âÝ 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance Œ≤ H A B` | `Cov_Œ≤(A, B) := ‚ü®A ¬∑ B‚ü©_Œ≤ ‚àí ‚ü®A‚ü©_Œ≤ ¬∑ ‚ü®B‚ü©_Œ≤` (canonical-ensemble complex covariance) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_eq` | unfolding lemma for `gibbsCovariance` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_self_eq_variance` | `Cov_Œ≤(O, O) = Var_Œ≤(O)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_sub_swap_eq_commutator` | `Cov_Œ≤(A, B) ‚àí Cov_Œ≤(B, A) = ‚ü®A ¬∑ B ‚àí B ¬∑ A‚ü©_Œ≤` (antisymmetric part = commutator expectation) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_add_left` | `Cov_Œ≤(A‚ÇÅ + A‚ÇÇ, B) = Cov_Œ≤(A‚ÇÅ, B) + Cov_Œ≤(A‚ÇÇ, B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_add_right` | `Cov_Œ≤(A, B‚ÇÅ + B‚ÇÇ) = Cov_Œ≤(A, B‚ÇÅ) + Cov_Œ≤(A, B‚ÇÇ)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_smul_left` | `Cov_Œ≤(c ‚Ä¢ A, B) = c ¬∑ Cov_Œ≤(A, B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_smul_right` | `Cov_Œ≤(A, c ‚Ä¢ B) = c ¬∑ Cov_Œ≤(A, B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_const_smul_one_left/right_eq_zero` | `Cov_Œ≤(c ‚Ä¢ 1, B) = 0` and `Cov_Œ≤(A, c ‚Ä¢ 1) = 0` (when `Z ‚âÝ 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_const_smul_one_left/right_eq_zero` | `Cov^s_Œ≤(c ‚Ä¢ 1, B) = 0` and `Cov^s_Œ≤(A, c ‚Ä¢ 1) = 0` (when `Z ‚âÝ 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm Œ≤ H A B` | `Cov^s_Œ≤(A, B) := (1/2) ¬∑ ‚ü®A ¬∑ B + B ¬∑ A‚ü©_Œ≤ ‚àí ‚ü®A‚ü©_Œ≤ ¬∑ ‚ü®B‚ü©_Œ≤` (symmetric / real-valued covariance) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_self_eq_variance` | `Cov^s_Œ≤(O, O) = Var_Œ≤(O)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_im_of_isHermitian` | `(Cov^s_Œ≤(A, B)).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, A, B` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_comm` | `Cov^s_Œ≤(A, B) = Cov^s_Œ≤(B, A)` (symmetric in observables) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_add_left/right` | additivity of `Cov^s_Œ≤` in each argument | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_smul_left/right` | scalar pull-out from each argument of `Cov^s_Œ≤` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_add` | `Var_Œ≤(A + B) = Var_Œ≤(A) + Var_Œ≤(B) + 2 ¬∑ Cov^s_Œ≤(A, B)` (sum-of-observables variance identity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_one` | `Var_Œ≤(1) = 0` (when `Z ‚âÝ 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_smul` | `Var_Œ≤(c ‚Ä¢ A) = c¬≤ ¬∑ Var_Œ≤(A)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_smul_one` | `Var_Œ≤(c ‚Ä¢ 1) = 0` (when `Z ‚âÝ 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_neg` | `Var_Œ≤(‚àíA) = Var_Œ≤(A)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_add_const_smul_one` | `Var_Œ≤(A + c ‚Ä¢ 1) = Var_Œ≤(A)` (when `Z ‚âÝ 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_eq_symm_add_half_commutator` | `Cov_Œ≤(A, B) = Cov^s_Œ≤(A, B) + (1/2) ¬∑ ‚ü®A ¬∑ B ‚àí B ¬∑ A‚ü©_Œ≤` (symmetric / antisymmetric decomposition) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_eq_half_add_swap` | `Cov^s_Œ≤(A, B) = (1/2) ¬∑ (Cov_Œ≤(A, B) + Cov_Œ≤(B, A))` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_eq_symm_of_commute` | for commuting `A, B`, `Cov_Œ≤(A, B) = Cov^s_Œ≤(A, B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `Matrix.trace_mul_conjTranspose_swap_of_isHermitian` | `star Tr(œÅ ¬∑ X) = Tr(œÅ ¬∑ X·¥¥)` for Hermitian `œÅ` (generic helper) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_star_swap_of_isHermitian` | `star ‚ü®A ¬∑ B‚ü©_Œ≤ = ‚ü®B ¬∑ A‚ü©_Œ≤` for Hermitian `H, A, B` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_anticommutator_im` | `(‚ü®A¬∑B + B¬∑A‚ü©_Œ≤).im = 0` (anticommutator is real) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_commutator_re` | `(‚ü®A¬∑B ‚àí B¬∑A‚ü©_Œ≤).re = 0` (commutator is purely imaginary) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_mul_hamiltonian_im` | `(‚ü®H ¬∑ O‚ü©_Œ≤).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, O` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |

### Heisenberg chain (Tasaki ¬ß3.5)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ¬ß3.5, p. 89.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.couplingOf G J` | the canonical pairwise coupling `Œõ ‚Üí Œõ ‚Üí ‚ÑÇ` of a `SimpleGraph G` with uniform edge weight `J`: returns `J` on edges of `G`, zero otherwise (graph-centric bridge) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.couplingOf_symm` / `_self` / `_real` | symmetry (from `G.Adj` symmetry), vanishing on the diagonal (from irreflexivity), and reality (for real edge weight) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.pathGraph_adj_iff` / `cycleGraph_adj_iff` | path / cycle graph adjacency in the explicit `x.val + 1 = y.val ‚à® ...` form used elsewhere in the codebase | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling N J` | coupling `Fin (N+1) ‚Üí Fin (N+1) ‚Üí ‚ÑÇ`: returns `-J` on nearest-neighbour bonds, zero otherwise | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainCoupling N J` | coupling `Fin (N+2) ‚Üí Fin (N+2) ‚Üí ‚ÑÇ`: returns `-J` on nearest-neighbour bonds (mod N+2), zero otherwise | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling_eq_couplingOf` | the open-chain coupling is `couplingOf (pathGraph (N+1)) (-J)` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainCoupling_eq_couplingOf` | the periodic-chain coupling is `couplingOf (cycleGraph (N+2)) (-J)` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_isHermitian_of_real_symm` | for any real symmetric coupling `J` the Heisenberg Hamiltonian `H = Œ£_{x,y} J(x,y) ≈ú_x ¬∑ ≈ú_y` is Hermitian | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_couplingOf_isHermitian` | **graph-centric** Hermiticity: for any `SimpleGraph G` and real edge weight `J : ‚ÑÇ`, the Heisenberg Hamiltonian `heisenbergHamiltonian (couplingOf G J)` is Hermitian. The chain instances are corollaries via the bridge theorems | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianOnGraph G J` | named wrapper = `heisenbergHamiltonian (couplingOf G J)` (parallel to `isingHamiltonianOnGraph`) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianOnGraph_isHermitian` / `_commute_totalSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}` / `_commute_totalSpinHalfSquared` | corollaries re-exposed under the named wrapper | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergGibbsState_eq_onGraph` / `periodicChainHeisenbergGibbsState_eq_onGraph` | rfl bridges: chain Gibbs = graph Gibbs on pathGraph/cycleGraph | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_eq_isingGibbsStateOnGraph` | chain Ising Gibbs = `isingGibbsStateOnGraph (pathGraph (N+1)) Œ≤ (-J/2) h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingCycleGibbsState_commute_hamiltonian` | the periodic Ising Gibbs state commutes with the periodic Ising Hamiltonian (free corollary of `gibbsState_commute_hamiltonian`) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingCycleGibbsExpectation_zero` / `_im_of_isHermitian` / `_commutator_hamiltonian` / `_hamiltonian_im` / `_hamiltonian_pow_im` / `isingCycle_partitionFn_im` / `_ofReal_re_eq` / `isingCycleGibbsState_pow_trace` | periodic-Ising expectation companions of the open-chain `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation*` family: Œ≤ = 0 closed form, real-valuedness for Hermitian observables, conservation `‚ü®[H, A]‚ü© = 0`, energy / energy-power expectations real, partition-function real, real-cast `((‚ü®O‚ü©_Œ≤).re : ‚ÑÇ) = ‚ü®O‚ü©_Œ≤`, R√©nyi-n trace `Tr(œÅ_Œ≤^n) = Z(nŒ≤) / Z(Œ≤)^n` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `hubbardGibbsStateOnGraph N Œ≤ G J U` | Gibbs state of the graph-built Hubbard Hamiltonian | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardGibbsStateOnGraph_isHermitian` / `_commute_hamiltonian` | Hermiticity / commute corollaries | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardChainGibbsState_eq_onGraph` | rfl bridge: `hubbardChainGibbsState = hubbardGibbsStateOnGraph (pathGraph (N+1)) (-J) U` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `jwStringAbstract i` | Jordan-Wigner string for any `[Fintype Œõ] [LinearOrder Œõ]` ‚Äî product of `œÉ^z_j` over `j < i`; generalises the Fin-specific `jwString` | `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean` |
| `jwStringAbstract_isHermitian` / `jwStringAbstract_sq` / `jwStringAbstract_commute_onSite` | basic structural identities | `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean` |
| `fermionAnnihilationAbstract i` / `fermionCreationAbstract i` / `fermionNumberAbstract i` | abstract-Œõ annihilation / creation / number; rfl-bridges to the Fin-specific versions | `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean` |
| `fermionAnnihilationAbstract_conjTranspose` / `fermionCreationAbstract_conjTranspose` / `fermionNumberAbstract_isHermitian` | adjoint relations and Hermiticity in the abstract form | `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean` |
| `fermionAnnihilationAbstract_sq` / `fermionCreationAbstract_sq` | `c_i¬≤ = 0`, `c_i‚ÄÝ¬≤ = 0` (Pauli exclusion) | `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnticommAbstract_self` / `fermionNumberAbstract_sq` | `{c_i, c_i‚ÄÝ} = 1` and `n_i¬≤ = n_i` (abstract same-site CAR + idempotency) | `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.boxProd_decidableAdj` | `DecidableRel (G ‚ñ° H).Adj` for the box product (mathlib provides only the bare definition); enables 2D / nD lattices via `couplingOf` | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.integerChainGraph` | the **infinite** one-dimensional chain `‚Ñ§` as a `SimpleGraph` (= `hasse ‚Ñ§`), the infinite-volume analogue of `pathGraph (N+1)` | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.integerChainGraph_adj_iff` | adjacency: `a ~ b ‚Üî b = a + 1 ‚à® a = b + 1` | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.integerSquareLatticeGraph` | the **infinite** 2D square lattice on `‚Ñ§ √ó ‚Ñ§` as `integerChainGraph ‚ñ° integerChainGraph`; infinite analogue of `squareLatticeCoupling` | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.integerSquareLatticeGraph_adj_iff` | adjacency: nearest neighbour in one coordinate, equal in the other | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.hypercubicLatticeGraph` / `hypercubicLatticeGraph_adj` | the **infinite** `d`-dimensional hypercubic lattice `‚Ñ§·µà` on `Fin d ‚Üí ‚Ñ§` as a `SimpleGraph` (adjacency = differ in exactly one coordinate by `¬±1`, the nearest-neighbor bond set `B‚àû`, Tasaki eq. (4.3.1)) + a `DecidableRel` instance; the substrate of the thermodynamic-limit / infinite-volume frontier (Issue #4557) | `Lattice/HypercubicLattice.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.hypercubicBox` / `mem_hypercubicBox` | the centered finite box `Œõ_n = {x ‚àà ‚Ñ§·µà : ‚àín < x·µ¢ ‚â§ n}` (even side `2n`, Tasaki eq. (3.1.2)) as a `Finset`, with its coordinatewise membership criterion | `Lattice/HypercubicLattice.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.hypercubicBox_subset_succ` / `hypercubicBox_monotone` / `exists_mem_hypercubicBox` / `iUnion_hypercubicBox` | the **monotone exhaustion** of `‚Ñ§·µà` by the boxes: nesting `Œõ_n ‚äÜ Œõ_{n+1}`, monotonicity, every site lies in some box, and `‚ãÉ‚Çô Œõ_n = ‚Ñ§·µà`; the increasing-region API for constructing the thermodynamic limit | `Lattice/HypercubicLattice.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.hypercubicBox_card` | the box **volume** `\|Œõ_n\| = (2n)·µà` (side `2n` in each of `d` directions); the volume normalization `L·µà = (2n)·µà` of the bulk-density / energy-density limits (Tasaki eqs. (4.3.4), (4.3.6)) | `Lattice/HypercubicLattice.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.hypercubicBoxGraph` / `hypercubicBoxVertex` / `hypercubicBoxGraph_adj` | the **finite-volume graph** on the box `Œõ_n` (a `Fintype` subtype of `‚Ñ§·µà`): the induced subgraph `(hypercubicLatticeGraph d).induce ‚ÜëŒõ_n`, the graph-centric finite-volume substrate `Œõ_n ‚Ü™ ‚Ñ§·µà` of the thermodynamic limit | `Lattice/HypercubicLattice.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.hypercubicBoxCoupling` / `hypercubicBoxParityColoring` / `hypercubicBoxGraph_isBipartite` | the uniform nearest-neighbor coupling `couplingOf` on `Œõ_n` (finite-volume many-body Hamiltonian input), and the inherited bipartite structure (parity coloring restricted from `‚Ñ§·µà`) | `Lattice/HypercubicLattice.lean` |
| `boxHeisenbergHamiltonianS` / `boxAFMHeisenbergHamiltonianS` (+ `_isHermitian`, `_eq_heisenbergHamiltonianS_boxCoupling`) | the **concrete finite-volume spin-`S` Heisenberg model** on the box `Œõ_n ‚äÇ ‚Ñ§·µà` (`ManyBodyOpS (hypercubicBoxVertex d n) N`), via the graph Hamiltonian on `hypercubicBoxGraph`; the AFM specialization `ƒ§_{Œõ_n} = Œ£_‚ü®x,y‚ü© ≈ú_x¬∑≈ú_y` (`J=1/2`, Tasaki unordered-bond convention) is the finite-volume model whose `L‚Üë‚àû` limit is the ¬ß4.3 `InfiniteSpinSystem`. Hermitian for real `J`; ties to PR5's `hypercubicBoxCoupling` by `rfl`. Axiom-free | `Quantum/SpinS/HypercubicBoxModel.lean` |
| `boxGroundEnergyS` / `boxGroundEnergyS_le_eigenvalues` / `boxBondCount` / `boxGroundEnergyDensityS` | **finite-volume ground-state energy** `E_{GS,n}` (least eigenvalue of the Hermitian box AFM Hamiltonian, via `hermitianMinEigenvalue`), the **bond count** `\|B_n\|` (edges of `hypercubicBoxGraph`), and the **energy density** `E_{GS,n}/\|B_n\|` (Tasaki ¬ß4.3 eq. (4.3.4)) ‚Äî the provable finite-volume groundwork for the thermodynamic-limit bridge (Issue #4564). Axiom-free | `Quantum/SpinS/HypercubicBoxModel.lean` |
| `boxBondCount_pos` / `boxGroundEnergyDensityS_tendsto` | `boxBondCount_pos` (**proved**): for `0 < d`, `1 ‚â§ n` the box has ‚â•1 bond (origin & `e_i` adjacent), so the energy-density denominator is positive on the tail (non-vacuity). `boxGroundEnergyDensityS_tendsto` (**documented AXIOM**, Tasaki ¬ß4.3 eq. (4.3.4)): for `0 < d`, `0 < N` the finite-volume per-bond ground-state energy density converges as `n‚Üí‚àû` to the infinite-volume energy density `Œµ_GS(d,N)` (existentially pinned to the genuine limit ‚Äî the thermodynamic-limit existence is the deep analytic content) | `Quantum/SpinS/HypercubicBoxModel.lean` |
| `boxGroundEnergyDensitySLimit` / `boxGroundEnergyDensityS_tendsto_limit` / `IsAFMThermodynamicLimit` / `afmThermodynamicLimit_energyDensity` / `afmThermodynamicLimit_exists_omega0` | **thermodynamic-limit bridge** (Issue #4564 capstone): names the box energy-density limit `Œµ_GS(d,N)` (`boxGroundEnergyDensitySLimit`, proved to be the limit); the **documented predicate** `IsAFMThermodynamicLimit S N` (`S` is the `L‚Üë‚àû` limit of the concrete box AFM model ‚Äî not an identification of matrix algebras with the abstract C*-algebra); the **documented AXIOM** `afmThermodynamicLimit_energyDensity` (such `S`'s abstract energy density = the concrete box limit, Tasaki eq. (4.3.4)); the **PROVED** `afmThermodynamicLimit_exists_omega0` (symmetric infinite-volume ground state `œâ_0` exists for the box AFM model's limit, via Theorem 4.20, eqs. (4.3.7)/(4.3.9)); and the **PROVED** `afmThermodynamicLimit_exists_omegaN` (the symmetry-breaking ground states `œâ_n` with N√©el magnetization `(‚àí1)^x m‚àó n_Œ±`, given `HasStaggeredLRO`, eqs. (4.3.8)/(4.3.10) ‚Äî vacuous in 1D) | `Quantum/SpinS/HypercubicBoxThermodynamicLimit.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.hypercubicLatticeGraph_adj_parity_ne` / `hypercubicEvenSublattice` / `hypercubicOddSublattice` | a nearest-neighbor bond **flips the coordinate-sum parity**; the even sublattice `‚Ñ§·µà_even = {x : Œ£·µ¢ x·µ¢ even}` (A-sublattice, Tasaki eq. (4.3.2)) and its odd complement (B-sublattice) | `Lattice/HypercubicLattice.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.hypercubicLatticeGraph_isBipartiteWith` / `hypercubicParityColoring` / `hypercubicLatticeGraph_isBipartite` | `‚Ñ§·µà` is **bipartite** with the even/odd sublattices as the two parts (`IsBipartiteWith`), via the parity 2-coloring `Coloring (Fin 2)`; hence `IsBipartite` (`Colorable 2`) ‚Äî the combinatorial structure underlying antiferromagnetic / N√©el order | `Lattice/HypercubicLattice.lean` |
| `squareLatticeCoupling N J`, `squareLatticeHeisenberg_isHermitian` | the 2D open-boundary square lattice on `Fin (N+1) √ó Fin (N+1)` realised as `couplingOf (pathGraph (N+1) ‚ñ° pathGraph (N+1)) (-J)`; Hermiticity is a one-line corollary of the graph-generic theorem above | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `squareTorusCoupling N J`, `squareTorusHeisenberg_isHermitian` | the 2D periodic square lattice (discrete torus) on `Fin (N+2) √ó Fin (N+2)` realised as `couplingOf (cycleGraph (N+2) ‚ñ° cycleGraph (N+2)) (-J)`; Hermiticity is a one-line corollary | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `cubicLatticeCoupling N J`, `cubicLatticeHeisenberg_isHermitian` | the 3D open-boundary cubic lattice on `Fin (N+1)^3` realised as `couplingOf (pathGraph (N+1) ‚ñ° pathGraph (N+1) ‚ñ° pathGraph (N+1)) (-J)`; Hermiticity is a one-line corollary | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `squareLatticeHeisenbergGibbsState` / `_isHermitian` / `_commute_hamiltonian` | Gibbs state of the 2D open-boundary square-lattice Heisenberg Hamiltonian + Hermiticity + commute pair | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `squareTorusHeisenbergGibbsState` / `_isHermitian` / `_commute_hamiltonian` | Gibbs state of the 2D torus Heisenberg Hamiltonian + companions | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |

| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_eq` | for `N = 1` (the 2-site open chain on `Fin 2`), `H_open = -2J ¬∑ spinHalfDot 0 1` (explicit one-bond reduction; Tasaki ¬ß2.4 simplest concrete instance) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `H_open(N=1) ¬∑ |‚Üë‚Üë‚ü© = -(J/2) ¬∑ |‚Üë‚Üë‚ü©` ‚Äî `|‚Üë‚Üë‚ü©` lies in the `S = 1` triplet sector and is an exact eigenvector with eigenvalue `-J/2` (this is the ferromagnetic ground state for `J < 0`) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_singlet` | `H_open(N=1) ¬∑ (|‚Üë‚Üì‚ü© - |‚Üì‚Üë‚ü©) = (3J/2) ¬∑ (|‚Üë‚Üì‚ü© - |‚Üì‚Üë‚ü©)` ‚Äî singlet eigenvalue, the antiferromagnetic ground state for `J > 0` (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 concrete instance) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_all_down` | `H_open(N=1) ¬∑ |‚Üì‚Üì‚ü© = -(J/2) ¬∑ |‚Üì‚Üì‚ü©` ‚Äî all-down state has the same eigenvalue as all-up (both are `S = 1` triplet states) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_triplet_zero` | `H_open(N=1) ¬∑ (|‚Üë‚Üì‚ü© + |‚Üì‚Üë‚ü©) = -(J/2) ¬∑ (|‚Üë‚Üì‚ü© + |‚Üì‚Üë‚ü©)` ‚Äî triplet `m = 0` state, completing the 3-fold degenerate triplet representation `S = 1` with eigenvalue `-J/2` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_three_site_eq` | for `N = 2` (the 3-site open chain on `Fin 3`, 2 bonds), `H_open = -2J ¬∑ (spinHalfDot 0 1 + spinHalfDot 1 2)` ‚Äî explicit two-bond reduction | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_three_site_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `H_open(N=2) ¬∑ |‚Üë‚Üë‚Üë‚ü© = -J ¬∑ |‚Üë‚Üë‚Üë‚ü©` ‚Äî confirming the linear scaling `E(|‚Üë..‚Üë‚ü©) = -N¬∑J/2` (here `N = 2` bonds, `J = 1` per bond) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling_sum_eq` | for any `N : ‚Ñï`, `Œ£_{x,y ‚àà Fin (N+1)} openChainCoupling N J x y = -(2N ¬∑ J : ‚ÑÇ)` (the bond-counting lemma: each of the `N` unordered nearest-neighbour bonds is counted in both orientations) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_const` | for any `N : ‚Ñï`, `J : ‚Ñù`, and constant `s : Fin 2`, `H_open ¬∑ |s..s‚ü© = -(N¬∑J/2 : ‚ÑÇ) ¬∑ |s..s‚ü©` ‚Äî both `s = 0` (all-up) and `s = 1` (all-down) share the same eigenvalue by SU(2) symmetry | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `s = 0` specialisation of the above (Tasaki ¬ß2.4 (2.4.5)/(2.4.1) ferromagnetic ground-state energy `E_GS = -|B|¬∑S¬≤` for `S = 1/2`, `|B| = N` bonds) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_all_down` | `s = 1` specialisation: same eigenvalue `-(N¬∑J/2)` for the all-down state by SU(2) symmetry | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpMinus_pow_basisVec_all_up` | for any `N : ‚Ñï`, `J : ‚Ñù`, `k : ‚Ñï`, `H_open ¬∑ ((≈útot^-)^k ¬∑ |‚Üë..‚Üë‚ü©) = -(N¬∑J/2 : ‚ÑÇ) ¬∑ ((≈útot^-)^k ¬∑ |‚Üë..‚Üë‚ü©)` ‚Äî the unnormalised Tasaki ¬ß2.4 (2.4.9) ferromagnetic ground states `|Œ¶_M‚ü©` made explicit on the chain (combines PRs #82 + #98) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpPlus_pow_basisVec_all_down` | dual ladder from `|‚Üì..‚Üì‚ü©`, same eigenvalue `-(N¬∑J/2)` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | `H_open` preserves every magnetisation subspace `H_M` (chain specialisation of PR #91) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | `H_periodic` preserves every magnetisation subspace `H_M` (chain specialisation of PR #91) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |

### Perron-Frobenius theorem (`Math/PerronFrobenius.lean`, `Math/PerronFrobeniusPrimitive.lean`, `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean`, `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean`)

Perron-Frobenius theorem for nonneg irreducible/primitive matrices (Issue #405, closed).
The sorry in `exists_pos_eigenvec_max` is eliminated via the Collatz-Wielandt port (PRs A‚ÄìC).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `Matrix.IsPrimitive.of_irreducible_pos_diagonal` | irreducible nonneg + positive diagonal ‚Üí primitive (Seneta ¬ß1.1, Prop. 1.3, p. 17) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusPrimitive.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.collatzWielandtFn` | CW function `min_{i\|x_i>0} (Ax)_i/x_i` (Seneta ¬ß1.2, p. 27) | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.le_any_ratio` | `CW(x) ‚â§ (Ax)_i/x_i` for `x_i > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.le_mulVec` | fundamental inequality `CW(x)¬∑x ‚â§ Ax` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.smul_eq` | scale invariance `CW(cx) = CW(x)` for `c > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.upperSemicontinuousOn` | CW is upper-semicontinuous on stdSimplex | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.exists_maximizer` | CW attains its max on stdSimplex (EVT for USC, Seneta ¬ß1.2, p. 28) | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.eq_eigenvalue` | `CW(v) = r` when `Av = r¬∑v`, `v > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.lt_of_all_ratios_gt` | all ratios `> c` ‚üπ `CW(x) > c` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.pos_of_nonneg_eigenvec` | irreducible nonneg + `Av = Œºv`, `v ‚â• 0`, `v ‚âÝ 0` ‚üπ `v > 0` (standard propagation argument) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.exists_positive_eigenvector_of_primitive` | primitive nonneg ‚üπ ‚àÉ `r > 0`, `v > 0` with `Av = rv` (Seneta ¬ß1.2, pp. 27‚Äì28) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.exists_positive_eigenvector_of_irreducible` | irreducible nonneg ‚üπ ‚àÉ `r > 0`, `v > 0` with `Av = rv` (Seneta ¬ß1.2, pp. 27‚Äì28) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `exists_pos_eigenvec_max` | (**sorry-free**) irreducible nonneg Hermitian ‚üπ max eigenvalue has strictly positive eigenvector | `Math/PerronFrobenius.lean` |
| `pos_eigenvec_unique` | strictly positive eigenvector unique up to positive scalar | `Math/PerronFrobenius.lean` |
| `PerronFrobenius.eigenvec_proportional_of_pos_eigenvec` | **geometric simplicity of the Perron eigenvalue**: irreducible nonneg with a strictly positive eigenvector `v` at `Œº` ‚üπ every real eigenvector at `Œº` is `s ‚Ä¢ v` for some scalar `s` (the `Œº`-eigenspace is 1-dimensional). Perturbation trick: `v + t ‚Ä¢ u > 0` for small `t > 0` is an eigenvector at `Œº`, hence `‚àù v` by `pos_eigenvec_unique`, forcing `u ‚àù v`. Used to show a Perron ground state is a joint eigenvector of any commuting operator (e.g. the total Casimir) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusSimple.lean` |

References: E. Seneta, *Non-negative Matrices and Markov Chains* (3rd ed.), Springer 2006, ¬ß1.2 (pp. 27‚Äì28);
or4nge19/MCMC: `MCMC/PF/LinearAlgebra/Matrix/PerronFrobenius/`.

### Spin-`S` Marshall‚ÄìLieb‚ÄìMattis on the magnetization sector (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.2 generic S, sector form)

> **Route correction (2026-05-26).** The earlier "saturated-ladder-iterate"
> route toward `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` was found **unsound**: it reduced the
> theorem to a leaf hypothesis (lowerable attach-sum dominance) that is **false**
> ‚Äî the ferromagnetic ladder iterate `(≈ú‚Åª_tot)^M|all-up‚ü©` is *not* the
> Marshall-positive antiferromagnetic ground state (concrete counterexample:
> spin-1/2, `V={a,b}`, `A={a}`, `B={b}`, `M=0‚Üí1`). The 40 conditional-wrapper
> modules of that route were removed; their detailed rows below were deleted with
> them. The **sound** route keeps the per-sector Perron‚ÄìFrobenius ground state
> (`marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergHamiltonianS_groundState_full`, sorry-free) and chains sectors by
> SU(2) invariance `[ƒ§, ≈ú‚Åª_tot] = 0`; some rows below still reference helper
> lemmas from the removed route pending re-documentation. See
> `.self-local/docs/tasaki-2-5-pf-route-design.md` and Issue #3542.

Generic-spin (`N = 2S`) version of Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.2 applied to the
**magnetization-`M` sector** of the un-dressed antiferromagnetic
Heisenberg Hamiltonian on a bipartite graph. The sector subtype
`magConfigS V N M := { œÉ : V ‚Üí Fin (N + 1) // magSumS œÉ = M }` is the
natural index type since the dressed Heisenberg matrix is irreducible
on each sector. The core sector-matrix API lives in
`Quantum/SpinS/DressedMatrixOnMagSector.lean`; the trailing
eigenvalue-uniqueness + complex-form MLM Theorem 2.2 statements
were moved to the companion
`Quantum/SpinS/DressedMatrixOnMagSectorEigenvalueUnique.lean` in
refactor #30 (PR #2829). The two bundled sector-level MLM Theorem 2.2
ground-state results (`marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergSector_groundState`,
`marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergSector_complexGroundState_full`) were
subsequently relocated to
`Quantum/SpinS/MarshallLiebMattisSectorBundled.lean` (#4569, re-proved via the
canonical `*Structural*` twins). Tracked in Issue #412.

| Lean name | Statement |
|---|---|
| `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_succ` | **Sound PF adjacent-sector ladder link (lowering)**: if a magnetization-`M` sector vector `magSectorEmbedding Œ¶` is a Heisenberg eigenvector at energy `Œº` and a total-Casimir eigenvector at `Œ≥` away from the sector's lowering-kernel value `(card¬∑N/2‚àíM)(card¬∑N/2‚àíM‚àí1)`, then `≈ú‚Åª_tot ¬∑ magSectorEmbedding Œ¶` is a Heisenberg eigenvector at the **same** `Œº` (via `[ƒ§, ≈ú‚Åª_tot]=0`), is non-zero (total-Casimir non-vanishing criterion), and lies in the next magnetization sector. This is the energy-preserving step that chains the per-sector Perron‚ÄìFrobenius ground states (`marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergHamiltonianS_groundState_full`) across the admissible range; it uses **no** Marshall positivity of the lowered vector (the false hypothesis of the removed ferromagnetic-ladder route). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23PFLadderLink.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_pred` / `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_pred_of_mem_predictedGS` | **Sound PF adjacent-sector ladder link (raising)** and its predicted-GS specialisation: the raising companions of `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_succ(_of_mem_predictedGS)`. `≈ú‚Å∫_tot` maps a sector-`K` Heisenberg eigenvector (total-Casimir away from the raising-kernel value `(card¬∑N/2‚àíK)(card¬∑N/2‚àíK+1)`) to a non-zero same-energy eigenvector in the previous sector; predicted-GS membership in sector `M+1` strictly above the left endpoint discharges the Casimir hypotheses via `tasaki23_predictedCasimirValue_ne_raising_kernel_value_of_mem_of_left_lt`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23PFLadderLink.lean`) |
| `sublatticeMagSubspaceS` / `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS` (with `mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS_iff`, `basisVecS_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS`, `sublatticeMagSubspaceS_disjoint`) | **Sublattice magnetization grading**: the `≈ú_A^(3)`-eigenspaces `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M` and the diagonal eigenvalue `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS A œÉ = ‚àë_{x‚ààA}(N/2‚àíœÉ_x)`. Disjointness of distinct eigenvalues and basis-state membership (Submodule membership uses mathlib's standard `.zero_mem`/`.add_mem`/`.smul_mem`/‚Ä¶ directly ‚Äî no dedicated wrapper lemmas). Sublattice analogue of `magSubspaceS`/`magEigenvalueS`; scaffold (Issue #3658, PR 5) for the sublattice highest-weight argument bounding `(≈ú_A)¬≤`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMagnetization.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_groundState_sublattice_casimir_re_le` | **Sublattice Casimir bounds for the toy ground state**: the per-sector Marshall-positive toy GS is a joint Casimir eigenvector (#3657) whose `(≈ú_A)¬≤`/`(≈ú_¬¨A)¬≤` eigenvalues `Œ≥_A, Œ≥_B` obey `Œ≥_A.re ‚â§ s_A(s_A+1)`, `Œ≥_B.re ‚â§ s_B(s_B+1)` (apply the spectral max bound #3672 to the non-zero Marshall-dressed embedding). Discharges two hypotheses of the toy total-Casimir pin #3676 (Issue #3674, PR 3). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23StructuralPFJointCasimir.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_extremal_sector_magnetization_re_eq_predicted` | **Extremal sector realizes the predicted total spin**: for the left-endpoint sector `M = min(|A|,|¬¨A|)¬∑N`, the magnetization eigenvalue `|V|¬∑N/2 ‚àí M` has real part `= tasaki23PredictedTotalSpin A N = |s_A ‚àí s_B|`. Supplies the extremal-sector hypothesis `hM` of the toy total-Casimir pin #3676 (Issue #3674, PR 4). Distinct `k_A` give distinct `A`-magnetizations, yielding (with non-vanishing) the linear independence of the diagonal family (Issue #3687, PR 13). Generalizes the single-sublattice ladder identity (#3693) to the joint iterate via the joint maximal `A`-Casimir (#3698) and `A`-magnetization `s_A ‚àí k_A` (#3700). | `toy_joint_eigenvector_energy_re_ge` | **Toy minimum-energy bound `ƒ§_toy ‚â• E`** (option (a) ‚Äî coupled bound #3723 + arithmetic #3716): a joint `(≈ú_tot)¬≤`/`(≈ú_A)¬≤`/`(≈ú_¬¨A)¬≤`-eigenvector `œà` (eigenvalues `Œ≥_tot, Œ≥_A, Œ≥_B`) in a magnetization level (with `|¬¨A| ‚â§ |A|`) has toy energy `(Œ≥_tot ‚àí Œ≥_A ‚àí Œ≥_B).re ‚â• E = (s_A‚àís_B)(s_A‚àís_B+1) ‚àí s_A(s_A+1) ‚àí s_B(s_B+1)`. Writing the (non-negative #3724, `‚â§ s(s+1)` #3672) sublattice Casimir eigenvalues as `a(a+1)`, `b(b+1)` (real spins via the square root), the coupled bound (#3723) gives `Œ≥_tot.re ‚â• |a‚àíb|(|a‚àíb|+1)`, hence `(Œ≥_tot‚àíŒ≥_A‚àíŒ≥_B).re ‚â• |a‚àíb|(|a‚àíb|+1) ‚àí a(a+1) ‚àí b(b+1) = f(a,b) ‚â• f(s_A,s_B) = E` (#3716). This is the interior-sector toy minimum-energy bound (pins `r_min(M) = E` with the predicted-energy witness #3715) (Issue #3542). | `tasaki23_toy_min_energy_arith_eq` | **Strict (equality) arithmetic core** (option (a)): if `f(a,b) = f(s_A,s_B)` on the box `0 ‚â§ a ‚â§ s_A`, `0 ‚â§ b ‚â§ s_B ‚â§ s_A` with `s_B > 0`, then `(a,b) = (s_A,s_B)` (the minimiser of `f(a,b) = |a‚àíb|(|a‚àíb|+1) ‚àí a(a+1) ‚àí b(b+1)` is the corner). When the toy energy attains its minimum `E` the toy-minimum-energy chain is tight, so this pins the toy ground state's sublattice spins to the maximal `(s_A,s_B)`, hence its total Casimir to the predicted value (Issue #3542). This is the SU(2) non-vanishing input for the inward-ladder discharge of the non-admissible-sector lower bound: an eigenvector outside the band can be moved to the band edge by `≈ú‚àì_tot` without annihilation (Issue #3542). At spin-1/2 (N=1) this is the **first truly-unconditional Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.3 closure**. The already-`h_intermediate`-free pieces (`tasaki23_eigenvalue_ge_common`, `tasaki23_toy_sector_groundEnergy_le_of_witness`, `exists_predictedEnergy_sector_eigenvector_of_mem`) are reused directly. This removes BOTH the toy-coupling restriction of PR #3891 and the vacuous-at-N=1 `h_intermediate` restriction of the older #3738 capstone. | `anisotropicHeisenbergS_unique_groundState_has_zero_magnetization` | **Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4 partial conclusion: unique GS has zero `≈ú¬≥_tot` magnetization** (Issue #3739, PR #3896): if the anisotropic Heisenberg ground-state eigenspace has `finrank ‚ÑÇ ‚â§ 1`, every non-zero ground state `Œ¶` satisfies `≈ú¬≥_tot Œ¶ = 0`. Proof: Œ¶, ≈ú¬≥_tot Œ¶, Œò Œ¶ all lie in the GS eigenspace (`H` commutes with `≈ú¬≥_tot` (#3741) and with the reflection `Œò` (#3745)). From `finrank ‚â§ 1` + Œ¶ ‚âÝ 0 and `Module.finrank_le_one_iff`, `≈ú¬≥_tot Œ¶ = Œ≥ Œ¶` and `Œò Œ¶ = Œ¥ Œ¶` for some scalars. `Œò¬≤ = 1` ‚üπ `Œ¥¬≤ = 1` ‚üπ `Œ¥ ‚âÝ 0`. Apply the anticommutation `Œò ≈ú¬≥_tot = -≈ú¬≥_tot Œò` (#3744) to Œ¶: get `Œ¥Œ≥ ‚Ä¢ Œ¶ = -Œ≥Œ¥ ‚Ä¢ Œ¶` ‚üπ `2Œ¥Œ≥ ‚Ä¢ Œ¶ = 0` ‚üπ `Œ¥Œ≥ = 0` ‚üπ `Œ≥ = 0` (since Œ¥ ‚âÝ 0). This is the second conclusion of Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4 once uniqueness is established (uniqueness itself awaits the deformation argument from the SU(2) point, separate forthcoming work). The first lemma gives the direct eigenspace identification; the second specializes PR #3896 to the SU(2) point, giving `≈ú¬≥_tot|Œ¶‚ü©=0` for any non-zero GS of the isotropic Heisenberg Hamiltonian under the GS uniqueness hypothesis. Prepares the SU(2) base case for the deformation argument: once uniqueness is established at the SU(2) point (for symmetric `|A|=|¬¨A|` via Theorem 2.2 + SU(2) singlet structure, separate forthcoming work), the `≈ú¬≥_tot|Œ¶‚ü©=0` conclusion follows immediately. When `magSumS œÉ ‚âÝ magSumS œÑ`, Nat-cast injectivity gives `magEigenvalueS œÉ ‚âÝ magEigenvalueS œÑ`, so `H œÉ œÑ = 0`. Direct consequence of `magSubspaceS_disjoint` (existing): for `a ‚Ä¢ Œ¶‚ÇÅ + b ‚Ä¢ Œ¶‚ÇÇ = 0`, get `a ‚Ä¢ Œ¶‚ÇÅ ‚àà magSubspaceS Œõ N M‚ÇÅ ‚à© magSubspaceS Œõ N M‚ÇÇ = ‚ä•`, so `a ‚Ä¢ Œ¶‚ÇÅ = 0`, giving `a = 0` (since Œ¶‚ÇÅ ‚âÝ 0), and similarly `b = 0`. Key technical lemma for the sector-decomposition + reflection argument toward Theorem 2.4 obligation (2.a): non-zero vectors in three pairwise distinct sectors form a 3-LI family, contradicting obligation (1)'s `finrank ‚â§ 2`. Proof: PR #3902 gives `{Œ¶, Œ®, Œò Œ®}` linearly independent; `Œò Œ®` is also at energy Œº via Œò commutes with H (#3745); the 3-LI family lifts to the eigenspace `‚Ü•E` via `LinearIndependent.of_comp E.subtype`; `LinearIndependent.fintype_card_le_finrank` gives `3 ‚â§ finrank ‚ÑÇ ‚Ü•E`, contradicting `finrank ‚â§ 2` via `omega`. This is the direct contradiction in the SU(2) symmetric `finrank ‚â§ 1` argument. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43-44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem24FinrankLeTwoContradiction.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_eigvec_nonadmis_projection_zero` | **Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4 eigvec non-admissible projection = 0** (Issue #3739, PR #3904): under `finrank ‚â§ 2` at `Œº` + admissible eigvec exists, any eigvec Œ® at Œº has zero `magSectorEmbedding (magSectorRestriction (M := M) Œ®)` for every non-admissible sector index `M` (with `2M ‚âÝ |Œõ|¬∑N`). Proof: the projection is an eigvec at Œº via PR #3899 and lies in `magSubspaceS Œõ N M'` with `M' = |Œõ|¬∑N/2 - M ‚âÝ 0` (non-admissible ≈ú¬≥_tot eigenvalue). If non-zero, PR #3903 derives False; hence the projection is `0`. Penultimate step toward the SU(2) symmetric `finrank ‚â§ 1`: combined with `eq_sum_magSectorEmbedding_magSectorRestriction`, the eigenspace at Œº is contained in the admissible sector. Final piece toward the SU(2) symmetric `finrank ‚â§ 1` argument: combined with within-admissible-sector PF `finrank = 1`, the full Hilbert space finrank at Œº is `‚â§ 1`. | `parityReachableS_concentrate_AB` | **Combined A+B concentration to a two-site canonical form** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3806): composing #3804 (A-concentration via `b‚ÇÄ` as temp) with #3805 (B-concentration via the post-A-concentration `a‚ÇÄ ‚àà A` as temp).  The resulting `œÉ_AB` has `œÉ_AB a‚ÇÄ = (œÉ a‚ÇÄ).val + Œ£_{aSitesExcept} (œÉ s).val` (total A-mass), `œÉ_AB b‚ÇÄ = (œÉ b‚ÇÄ).val + Œ£_{bSitesExcept} (œÉ s).val` (total B-mass), and `œÉ_AB = 0` on `aSitesExcept ‚à™ bSitesExcept`.  Hypotheses: `A a‚ÇÄ`, `¬¨ A b‚ÇÄ`, A/B totals fit at their targets, plus `(œÉ b‚ÇÄ).val + 1 ‚â§ N` (room at `b‚ÇÄ` for A-concentration) and `(œÉ a‚ÇÄ).val + Œ£_{aSitesExcept} (œÉ s).val + 1 ‚â§ N` (room at the post-concentration `a‚ÇÄ` for B-concentration). A semantically-renamed specialisation of #3801 used as a black-box in the canonical-to-canonical chain of (d) reachability totality. | `matrix_similar_eigenspace_inter_finrank_eq` | **(g.3) similarity-invariant intersection eigenspace finrank** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3835): generalises `matrix_similar_eigenspace_finrank_eq` (#3746 family) to intersections with a second operator's eigenspace, provided the similarity `U` commutes with that operator `Q`. Given `U * Uinv = 1`, `Uinv * U = 1`, `H' = Uinv * H * U`, AND `Q * U = U * Q`, then `finrank ‚ÑÇ (eig H' Œº ‚äì ker(Q=c)) = finrank ‚ÑÇ (eig H Œº ‚äì ker(Q=c))`. The core technical step for transferring per-parity-block PF bounds from the dressed `ƒ§'_dressed` to the bare `ƒ§'`, since the Marshall sign diagonal `Œò_A` commutes with the diagonal `magParityDiagS P`. Result for `p < 2`: `‚àÉ ŒΩ : ‚Ñù, finrank ‚ÑÇ (eig (axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS J lam D N) (ŒΩ : ‚ÑÇ) ‚äì ker(P=(-1)^p)) ‚â§ 1`. The remaining gap to unconditional obligation (1) is the ŒΩ-sector matching (PF eigenvalues of even/odd sectors coinciding at the ground-state energy), which is mathematically distinct (Hermitian spectral minimisation) and deferred. Combined with the forward direction (#3832), gives the finrank equality `finrank ‚ÑÇ (eig M.submatrix ŒΩ) = finrank ‚ÑÇ (eig M ŒΩ ‚äì ker(P=(-1)^p))` for `p < 2`. The equality unlocks ŒΩ-sector matching arguments: per-block submatrix eig finrank equals the full eig ‚äì ker(P) finrank, so per-sector PF simplicity (#3834/#3836) directly transfers to constraints on the FULL eigenspace at that energy. For finite-dim `T, P` with `T‚àòP = P‚àòT` and `P‚àòP = id`, the `T`-eigenspace at any `Œº` splits as a **direct sum** across the two `P`-eigenspaces `¬±1`. The `‚â§` was tight because the two intersected `¬±1` eigenspaces of `P` are disjoint (a single eigenvector cannot have two distinct `P`-eigenvalues), so `Submodule.finrank_sup_add_finrank_inf_eq` collapses to a sum (inf = 0). The PR exposes no-argument wrappers for the axis-swap eigenspace-dimension equality, the conditional parity-block and submatrix-block `‚â§ 2` bounds, the min-block `‚â§ 2` bound, the Hermitian-minimum equality, and the structural general-N capstone `anisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_unconditional_general`. This discharges the explicit axis-swap-unitary argument in the strict case-(i) degeneracy chain; the SU(2)-uniqueness target wrappers are handled in the later obligation-(2) row, and Tasaki case (ii) now has conditional, strict-gap, and no-full-finrank bridges recorded in the case-(ii) rows, with the strict-gap derivation still explicit. | `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_balanced_sector_pf` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_groundState_zero_magnetization_of_balanced_sector_pf` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_balanced_sector_pf_at_target` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_balanced_eq_full` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_groundState_zero_magnetization_of_balanced_eq_full` | **General spin-S target conditional bridge with balanced-sector PF discharged** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): packages the final linear-algebra step after the general spin-S `finrank ‚â§ 2` degeneracy capstone. If the target full ground eigenspace has `finrank ‚â§ 2`, the balanced sector attains the full ground energy, and the balanced-sector ground eigenspace has `finrank ‚â§ 1`, then the full target ground eigenspace has `finrank ‚â§ 1`; the companion wrapper then gives zero total `≈ú¬≥` magnetization for every non-zero target ground state. The new general spin-S PF wrapper chooses a finite strict diagonal shift and applies the existing anisotropic sector PF/minimum theorem, so the PF-free target wrappers no longer take an explicit balanced-sector PF callback. The direct balanced-sector/full-ground equality input is superseded by the strict-gap wrappers in the next row; for case (ii), the conditional, strict-gap, and no-full-finrank bridges are recorded in the case-(ii) rows and the strict-gap derivation remains explicit. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43‚Äì44 (files `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSTargetConditionalBridge.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSBalancedSectorPFTarget.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergParametricPath_in_case_ii_region` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_balanced_eq_full` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_balanced_eq_full` | **General spin-S case-(ii) path and conditional target bridge** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): proves that the deformation path from `(1,0)` to a target satisfying `1 <= lambda`, `D <= 0` remains in the case-(ii) region on `[0,1]`, then repackages the balanced-sector PF target bridge under case-(ii) hypotheses. These first case-(ii) wrappers keep the remaining target inputs explicit as balanced-sector/full-ground equality plus full `finrank <= 2`; they do not reuse the case-(i) axis-swap sign argument. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43--44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIConditionalBridge.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_strict_gap` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_strict_gap` | **General spin-S case-(ii) target bridge from strict sector gap** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): composes the arbitrary-parameter strict-gap target bridge with the case-(ii) theorem surface. The direct balanced-sector/full-ground equality input from the first case-(ii) wrappers is replaced by the strict gap `E_balanced < E_M` over every non-balanced non-empty magnetization sector under `1 <= lambda`, `D <= 0`; the remaining full ground eigenspace `finrank <= 2` input stays explicit in this intermediate wrapper. This is a case-(ii) bridge step, not the final case-(ii) endpoint. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43--44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIStrictGapBridge.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_groundState_mem_balanced_sector_of_strict_gap` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_strict_gap_no_full_le_two` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_groundState_zero_magnetization_of_strict_gap_no_full_le_two` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_strict_gap_no_full_le_two` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_strict_gap_no_full_le_two` | **General spin-S target bridge from strict sector gap without the full `finrank <= 2` input** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): proves that if the balanced sector attains the full ground energy and every non-balanced non-empty magnetization sector has strictly larger minimum, then every full ground vector lies in the balanced magnetization sector. This uses the sector-projection eigenvector bridge and `hermitian_eigenspace_eq_bot_of_real_lt_min`, not the case-(i) parity-block `finrank <= 2` contradiction. Composing this containment with balanced-sector Perron--Frobenius simplicity gives full target uniqueness and zero total magnetization from strict sector gap alone; the case-(ii)-named wrappers keep only the theorem-facing `1 <= lambda`, `D <= 0` hypotheses plus the strict-gap input. This removes the explicit case-(ii) full `finrank <= 2` input; deriving the case-(ii) strict gap remains future work. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43--44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSTargetFromStrictGapNoFullFinrank.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_strict_gap_all_M_of_crossing_contradiction` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_crossing_contradiction` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_crossing_contradiction` | **General spin-S case-(ii) strict-gap and target bridge from a crossing contradiction callback** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): compresses the remaining case-(ii) strict-gap derivation to one theorem-shaped callback. If every non-balanced target crossing `E_M <= E_balanced` contradicts the case-(ii) hypotheses `1 <= lambda`, `D <= 0`, then order duality gives the strict gap `E_balanced < E_M` for every non-balanced non-empty sector. The target wrappers feed that strict gap to the no-full-finrank bridge above, so the public endpoint surface no longer exposes either balanced/full equality, full `finrank <= 2`, or strict gap directly; the remaining mathematical task is proving the crossing contradiction callback. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43--44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIStrictGapFromCrossing.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_crossing_contradiction_of_path_crossing_contradiction` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_path_crossing_contradiction` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_path_crossing_contradiction` | **General spin-S case-(ii) target bridge from a path crossing contradiction callback** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): evaluates the case-(ii) deformation path at `t = 1`. If every crossing along the path inside the region `1 <= lambda`, `D <= 0` contradicts the path hypotheses, then a target crossing `E_M(lambda,D) <= E_balanced(lambda,D)` is impossible. The target wrappers feed this contradiction to the crossing-callback bridge above. The remaining mathematical task is now the path crossing contradiction callback itself. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43--44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIICrossingFromPath.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_path_crossing_contradiction_of_set_contradiction` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_set_contradiction` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_set_contradiction` | **General spin-S case-(ii) target bridge from a crossing-set non-emptiness contradiction** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): a pointwise path crossing is a witness that `perMCrossingSet M ‚à© Icc 0 1` is non-empty. Therefore, if every non-balanced non-empty sector has contradictory crossing-set non-emptiness, every path crossing is impossible, and the target wrappers feed that path contradiction to the path-callback bridge above. The remaining mathematical task is now the crossing-set non-emptiness contradiction callback, matching the first-crossing `hne` surface used by the existing scaffold. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43--44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIPathCrossingFromSet.lean`) |

Thus a contradiction at that first-crossing point rules out crossing-set non-emptiness, and the target wrappers feed the result to the crossing-set bridge above. | `axisSwappedParityBlockPFMinAt` / `axisSwappedParityBlockPFMinPath` / `axisSwappedParityBlockStrictRawSupportPath` / `axisSwappedParityBlockLambdaOneRawSupportPath` / `axisSwappedParityBlockDZeroRawSupportPath` / `exists_parityBlock_dressed_diag_strict_upper_bound` / `axisSwappedParityBlockStrictRawSupportPath_of_reachability` / `axisSwappedParityBlockLambdaOneRawSupportPath_of_reachability` / `axisSwappedParityBlockDZeroRawSupportPath_of_reachability` / `axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_submatrix_finrank_le_one_at_full_min_of_pf_min` / `caseII_axisSwapped_submatrix_blocks_path_of_pf_min` / `axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_submatrix_pf_min_path_of_caseII_raw_support` / `caseII_axisSwapped_parityBlockPFMinPath_of_raw_support` / `caseII_axisSwapped_submatrix_blocks_path_of_raw_support_pf_min` / `caseII_axisSwapped_parityBlockPFMinPath_of_reachability` / `caseII_axisSwapped_submatrix_blocks_path_of_reachability_pf_min` / `caseII_coupling_eq_zero_of_not_bipartiteCompleteGraph_adj` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_block_pf_min_path` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_block_pf_min_path` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_raw_support_pf_min_path` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_raw_support_pf_min_path` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_reachability_pf_min_path` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_reachability_pf_min_path` | **General spin-S case-(ii) target bridge from pathwise parity-block PF/min callbacks** (Tasaki ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): turns the previous row's full-ground-energy parity-block simplicity input into the existing bare-submatrix conditional form. For each parity block, if a PF eigenvalue `ŒΩ` has `finrank <= 1` and equals that block's Hermitian minimum, then the block has `finrank <= 1` at the full anisotropic ground energy: if the full ground energy equals the block minimum, this is the conditional theorem; if it is lower, `hermitian_eigenspace_eq_bot_of_real_lt_min` makes the block eigenspace zero. The path wrapper supplies both parity blocks along `Œ≥(t)`, and the target wrappers feed the result to the block-path bridge above. The raw-support abbreviations name the strict, `lambda = 1`, and `D = 0` reachability/PF inputs for one parity block. The one-block selector chooses among the fixed raw-support consumers by the case-(ii) path inequalities and leaves only the corner `(lambda,D)=(1,0)` PF/min callback explicit; the even/odd raw-support wrapper applies this selector to both parity blocks, and the full-min wrapper feeds those callbacks into the block-path PF/min transfer. The raw-support target wrappers derive the support-zero hypothesis from `hJself` and `hJbip`, then expose target uniqueness and zero magnetization directly from the raw-support input surface. The reachability wrappers use finite parity blocks to supply strict diagonal shifts automatically, so the public reachability-level target wrappers require only the even and odd strict, ion-only, and bond-only reachability totality hypotheses plus the two explicit corner callbacks. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43--44 (foundational parity-block PF/min path lemmas in `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIBlockPFMinCore.lean`; the packaged `caseII_coupling_eq_zero_of_not_bipartiteCompleteGraph_adj` helper and the six `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_*` endpoints in `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIBlockPFMin.lean`) |

The bipartite complete graph totality wrappers then supply the block-level reachability hypotheses from `hA_ne`, `hB_ne`, and the spin-size hypotheses (`1 <= N` for strict and bond-only, `2 <= N` for ion-only). The final wrappers feed those totality results into the case-(ii) reachability-level PF/min bridge; the later corner and total-reachability target bridges reuse this layer to close the current `2 <= N` case-(ii) endpoint. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ¬ß2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43--44 (files `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIBlockReachabilityCore.lean` for the lifts + totality reachability bridge + `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIBlockReachability.lean` for the case-(ii) wrappers, split for build speed) |

The wrapper dispatches `-1 < Œª < 1`, `D >= 0` to the `D_nonneg` case-(i) endpoint; `Œª = 1`, `D >= 0` to the explicit SU(2) endpoint when `D = 0` and to the `lambda_one_D_pos` endpoint when `D > 0`; and `Œª >= 1`, `D <= 0` to the case-(ii) total-reachability endpoint. The second theorem applies the packaged uniqueness statement and the existing uniqueness-implies-zero-magnetization theorem, yielding `≈ú^3_tot Œ¶ = 0` for every non-zero target ground state in the covered parameter region.
